It's just that if you don't know, you'll be in agony and come back almost dead and haggard. It's like watching a rerun of a comeback victory in a Korea-Japan game, mistaking it for a live broadcast. We know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him, who have been called according to his purpose. -Romans 8:28

David's path. Just as Jacob had Bethel and the disciples had Galilee, there is only one way to live: to flee to God. No matter how cleverly the world advises you to flee to the mountains like a bird, even if the foundation crumbles and there is no place for the righteous to stand, ultimately, the place to return to is God. Then God said to Jacob, "Arise, go up to Bethel and dwell there, and make an altar to God who appeared to you when you fled from your brother Esau." -Genesis 35:1 Why do you say, like a bird, "Flee to your mountain"? For I have taken refuge in the Lord. -Psalm 11:1

Love over confirmation. When I couldn't contact my parents in my home country for a while, they always welcomed me and worried about me when I called. In a romantic relationship, that would be met with "Get lost!" A mother does not fall into temptation. This is because she is filled with a desire to love rather than to confirm love.

Hurt feelings. One of the greatest enemies of humanity and faith is "hurt feelings." If it weren't for these hurt feelings, the church would have been united, and world evangelization would have been achieved much earlier. If the devil has "hurt feelings" as a secret weapon, God's people have the asymmetric power of "gratitude." The Christian life is the clash of these two.

Prayer that overcomes worry. You are told not to worry, but you say it's hard because worry comes naturally. Of course, worry comes. That is why we pray and make supplications, and then the power of thanksgiving comes, and we ultimately receive an answer in peace. You just need to pray enough so that worry doesn't come.
A fine line. Do not grieve over unfulfilled desires, but be thankful for the sufficient grace you have already received. Hell will become heaven, and life will become life.
The Lord's mercy. He knows my constitution and remembers that I am but dust. Therefore, He allows me to live and see the glory of God, not to die.

The Gospel preached by John. In the Gospel of John, John tells unbelievers to believe in Jesus. In his epistles, John exhorts believers to love. In Revelation, he tells those living in the end times to "overcome." Believe the gospel, receive eternal life, enjoy that eternal life, and be victorious.
The reason for mercy. God the Father has mercy on us as a father has mercy on his child (Psalm 103). Because He remembers. For He knows our frame; He remembers that we are but dust. -Psalm 103:18

Jesus' clear purpose. Jesus had a very clear purpose for coming to this earth: to give life and to obtain salvation. Jesus was not concerned with anything other than the purpose of the cross. However, He became angry when His purpose of dying and the reason for bearing the cross were opposed. Jesus turned and said to Peter, "Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me; you do not have in mind the concerns of God, but merely human concerns." -Matthew 16:22-23

My symptoms, God's prescription. The Lord acknowledges that we may be distressed for a while due to the difficulties we are currently experiencing. However, He also wants us to know that they cannot be compared to the glory that will be revealed in us. For I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing with the glory that is to be revealed in us. -Romans 8:18
The reason why it is unexpectedly difficult. Let go so that the Lord's grace may work within you (1 Cor 15:9,10). Then you will experience Him holding you up.
Zeal vs. Letting Go. Are you confused by the conflict between these two concepts?

What is more needed. What we need is not the assurance of success. Even if we fail, we should not deny the Lord, but rather give glory through faith. Is that not how we will live, testifying that 'by the grace of God I am what I am'?

Today's choice tells the future result. Everything is given by grace. It is by faith that we respond and receive it. Even what seems difficult and impossible, we must go forward believing in the omnipotence of the Lord's grace. Let grace and faith, not myself, decide.

Faith still works today. The chief eunuch treated Daniel kindly and favored him not because he was a person of good character. Why would someone who survived the intrigues and schemes of the ancient Babylonian royal court and gained status risk his life for the requests of orphaned children brought as captives? (Daniel 1:10) It was grace. Because Daniel was a man of faith. Faith works even in unbelievers. God caused Daniel to receive favor and compassion from the chief eunuch. -Daniel 1:9

Pioneer and Perfecter of Faith. When great damage is expected, reducing losses by giving up early is called 'management.' However, looking forward to resurrection while knowing you will die is called 'faith.'

The captain who is not flustered. Skilled fishermen panic inside a sinking ship during a storm. But Jesus is not flustered at all. It is not because He is the Son of God, but because He had 'faith' as a human being who needed to sleep because He was tired. Jesus was in the stern, sleeping on a cushion. The disciples woke him and said, "Teacher, do you not care that we are perishing?" -Mark 4:38

The strength to rise again. Resurrection is life that presupposes death. Therefore, the power of resurrection is experienced by overcoming my desperate death. Is there any resurrection without the cross?

How to give glory. It is easy to think that giving glory to God means receiving an award and flowers at an awards ceremony and saying "Glory to God," or testifying about worldly success. However, true glory is given when faith does not weaken in a situation where everything has collapsed. He did not weaken in faith when he considered his own body, which was as good as dead, since he was about a hundred years old, or when he considered the barrenness of Sarah's womb. No unbelief made him waver concerning the promise of God, but he grew strong in his faith as he gave glory to God. -Romans 4:19,20
When faith is needed. Faith is not needed when making money, getting accepted to school, or gaining popularity in the world. It is truly needed when it is 'time to win.' Win. Win by faith.
Please believe in the Lord's work even in the midst of a collapse that seems so certain. That is the situation where faith is truly required, and it is the faith the Lord desires.

One fortunate thing is that when I am too weary to go on, God has given me fellow intercessors. I have been that for someone, and someone has been that for me. When you are alone and your heart is breaking, someone is praying for you – someone is praying for you.
Faith is not hearing what you want to hear, but hearing the word you need to hear. That is truth.
A person with a firm foundation is not shaken by the fiercest storms and is at peace. Most people look at outward appearances, but God loves the person who works from the heart.
Even if disappointing evaluations are given, do not dwell on them. More importantly, even if you receive good evaluations, do not rely on them. Rely only on the Lord.

Do not quench the fire on the altar. Israel does not fall through illness or war. Even in idolatry and corruption, they have turned back and experienced revival. But when the fire on the altar went out, they perished, no matter how strong they were. Under no circumstances, for any reason, should you quench the fire on the altar. The enemy will persistently desire that above all else, even at the cost of everything. Keep the fire burning on the altar continually, that it may not be quenched. - Leviticus 6:13
Grace is precious, but it is received freely. Something so valuable cannot be repaid. Therefore, it is received by grace. The only fitting response is to be overwhelmed with emotion and give thanks.
If you cannot afford to lose it, you cannot protect it. For God spared not his only begotten son... - Genesis 22:12

The characteristics of fallen humanity after the fruit of the knowledge of good and evil are 'shifting blame' and 'self-centeredness.' Thus, even in small matters, we have become beings who pursue a sense of victimhood and selfish gain. The cross begins with recognizing one's sinfulness and repentance, and by crucifying one's selfish self.
Believing in Jesus means being saved from the idea that one must succeed in the world. It is to be so overwhelmed by salvation that success or failure in the world becomes insignificant.

Disappointment and grumbling are very difficult subjects. If these two subjects were passed well, world evangelization would have been advanced by about a thousand years. Let us believe in God and love souls. Then, at least, we will not be hurt.

Moses, a former convict with a fiery temper; Gideon, with an inferiority complex; David, possessing a fatal moral flaw – all of these are God's works. God's power is never limited by human flaws. Whether God uses me or not is not a matter of my weaknesses, but purely of God's sovereignty. He is the one who puts precious treasure in a common, chipped clay pot. We have this treasure in clay jars, so that the excellency of the power may be of God, and not of us. - 2 Corinthians 4:7
I often see people boasting about reading the Bible 500 times. That's impressive. Boasting about eating 500 bowls of rice...
Not a single person in the Bible knew their destiny. Their lives went contrary to their desires, but they lived by God's hand with a vision on God's level.
The Bible does not belittle personal effort. However, the work of the Lord is not done, but accomplished. It is not achieved by effort alone, but by grace.
While everyone focuses on the capable, God takes the truly weak and insignificant to receive glory.
The Bible does not say, "Do not sin, and then you will be a saint." Instead, it says, "Since you have become a saint, do not sin."
The Bible speaks of life as a vessel. The Lord is asking, not "Who are you?" but "What is inside you?" The material of the vessel is not important; the contents are.
When I read the Bible, I relate to David and see King Saul as my adversary. However, a wise person reads it differently. From King Saul, I must realize my own wickedness. All evil begins with pride.

If one wing is broken, be faithful with the remaining wing, fulfilling the purpose for which it was left. If the other wing is also broken, gather your weak wings to cover your inadequate face and look only to the Lord.

The Bible likens us to vessels. The sole function of a vessel is to contain. If a vessel does anything other than contain, it is not a vessel. Receiving, believing, and hosting Jesus are the purposes of a vessel.

Success is having more than others. It becomes sin when you possess what you were not given, rather than what you were meant to have. Doing what you are moved to do is victory. Even if there is nothing left, if it is the Lord's will, doing it is obedience, and that is true victory.
Interpretation is faith. It is not the situation that torments me, but my interpretation of it. Fallen leaves fall without thought, but for some they become romance, and for others, sorrow.

We do not cling to the Lord's blood to succeed. If you cling to the Lord's blood, you have already won, regardless of the outcome. If you squeeze your lips and your heart, what comes out? Is it the blood, or something else?

God's grace governs me, renews me, frees me from sin and death, transforms me, and fills me. Since God is almighty, His grace is also almighty, and I truly believe He will do this for me and my fellow workers.

Saints win through confession. It is a powerful force that restores from deep wounds and turns around community crises. We must speak words that change the situation and claim it with the blood. "And they overcame him by the blood of the Lamb and by the word of their testimony, for they did not love their lives to the death." (Revelation 12:11)

When saints go through tribulation, their true nature is revealed. They do not lose their freshness even in difficulties. This is because their motivation is not the superficial charm and recognition the world offers, but the strength God supplies. Those deeply rooted in the Word will never fall with the power to ignore.
No soldier gets entangled in civilian affairs, but rather seeks to please him who enlisted him.
Saints are not those who avoid tribulation by chance, but those who overcome and pass through it. If you acknowledge this from the beginning, you can overcome with boldness.

The word "saint" implies being transformed into holiness. When a barren place becomes holy, the cause of tears and brokenness changes. This is because the Lord lives in the place where I used to live. When the Lord becomes the master, I will surely listen to His word.

The fire of the Holy Spirit and the Word compels us to boldly abandon what we cherish. Letting go, self-control, is power. A person of God is like a lion grasping its prey, possessing absolute time and focus.
The Holy Spirit does not work through any amount of sincerity, threats, or comfort. He works only in the name of Jesus.
By the anointing of the Holy Spirit, the Lord will accomplish it.

* If you feel weary and burdened, the burden is not from the Lord. It is not a burden of gentleness and humility. Jesus' yoke is light, giving rest and peace, not because it is a heavy burden to protect oneself, but because we walk with the Lord, dying to ourselves, in gentleness and humility. "Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy and my burden is light." - Matthew 11:28-30
Without being filled with the Holy Spirit, worship and service are nothing more than burdensome religious acts. We will earnestly pray and prepare, and God will give us the promised Holy Spirit.

Being filled with the Holy Spirit is not about receiving gifts and manifesting power. It is confessing and asking that Jesus alone is sufficient. If it is true that you have died on the cross, you will seek Jesus alone as your life.

KakaoTalk Question 1) In today's Quiet Time, it said that God regretted appointing Saul as king. But if God, who is omniscient and omnipotent, knew beforehand, can He regret it? The word "regret" also implies some unexpectedness. In the Bible, when God says "regret" (nacham), it is connected to human stubbornness and corruption, meaning to grieve deeply, to lament, to suffer emotional damage. In this case, it means God patiently waits for sinners, and when the time comes to discipline them, He laments and grieves. When "regret" (nacham) is connected to God's people who are already suffering punishment and pain, it can be interpreted in two ways. First, it means God "feels compassion" for His suffering people. Second, seeing the extremity of their suffering, He "changes His mind" to withdraw the punishment. In this case, contrary to the first, it means the period of punishment for His people has ended, and the time of grace has returned. Simple examples) 1. An extreme approach that God regrets because He is not omniscient and omnipotent. 2. An extreme and cruel approach that God is omniscient and omnipotent, so it is fitting for humans to be miserably destroyed (what goes around comes around). 3. God is omniscient and omnipotent, so there is no regret, but as a God of love, He deeply laments the destruction of sinners resulting from their stubbornness. This kind of expression is theologically called anthropomorphism. It is a way of expressing God's unfathomable depth with human emotional language.

* As important as fervent prayer is, so are the thoughts that align with that prayer. The saying, "Whatever you ask for in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours," is not mere rhetoric; it means to mentally visualize and give thanks and praise for what you have asked for, believing it will be accomplished.
A mature person is sensitive to their own faults and sins, and insensitive to the faults and sins of others. Someone who is not mature is the opposite.

We live in an age that treats sexual temptation lightly. Joseph not only refused but went to prison. His confession was, "How then can I do this great wickedness and sin against God?" We should not take lightly the sin that Joseph, a man of God, so guarded against, even to the point of imprisonment. Everything done in the name of love, or in the name of youth, can never be justified. Saul was a man who was ruined by justification (1 Samuel 15). Sexual corruption will inevitably find that sin and ruin the person. God judges with righteousness. All sin is forgiven at the cross. However, justification bypasses forgiveness. God's amazing plan is aborted due to arrogant pride, and pride leads to self-justification, blaming others, and seeking human approval more than God's. (justify one's actions, blame others, concerned with keeping up appearances than with what God thought of him) May you become like Josephs, who love God, repent, and turn from sin, saving yourselves and the world.

* A Life Without Calculation The world teaches that life is about probability. Therefore, it wastes our efforts on the wrong things, namely calculating probabilities and imagining outcomes. If life were about probability, the Bible would be full of mathematics and statistics. We live by faith. "The righteous will live by faith."

* Futile Attacks Trials and tribulations may come, growl, and tear apart my weak body and mind, leaving me in tatters. But do not fear. I am no longer who I was; the new me is in the Lord. Satan will only confirm our victory.

We learn more through mistakes than through achievements. That is why even in places where we are led by the Lord, 'famine' can occur. It is not that we were led incorrectly. Every sitting and rising, every tear and meditation of a Christian is not in vain; it is all seen, remembered, and will work for good.

Achieving something brings joy. But only for a moment. So we keep setting new goals. My푯대 (goal/aim) is not achievement, but His presence and fellowship. It is what I have been captured by Christ Jesus for. That experience changes life and makes us pursue that direction continuously. That is revival.

* When Faith Falters, We Begin the First Lesson. The Lord is looking for people of faith at this time. And He asks, "Do you truly believe in me?" There is a purpose to the storm, and that purpose is to test our complete faith. Will we look at the situation and calculate, or will we stand at the cliff's edge, whether we live or die, and look only to the Lord? If you are not at peace, you are already in the midst of the test.
Never agree with worldly advice. If you do not agree to the end, you will live according to God's counsel. No matter what anyone says...

There is a reason for every 'repetition' in the world. There is also a reason for the trials I repeatedly fall into. That is my weakness and something that needs to be corrected. The subject may change, but the same type of 'emotional state' or 'victim mentality' that keeps recurring is more of a problem with oneself than with others. Before God gives great blessings, revival, restoration, and healing, He gives one gift first: repentance, the realization that "I am a sinner." No one will see the Lord without repentance.

To the eyes of the world, living by faith seems unsophisticated and incomprehensible. Naturally. How can someone living on earth understand the style of someone living in heaven? Those who value credentials can never understand the depth of a story. We have His story (history).

* Sweet Worry and Noble Knowledge Worry and anxiety have power and quickly seize the heart and situation, dragging them into negativity. They are so powerful that they are difficult to resist, making one dejected, unwise, and prone to avoiding the situation. There is only one way to refuse such strong and sweet invitations: with the noble knowledge of Jesus, which renders all relative values to the level of refuse.

Worldly people are chased and herd together; they can never wait. A person of faith consistently lives the path they are meant to walk. Because we have prayed, we can wait. Because we know He will accomplish it, we have the 'power of waiting.'

We do not compromise (harmonize faith and greed) as worldly people do, and as many believers do. Because even if we wanted to, it is fundamentally impossible to mix them. When the value and treasure of Jesus enter the heart, all other things become rotten values and recede.

* Reasons Not to Worry The Lord commands us not to worry. Worrying and fretting means focusing on what we cannot do. These are areas where the Lord must intervene and give grace, but we try to calculate and imagine them ourselves. Worry is not receiving the Lord's voice but giving our hearts to Satan. Therefore, the decisions that follow worry are all worthless things that we regret with a heavy heart.

Worldly thoughts and human thoughts are non-existent thoughts and have no communion. They are just thoughts. However, the thoughts of Jesus make Jesus' presence real and allow us to experience God who personally comes amidst the longing of His people.
The best and proven way to navigate worldly temptations and trials when they come is to live by following the Spirit and making heavenly choices. Then you will not bear the fruit of darkness.

Those who seek worldly approval will be shipwrecked in their faith. Worldly approval is pride and a great temptation that is difficult to overcome. However, those who live by faith are comforted from above. Whatever they encounter, they will not fear and will overcome. - Hymn 434

Worldly wisdom calculates. This is because there is always the fear of needing to avoid danger. However, a person of faith trusts in the Lord's wisdom and is bold. The original author has already completed all calculations before the foundation of the world.

The world and Satan challenged Jesus' sonship with God. They told Him to prove it if He could. A life spent proving oneself is exhausting. The Lord did not need to prove anything to the world because He received testimony from God the Father. Our identity naturally manifests when we abide with the Lord. "Be still, and know that I am God." May you live a life of testimony, not of proof.

* Superstition and Faith Wanting God to bless a decision we have made is superstition. Faith is choosing what God blesses, even if it is difficult. Living by superstition will surely lead to ruin, but living by faith will see glory. Aaron took the gold rings from them and fashioned them with a craftsman's tool and made it into an idol of a calf. Then they said, "These are your gods, Israel, who brought you up out of Egypt." - Exodus 32:4
When the world and darkness approach, cry out like this: "I am the one who loves Jesus the most!"

* The Urge to Deviate from the Focus The devil's purpose is to steal, kill, and destroy. There is no reason to agree with such intentions. He wants to get us away from the cross at all costs because he knows the consequences better than anyone. "The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full." - John 10:10
Even though there are many contradictions in the world and within us, only love can resolve them.

We must let go of the advantageous positions that the world considers important and trust only in the Lord. When we cling more to our own advantage than to the Lord, God tells us to let it go. If we let go of our advantage, we hear less about the reputation we so desire, and yet, is the Lord alone enough?

When people in the world speak evil and harsh words, there is no reason to focus on them. No one meditates on garbage. (How dirty would it be? What would it smell like?) You can just throw it away. Throw away thoughts and words like garbage and follow the word of truth.

Grace drives out fear. To be afraid is to deny that what has come this far is God's grace. The God of Ebenezer who has helped us until now will help us now and in the future. Be at peace before the God who helps. Samuel took a stone and set it up between Mizpah and Shen, and called its name Ebenezer, saying, "Thus far the Lord has helped us." - 1 Samuel 7:12
Faith is not about forcing what we value onto God, but about valuing what God values.

To bear witness to God in the world is to rely on the Almighty God in our lives. If we live as if God is dead or retired, the world will misunderstand such a God. The God we believe in is the Almighty God.

There is no neutral zone in the world. You are either used by the devil or you are definitely used by God. You must become a person who is impossible for the devil to use and easy for God to use. The reason Judas Iscariot was used was his desire for vain things (greed, the devil) and his conscious disregard for Jesus' words and warnings. Then, after Judas received the piece of bread, Satan entered into him. So Jesus told him, "What you are about to do, do quickly." - John 13:23 One of the twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests and said, "What will you give me if I deliver him over to you?" And they paid him thirty pieces of silver. And from then on he sought an opportunity to betray him. - Matthew 26:14-15 One of his disciples, Judas Iscariot, who was about to betray him, said, "Why was this perfume not sold for three hundred denarii and given to the poor?" He said this, not because he cared about the poor, but because he was a thief, and having charge of the moneybag he used to help himself to what was put into it. - John 12:4, 6
Today is not a given, but a special gift from God. That is why the remaining days are precious.

When facing urgent matters in the world, or when in peace, or when expecting to achieve something, we must first look to the Lord's cross. This is so that what God intends can be accomplished in all those things. Any joy or achievement not intended by God is sin.

When you love with precious love, everything becomes grace and inspiration. Those who live loving with the Lord's love are prepared people who know what to do and feel. Because we are free from needing to be loved, we deeply realize that being loved is an undeserved blessing, gratitude, and boundless grace. You gain the ability to receive without fear or awkwardness when you have the skill to give.

In the world, becoming rich, attending a good school, getting a good job, and getting married are considered 'prosperity'. However, the most important element of prosperity is 'Immanuel', meaning 'Is God with me?'. The prosperity of a person of faith is singular: 'God is with me.'

The foolish thing in the world is to seek approval from people. It is as foolish as seeking water in a desert, and those who pursue it move away from God and ultimately reap only emptiness. All approval comes from the Lord.

Let go of the wearisome and heavy burdens of striving to be loved in the world apart from God, and come and learn from Jesus, who is humble and gentle in heart, and you will find rest. Clinging to and fearing reactions is laborious, heavy, and tiring, but rather, abandoning yourself and following Jesus in love is an easy and light yoke. This is because we have already received eternal and immeasurable love. "Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy and my burden is light." - Matthew 11:28, 30

A skilled salesman in the world deeply trusts the product he is introducing. At least, he must appear to trust it, even if he doesn't fully. How can you sell a product you can't even trust yourself? "Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved, you and your household." - Acts 16:31 "But it's hard to believe, isn't it? It didn't work even when I believed. There are easier ways." There is no work of God in such faith. What the Almighty God has spoken in the Bible is unchanging, not one jot or tittle. The problems you have are incredibly complex. Yet, the condition for salvation is simple: "Believe in the Lord Jesus~"

In the world, the person holding the reins is called 'A', and the person struggling on the other side is called 'B'. That is why people strive so hard to become 'A'. Whether you are boasting with the reins in hand or at the tip of the sword, everyone is in the Lord's hand. Even if you are at the bottom, look up to the heavens.
Though you face tribulations in the world, take heart; I have overcome the world. If the Jesus who overcame the world is within me, I will not fear.
Give & Give rather than Give & Take. If you love to receive love, to serve, you will soon feel miserable. It is because we have received unspeakable love that we love. That is why we can be happy.

In the world, people seem wonderful when you meet them, but when you return, your heart feels empty. This is because of the "show." You do not become more precious by the thickness of your self-packaging; rather, you become more hollow.

In the world, effectiveness and efficiency are very important. That is why numbers are used. The Almighty God is not interested in them. What God sees is our attitude. In the feeding of the five thousand, what was needed was not numbers, but a small offering and faith.
Priorities determine the outcome. The devil tells Jesus to eat. Jesus says he will do (the Word) first, then eat. Jesus triumphed through the cross, and the devil was ruined by the cross.

You desire worldly achievements so much that when you obtain them, you move away from God. When you move away from God, the pressure of holiness and life within you decreases, and eventually, the world's turbidity and sin flood in. There is no one, believer or unbeliever, who does not fall into habitual sin if they distance themselves from regular intimacy with God.

The world is not something to be cut off, but something that cuts you off. When you meet someone truly precious, you begin to dislike impurity. When the knowledge of Christ becomes supremely valuable, other things become rubbish. This is because the world and I disappear, and only the Redeemer is seen.
The world urges you to despair, but I hope you will hold onto hope.

The world tries to achieve success and happiness through much learning and research. There is something that such pursuits overlook, something unseen and unheard by the world's eyes: the relationship with God. True victory and happiness lie in the relationship with God. It is the most basic and irreplaceable high calling.

The world is a place where the simple genuine competes with the flashy fake. Therefore, disputes over value assessment arise. If at least we have the opportunity to prove Jesus' value—by focusing on worship, prayer, and the time of the Word—more salvation will occur. Today's believers are too busy to focus on the cross.

The world imposes limits on us through media and customs. However, people of faith do not work within limits. By working with faith, they overturn all limits. The power to break limits lies solely in prayer. Pray alone in your room and pray together in worship, and let power overflow.

The world lives according to greed. People have desires of the heart and things that their calling leads them to. If you live by your calling, you will live a different life. Truth is solitary, but it grows bolder daily.

More Important Relationships. Relationships testify to value. That is why people strive and worry so much to enter into relationships they consider so valuable. Worldly relationships prove temporary and hollow value, while the relationship with the Lord proves eternal and glorious value.
The world makes us cling to what is good, causing us to miss the best. What appears good will perish, but the best is eternal.
The world says, "Stop now." It urges us, "That's enough." It threatens, cajures, and tries to bargain with us using our deepest pain. But we cannot do that. This is because Jesus is most precious.

The world gives conditions. It says "I love you because of this." If the condition disappears, the love disappears. But the Lord loves you regardless. That is why Jesus died on the cross for us when we were sinners. It is impossible to understand through give and take.

The world is certainly uncertain, and in times of crisis, there are no alternatives. In desperate moments, Christians save the world. The Lord is preparing people of faith with anointing, holiness, and expertise, like the sharp sword of tradition.

Do not fear the world. God is the one who gives power. Nothing can be done arbitrarily without God's permission. He is greater than any professor, boss, or anyone else. Conquer. (1 John 4:4) "You, dear children, are from God and have overcome them, because the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world."

If you fight by imitating the world, you will be defeated a hundred times out of a hundred. Christians have God's way, God's wisdom, and God's faithfulness. If David had worn armor and imitated Goliath, he would have lost a million times. It is important to the opponent that they cannot give up, but for me who believes in Jesus, giving up is possible. The world is not a big deal, but for me who has faith, it is precious. This is not a handicap, but rather a gift and power. Wounds become power. Being reviled will be the salvation that rescues them.

* The fruit of the tree of knowledge of good and evil is tempting, but the humble fruit of life gives life. Happiness is not about achieving what I desire, but about beautifully keeping what the Lord has allowed. Adam took what he desired and became unhappy, and he abandoned what was given and became fearful. The fruit of life is not as desirable to look at, as pleasing to the eye, or as tempting to make one wise as the fruit of the tree of knowledge of good and evil, but it leads to life. He grew up before the Lord like a tender shoot, and like a root out of dry ground, he had no beauty or majesty to attract us to him. -Isaiah 53:2,3

Changing the world is not easy. It is because you must change yourself first. It requires about three times the amount of love and forgiveness you currently have. The level God requires is somewhat high. It is about three times the current level.

We look to the world and to people because we want to be happy. But after some time, we find only extreme thirst and disappointment. There is nothing there. If we look only to the Lord, everything falls into order, and we can reach true, balanced happiness.

In life, we often encounter situations that are no one's fault. These things are inevitable. Stop blaming and arguing about responsibility, and step back to see with the Lord's eyes. Then, within compassion and love, you will see paths and methods that were previously unseen. At the council in Jerusalem, the apostles said: "It is the decision of the Holy Spirit and of us not to place any further burden of any kind upon you other than these necessary things..." -Acts 15:28 Simply put, hold onto what is necessary, important, and the core values. They say they will not demand much. That is the will of the Spirit, the will of God. God does not want us to be misunderstood, but He wants us to misunderstand even less. Forcing perfectionism becomes violence to ourselves and to others. Hold onto what is important. And embrace the rest. That is what the Lord wanted.

* Love is not a luxury, but a skill. Looking back, I was truly able to love fervently when I secured absolute prayer time and opportunities for determined unity in prayer. Love is not pleasant entertainment; it is an essence that overflows uncontrollably when one deeply encounters and is filled with God. Without prayer, love is out of the question; only self-centered desires exist.
It makes no sense to say you will be freed from sin by serving the world. If you choose the right master, you will live a righteous life.
In the spiritual world that governs the world, the ultimate martial art is - perseverance.
* The nature of hatred and love. There has never been a good hatred. And love has never been bad. With what you have been given, do your best with gratitude and love.

The rule of the world is that you must fiercely survive in competition for your own comfort. If you don't meet that standard, you think you will truly be a failure. However, those who live by God's standards live by His word. They delight more in willingly suffering with Christ than in trusting the Lord's help and living in the enjoyment of evil. Moses grew up and refused to be called the son of Pharaoh's daughter, choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God than to enjoy the fleeting pleasures of sin...
We run not for the glory of the world, but solely for the prize that is from above.
* Insight of Grace To a hardened heart, everything is troublesome and suspicious, but to a person living by grace, everything is a precious memory and a gift that cannot be missed.

The desires of the world cause change. However, they are never fulfilled, nor do they bring happiness. That does not mean turning away from the world is God's will. You must enter the world. No, you must charge into it. Not to become like the world and enjoy it, but to serve with a distinct appearance. You must be salt and light, exerting deep and wide influence. To do that, you will need to study a lot. You will need to earn money diligently. Then you can serve more.
When people say, "That's just how the world is...", I hope you will be people of faith who can say, "The world is not like that."
* The secret to receiving 'grace' Grace is something given undeservedly but thankfully. Therefore, I am the most graced person, and that grace can only be described as boundless.
Be courageous in the Lord who has overcome the world. Be sober before the Lord, the King of kings.
God is the one who builds. A life focused on the right thing is powerful. The Lord is the focus.
As time passes, we encounter stronger opponents. The enemies challenge us more fiercely than just a few months ago. Therefore, we resolve to become weaker. In the Lord...

* An ignorant but brave(?) answer Moses tells Pharaoh to let his people go. Pharaoh naturally refused, and was destroyed by the ten plagues. This is because he did not know God. If you do not know God, you cannot be free, nor can you obey. Pharaoh said, "Who is the Lord that I should obey him and let Israel go? I do not know the Lord, nor will I let Israel go." -Exodus 5:2

* What is more important than misunderstanding. What is more important than being misunderstood is that you yourself do not misunderstand, despite everything. The forces of Satan end there, and life and grace begin to work.
Rejoice in hope. Do not be a life shaken by people and the world, but be a person of faith who fears only God.

There was a person who, upon hearing just a rumor, risked their life to follow Jesus and touched the fringe of his cloak. The Lord's power flowed. However, even if countless proclamations of the word are heard and acknowledged, if one is dry, nothing will happen even if the Lord Himself lays hands on them. Matthew 5:21-34) Hearing about Jesus, he joined the crowd, and coming up behind him, he touched the fringe of his cloak. Hearing about Jesus, he joined the crowd, and coming up behind him, he touched the fringe of his cloak. For he thought, "If I but touch his clothes, I will be healed."

* It's not about solving it, it's about being free. This problem is simply a matter of being free. But we get entangled by the words of others, rumors, and even by imagining their inner thoughts. That is not my concern, and I just need to focus on the truth. "Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free." - John 8:32
Do not pay too much attention to rumors and words. The world passes away like the flowers of the field, but the word of God remains forever.
A sense of belonging alleviates our anxiety. Sometimes it gives us pride. However, God is not interested in where you belong. He says what matters is how you stand before Him.

In a situation of alienation, where self-pity, anger, rejection, and a sense of victimhood flood in, David chose worship. When the presence of the Lord comes in worship, all negative influences recede, and He builds you up through His reign.

* Satan's professional spirit Satan never quarrels or opposes himself. This is not because Satan's nature is gentle or good. Satan is never that foolish. At least he knows what to do in war. If you have no faith, you should at least have a business mind. "And if a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand." -Matthew 12:26
Your desires speak of your faith. What do you truly desire?

Are you happy? The reason people fight is to have more. Whether it's material possessions or affection, they fight, envy, deceive, and are deceived to gain more. They even try to deceive themselves and God through justifications. Are you truly happy? If not, something is hidden. Something you don't want to lose, something you don't want to let go of. Are you happy? You should be. For you have given up so much to gain that place. - Dae Jang Geum http://youtu.be/EJmKyoB8Nxg?t=1m50s
When we lose something precious, our faith grows wider.

There are times when we want to be deceived, even knowing we are being deceived. This is called self-pity. True faith is needed here. We must remember who called us in the first place. "For whoever would draw near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards those who earnestly seek him."

Breaking News> Prayer meeting extended. It is extended by longing. It's an extension for 21 days, but after 21 days, 40 days await, and after 40 days, a 100-day commitment awaits, so you can consider it unlimited. Please pray that I can keep the place of prayer alongside my job.

Why He is our strength in weakness: God uses the weak because He transforms them. A coward was transformed into an apostle, and a persecutor and abuser became a grace-filled person who sought only the Lord's glory, whether living or dying. This is because transformation is glory.

Not a guest, but a worshipper: You should not act like a guest before the throne of grace, as if ordering at a restaurant. Instead, you must humble and deny yourself infinitely, relying on the blood of the cross and the guidance of the Holy Spirit to serve the Lord. The throne of grace is not a place to fulfill desires and strive for them, but a place to let go, become humble, and be exalted.

How comforting it is that, despite having nothing in my hands, I believe in Jesus, rely on the merit of His blood, and can pray to Him. This is not just peace of mind, but a wondrous grace that manifests in our lives.

To refuse to suffer loss, to abhor loss without reason, is contrary to the spirit of the cross. Like Jesus, who went the second mile when asked to go one, I hope you will preach the gospel and bless others even more.

Saint, are you very flustered? Even if the patient's family is flustered, the doctor should not be. They should not be, even if the surgical patient is their own family. Entrust everything to God as you do the work of saving lives. Fluster comes from areas outside of my control. Do not be afraid! You will no longer suffer shame. Do not be dismayed—Isaiah 54:4 (New Korean Standard Version)

Solomon sought wisdom in his youth but sought truth in his later years. While wisdom gave him excellence, truth, hidden by all success, leads me back to see myself before the Lord. True blessing is living in obedience without denying the Lord.

Solomon's outstanding wisdom is a "listening heart" (lev shomea). The wisest person is one who hears God's voice. It is a heart that can hear, not an analyzing intellect. "Give to your servant therefore an understanding mind to govern your people, to discern between good and evil, for who is able to govern this great people of yours?"—1 Kings 3:9

God gives wisdom when Solomon asks for ability for the community. Simon the sorcerer was cursed when he sought the power of the Holy Spirit for popularity. The motive I pursue tells me who I am. Who are you?

What is laborious, time-consuming, and receives no proper recognition or evaluation is called 'waste.' Avoiding this 'waste' is human instinct. However, without this waste, I would not have been saved. The holiest waste is the Son of God, Jesus Christ, giving His life in place of a creation bound by sin.
Come, all you who are weary and burdened,

The role is different because the destination is different. Say this to the devil: You do your work of envy, slander, schemes, and lust. I will do my work. "whether I live or die, Christ will be honored in my body..."—Philippians 1:20
If the retreat is a huge success, the cell leader has nothing to do. Grace covers everything. Leader, is it hard? Receive grace and impart grace.

What do people live by? Without God's grace, we cannot live. Therefore, there are two things we must do: acknowledge and believe that all is futile labor without God's grace, and earnestly 'pray' while looking forward to it. Thus, people live by grace, and the method is to believe and pray. It's that simple, isn't it?
Why even professionals falter: Even seasoned warriors of faith who made the gates of hell tremble can fall. This happens when they neglect the basics. Use your faith.
Grace that overwhelms disappointment and injustice: We live by faith, but we are judged by people without faith. There is no need for disappointment because God's grace is immeasurable.

Moments seem like nothing. However, for people of faith, every moment is precious. If we do our best in each moment, ordinary times relying on the Lord will gather to bring a glorious day of great waves.
We dedicate long hours to protect a moment. A brief encounter with God's people is better than a thousand days in the world.
Pure gold needs no plating. Only fakes are plated. Those who have discovered their worth in the Lord do not lie or envy to prove their value. It is a jewel-like life that needs no plating.

Why human approval is not needed: Because we discern situations through truth. "Jesus did not entrust himself to them, for he knew all people. He did not need human testimony about marked people, for he knew what was in each person."—John 2:24,25
When He asks for obedience, it is not oppression but protection for us.
There are times when it is difficult to make a decision to obey. There are times when we are afraid to let go of what is familiar. However, the Lord promises to be with us in the midst of it.

For a soul that obeys, the light of revelation becomes clearer and clearer. This is because when God reveals His will, He manifests it through 'commands that require immediate obedience.' If you obey one step, you will see only the next step. That is all I need to see. When Paul was converted on the road to Damascus, the Lord did not say he would become a great apostle, an unparalleled missionary, or the greatest theologian of all time. He only said, "Get up, go into the city, and you will be told what you must do" (Acts 9:6). As you walk the path of obedience, you will come to know God's will for your life.
Victory over trials is the power of His blood. It allows us to discern the enemy's schemes, avoid being deceived, gain freedom, and achieve victory over trials.

When you obey, you hear God's voice clearly. Those who want to do God's will, that is, those who want to obey, will discern what is God's teaching and word. "The one who speaks from himself is seeking his own glory; but he who is seeking the glory of the one who sent him is a truthful man, and no righteousness is in him."—John 7:17

Drunkenness, debauchery, and all sorts of corruption fill the world. God prepares new people for new things. Just as Abraham built an altar by faith and called upon 'Jehovah' in an era without reverence for God, we too must call upon the Father's holy name in this land of London. The hope of this nation begins with my repentance.

If the Lord does not grant grace, you will have received nothing. Do not focus on what you lack, but be thankful for what you have been given. Whether it is big or small, God gave it to you for the sake of life, and He gave it to transform it into good through His work. For no one can receive anything unless it has been given to him from heaven. -John 3:27

Let us proclaim the Lord's sovereignty in schools, workplaces, and communities filled with drunkenness and debauchery. Declare that those places also belong to the Lord and that He still reigns, and lift your hands in prayer. The gateway where the Lord's reign begins is the church, but the church is also the gateway through which that reign extends to the world. Let us proclaim and pray for the kingdom of God to come to our workplaces, schools, homes, and communities.
It was not easy, but when I did not give up, I saw the Lord's glory. That glory is transformation.

In the face of great and small challenges that are not easy, we must come to the place of prayer. That is why we should earn nicknames like this from the demons living in our neighborhoods: 'Don't mess with them, they pray if you do.' Therefore, we must become believers who give demons depression. - 1/3/2010
Gratitude: All of this is by God's grace, and it is His love that has always been more than I deserved. I confirm that love on the cross.

Having a smartphone does not make you smart. Rather, it is easy to become a foolish person lacking concentration. If you practice self-control, you become smart (wise). There must be self-control in all things.

Those who boast of themselves are foolish. Because even after ten years, they will not change. This is because they see the speck in others' eyes but do not realize the log in their own. It is instinct to hear what we want to hear and see only what we want to see, but it is faith to see what the Lord desires. You will be set free only after you receive the Lord's approval. Let the one who boasts, boast in the Lord. For it is not the one who commends himself who is approved, but the one whom the Lord commends. -2 Corinthians 10:17,18
If you do not choose your actions yourself and only react to people your whole life, your life force will weaken.

The power to get drunk and the power to gain power come from the authority to let go. Those who cannot let go are not worthy to get drunk. "I have the authority to let go and the authority to take it back. This commandment I received from my Father." -John 10:17,18

Only Jesus can say "Peace be with you" to those who are trembling with their voices lowered, doors locked tight, after killing their master. Jesus is the only one who can tell me "Peace" in the moment I am in, towards my life and my soul.
It is not by overturning style and individuality that good results are achieved. Power appears when the anointing is poured upon our 부족한 selves, just as we are.

Stress makes us feel like we have no room to spare and makes us believe we cannot solve it. Problems seem bigger, and our self-esteem lowers. Christians need humor. And they need friends like that. If you face it alone, you will fall, but if you are together, you can endure it. Delusion is suffering from problems that are magnified and imagined. Let us look to the Lord who calls things that are not as though they were.
The reason for doing our best: Not doing our best leads to regret. Because today could be the last day. Doing your absolute best is 'loving'.

In sports, what is important is to dominate the flow of the game. King Nebuchadnezzar tries to dominate the situation by threatening with a furnace seven times hotter. What breaks through these worldly challenges is 1. 'Faith' that the Lord will deliver, and 2. 'Sacrifice', that is, the cross, by which we will not be corrupted even if He does not deliver us. When we have faith that completely trusts God and bear the cross with a "If I perish, I perish" attitude, we will experience the Lord who overwhelms the enemy and controls the situation. God has put all things under the authority of Christ and has made him head over all things for the benefit of ... God has put everything under the control of Christ. Ephesians 1:22
Fear of fate: If you fear the Lord, you will not fear fate. You fate, Bring it on!

Victory is not something we achieve through our own effort and struggle, but rather something we look to and enjoy from the Lord who has already won within us. There is no need to win again; it is resting in the victorious Lord.
Under control: The evidence of controlling a situation is 'gratitude'. If you are grateful, you will not be controlled, but you will control.

A visually impaired person lost their way on a familiar path. This is because the tree that helped them gauge their location had been cut down. After wandering for a long time, they received help from someone. As they were about to speak warmly and express their gratitude, the other person thanked them instead. "Actually, I was going to commit suicide on that bridge earlier, but after meeting you and talking while walking, I regained the courage to live. I am grateful."
Difficulty: It is only when the most difficult thing is given that it becomes a test. That is why God asked Abraham for Isaac, not Sarah.
I give my time, heart, and soul. If it is for myself, it will be in vain, but if it is to fulfill the Lord's will, it will be glorious.
To those who give their time, who wait for God, who entrust all their problems to the Lord as their master, the wind of the Spirit blows.
God hears the prayers of those who close their eyes for a moment, stealing time. Prayer changes lives. Because it changes our hearts.
Be faithful unto death: If you are faithful, you will not waste your years.
As time passes, memories fade. But we love Jesus more. Because we think of Him every moment.

One of the things we regret as time passes is not loving more. Another is being stingy with expressing love, even when we did love. We hope to love without regret in our youthful days. We hope for a love that shares in pain and sheds tears together, a love that encourages and prays for each other.

The attitude of receiving insults: King Saul was sensitive to the people's reactions through religious rituals because he feared people, but David feared God, so he did not react to people's curses. David also said to Abishai and all his servants, "If my own son seeks my life, how much more may this Benjamite do so? Let him alone, and let him curse, for the Lord has told him to." -2 Samuel 16:11
Joy does not come from possessing many things, nor from being recognized by people. The greatest joy is living with the Lord. That is the treasure and the pearl.

The reason for not fearing rejection - Psalm 3:5,6: Even if all the people in the world turn away from me, I will not fear, because the Lord upholds me. I lie down and sleep; I wake again, for the Lord sustains me. I will not be afraid of ten thousand people who surround me. -Psalm 3:5,6
It has begun. The season of special prayer, filled with the Lord's power.

Boasting in weakness: It is not because I have managed my trials reasonably that grace is sufficient. Grace is sufficient because the Lord's grace is sufficient. The Lord has given abundant grace, and His power will work perfectly in my weakness.

Psalm 1 says the blessed person does not follow, stand, or sit. Blessedness is not an achievement but an attitude and position. It is blessedness to keep one's place and stand as the Lord has commanded. Blessed is the one who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked, nor stand in the way of sinners, nor sit in the seat of mockers. But his delight is in the law of the Lord, and on his law he meditates day and night. - Psalm 1:1,2
The verse from Psalm 23:4 calms me tonight as I write this, with a full stomach from the church's bulgogi.

What David confessed through the Psalms was not the betrayal of blood relatives or the turning away of close friends. It was the estrangement from God. For David, who fled from King Saul for over ten years, God, who was his strength, power, and hope, was the most important person in his life. As king and poet, this truly broke his heart. "O Lord, do not forsake me; O my God, do not be far from me." - Psalm 38:21

* Though I am insufficient, Your grace is sufficient. In extreme suffering, just before being set free, we realize something. Because of that realization, bitterness and defeat vanish, and suffering transforms into glory. He said to me, "My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness." Therefore, I will boast all the more gladly about my weaknesses, so that Christ's power may rest on me. - 2 Corinthians 12:9 http://youtu.be/2ejtqbWl8lM

The reason for being tested is not to be ashamed, but to rely on the Lord and overcome in the face of trials. When we pass through this long tunnel, God sees our wounds and rejoices, not as servants. "Let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us, fixing our eyes on Jesus, the pioneer and perfecter of faith." - Hebrews 12:1-2

I pray specifically for those who are undergoing tests, assignments, and projects. Pour out diligence, passion, wisdom, and ideas among them. Assure them of Your love and let them not fear. (1 John 4:18) Grant inspiration and power in their practice, study, and work. (Exodus 31:1-11) Let them reveal You through their exemplary conduct and excellence in their companies and society. And Lord, if it's not too much to ask, I personally request that much of it pour out from where they have studied. ^^;

* Only when you know the truth can you be truly free. A free person passes through storms with a relaxed expression. Be free from attachment, fear, and anger. God has never failed and cannot fail. God is the truth.
Are there trials and obstacles? Break through them with prayer. Trust in the faithful and almighty Lord. He will help me in the morning.
I think we are tested because the other person is wicked or because we are weak. No. The fundamental reason for being tested is our inability to believe in the Lord's work. Unbelief is the cause.

* The reason for mental breakdown. There are people who smile, but their smiles reveal anxiety. Such people inevitably experience mental breakdown soon after, either disappearing because they lack the ability to explain, or putting on a mask and shifting the cause of their negative emotions to others. The further we distance ourselves from God, the more we try to validate our existence through 'self-satisfaction' and 'relational acceptance,' and we are constantly anxious. If we truly believe in what Jesus accomplished on the cross, we will live in 'glory' with unashamed expectations, rather than being 'mentally broken' by futile pursuits. Love never fails. Love never ends, but - 1 Corinthians 13:8

We are told to be watchful and pray so that we may not fall into temptation. So what happens if we don't pray? Don't worry. The rooster will crow twice soon after. Of course, you will realize that you have already fallen into temptation.
The reason for falling into temptation is not that the circumstances are bad. Simply put, it is because we do not love the Lord the most.

* The resolve for failure rather than the certainty of success. When pursuing success, we become stronger and freer when we are prepared for failure. This is because failure in the Lord is not failure. There is a fundamental difference between someone who has already won playing a game and someone playing to win. The one playing to win fears losing everything even when things are going well, but the one who has already won rejoices even in critical situations because of the Lord who has already accomplished good. It is finished... To worry about something that is already finished is truly madness. After receiving the sour wine, Jesus said, "It is finished," and he bowed his head and gave up his spirit. - John 19:20

Trials sift the wheat from the chaff. They reveal the truth of the words spoken when we received grace. The statement "I am satisfied with the Lord alone" will be validated in the face of trials. We will know if it was false or true by enduring the trial.

When trials come, instead of being flustered, we should consider it as 'God's time to work.' Two truly baffling situations: 1. Going to the doctor for a real problem, but pretending not to be in pain in front of the doctor. 2. Calling a computer technician for a broken computer, but it works perfectly fine. In technical terms, it's called 'driving oneself crazy.' If it's not revealed, it cannot be fixed.
A trial is when the obstacle before us appears greater. We must look to the great God. To see the Lord greatly is prayer.

* The reason for being called. We are not called to do well. That is the Lord's domain. We are called to be faithful. If you have been faithful and loyal, there is nothing to be ashamed of. What is required of stewards is faithfulness. - 1 Corinthians 4:2
Stop worrying (it's none of your business) and look only to the Lord. You will receive power and become witnesses. Faith is simple.

* The reason for not being ashamed. Paul was not ashamed because he had unashamed expectations and hopes. And hope does not put us to shame, because God's love has been poured into our hearts through the Holy Spirit, who has been given to us. - Romans 5:5

* The reason for not fearing, even if we hate it to death. We hate poverty intensely, but we are not afraid of it. The loss of relationships, like Isaac to Abraham, can be a heart-wrenching pain, but it is not terrifying. This is because we 'expect' only the Lord and enjoy 'security' under His rule within Him.

* Love that overcomes fear. We are not tempted by things that are not fearful. We do not fear unexpected things either. Therefore, vain expectations become the root of fear of loss and rejection. Those who expect only the eternal One fear only the departure of God's presence and are not tempted by other things.

Glory to God who used the youth group at the New Year's conference. Through your prayers and dedication, we see lives being saved and continents and mission fields being awakened. Simple people are powerful. I hope you young people will be simple in Jesus.
* The initiative of temptation. We are not afraid because temptation comes. We fall into temptation because we are afraid. Job said that what he feared had come upon him.

Many people fast and pray at the New Year. This means they don't want to suffer hardship by straying from the Lord's word in 2012. Starting next week, the youth group will have evening prayer. 7:30 PM at the Mission Education Center (Mon, Tue, Thu). On Wed and Fri, we will pray on Facebook at our respective locations at the scheduled time. (I will explain later.) And on Saturdays, we will receive the Word. There will be a retreat in February. The German fasting conference is in early April. The youth leader conference is at the end of May. The youth group summer retreat is on July 2nd.

The one who takes the bride is the bridegroom. But the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly at the bridegroom's voice. Therefore this joy of mine is now complete. - John 3:29
Faithful God, as we look to You, have mercy on us.
* Though I am like dust. By Your grace, I have lived another day. What more could this infinitely insufficient and weak person ask for? Your grace has been sufficient for me.

In my experience of faith, it is a fact that even though we want to walk the glorious path together, someone always falls behind. However, I have a desire. A desire to reach that place without losing anyone.

Nothing is as fair as faith. This is because there is judgment, and furthermore, that judgment is just. Faith is not something you can't do because you don't have money, or because you don't have air conditioning or even a building. You can't do it simply because you don't have the heart. If you have the heart, you will see the glory of the Lord even on a deserted island.

* The root of fear. To be afraid is because our expectations might be shattered. Those expectations are about being accepted and not rejected, but by avoiding emotional pain and defending ourselves, we choose anger, frustration, depression, attachment, addiction, or isolation, and ultimately become more lonely. If we change our expectations, our fear also changes. Paul's expectation was "whether I live or die, may Christ be honored in my body." Before this expectation, all trivial values become trash and refuse. Just throw away the trash. According to my earnest expectation and hope, that I shall not be put to shame in anything, but that with all boldness, now as always, Christ will be honored in my body, whether by life or by death. - Philippians 1:20

Christian life is not easy. Because it must be sustained. Persistence is true skill. Scoring well once or twice is not yet proven. Love is patient. The devil's every test and attack on relationships without love does not last long.

Christian life is not about squeezing yourself. It is about being filled and overflowing. Nothing but the power of the Spirit of life can rescue us from this body of death. Cunning must cease. There is no path of faith without paying a price.

How to live a good Christian life: 1. Set a goal (Jesus Christ). 2. Run towards it. 3. Ignore minor resistance and hardships. 4. Ignore major resistance and hardships as well. 5. Be held by Christ. Hallelujah!
* Worry-free. Only Jesus touches my heart. It is not a problem to be worried about, but a problem to be touched. So let go and be at peace. The Lord reigns.

Living a good Christian life is like swinging on a trapeze in a circus. You must boldly let go of your own trapeze, fly, wait, and be caught by your partner on the other trapeze. There is not much you have to do. You let go, wait, and are caught.

Christian life is simple. It is 'looking' and 'running'. Therefore, since we are surrounded by such a great cloud of witnesses, let us throw off everything that hinders and the sin that so easily entangles us, and let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us, fixing our eyes on Jesus, the pioneer and perfecter of faith. (Hebrews 12:1, 2) To run, we must not be distracted by other things. Therefore, we must look to Jesus, who ran ahead of us. (Hebrews 6:20 speaks of the Lord as the forerunner.) That is the secret. To overcome the law of sin and death, the law of the Spirit of life must become stronger. This is possible when we are under grace. When we look to the Lord and run, we are under grace. Romans 6:14 gives the answer: "For sin shall not be master over you, for you are not under law, but under grace." Romans 6:14

The most heartbreaking thing in Christian life is not loneliness, being forgotten, or being hated. It is when the Lord's glory is obscured because of me. Obeying what I have received from the Lord with a repentant heart is the way to follow Jesus, even if I am a flawed disciple.

Faith undeniably has an element of struggle. We must not fear or avoid the fight. It is about enjoying peace in the Lord on the premise of actively fighting against sin and darkness. Do not be discouraged or flee, but overcome evil with good.

The hardest thing in faith is doing nothing. From an unbeliever's perspective, prayer is doing nothing. However, when you lift your spiritual eyes, the greatest and most significant work is happening in the air.

The hardest thing in faith is to be still. When we acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty and remain still, we come to know that He is God and He reveals His glory. Let us stop toiling and enter into the power of remaining. So that worship may be worship...

Faith is a relationship. And the most important relationship is with the Lord. Relationship is power, wisdom, and everything. Therefore, interaction with the Lord is important at every step. We must move forward relying on Him, not on our own wisdom and strength.
Faith is not something we have to live, but something we cannot help but live.

Faith is the life of a sojourner. A sojourner leaves behind a comfortable home and clings only to the Lord amidst the pain of broken relationships. They advance through the wilderness without settling or being greedy.

* The power of cleaning. To drive away flies or pests, you need to clean up the trash. However, some people cover the trash with a blanket, spray perfume, and claim there is no problem. Everyone has a root of bitterness. The choice is whether to put on a mask over it or to clean it. See to it that no one falls short of the grace of God, that no root of bitterness springs up and causes trouble, and by it defiles many. -Hebrews 12:15
Faith is not digging a ditch. It begins when we abandon our efforts. When our defenses, our pride, our reputation, and our zeal are frustrated, the Lord can then help us.
Faith is about focusing. It is looking only at the Lord so that nothing else enters your sight.

* The cause of inner conflict. It is not only that the ground produced thorns and thistles after sin entered. The human heart also becomes dry and craves love but cannot be filled. That is why we become greedy and envious. When we crucify the ego's desire to become God, we will experience salvation in all areas where we try to avoid fear, addiction, and rejection by choosing loneliness. What causes quarrels and fights among you? Is it not your passions that are at war in your limbs? You covet and do not have, so you murder. You are envious and cannot obtain, so you fight and quarrel. You do not have because you do not ask. You ask and do not receive, because you ask wrongly, to spend it on your passions. -James 4:1, 3

Expertise is required even in leading faith. Just because someone has handled meat for 30 years in a butcher shop does not mean they can perform heart surgery. Much less, if you are entrusted with souls, you must deal with life with prayer and tears.

Losing the wonder of faith is a disaster. One of two reasons is losing focus: 1. Not revering God and not rejoicing in Him forever (Paul called it not wanting to have God in their minds). The other is 2. Not becoming one with each other, just as the Father and the Son are one. If we stake our lives on revering God, His two desires, and become His hands and feet to bless and seek our brothers and sisters, God will not leave us alone. If we live and die for the Lord's eternal name, not for our own glory, there is no reason why God would not support us.

The measure of faith is not the 'quantity' of what we have accomplished. It is the 'distance' from God. Power comes from distance and relationship. If there is no relationship with the Lord, no matter how much we do, the Lord will say on that day, "I never knew you..." Matthew 7:23 I never knew you, man!
* How to hear God's voice. Deny yourself, humble yourself, do not follow your desires, and seek the cause within yourself. Of course, if you do the opposite, you will hear other voices.

Maturing in faith means shifting your focus from self-centered interpretation to the Lord's interpretation, and from selfish requests to loving consideration. This will happen if you stand before the Lord every moment.

It is very easy to improve in faith. Especially when you hold a position. However, it is truly difficult to do so within the family. True faith is ignited from the hidden depths of our being and purified.

* The method of love 7 I loved the group called Ebenezer, through which I came to believe in Jesus, so much that I firmly resolved, "I must live for this group." However, later I was misunderstood and condemned as 'demon-possessed' by the community I loved, and I had to apologize (?) in front of everyone without knowing why. Then, as I came down from the prayer mountain alone, with everyone else leaving in pairs, I decided to change my hasty resolution. "Before, I said I would live for this group, but from now on, I will die for this group I love."

To improve in faith is to know the Lord more. The God David experienced has no misunderstanding about me, knows everything, and it is spiritually impossible to leave the Lord beyond space and time. In the end, realizing that every moment I breathe is a moment held in His hand, I come to cherish His word and examine myself, seeking His guidance.
To say that faith is improving means that the subjects of suffering are changing.

For a believer to be blessed does not mean that various challenges and trials are exempted. Rather, it means experiencing a Lord who is greater than those problems. The Lord's blood is deeper than my pain, and the Lord's grace is greater than my problems.

* The method of love 6 I love with all that I have. And I love what I do not have through prayer. And I place all my expectations and hopes only in the Father who is in heaven. Only He will record and judge all these things.

The most important thing for a believer is humility. When Joshua conquered Jericho and, seeing the small city of Ai, briefly took off his garment of humility, and when Samson's hair was cut and the Spirit of God departed from him, they simply thought God was working as before. A proud person is someone who does not know that God's power has departed from them. Humble yourselves.
* Mission. It is doing what God told you to do where He told you to stand. And it is continuing faithfully until the final whistle blows. Everything else that happens is not my concern.

Believers struggle because they try to avoid loneliness and hardship at all costs, fearing them. What if you are a little lonely? What if it's a little difficult? I don't like loneliness or hardship, but that doesn't mean I fear them. If the Lord is glorified within me, even death is profitable.

It is only natural for believers to be hurt in an unchaste and rebellious world. Lot also said his righteous soul was grieved. However, it is more important to come before the Lord to be healed and restored from that natural hurt. Do not try to win the battle, but win the war. To win the war, you must be continuously supplied. This war does not belong to me, but to God.

* The Way of Love 4 If you love with the Lord's love, you will truly be joyful. You will be so joyful that you will not look for or expect anything else. That love is real. If you have many expectations, it is not love, but merely investment and management. For love is here, not that we loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son as a propitiation for our sins. -1 John 4:10
Believers worry that their lives will become a loser's life. This means they worry that the Lord will make a mistake. It is a truly blasphemous worry.
Religious actions can never be a cause for boasting. It is like boasting about having seen a good deed 300 times. If you truly want to boast, you should boast that you 'truly loved'.
If God had come in the form of a god, we would all have been judged and perished. God came in human form. That person is Jesus, and that is why we are saved.

To be disappointed means that you must have had expectations. If you seek vain things, you will surely be disappointed. For they soon wither away. Those who pursue eternal things will obtain incorruptible glory.

* The Way of Love 2 It is shameful that I cannot eat like Jesus, sleep like Jesus, pray like Jesus, or shed tears like Jesus. This is because Jesus reached out his hand but was rejected, healed the sick but was ignored, took the cross but became a sinner, and loved but was hated.

To say you are disappointed means you had great expectations. Greed always leads to disappointment. If you cannot be satisfied with the Lord and Jesus is not enough, you will be disappointed more often.
There are many people following in real-time. ^^ Shall we stay up all night tonight?

* The Way of Love It is difficult because you try to give back only what you have received. Love is giving and giving, and even after giving everything, feeling regret for not being able to give more. It is not about being recognized as loving or being known by anyone, but doing it because it fulfills the purpose and it is what He asks. Just as waiting does not need to have meeting as its purpose to be good...

In reality, it doesn't matter what abilities we have. No matter what trials and challenges we face, it is not my strength but 'He who strengthens me' that is important. No matter how rugged the mountains, if the one who strengthens me says "you can do it," then you can just do it without worry or fear. Believe this word, "I can do all things through Christ who strengthens me," and declare that you will be bold.

It is when the Lord reigns that tangled problems are unraveled like taut strings, places overflowing with sin and debauchery become holy, and crises turn into opportunities. Take off your sandals and follow the Lord. That land will become holy.

When we fail, everyone is tempted to avoid that place and want to leave. However, as time passes, one thing becomes clear. It is the fact that I love the Lord. That failure does not change this fact. ...Do you love me?
Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven... -Matthew 5:3
* The Gospel is Enough The gospel and prayer are enough. True gospel always brings change.
What needs to be confirmed as much as the beating of the heart is the presence of the Lord within me. Without His presence, it is NOTHING.

Those who are steadfast and those who trust in the Lord are the same person. The Lord holds and keeps in peace those who give their hearts to Him and entrust themselves to Him every minute and second. The Lord will keep in perfect peace those whose minds are steadfast, because they trust in Him. -Isaiah 26:3
* The Reason for Happiness Are not all these things things to be thankful for, rather than to complain about?
Judgment is terrifying. However, grace is the only power that can overcome judgment. When sins are washed away by grace, the body, soul, and failures are restored and revived.

Judgment begins in the house of God. For the Almighty God who called Gideon, military strength, the enemy's situation, and weapon systems were not issues. He only wanted Gideon to first break down the idols in his house. Only when God's people are purified can they change the world. God's concern is with 'me'. The moment I change, Esau and King Saul, who tormented me, are no longer needed.
The secret to excellence that surpasses ten years is obedience. If you try to obey, you will hear a voice, change will occur, and things that a nation cannot do will be accomplished.

* The Reason for Peace and Boldness God's love is incomparably stronger than the devil's slandering and schemes. Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword? Yet in all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us. (Romans 8:35-37)
When preaching the gospel of the cross, I always feel that the work of life, restoration, and healing invariably takes place. @kingston University
When the cross becomes dim, the Lord allows loss. When we lose what we cherish, we realize who the true owner is. Loss is not about losing, but about clarifying what to focus on.
* A Lamp in the Darkness Please pray that the Lord alone may be your lamp of hope in the darkness.
