Jesus' commands have power. When He says, "Pick up your mat and walk," even a man paralyzed for 38 years gets up. I hope you listen to the Lord's commands. In them lies the power of care, healing, restoration, and fulfillment.

About 70% of Jesus' parables are related to money, and there are about 3,000 financial stories in the Bible. From God's perspective, money is the most common thing, and there are few people who are most worthy of receiving its surplus.

Jesus' life had a consistent intention that transcended circumstances and emotions. It was to fulfill the Scriptures. I hope you respond to the Lord's will, not to the devil's requests and intentions. What is the Lord's request?

The final temptation in Jesus' atoning work on the cross was the 'temptation of self-vindication.' "If you are truly the Son of God, come down." The true grace is being able to come down but choosing not to. When misunderstood or hurt, do not try to prove yourself; pray like Jesus. The Lord will work all things for good.

Do not try to fix the Christian's R&R (Rest and Relaxation) problem; be fixed by God. Solving problems is not my job, so the more I try to solve them, the deeper I fall into a maze. My job is holiness and trust, and God's job is healing and revival.

Many people, then and now, touch the fringe of Jesus' garment. However, salvation does not come to everyone. When you reach out in faith, the Lord is never displeased; instead, He bestows salvation. He healed the woman with the hemorrhage.
Being loved by someone is not proof that I am a good person, but rather God's boundless grace.

If you desperately know who Jesus is, the former things become refuse. A person whose value system has truly changed is a truly transformed person. "But whatever was to my profit I now consider loss for the sake of Christ. What is more, I consider everything a loss compared to the surpassing worth of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord, for whom I have lost all things and consider them rubbish, that I may gain Christ and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own that comes from the law, but that which is through faith in Christ—the righteousness that comes from God on the basis of faith." (Philippians 3:7, 9)

All the solutions Jesus taught in His parables and teachings ultimately lead to the cross. The cross has two attributes: complete trust in the Lord and relinquishing one's rights. "Then he said to them all: 'Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross daily and follow me.'"

It is regrettable to not have time to blow out the candle because the heart is filled with the world. If there is joy in being filled with Jesus because of my shortcomings, then weakness becomes a cause for boasting. The candle must be extinguished before the rising sun.

Jesus declared that He has overcome the world and its ruler. And He told us that word. He said He told us so that 'though you face trials, you will have peace in me forever.' From our perspective, we must listen to what He said. Those who hear the Lord's voice have peace and victory. Those who listen to the voice of the world and the voice of people remain in darkness and stumble.

Jesus did not come to improve attitudes or morality. He came so that we sinners, who were inevitably enslaved by the devil due to the law of sin and death wielded by the devil, could live by God's life. Because of the cross, the Holy Spirit came, and this Comforter gives us God's life and the power to live by that life, which is why He is called the 'Spirit of life,' and further, the 'law of the Spirit of life (power)' (Romans 8:2). If we live according to the flesh, we cannot please God, but if we dwell in the Spirit 'in Jesus,' we live by God's life, not our old sinful life, and by God's power. Faith is believing that.

There is no moment, not a single step I walk, not a single breath I take, where I do not need the Lord's grace. The more I rely on myself, the more I have to rely on, and whatever I encounter, I rely on Jesus.

The way Jesus wants us to rejoice is not by performance but by identity. Incentives are how companies motivate employees, and Love is how God treats us. The cross is love. "However, do not rejoice that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven." (Luke 10:20)

If you cannot be truly satisfied with Jesus, it is normal that you cannot be satisfied with anything else. But the problem is that sometimes or often, things that are supposed to be satisfying are absurd. It is because they are fake.
The statement "Jesus is enough" does not end with words. It is proven by the subject of my pain and the owner of my heart.
If you have Jesus, you have the best thing. There is no reason to be discouraged by anything else. An earthenware vessel is glorified only by the treasure it contains.
Believing in Jesus means having peace. This is because we look only to Jesus. Honestly, the word "believe" is not easy to say. It means He possesses me exclusively.

Does the saying "Jesus is enough, Jesus is sufficient" truly need to be proven? Even the value of good food or luxury goods, once known and enjoyed, doesn't need to be reminded; rather, it leads one to seek the good thing with a fierce determination. If you truly experience that 'Jesus is enough,' there is no need to say 'do this' or 'don't do that.'
Everything is possible in Jesus.
Do not hesitate with "what if" by relying on anything other than Jesus. If you run solely for Jesus, you will see glory.
Rather than becoming an expert who understands the situation and provides solutions, one should first examine their own heart. God only uses humble people.

I remember a song we sang when I was a college student leading a prayer meeting. It was a song created purely for prayer meetings. "The praying soul will be blessed in all things. Because the Lord does not abandon me, even if I face trials or threats to my life, I will pray to the end. The praying soul will cry out to God with deep anguish and tears, just like the Lord. Because the prayer's desire is only told to God, it will be fully resolved. Though He had no desires of His own, I will remember the Lord who became perfect through obedience. The praying soul..."

I don't think I ever bent before. I couldn't bend. But after realizing what Jesus did on the cross, I could bend. Now it's almost automatic. To save others, I must die. If I die, I live and others live. Conversely, if I live, everyone dies. One way to die is fasting. Anger does not achieve God's righteousness.

What was once shame has now become a song of praise from my lips through God's love. I will not be shaken even if an enemy comes. I trust in God and have nothing to fear. I will surely live and see the glory of God.

Worry not only fails to achieve God's righteousness but also causes pain that withers the bones. It is not the Lord's will for your heart to be troubled by matters that will soon be forgotten and insignificant when entrusted to the Lord's hands. If you strive for your own work and holiness, the Lord will add His work and all these things.

The essence of faith is no different now than it was in the past. In the past, if you did not deny the Lord, you would be thrown into a lion's den; today, if you do not love other beings more than the Lord, you are threatened with failure and despair. To shake off these things, you must still cling to the cross, shedding blood. Resolve to shed blood. You will find peace instead.

There is an old saying that it is not people who lie, but money that lies. It means that people are driven to such actions not by their true intentions but by desperate reality and circumstances. Without spiritual assets, without Jesus, we are prone to anger, frustration, and easily wounded by the slightest provocation. If we do not have the resurrected Jesus, all other problems are merely the result of His absence. I pray that the Lord be with your spirit; grace be with you all. - 2 Timothy 4:22

Two Minds. What I care about and am concerned with is my purpose. If it is one's own desire, then greed is the purpose; if it is God's will, then it is a mission. A person cannot serve two masters, and if one claims to serve one master while pursuing other desires, they have a double mind and are unstable. God seeks those who fear Him.
Spiritual authority is so important that Satan does his utmost to hinder its establishment. When it is established, God's influence floods into my life.

Today, in the church, the house of prayer for all nations, I intend to light the fire of prayer with the heart given by the Father. No one asked for it, and even if only one person remains, the duty of believers is the mission of prayer. Prayer continues.

Source of Supply. Rest is not doing nothing. True rest is obtained when God causes us to rest. If we pursue vain things when we should be resting, that is, when we should be meeting the Lord, the Lord will surely make us rest. Ultimately, we need to be supplied with strength, and that comes from God. God's calling is to give new strength to the exhausted us. O Lord, my strength, I love you. - Psalm 18:1

The reason many churches are struggling today is not that there are few faithful people. Rather, it is a problem because there are too many. There are many who, after being faithful, claim their "own share." The Lord rejoices with tears over our dedication and labor, but He wanted us to say, "We are unprofitable servants." (Luke 17:9)

Do Not Be Discouraged, But Fear. The reason for discouragement is relying on oneself. The reason for separation is relying on people. The reason for ruin is relying on material possessions and circumstances. Only those who fear the Lord will shine like the stars in the sky.

When you wake up this morning, before getting out of bed, as soon as you open your eyes, confess this: Today, more than anything else, I want to know God more and love Him better. Life is preparation for eternity. The present life is practice for what we will do eternally in the eternal kingdom. Faith, hope, love, peace, gentleness, self-control, intimacy, praise, worship...

Reason for Frustration. Frustration indicates two things: that you are not looking with the Lord's heart, and that the possibility of using human shortcuts is very high. It is better to be ruined clinging to the cross of Jesus. Whether we live or die, we live and die for the Lord, so whether we live or die, we belong to Him, don't we?
Source of Worry. Worry stems from greed. If you are content, you will experience the power of peace. Contentment comes from trusting the Almighty Lord.
Self-Denial. Do not try to fulfill your desires; strive to fulfill your mission. If you cannot deny your desires, you will achieve shame, but the mission will bring life, honor, and infinite glory.

Did you pray before leaving home today? Did you pray for the grace you would receive today? This is a popular gospel song from the 80s. Please pray when you write emails, use social media, make phone calls, meet friends, or turn on your computer at work. Pray that He will govern my words, actions, thoughts, and emotions, not only protecting me from sin but also enabling me to speak and act in a way that reveals God's righteousness. Especially in conversations, pray to ask the Lord, to cast away human desires, and to fulfill His will.

Reason for Joy. Why would someone in prison rejoice as if they had something good? But Paul is someone who laughs even in his sleep if it's about the "Gospel." When he saw the disciples in Philippi fellowshipping in the Gospel and living by it, the chains could not stop his joy. "And I pray that you may be able to share in the fellowship of the Gospel from the first day until now, with joy." - Philippians 1:4, 5
The crisis in families today is not the lack of health, wealth, or children, but the lack of prayer.

Today, darkness tempts us to grasp more, to possess more because it is good to have, even to become winners in the world through productivity that turns stones into bread. To overcome Satan, we must offer ourselves to God and abandon our lusts.

Reason for Restraint. "For I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory that is to be revealed to us." - Romans 8:18 The reason Paul speaks of suffering in the world is that the world hates us because "1. we are heirs, 2. we are with God, 3. we are partakers of His glory, and 4. we are recipients of the Spirit's testimony." We must persevere and win, holding the line of glorious inheritance amidst suffering. The Lord's glory is great.

Unstoppable Acceleration. If the heart beats, it runs. If breath is drawn, it runs. For those running to fulfill their mission, the words "God is good" are sufficient. We do not believe the words of people saying they have "discarded their foolishness." Truly, those who hope only in the Lord speak through their lives. If we follow without regret until the time the Lord tells us to run, and to the place He tells us to run, that is the glory of this 부족한 자.

I cannot sleep tonight. It is not insomnia, but because each person feels so precious. I too had my youth. I had spiritual mentors who offered me their shoulders. I will offer my shoulder to the youth without regret, so they can step on it. When their visions are fulfilled, that is when my vision is fulfilled. That is why He makes me pray for souls.

If I were to summarize today and stand before God tomorrow, I would ask for love. To be able to love the souls given to me until the end, to become blind in love, unable to see their strengths and weaknesses.

"Come." So Peter got out of the boat and walked on the water to Jesus. - Matthew 14:29 Faith is jumping out of the boat without Jesus. Even if it feels like death, you must jump from the boat without Jesus onto the water where Jesus is. It is believing and serving what you boast about, rely on, and are drawn to.
The wounds of the heart, like rose thorns that used to hurt when touched for a long time, will someday become precious and beautiful memories. Because even pain is a valuable part of my life.

"The secret things belong to the Lord our God, but the revealed things belong to us and to our children forever, that we may do all the words of this law." - Deuteronomy 29:29 The secret realm belongs to God, and the revealed realm belongs to humans. We should entrust to God the areas we cannot comprehend and obey the revealed areas. Do not lament what you do not know; rather, keep what has been revealed.

A Good Loss. Some people, despite doing their best, experience a decline in faith. They feel their shortcomings and know they must change to live, yet they always seem to be moving backward. The reason is that they are not prepared to lose. A greater value can only be obtained after losing a lesser value. You cannot have both revival and the world. You must make a firm decision and boldly discard what needs to be discarded. Only then will you receive the blessing of having all these things added.
Only through His Son can we approach the Father, and only by the Holy Spirit's influence can we believe in the Son. Not by strength, nor by power.
Only two choices are possible: flee or fight. If you evade, it is easy, but you will flee your entire life; if you confront, you will see the path God has given you.

All attention and thoughts must be filled with Jesus. You must think only of Jesus 24 hours a day. Living in obedience is more important than preaching the Word. The reason we cannot live as we preach is not that the truth is not truth, but because we have not met Jesus, have not walked with Him, have not become one with Him, and do not think of Him. Whom do you think of when you open your eyes in bed in the morning? (Hebrews 3:1) Therefore, holy brothers, who share in the heavenly calling, consider Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession.

The Utmost Great Reward. God promised Abraham an exceedingly great reward. However, that reward was invisible. It was God Himself, who is unseen. He wanted to give His eternal self, not a fleeting blessing.
Only a Loving Heart. The Lord gives it.
The righteous live by faith alone. The boldness of being God's child, received through the sprinkling of Jesus' blood, that is 'righteousness'.
I hope you strive to stand firm only before the Lord. Remember, it is only the Lord. Not man. To try to stand before man is to become a whitewashed tomb.

* To be faithful where no one is committed and to go out into the world is to gain strength and become someone no one can ignore. However, diligently serving in the church is not recognized by anyone. Because no one recognizes it, you can run. Only the goal will be visible.
Let us look only to the Lord. When we look to the Lord, the things we could not let go of are discarded like dung.
Stand without name or light, so that only the Lord may call you. Become a secret.

Only those who love the Lord can break through this tribulation and trial. For those who love have no fear. Love the Lord, and all problems will become insignificant before His love. I trust in the Lord and run into the enemy's camp; my God, I trust and leap over the wall. - 2 Samuel 22:30

* Persistent humility. Humility is not seeing my shortcomings, but seeing the Lord's glory. An attitude based on myself is always a roller coaster, but if the Lord becomes the rock, I will not be shaken. My shortcomings are not the matter; who the Lord is, is the reason.
The realm that only God does is grace and transformation. I see many miracles around me. They were originally robbers with knives, but now they are angels without wings.
It is only by God's grace. Think about it. There is nothing I can do.

* If you overlook, you will be overlooked. Young people have great visions and dreams. But what they overlook is losing the big things by missing the small ones. Depending on the perspective, small things can be easily overlooked, but they are also issues that can be immediately obeyed. Small things like eating habits create habits, and habits determine destiny. Daniel resolved not to defile himself with the king's food or the wine he drank, and asked the chief of the eunuchs to allow him not to defile himself. - Daniel 1:8

An orchestra musician must focus on the conductor. They should not be distracted by the gestures of those in the audience, their gazes, their whispers and evaluations, or even the expectations of their accompanying family. If they start to be conscious of these things, they will play differently, and ultimately, it will be an unhappy performance. Sometimes it may not make sense, but follow the conductor. Jesus is my only conductor. Living the life the Lord has given requires faith and courage. You must refuse to live other people's lives.

Even if the whole world forsakes me, Jesus will not forsake me; He looks after me until the end. I fear not water or fire, nor am I afraid of swords and spears; the Lord is my high fortress and my shield.
When we look to the Lord with all our hearts, dying souls and dry hearts come back to life. We must look to the Lord with all our being for His anointing.

* You cannot run into the enemy's camp with common sense. There are two paths people seek: whether a path is recognized by others, or whether it is the most stable path. For that, they gather information from all directions. Living this way is bound to end in complaint. It ends in complaining about others, blaming oneself, or regretting. However, a life lived by asking the Lord, not by the choices of others or oneself (greed), does not end in regret and is glorious. Therefore, you must choose: either sink slowly within the bounds of common sense, or trust the Lord and run into the enemy's camp.

Gentleness is directed towards people. Faithfulness is done before the Lord. If you are faithful before people, it is not faithfulness but hypocrisy. It is right to be faithful and not expect a response. Be lonely for the Lord; He will fill you.

Blessed are the gentle (those who can control their reactions). They will inherit the earth. You must prepare not for 'if' you get hurt, but for 'when' you will be hurt. We must treat wounds with love.
Unhealthy attention is called obsession. It is a strong warning that there will be no peace. It also becomes a mistake of choosing unhappiness.

* The secret of relationships. It is my delusion that sharp intellect and decisive action will smartly protect my relationships. Relationships are a blessing and must be protected by God. After sin and fleshly desires entered the world through Adam, relationships are the first to be destroyed. Relationships are protected by the fruit of the Spirit. I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh. - Galatians 5:16
Choose only what is right. You will never regret it. You are doing things that bring glory and blessings.

Those with many options are those who do not rely on the Lord. God's people not only live simply but also have few varieties of choices. It is like a complex cockpit for unbelievers, but for believers, it is just a single switch. The name of that switch is 'faith'. Please use faith. Please hope and believe.

* Humiliation. Everyone worries about being humbled, being looked down upon by others, and puts on a show. A person of faith should rather worry about pride in their heart. As much as possible, you should humble yourself by carrying out the Lord's will. Only then can the Lord use you.
A perfect picture does not come from a perfect sketch. Faith is not following one's own stubbornness, but following God's guidance.
I'm stopping by. Have a happy day today. I will definitely go to England if I get the chance ^^^^
I'm stopping by~ It seems like our country is still a good place to live~ I will go to England someday~! I want to go to America ㅠ I want to go if the opportunity arises. Have a pleasant evening~~!!

* When the path is not visible, when the path is not visible and it is dark, the Lord becomes my path. While many people in the world strongly speak of the direction of the path, the only path is Jesus. I am the way, the truth, and the life...
Royal priests do not fear people. They do not hate them either. This is because they know they have received a much greater calling.

The word "king" means the best in that field, and "ruling" means continuously exercising broad influence. We must become unparalleled individuals with high authority and influence in specific areas. That area is 'life'. Romans 5:17
* Shaking faith in Jesus is due to a lack of faith. It is because we do not know who God is and do not believe in Jesus. If you love Jesus, you will not live that way.
My God, my King, I will exalt You and praise Your name forever.
Why do you study? Why do you work? Why do you toil so much? Why can you endure so much? Why do you pray and fast so much? Why do you reach out your hand even while shedding tears? My dream is love.

Clear and Simple solution! If there is disorder in life, there must be a solution that fits it. That is a clear goal (caught by Jesus) and burning passion (running towards it). It is a reaction that appears when you know Jesus. Know Jesus. Know Him more. Continue~~ Not that I have already obtained it or am already perfect, but I press on so that I may lay hold of that for which I also was laid hold of by Christ Jesus. Philippians 3:12

Why do Christians live in the world without power? The first reason: thinking of God's grace as mere fire insurance... God's grace fills you with greater power than Daniel or John the Baptist. Grace is needed in every area.

Why is the 'power of the blood' the only way to overcome all the trials and challenges that humans face? It is because no other name has been given to mankind by which we must be saved. Jesus alone is the way, the truth, and the life. All clever tricks and calculations that do not hold onto Jesus are just the suffering of 'kicking against the goads.' The Lord is the answer to all problems, the cause of all happiness, and the Savior who will be praised from everlasting to everlasting.

Why do you worry and seek out anxieties that you are told not to? It is because you cannot be thankful for what you have been given. Trust in the Lord's sovereignty and be bold. The Lord has already given you much. "My grace is sufficient for you."

Why can't you kill yourself and are dragged by worldly values? It is because you are not prepared to lose everything. It is because you cannot crucify everything except the Lord so that it does not lead you. Unless you have the heart to give up everything to gain the Lord alone, the roller coaster of faith will continue. However, whoever wants to save their life will lose it, and whoever loses their life for my sake will find it. We invite you to the Saturday meeting.
If you ask why I am here, I would answer Jesus. Since you asked for a reason, I will give you the reason. Jesus is the only reason for my life.

Why are you shaken? If the foundation is not firm, the higher you go, the more you will shake. Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God— Ephesians 1:1 "I am an apostle of Christ. I am a tool of Christ. I am a prophet conveying God's will." This is Paul's confession. That is why he is not shaken by imprisonment, stoning, danger from robbers, danger from rivers, or thirty-nine lashes. Do you lack confidence? Then you will be shaken. Press on, holding onto the truth and your calling. If you lack confidence, pray until you receive it. If you lack confidence, hold onto the Word until you receive it. You are an irreplaceable confidence of Christ in the world, the only hope.
Why do I feel my head bowed when I try to speak informally... Why do I keep feeling guilty?
When loneliness challenges you, you must choose one of two things: either pity yourself and become solitary, or lift your eyes and empathize with the needs of others.
If you feel lonely, try looking at those who are more marginalized and in the shadows. The Lord Himself is there.
* The Name of Failure Failure is a strong signal that I need to draw closer to the Lord and be filled by Him completely. It is so grateful. The fact that I need the Lord...

The left-handed judge Othniel killed King Eglon and saved Israel, and there was peace for 40 years. The solution to life and peace in relationships is given by the Lord. The peace and redemption of a nation, people, and individual are possible when acting in faith.
Cooking is affection. Towards the other person...
I don't like cooking. It's because I'm a choleric type who dislikes bothersome things that are not very necessary or efficient. That's why I don't cook. I only do what my loved ones like.

* The Weight of Hate and Love The warm love of important people who understand me well is a much more precious value to my heart than the hurt inflicted by unimportant people who don't even know me well. Focus on what is important and let go of the chaff. That is love. Pursue love - 1 Corinthians 4:1

For Joseph, life in prison was simple. In the monotonous daily routine, Joseph's only option was to look to the Lord. To trust in God who guides me, holds my life, and never makes mistakes. Only then did peace and even leisure begin to come amidst the hardship of prison. And then he began to read the expression of the cupbearer and notice him. That was the beginning.

Joseph looked to and 향한 the Lord from his position in prison. David 향한 the Lord through psalms while being chased in the barren wilderness. Abraham looked to the Lord, the Almighty, by looking at the heavens that the Lord showed him in his dead-like, barren age. Direction is more important than speed.

Joseph does not consciously try to dream. It is because the Lord gave it to him. However, he kept it in his heart. By renewing your mind, you will be transformed and discern God's will, abandoning greed and ambition, and redeeming the time. A foolish heart cannot get past adolescence even after middle age.

Joseph was not a person preoccupied only with his own problems. When he looked at the cupbearer's face, he found a clue to solving his own problems along with an opportunity in life. Lift your eyes and serve. Your problems will be solved along with the lives of others.

Joseph's life was full of problems. He had no ability to solve them and could not understand them. That chaos, which seemed like only misfortune, was, when he moved forward with faithfulness and reverence, finally realized to be God's intention to save lives. The Lord's thoughts are different from my emotions.
* The Reason Grace is Needed The more sinful, flawed, and weak I am, the more it proves that I need Jesus. Are you lacking? You must go to Jesus. He will give generously and without finding fault.

Joseph's dream is extraordinary. The sun and eleven stars bowing to his star, and the sheaves of his brothers bowing to his sheaf. Simply put, 'I'm the best.' The dream was given, and immediately after, he faced the threat of death from his brothers, became a slave, and went to prison, becoming a criminal. In that place of hardship and failure, Joseph learned faithfulness and complete trust. Prison and failure are not the end; they become a jewel discovered within them, revealing God's will, and he returns. To fully fulfill the dream... Hardship is a blessing.

If Joseph had believed, "I believe I will go home this year," his faith would have shipwrecked. Joseph believed in God who was with him. Therefore, he could be led on a path he had never taken before. Do not whine to fulfill your desires; rather, consider whether the Lord is with you. The Lord asks, "Where is your faith?"
* Faith is a Gift Faith is a gift, but it grows when used.

When Joseph interpreted the cupbearer's dream, he thought he had met an opportunity that would open his life's path. At that time, the Lord, instead, gave him another two years in prison, a time for patience, prayer, and seeking only the Lord. When cooking rice, there is a steaming period. This makes the rice become rice. If you eat rice without steaming, you eat raw rice.

The reason Joseph did not curse his circumstances was that God was the one who allowed that environment. Sometimes, a higher love may cause pain that cannot be immediately understood, but everything will be accomplished in His time.

I will share something that might be curious from the Deuteronomy quiet time content these days. The law is not made of one thing. The law consists of: first, ethical law – the law that directly reflects God's character and intention (e.g., the Ten Commandments); second, national law; third, ceremonial law; and fourth, health law. Among these, the laws abolished by the cross are the national law for the people of Israel and the ceremonial law that foreshadowed Jesus' sacrifice. National law, being a spatial and temporal application of the Ten Commandments, does not need to be observed by us in the specific external forms explained to Israel, but its principles have not been abolished. Furthermore, the laws that cannot be abolished are health laws, which are immutable as long as human cells do not change, and the ethical and moral laws of the Ten Commandments, which are immutable as long as human nature does not change.
You've been quiet on Facebook lately^^ It's not because of the Olympics, is it? Are you doing well?
These days, during the Sunday meeting... I somehow feel like a 'master.'

Nowadays, in this smart era, we can confirm each other and share news from afar. However, for thousands of years, Christians, though physically apart, have understood each other's situations in real-time through love and intercession for one another. When there is love, we become one even when separated. When we are one, the world sees the glory of the Lord.
Requests and prayers should be motivated by love. The Bible says it is a better way.
Greed + Anxiety = Attachment / Purpose + Passion = Focus / Life - Faith = Worry / Life + Faith = Hope / Life = Worry + Hope (Life is two things: hoping or worrying)
If you look to the Lord beyond greed and ambition, you will surely see the Lord.
Seek to fulfill God's will rather than your own desires. He will fulfill your purpose and grant you peace and honor.
To fulfill your desires would make you anxious, but to fulfill your calling would make you earnest. Escape anxiety and dwell in peace.

* Worry about not being able to retire / Do not love money, but be content with what you have. For He Himself has said, "I will never leave you nor forsake you." Therefore we may boldly say, "The Lord is my helper; I will not fear. What can man do to me?" - Hebrews 13:5,6 / Simply put, "Please stop worrying so much!! Didn't I say I would help you?"
You must fulfill the Lord's will, not your own desires. To fulfill the Lord's will, you must become a person who pleases God's heart.

Job's three friends tried to explain things simply. Job's suffering was beyond the comprehension of his three friends. Suffering comes selectively. Before trying to give answers, please empathize first.

Those who tolerate VS those who hate / 1. To his brothers, Joseph was not so much a younger brother as a favored son of their father, so they tried to kill him. / 2. To Joseph, his brothers were the family God had given him, so he forgave them for the unforgivable wounds they inflicted. → Behold the robe of your son / VS / I am your brother Joseph... / 3. Many people realize, "Ah, Joseph was hurt, but he forgave because of God." Nevertheless, in reality, no relationship improvement occurs. This is because there is a problem with application. / 4. I was the brothers. It is my fault for not recognizing them as the people of God given to me. That is why I hated, envied, and was jealous of, and tried to kill, many Josephs beside me. Recognizing myself as Joseph is a pleasant realization, but discovering that I am the brothers behind the curtain of hypocrisy brings forth weeping and repentance. Have you seen change without repentance?

* Victory over success / Daniel was a highly successful politician who served across administrations and nations, an excellent superior, and a loyal subordinate. His career and influence extended to the ancient Near East and beyond. However, what Daniel desired was not such a rosy success, but 'present holiness' by setting his mind (Daniel 1:8). If you are holy, the Lord will establish you. Because the Lord needs holy people.
Forgiveness and understanding do not lead to love. It is because we love that we forgive and understand.
Forgive. Otherwise, you will become like that person. And you will marry someone like that.
Our beloved youth are now beginning the Saturday meeting praise. Though I am in Korea, my heart is overwhelmed. Our Lord God is worthy of all glory.

The enemy that hinders God's amazing plan for our lives is the sin of stubborn pride. We can see this in King Saul. / 1. He blamed others. / 2. He hid his own faults. / 3. He justified himself. / And above all, he was more concerned with how he appeared to people (appearances) than with how he was thought of by God. Compare the confessions of David and Saul. It becomes clear. / "God, don't take Your Holy Spirit from me! All I want is to please You. I know I have failed You but please forgive me. Cleanse my heart." - David from Psalm 51 / "Don't take my kingdom from me. Please stand with me so I won't look bad in front of my people." - King Saul from Samuel 15:22-35 / Pride leads to destruction.
If what we pursued fiercely in our lives was love, we have no regrets.

Before our Almighty God, nothing about me is hidden or unknown to Him. He even has faithful and amazing plans and purposes for my life, and all of them are written in His book before a single day of them has begun. That means the Lord is with me, not only by my side but also in my heart and thoughts.

* The courage to stand at the edge of a cliff at work - At the request of a junior, I hope you will have the courage to stand at the edge of a cliff, acknowledging that it is by God's grace that I am who I am, not by the forced conditions of the world, entrusting everything, and not fearing fate. Believe that God permits everything and that God, who turns evil into good, is working behind all these things. Storms are not overcome by rowing, but problems are solved with humility and reverence. Because the purpose of the storm is to be humbled and obedient. And do not fear, entrust everything, and draw a spiritual line like Daniel, maintaining consistent behavior. Do not fear those who breathe through their noses, but calmly stand at the edge of the cliff. Daniel resolved... - Daniel 1:8
Our good God the Father gives us grace. One of those graces is 'opportunity'.

Even if we don't know the path we are taking and it seems dark, we can rejoice because the Holy Spirit is with us. When the Holy Spirit is with us, God's will begins to be poured out and fulfilled, regardless of our own abilities. We rely on the Holy Spirit.

Our worth and significance are not something we can prove. Many people in the world are busy pouring their energy into trying to prove this. The value of our existence is found in God. Leaving the joy of a full net behind, Peter stood before Jesus and saw his true self, becoming a fisher of men. The qualification to live as one's true self, without clinging to meaningless or worthless things, is possible when standing before the Lord.

When we pray, sometimes God allows both paths. This is because God sees the hidden motives within us. This is consideration for our weakness. The truly wise person here returns to their initial intention and asks God again through prayer.

If we love someone, we cannot turn a blind eye to their pain and difficulties. We feel sorry for them and would gladly suffer in their place if we could. God's love conquers all. May you possess the Lord's tears.

* The ability to persevere / If you do not stop, you win. Because Jesus becomes the purpose and loving Jesus becomes the passion. Not that I have already obtained it or am already perfect, but I press on to make it my own, because Christ Jesus has made me His own. - Philippians 3:12

We are not permitted to hurt anyone, but there is one being who is permitted. It is the devil. Remind the devil of his destination and proclaim it. Subdue him so that the defeated one cannot accuse us. The more we do this, the more the devil's heart will be hurt, and he will have to flee with his head down to attend an inner healing seminar. ^^

The grace we enjoy is not a given. It means there are leaders holding aching hearts and shedding blood. They strive for the sheep as if they are debtors. Thank God for giving us good pastors and churches.

This peace we enjoy is a peace that the world cannot know or understand. Because to know that peace, one must know Jesus, and the world has no interest in Jesus and does not know Him. "Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid." - John 14:27

The reason we are not bold is because we distance ourselves from Jesus' suffering. That glorious Son of God had to be dragged along by sinners, often limping, and beaten severely to save us, who were utterly hopeless. The one who is in the form of God was dragged to His death. If this suffering is in your heart, you cannot continue to live like this. There is nothing to be proud of or to boast about. Nothing but the cross...

What we demand and receive as if it were natural is actually all the boundless grace of the Lord. Having money and leisure time does not guarantee a happy journey. The peace and joy that the Lord gives are entirely grace, and therefore they must be sought through prayer.
The suffering we endure is not worthy to be compared with the glory that we will enjoy in the future.
* Not coincidence, but destiny / It is not that you were born in the wrong era or are unlucky, but that the Lord has led you this way. It is to become more humble and obedient. The Lord is Almighty.

The reason we should not be troubled in our hearts is the declaration that the Almighty God dwells among us as Immanuel. Because He affirmed, "Believe in God, believe also in me," we can approach His throne without any fear.

When you try hard but it doesn't work. You try hard, steal time, and do your best until the last moment, but there is no change and you are always in the same place. It is because you are not overcoming your limits. It is not about effort, but about changing your class.

If we have many spiritual experiences, receive smooth guidance, gain the praise and support of other believers, and are all cheering and rejoicing, but turn away from Jesus carrying the cross who stood silently in the dark corner at the end of the cheering crowd, it will be the most tragic failure and a nauseating thing. More important than the gift is the giver of the gift.

If we live by faith, we will find a way to live even when the heavens are falling. A different person dwells among the people of faith. At the last Passover supper, the disciples ate Jesus' life, blood, and flesh, but Jesus drank the cup of judgment and wrath for all humanity to the last drop. When Daniel's three friends were thrown into the extreme furnace, a fourth person was among them. We are not alone, and Jesus is among us, drinking a different cup than ours. Then the king spoke and said, "I see four men unbound, walking in the midst of the fire, and they are not hurt; and the appearance of the fourth is like a son of the gods." - Daniel 3:25 This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you. - Luke 22:20

When we share the grace we have received, we sometimes realize new grace. This is because when we become a channel of grace, it is God's will, and we experience God's help. The pipe of grace never runs dry.
We cannot help but speak of what we have seen and heard.

Our busyness stems from the greed to show a perfect image. The Lord earnestly desires us to surrender our entire being to Jesus without reservation. A clean person is one who frequently offers their dirty, smelly feet to Jesus. Those who hide become dirty, and those who reveal become clean. The power of holiness is the power to reveal.

Faith that leaves the familiar. When He tells us to leave what is familiar and relied upon, and to go forward without telling us the destination, it means that God Himself will lead us to the destination. Leave your country, your relatives, and your father's house, and go to the land that I will show you. - Genesis 12:1
We have survived not because we are strong. It is solely due to His boundless patience and mercy. Boasting and pride are mere pretenses; we survive only because of His unfailing love.

The promised land we hope for is not a place where milk and honey flow automatically due to favorable conditions. It is a land we advance into and conquer through our relationship with God. Since we would surely die if God did not help us, we advance with the confession, "If I perish, I perish."

"What shall we do?" Acts 2:37 When your heart is pricked, if you just maintain your dignity, it will rot and produce poison. Naturally, repentance like "What should I do?" should arise. I hope for a repentance of humility, confessing and prostrating ourselves in the place of worship, the place of the Word, the place of grace, the place of prayer, and the place of fellowship. That is what God desires. When people were stoning Stephen, they too were pricked in their hearts but ignored it, and instead became persecutors, murderers, and enemies of the cross. Never ignore the prick of conscience and the Word.
Spiritual growth begins when we face ourselves honestly. It is very painful. However, it is a part that must be dealt with, so it can never be skipped.
Although we cannot be saved more than once in this world, that revelation must daily flood anew into our hearts. I believe, Yes I believe.
As we live our lives, should we first build the ark of salvation according to the Lord's will? Or should we first build the tower of Babel for our own glory? We must seriously consider this.
Humility. Humility is lowering oneself before God. However, the verification of that humility is lowering oneself before people as well.
The only reason we can deny ourselves and take up our cross is because Jesus bore us and took up the cross for us.
We are not defeated by the enemy because we are weaker than them. It is because we fight evil with the same spirit they possess, the same wickedness. Overcome evil with good.
Even when we ignore the Lord because we don't know Him well, He knows us so well. O LORD, you have searched me and you know me. - Psalm 139:1

Love is.... Peter hesitated when asked if he loved the Lord. He felt too inadequate to say he loved Him, yet it was ambiguous to say he didn't. Love is being captivated. Therefore, it moves me. What does not move is not love.

We live with worry and anxiety, being chased, because we live only for ourselves. A life lived by bread alone is always hasty and arduous. A life lived by God's Word, a life that lives only by the grace the Lord gives, is balanced and abundant due to the Lord's sovereignty.

The most reasonable deal we can make with God is to give glory to God and also take care of ourselves to some extent. However, the Lord does not agree to any transactions. He sees hidden motives and judges according to deeds. Grace is given to the humble, and the proud are repelled.

Love is... Love is what occupies me. Therefore, through pain, we can know love. If you are in pain because of that older brother, it means that older brother is present, and if you shed tears because of her, she is present. What fills me without God giving it is called greed, covetousness. Therefore, it is said that covetousness is idolatry. It takes the place of the Lord's position, the place that fills me... Covetousness is idolatry. - Colossians 3:5

The primary goal of God's guidance for us is to lead us into a closer relationship with Jesus. All other goals should be secondary to this. If, while doing the Lord's work, we stray from His glory and focus, everything must cease, and we must return to the cross.
We can never satisfy those who love us. We will even disappoint them. However, after that trial, it will bring life to all and glory to the Lord.

The blessing of the rocky soil. It is a greater blessing to cultivate the rocky soil than to go to the fertile land and easily enjoy many things. The desolate valleys of Achor are not for cursed people, but a privilege that I, who have been called by the glorious God, can receive. At that time, the ability to give all honor, glory, and all power to the Lord without appropriating them arises.
We do not reveal ourselves or boast about ourselves. It is not because we were crucified for the world, but because Jesus was crucified.

The gaze is guidance. If we lose our focus from the Lord, our gaze is stolen by our circumstances, we begin to calculate the situation, and eventually fall into fear. Fearing that we will be left behind and become a loser, we cling to this and that, pursuing vain things. Only when we lay down even our last pride before God and look only to the Lord will we finally experience the Lord using and guiding us. If I pursue it, it will only move further away.
We have limits in loving others. We can love them "as ourselves." If we do not accept ourselves as the Lord accepts us, we cannot love others.
We humble ourselves. We will continue to humble ourselves until we weep. We will continue to weep until we are broken. Then we will discover true peace that the world cannot give.

Calling. It is not that we are used just because we want to be used. We are used when the Lord calls. Worrying about circumstances and boasting about popularity are nothing but futile delusions. Only the Lord is the One who can open my way.

We must choose each moment whether to be real or fake, regardless of the titles we hold or the evaluations of others. The standard for this decision is whether the motivation of our lives is the Lord. If we can gain the Lord and lose everything else, this decision will not only become easier but will actually lead us to gain everything.

We thirst for victory so much. We must win in academics, careers, relationships, and the world. We must win while alive. That sole victory comes only from faith. Therefore, the righteous focus on faith. Faith wins. For whatever is born of God overcomes the world. And this is the victory that has overcome the world—our faith. -1 John 5:4

When we remember someone, we are not remembering their information. What we remember more and want to remember is the meaning in their heart. Therefore, while information may fade, the impression deepens. In God's heart, it is all about my thoughts. Who in this world can receive such a heart-stirring love?

* True opportunities come when you humble yourself. Do you believe in the Lord's sovereignty? Is Jesus your Master? Then stand at the edge of the cliff without fear. You will be used when you humble yourself, and you will live when you die.

Although we don't openly express it, there are times when we live with an ungodly complaint that the Lord does not supply or resolve enough. Eventually, when we can no longer take a single step forward, we might explode saying, "What more do you want me to do?" The Lord quietly says, "My grace is sufficient for you."
We must ask for greater power. So that we can sacrifice and love more...

* The power of choice to become a shining star Daniel was a supremely glorious person among God, people, fellow believers, and unbelieving colleagues, earning the evaluation of a life that shines like the stars in the sky (Daniel 12:3). This was already decided long ago when he was taken captive (Daniel 1:8). Set your mind and decide your future now.

We resemble two types of people. First, we resemble those we love. This is because we constantly think about and cherish those we love in our hearts. Second, we resemble those we do not forgive. This is because, even while refusing to forgive and rejecting them, we hold them in our hearts and hate them. Therefore, we feel attracted to familiar people and eventually get married.
* A leader of grace is someone who bestows grace. If you are not becoming that, everyone will thirst. Therefore, leaders must be those who repent and bleed.
We will surely face trials. There is no avoiding them. This is because we must show whether this faith is genuine. When we face trials, it becomes evident whom we truly trusted and loved.

We must serve the neglected and unexplored places by humbling ourselves. To do this, we must prepare diligently and refine ourselves. If we are lazy during the time of preparation and refinement, we will not be able to serve sufficiently when the time comes to humble ourselves. Serving with humility requires skill.
* Sin is not to be endured but discarded. Jesus is so good and lovely. So much so that sin is discarded.
We want to reap all the fruits. Then we must sow. Even if we cry, now is the time to sow.
We worry and fear. We fret about people looking down on us, criticizing us, or ostracizing us. We fret about becoming failures in life. The Lord asks us, "Where is your faith?" -Luke 8:25

* Fear of evaluation, peace of love. If you compare and calculate, you will surely worry and fear. This is because there are so many things you have not achieved. However, if you let go of everything and love, you will have peace. This is because you are fulfilling the most important purpose. Loving is doing your best.
We will enter heaven only after fighting spiritual warfare. That war cannot be fought with emotions, logic, strength, or popularity. Only with the Word of God, who is Truth, can we win.
When we give what is ours, we only give impurities. Therefore, we must first seek 'Him' to come and fill us.

We have an instinct to protect ourselves. However, we can never protect ourselves, and it only makes our relationships difficult. Only the Lord touches my heart. Having died and lived in the Lord, there is no need to defend oneself anymore.

* To gain Isaac, Abraham gains a son twice. Once he gains him at a hundred years old, and again, he gains him on Mount Moriah more than ten years later. The first time he gained him through patience, but the second time he gained him through offering. To gain Isaac, you must offer Isaac.

We are weak beings who cannot protect ourselves. How futile are attempts to protect ourselves by our own strength! True peace and resolution come when we entrust ourselves to the hands of the Almighty.

We are not called to disseminate information (rumors). That is not the proper role of the anointed. We are called and anointed to testify of the cross and make disciples, and we have received power. In that sense, regardless of age or status, we are teachers.

We try human methods to avoid even a little pain. However, we do not realize that such cunning tricks bring greater pain. Standing right with the Lord is difficult at first, but it becomes the most peaceful and happy path.

* The power to advance We become discouraged when life doesn't open up and we walk in circles. It's a matter of priorities. What is clear is that repentance comes before progress. If we are not transformed, we cannot transform others.
We often disagree with or criticize the opinions of others. The Lord also often disagrees with our opinions, but He always stays with us.

We desperately need the Lord's intervention and governance. Acknowledging the Lord's sovereignty means abandoning our own strength and praying. It means abundant, overflowing prayer, not perfunctory prayer. "Lord, govern me."
* The Lord's Omnipotence Humble yourself and repent. And wait. This is the Lord's word.

We must decide our future now. If we live by the flesh, we will surely die, but if we put the flesh to death by the Spirit, we will surely live. For this to be possible, we must have a heart that fears the Lord. We must worship Him.
We are called to bless. The Lord has also given us such authority and power. When we bless in the Lord's name, we can become truly happy just by 'being together.'

We tend to entrust big steps to the Lord. No. We must be entrusted before taking a step, and after taking it, we must accept the circumstances permitted by the Lord. We fight where we are told to fight with faith and let go where we are told to let go.
We must believe and pray for the Kingdom of God, that is, God's most powerful reign, to come upon us and our land. May you enjoy that reign and presence in 14 hours. The Lord will do it.

We want a solution, and we want it now. But God has a slightly different perspective. What God wants from us is to 'earnestly know God.' Through crying out in our problems and hearing His voice, we come to know our Father God, who is greater than any solution. Earnestly know God.

We do not aim for the improvement of our actions as our primary goal. The transformation of life is a result of the transformation of our essence. Therefore, we do not force life. True freedom does not come from our satisfaction but from where we belong. In Jesus, there is no condemnation. Have you not read in the law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple desecrate the Sabbath and are guiltless? Matthew 12

Gentleness is an attitude of not doubting or resisting God's way of dealing with us, but considering it good. Both the situation and what we must learn through it are under the Lord's sovereignty. Let us entrust everything to the Lord's hands.

It is not our job, education, or career that holds us. It is the Lord who holds me. Then whom should we truly fear? Therefore, those who believe in Jesus are not threatened by such dilemmas. We can entrust our lives to the Lord and go.
We do not read the Bible to change God's will for us. We read the Bible to change our thoughts about God.
To overcome obstacles to His presence, we must come before God naked. A polished appearance, a masked self, cannot meet God, for God does not meet with fakes.

God's passion for us has never been abandoned or cooled. From eternity to eternity, His passion for us will continue, and the enemy who comes to harm will be ashamed and retreat. O LORD, though your hand is lifted up, they do not see it; but they will see your zeal for your people and be put to shame. Fire will consume your enemies. Isaiah 26:11

For God's power to be fully manifested within us, our ego must die. It must die to the level of a clay jar. That is why the Apostle Paul said that God's power was perfected in him when he was weak. If there is any remaining ground of confidence in us besides Jesus, it must be completely abandoned.

The mission of a minister is to let God's word flow through them, making people love Jesus. It is also to make those who reject the cross unable to sleep soundly and to make them uneasy. Peace without the cross is a sin.

Co-workers are extremely important to us. Having someone who can pray for you and someone with whom you can share grace is like having a channel for the Holy Spirit. The Holy Spirit works through love and forgiveness. Hudson Taylor, who ministered in inland China, said this: Even if you fast, read the Bible, and pray all day, if you have no co-workers, you will eventually collapse.

Not everything is permitted to us. When we try to give an answer for everything, we leave behind much frustration and many wounds. Let 'yes' be 'yes,' and 'no' be 'no.' Anything forced will be broken.

There are times when the Word clashes with me. However, there is no possibility that the Word will change. It has never done so since the creation of the world, nor will it do so in the future. Ultimately, the change must be in me, not in the Word. God never apologizes or compromises.

The reason we continue to experience pain and wounds is not because we do not know God. Rather, it is because we think we know God too well and try to act in His place. This is why a small matter becomes a great disaster. Do not be afraid. Shall I take God's place? - Genesis 50:19 God is the one who turns evil into good. Joseph was not someone who opposed this, but someone who obeyed and followed. For God meant it for good to bring about that many people should be kept alive, as they are this day - Genesis 50:20 It is not easy for an injured person to say this. It is only possible for someone whose wounds have become glory through the Lord's love.

There is a reason for everything that happens to us. Behind that reason, He is there. Through that reason, we must return to God. Do not confront the problem with the problem itself, but approach Him who speaks through the problem.

Many of the things that happen to us are unfair and incomprehensible. Such things can make us discouraged and cause us to abandon the values we hold dear. However, if we overcome prejudice with tolerance, oppression with compassion, and hatred with love, we will see the glory of God.
The definition of achievement is not what I diligently do. Achievement refers to what God accomplishes.
What we need is not a response of love, but to love 'simply' with the Lord's heart. That is our mission.

There is unavoidable suffering for us. It is suffered for the Lord and for the saints. When you feel you can no longer bear it due to exhaustion, and you overcome the final hurdle with God's help, you will see the Lord's glory. Be courageous. Much is prepared.
We have been given many tools and gifts for God's great purpose. However, if we are not careful and these strengths and gifts take the place of the Lord, it will be a sad thing.

Directionality comes from persistence. A community moving forward in one direction, albeit gradually, is due to a leader who aims for that goal. If there is a clear purpose, a푯대 (goalpost), that can overcome all obstacles, we will eventually move towards that direction. Giving up other things to gain Jesus is not easy and faces much resistance, but the one who goes to the end is the true leader. Because it is the path we must take, and there is no other path.
Our deepest life messages sometimes come from our deepest suffering. Therefore, God sometimes uses suffering to fully accomplish His purpose in our lives.
Our greatest mistake is not allowing God to do what only God can do. A greater mistake is to force and resent family, friends, colleagues, and ourselves to do what God alone can do.

We desperately need salvation in our relationships, finances, character, work, studies, and health, and in our vision. This means we need the kingdom of God. However, this salvation, which feels so distant and as if it requires a 40-day fast, this heaven, is unexpectedly close. If I turn away from sin, the kingdom of God, God's reign, is near. From that time Jesus began to preach and say, "Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand." Matthew 4:17 Those who wish to experience God's reign and salvation today, come to repent.

Quality comes from quantity. The purpose of ministry is to fulfill the Lord's will. However, many people mistakenly think it is about doing it wonderfully well and receiving praise. No. The Lord's will is the transformation of souls, so we must persist and continue until the souls are transformed. If people complain that the praise was repetitive or that we are going too fast, just shut up and keep going. This is about life.

Our hearts have countless tiny holes, so when we pour in the water of grace, it all leaks out over time. Rather than trying to plug the holes, it is more important to pour more in. He desires us to stand in the place of grace, in the place of worship.

The reason we do not fall and rise again amidst all our difficulties, fatigue, weakness, and frailty is the fact that the Lord makes no mistakes. Even if the path we walk appears pitch black, God makes no mistakes.

If we look beneath our displeasure, pain, and anguish, we find 'self-pity' and 'selfish self-love.' In Philippians 1:20-21, the Apostle Paul confesses that his concern was not for himself but was wholly focused on the Lord, and even more than the issues of life and death, the limitations and urgency of reality, he had a greater concern and passion for Christ to be proclaimed and glorified in his decaying body, for His excellency to be revealed. Who can stop such a person? His confession is, 'I have no interest in fleshly desires. I don't even dream of worldly exaltation or recognition in the church. I look solely to the cross and run.' When the aged servant, crucified with Jesus, offered his life like a burning flame for the Lord on the altar, the glory of Christ, admired even by angels, appeared.

Do you know where the crisis in our lives comes from? Is it money, romance, despair, pride, or laziness? The greatest crisis is 'not being satisfied with the Lord alone,' in other words, 'lack of faith.' If we are not satisfied with Jesus alone, no matter what we do or have, it will be insufficient. When we are insufficient, we become greedy, and when we are greedy, we do evil. If God is our shepherd, we will not only have no lack, but our cups will overflow, and our lives will be abundant. - Psalm 23

The reason idolatry is sweet is that it gives us what we want without sacrifice or obedience. God hates blessings without change and repentance, and the pursuit of greed rationalized, and He will surely destroy it with fire.
Idolatry never demands my change. However, fearing God persistently demands that I not give up my change and holiness. Idols let us go slowly to die in falsehood, while God seeks to save us.

One of the purposes of a great investment and strong dedication is to nurture people. It is to transform each and every person into young adults like vacuum cleaners, with a thirst in their eyes that eagerly devours the Word. It is to make them see the glory of God, which can never be satisfied by anything other than Jesus, and send them to London, Korea, and the world. On the day of the Lord's power, His people offer themselves willingly, clothed in holiness; Your young people, like the dew of the morning, come to You - Psalm 110:3.

The reason for dryness is simple: it is because there are no roots. A life not rooted in God is characterized by being overly sensitive to circumstances and placing trust in perishable worldly things. Such a person will wither and become barren. A life that is not dry is one that finds its security in God.
I do not fear destiny. I cannot know it, nor do I need to. It is because I will not live that way.

There are times when you earn less money or get lower scores to obey the Word. You may suffer losses, but you must not be shaken. It is easy if you know the conclusion of those who obeyed. Those who live by faith are honored from above. Whatever you do, God's help is with you.

Just because you are smiling does not mean your heart is smiling too. However, that does not necessarily mean it will end in unhappiness. Sometimes, when truth and our emotions do not align, walking according to the Spirit leads us to a place of peace.
There's no one at Waterloo. Everyone seems tired.
There are train issues for those coming from Waterloo. Delays are expected. Taking the District line from Victoria might be faster. If requested from Wimbledon, we will try to send the church van.

Originally, I am a person with endless shortcomings, unable to deal with precious young people, but by the grace of the Lord, I enjoy such happiness and blessings. Living a life as a bonus, even if it's just for a day of ministry, I earnestly pray to offer it truthfully out of love for the Lord and love for souls.

No one is naturally good-natured. True character comes from faith in the Lord. If you truly believe, authenticity will emerge. [1 Samuel 2:7] The Lord makes poor and makes rich; he brings low and he exalts.

You can do many things, but not all are beneficial. If you strategically focus your energy on what is most important, the rest will fall into balance. That is fearing God. Yet, even without such a strategic and meticulous approach, a deaconess in a rural church grasps the important thing at once. It is truly amazing.

The power of the original - the nature that only the Original possesses, which the world recognizes, does not need to imitate, and cannot be replaced by anything else. Let us repent to become the original.
If you wish, you can be made clean. I wish, be made clean. If the Lord wishes, all things are possible. Prayer is to embody with your whole being what the Lord desires.
Friends who are sending unwanted spam can go to info and block the respective spam from the application menu on the left.

Christians, with the wisdom of fearing the Lord, can discern and yield on trivial matters. However, they triumph because of their heart centered on God. A being that triumphs in letting go, that is a person of God.

A person who laughs on Monday and experiences depression on Wednesday is "thinking a part is the whole." Such a person will have much heartache. God wants us to grow in peace in His direction, and sometimes a change of direction is necessary for that. That is why He awakens us to a part. A life of "all or nothing" is a tiring way to live.

A person who risks their life over trivial matters is foolish. They miss what is truly important. However, a gentle person stakes their life on what is truly important. Therefore, other things can be skipped. A characteristic that appears when one becomes mature and gentle is that they no longer "lose their temper" as before. It is because they know what is truly important. Gentleness comes from wisdom, and wisdom comes from a "value system" that knows what is important. Therefore, fearing the Lord is the beginning of wisdom.

In a crisis, what is truly needed is not 'courage' but 'humility.' It is because we must stand before the Lord. Before God, not man, one is humble. If My people, who are called by My name, humble themselves, and pray and seek My face and turn from their wicked ways, then I will hear from heaven and will forgive their sin and heal their land.

The word 'crisis' (危機) is made up of the words danger (危) and opportunity (機). It means that it is 'dangerous' but can be an 'opportunity.' When you encounter a dead end in life, may you experience the Lord who makes a window in that wall. It means it is time to see the glory of the Lord through that window.

When faced with a crisis, humans instinctively shift blame. Then they attempt to compromise. However, none of these are proper solutions. When we accept everything that happens to us by faith, the work entrusted to us takes place. The only thing we can do is to entrust ourselves.

Even when a weaker person, who is extremely sinful and self-centered, leads me, I must be able to obey it as the Lord's guidance. It is because the Lord uses the weak. Therefore, we must cast aside pride and listen to the Lord's voice. God never uses the proud.
In moments of crisis, during times of testing, what is needed is the basics. The basic of faith is the gospel of the cross, and when we return to the cross, a path begins to appear in the desert.

In moments of crisis, what saves and sustains me is not training or experience. What prevents me from collapsing even when I want to, what prevents me from dying even when I want to die, what prevents me from breaking even when I am bent, is the mission given by the Lord. A person with a mission never dies. It is not that I carry out the mission, but that the mission carries me.

It is a blessing to know how to cherish what is precious. We must know how to cherish the worship we offer now, the collaborators given to us, and the grace we enjoy. A vague expectation that grace will continue can lead us to regard the current favor as a cheap courtesy, and it may eventually turn into ashes. Burn brightly now.
Great passion creates great young adults.

A great life lies in excellent choices. A good choice is not based on one's circumstances but on responding to a great proposal by faith. Joshua and Caleb passed that difficult test, breaking through a competition rate of one million to one.

A true leader is not a judge. Criticism never changes people. They leave judgment to God and are people who fully build up others. Therefore, a leader must be gentle and humble. It is because they must fulfill God's will without adverse effects.
Think of what is above, not of what is on earth. Die to possessions and greed, and be alive to prayer and faith.
A humorous person is appealing. It shows a sense of ease. However, a person who laughs readily is more lovable. A lovable person laughs heartily even at a silly pun.
Fame does not guarantee my success. Rather, pursuing it causes us to lose more precious things. The most renowned thing is to be known by the Lord. "I know you by name."
Temptations are not overcome. Rather, I am crucified to temptation, and the world is to me as such.

The reason things seem like they might work out but don't is because you try to use spiritual cunning when they are close to succeeding. If you let go of that cleverness and return to the basics, you will win. This means your "spiritual prime" is coming. Do not hesitate; decide on your life now.

Those who first satisfy their bodily desires will surely be excluded from all these things. The Lord clearly promised that He would add all these things to those who first seek His kingdom and His righteousness. Those who seek bodily desires will become spiritual outcasts.
Pray and then act.

The less grace there is, the more rules there are. The Pharisees were also overrun with rules about what to do and what not to do. This was because they were spiritually dry. What follows dryness is empty ritual. Did you know that anything not done in faith is sin? Even worship and prayer are like that. It's not what you do that matters, but whether you do it in faith.

Even when you pray, wounds are not completely erased as if by amnesia. However, through His presence and encouragement, the remaining scars of those wounds become a channel to embrace other wounds. A person who, lacking self-esteem, constantly scanned others' expressions with worried eyes, is transformed into a sensitive comforter of others' hearts within grace.
When grace comes, things fall into place. The discouraged find courage, and the proud humble themselves. And they remain in their rightful place.
Grace is not "Good Luck." It is the unstoppable flow of God's providence, which should rightfully come to pass, surging into my barren life. It is "God's grace" that uniquely sustains me.

Grace is God's gift, given freely. Free does not mean cheap. Rather, it is so valuable that it cannot be priced. It means it cannot be obtained through my own strength or effort. Therefore, what is important in grace is the spirituality of waiting. Those who wait will pray, and those who act first cannot wait.

To be healthy, you must exercise. Exercise is going against gravity. If left alone, faith deteriorates. There is a line that must be crossed, a line against the world and a line that puts an end to compromise. Resist greed and worldliness. You will see glory.

You must become someone who knows grace. If you lack faith, you should at least have loyalty. If you have received grace, you must remember it and repay it. This is not for others, but above all, for yourself.
Do not receive grace in vain. Do not begin in the Spirit and finish in the flesh.
Having received grace and being filled, my lips are opened.
The evidence of receiving grace is that we "look after one another." (1 Cor.) The evidence of a person who has received grace is that they serve with a prepared and willing heart.

God is your keeper. No one is insignificant before God. This is especially true for humans, who are made in God's image and redeemed by the blood of His Son. Even the sparrows live by God's mercy. How much more precious am I than a sparrow?

If the focus of your life is misaligned, unnecessary imaginations arise, and eventually, relationships crumble due to misunderstanding. If you look to the Lord and have the peace to lose everything, you can also keep everything. Do not be afraid.

Our concern is to remove difficulties. However, the Lord's concern is for us to be transformed through them and to trust and rely solely on Him. At least for now, such trials and hardships will not be removed. This is because the removal of my pride, stubbornness, unbelief, and hatred is a higher priority. Before I was afflicted, I went astray, but now I keep your word. - Psalm 119:67

Hearing a voice does not mean it is always right. Prophecy must be done according to the measure of faith. For edification, I know in part and prophesy in part. Jesus is the only one to hold onto. Let the speaker be sober, and let the listener discern.
Discern the voice. A man hears a voice: "Kiss her." A woman also hears a voice: "I heard it too." This is not the voice of the Holy Spirit, but the "voice of hormones."

In some countries, drunk driving is punishable by death. This means never get behind the wheel when you are drunk. When you are filled with worry, anxiety, or distress, you should not make any decisions or take any action. You kill yourself and others. When you are excited or fearful, it is not the time to decide or act, but the time to pray and stand before the Lord with your mouth shut.

Panic comes when you only look at circumstances and calculate. The worst way to calculate circumstances is to compare yourself with others. When you lose faith, situations are always exaggerated, and comparisons always end in misery.

Even in the promised land after receiving an answer, famine can come. It would have been hard for Joseph to believe that being bound in stocks and his feet in fetters, and his heart utterly broken, was the Lord's guidance. However, the Lord faithfully continued to work without stopping, faithfully accomplishing His purpose. He was behind all these events. - Genesis 45:5 God was behind it.
When you are discouraged, you need to eat, drink, and rest. This was God's first method of healing for Elijah.

Doing righteousness or becoming strong in weakness is not done by humans who have achieved enlightenment or possess strong execution abilities. It is simply done. Live by faith alone... Live by faith. "By faith they also did righteousness and received promises and became strong when they were weak..." - Hebrews 11:33
The righteous do not live by emotion. The righteous live by faith alone.
I hope you realize these three things. First, living according to God's will is our duty. Second, it is God's duty to take care of such people. Third, therefore, there is nothing to fear.

Consider whether this prayer will be answered and feared by the devil. Even if my desires are fulfilled and I become prosperous, the devil will not tremble with fear, but when believers deny themselves and give up their rights, the powers of darkness are broken. The cross is power.
Choose whether to complain about this problem or entrust it to the Lord's hands. The outcome will be as different as heaven and earth.
Let us pray tonight for all the members who need prayer. Let us look only to the Lord who will make rivers flow in our barren lives like a desert.

No one in this world can change the past. However, the Lord is the only one who can cover and restore a shameful person completely. The reason is that Jesus is the King of kings. There is no king in this world who wore such a thorny crown and shed blood for sinners. The Lord broke the power of death and Satan through the cross, forgave my sins, and gave me life.

What was the most extreme work of Satan in the history of this world? World War II? The 9/11 attacks? No. It was at Golgotha, the event of Jesus being crucified and killed. Sinners, designating the sinless Son of God as a sinner and crucifying Him, there is no greater sin in history. Then, what was the greatest good work that occurred in the history of this world? It was the event of Jesus being crucified at Golgotha. God turned the most wicked act that Satan or those deceived by Satan could commit into the greatest good deed in this world. In soccer, they call this an own goal.
Do not fear whether you can handle this task. This task is not mine, nor is it my business. It is my obedience to what He will accomplish.

There is no one in this world who will not tremble before judgment. It is so terrifying that in Revelation, the rich and kings wished for mountains to fall upon them to hide from the gaze of the Lord. The only act that will overcome the judgment of the last day, even without deeds or religion, is 'mercy.' Show mercy now. Then the Lord will show you mercy on the day of judgment.

The love that best reflects God's love in this world is the love of parents. One was a father who seemed so strong and tall when I was young, and as the years passed, he became a frail earthly parent. The Almighty God became weak for us. He came in the form of sinful flesh to embrace sinners. That is the love of the Almighty. The love of the Almighty is the strong one becoming weak, one who does not hesitate to fall into the mud with me, who is covered in filth, and one who, out of love, does not use force but grants freedom to choose love.
Blessed is the person who fulfills their purpose in this world and departs. Jesus said, "It is finished," before He breathed His last. It is because He loved us with His blood, without regret.

Believers will not be shaken by any attack or hindrance in this world. For there is no one who can snatch them from the Lord's love. The Almighty God is love, and that love is not abandoned.
