No trial is easy. Each one is so difficult that it requires risking your life and being prepared for death, but it is not frightening because of Jesus. I decide to win. Because I must win.
We must listen to God's word because I am an existential sinner and foolish in my actions. "Whoever listens to me will live in safety and be at ease, without fear of harm" (Proverbs 1:33).
Hoping in the hopeless is faith, but hoping for what should not be hoped for is greed.
Deal with problems in God's way. That is to respond with obedience and gentleness. Then you will see the Lord's intervention.

If you know God's love, God's story will never be boring. Unfortunately, many people find it boring. If you can hear these words regardless of your emotional state, speak honestly, and if a small motive for change arises, I guarantee that you are someone who knows God's love for you. "And we have known and believed the love that God has for us. God is love, and whoever abides in love abides in God, and God abides in him" (1 John 4:16).

The Son of God was silent towards those who reviled Him. He was worthy of more praise than anyone, yet He remained silent. This is because He spoke through the cross. You did not love me, but I love you.
The Son of God came to oppose the works of the devil. Let us oppose him with faith and be victorious.

Those who feel God's pain can never go astray. Jonah did not know God's heart for Nineveh, and the older brother did not know the father's heart. In the end, God's love conquers all, and there can be no love that does not know that pain.
God's grace, the grace of God that holds me.
The voice of God. Hearing the voice of God begins with believing that the faithful and loving God absolutely wants to speak to me.
Anyone who knows God's character will know that God never speaks through condemnation. God does not threaten or coerce. The devil tries to deceive us with guilt, but we are under the precious blood.
If you acknowledge God's sovereignty, you will experience the Lord's reign. Then the kingdom of God, which we go to after death, becomes a reality before you. Deny yourself and acknowledge Jesus.
There is no word that brings more peace and comfort than God's sovereignty. Since the Lord Himself reigns, there is nothing to worry about.
God called me not to be obsessed, but to be focused. Focusing on what has not been given to me becomes obsession, and approaching Jesus Christ becomes focus.
If God were only someone who granted my desires and wishes, I would be disappointed. I would even come to hate Him. God gave Himself and became our all.
For God so loved the world. In the Father's heart, who embraces impossible sinners and covers our shame, we run to Christ and break our lives.

Even with much, I feel anxious as if I were living in someone else's house. It is because it is greed, not what was given to me. Believing in Jesus and hoping brings heavenly peace. This is because Jesus is the best that was given to me entirely. The one who seeks only the Lord is the most peaceful and strong.
If you don't know, it's stress, but if you understand, you will be blessed.

God says, "Because he loves me, I will rescue him; because he knows my name, I will protect him. When he calls to me, I will answer him; I will be with him in trouble, I will deliver him and honor him." Psalm 91:14-15. Please confess with all your heart that you know the Lord's name. Confess passionately that you love the Lord. Then the Lord will rescue you.

If God does not give it, you have received nothing. (John 3:27) The Lord always gives the best. Because He is the good Lord. Therefore, the people and things that belong to me are undoubtedly the best options from heaven.
Cherish the heart that God seeks. It is a broken heart. The Lord seeks those who know they are poor in spirit and know how to mourn.
I pray that we may have a beautiful relationship blessed by God. I also pray for a parting that has even greater beauty. Christians leave beauty even in their absence.

The reason God shows signs and wonders is not to fulfill our desires. It is to make us believe and follow the Lord. Greeks seek wisdom, and Jews seek signs, but it is all just desire, and we cling to the cross.
The way to do God's work ignorantly well: We asked Him, "What must we do to do the works God requires?" Jesus answered, "The work of God is this: to believe in the one he has sent." John 6:28-29

Even if the sky falls and there are worries that make your head spin, if I believe and trust in God tonight, I have already won. The Bible says that what is believed is already reality. Faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen. -Hebrews 11:1

A rich young man fled from Jesus. The reason was not due to the young man's flaws, but rather his strengths. "When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he had great wealth." -Matthew 19:22
To love a soul unconditionally, to be able to love them as they are, is noble. In a world full of conditions, the Lord is our only condition.
Limits are not overcome by gritting your teeth, but by loving the Lord and being ruled by His grace.
We are having a mini retreat starting tomorrow in Korea. It would be great if those in Korea could join if they are available. Sister Misun Hong and Dookyo Chung are arranging it.

In Korea, we divide into humanities and sciences. If you are good at English, you go to humanities; if you are good at math, you go to sciences. Go to university or out into society. In economics and business, you are not even considered without advanced math. Engineers also have to read all new technologies in English. I hope you are not trapped by the limits people have created. Blessed is the life where age, language, family, studies, and appearance do not limit me.
Please continue to pray for Brother Hanseok. He is currently in the ICU. We cover him with the precious blood and intercede.
I am a person of endless 부족함, a person who needs forgiveness more, a person who desperately needs Jesus.
When I can't see an inch ahead, I just close my eyes and see the One within me. When I see again through His eyes, it becomes a flame that will never be regretted.

The Lord's policy is that if we pray in unity, it will surely be accomplished. That is why oneness is important. It is much better to maintain oneness and gaze at each other with affection and joy than to do a few things and fight.

Activists think that if they don't work, everything will fail. God works even when we are in a slump. It is not you who works, but grace that works. That's why my daughter's name is Eunhye. Soon, 'Kim Eunhye' will be doing a lot of work. ^^

Faith without works is dead faith. The Bible has a monistic view that when we say we have faith, we also have obedience. Along with the confession of believing, please also proclaim the confession of being transformed. This is only possible by the power of the Holy Spirit.
The Lord fills the empty soul. Even if you don't believe in the Lord, you can feel lonely. This is because it is a process of coming out of darkness and moving into light.
If you are accompanied by police stronger than fierce gangsters, you will not be afraid. This is because power has overwhelmed fear. The greatest fear is myself, and the strongest power is Jesus.

To find rest in a harsh world, you need skill. Without skill, you will always be chased. You will weigh gold for vanity and spend silver for emptiness. That skill does not mean influence in the world. Intimacy with the Lord, that eternal power, is true skill.

Fear comes even to those who have faith. It is no exception for pastors. Fear suddenly visits missionaries who seem to have crossed the line of death, risking their lives for missions in remote areas. Fear cannot be overcome by being human. Only the Lord's presence overwhelms fear. Only then can there be peace. Walk with Him. You will be more than peaceful; you will be bold. Jesus immediately said, "Take courage; it is I. Do not be afraid." -Matthew 14:27 "I have told you these things, so that in me you may have peace. In this world you will have trouble. But take heart! I have overcome the world." -John 16:33
It is a blessing to walk on dry land, unable to take a single step forward without the Lord's grace, rather than to live a life of arrogance, walking on luxurious carpets.

In chemistry, there is a chain reaction. The preceding reaction triggers the subsequent reaction. People typically call this an explosion. Iron sharpens iron. When people who have found their answers in faith and Jesus are put together, a chain reaction of revival occurs, and they become even more fervent. What kind of triggers do the people next to me give me now?
Choose the Lord over probability.
Act with confidence. If you have little confidence, you can do little. If there is much to do, you can pray more earnestly to dwell in great confidence.

What a person clings to in a day of trouble reveals their faith. To fear is evidence of having no life. A living person is not afraid and is at peace. Psalm 27:3 Though an army encamp against me, my heart shall not fear; though war arise against me, in this I will be confident.

Repent and believe the gospel. -Mark 1:15 The gospel is a promise and a covenant, but the prerequisite for believing it is repentance. Repentance here is not a detailed list of sins or a lament of one's situation. It is turning around, changing direction, and returning. Above all, it is abandoning "self-reliance." You must abandon the heart that trusts in yourself to believe the gospel. The prerequisite for the merit of Jesus' blood is to nail down my own merits.

A repentant heart is not a shameful heart, but a heart with a good conscience seeking God. Therefore, you call on the name of the Lord and are baptized and receive forgiveness of sins. It is a night where I feel deeply sinful.
Any grace I realize and any love I receive are given only within God's permission. Therefore, I am moved and give thanks every day.
The first step to restoration is 'humility.' This is because the first step to falling is 'pride.' "My people who are called by my name humble themselves..."

The assurance of restoration. Even wrong choices, mistakes, and wounds that are difficult to reveal will surely be restored and healed by God's love. Even if you are walking a dry path now, I am confident that God will work so that the intercessors for you and the cross gospel are not in vain. The proof of that assurance is the cross. For I am sure that neither death nor life, nor angels nor rulers, nor things present nor things to come, nor powers, nor height nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus our Lord. -Romans 8:38, 39

The fatal challenge for a company is not the aggressive advertising of competitors, but the lack of confidence in its employees. The most effective way to deny the Lord is not by an atheist who says the Lord does not exist. It is by a believer who claims to believe in God but is worried.

The best of a humanist is, "I will take responsibility for everything and go." God has not called us to take responsibility. He has given us the position to come to Jesus, who will be responsible for us eternally, and to rely on Him, and to enable others to rely on Him fully. We cannot even take responsibility for ourselves, let alone for others. I pray that you entrust everything to the Lord alone.

Success, as commonly spoken of, is not God's glory; rather, enduring and waiting becomes glory. If my faith does not weaken and I hold onto God's promise, I will give glory to God. Glory is given not by the fulfillment of desires, but by faith. not weakening in faith, but being strengthened by faith and giving glory to God. -Romans 4:20

People often say they give glory to the Lord when they receive praise that others envy. That is why the Lord does not exalt them. He exalts them to give glory. The Lord's humbling means I am not ready. Glory is not achieved by succeeding and being envied or by speaking of God's glory in interviews. Glory is given when, after waiting long without an answer, faith does not weaken and the promise is held without doubt. Glory is not given to just anyone. (Romans 4:19) He did not weaken in faith when he considered his own body to be as good as dead since he was about a hundred years old, or when he considered the barrenness of Sarah's womb. (Romans 4:20) He did not doubt God's promise through unbelief but was strengthened by faith, giving glory to God.

Have hope. No one can take it away. Have leisure and humor. If your faith is a luxury brand, your life will also be a luxury brand. If you put aside what you are chasing for a moment, the great things you are thinking of will not happen. Believe in the Almighty God and entrust everything to Him.

There is a saying, "Do not walk carelessly on a snow-covered mountain path." This is because those who follow may walk incorrectly. To become a leader of an era and a leader who saves a generation, you must have the "heart of a father." You must first have an experience of encountering God, an experience of God's heart being poured out, and the ability to convey that heart.
Faith is to turn away from the greed standing before us and to hold onto the true reality and evidence. That is Jesus.

They say they cannot give thanks because there are too many difficult things. No. It is because they cannot give thanks that there are many difficult things. He wants us to offer thanks for what we ask for and for what we lack. The most powerful prayer is thanksgiving, which turns things into reality.
In difficult and seemingly impossible situations, I hope you will break the rock with an egg of faith. Although the situation we face may seem difficult, it is a situation permitted by the Lord.

Before saying it's too hard and you're going to die, try praying. Don't cry while stumbling on the stairs before even stepping onto the stage; compete with your knees and faith. You will feel that it is truly manageable.

Giving up when things are difficult is the easiest and most convenient choice, but it is also the proven worst choice. The Lord did not give up on the cross and accomplished all things. To accomplish all things means to endure well. When you have faith in difficult times, you will win, and most faith manifests in the form of endurance.

The fact that my faith is constantly being challenged means that I must respond with faith. The reason this moment has become a fierce battlefield where neither side can retreat is because it is a truly critical moment. Please respond with faith.

* Sunday Leader Meeting 1. Life is a matter of fullness. 2. Those who know the future are more than peaceful; they are bold. 3. If you know the conclusion in advance, you can enjoy it with fun. 4. The more desperate the situation, the more fun it becomes. 5. Faith is seeing what has not yet come to pass. 6. If you have faith, you are bold and focused. 7. It is not a matter of having a good personality or willpower, but of having faith. 8. If you see the conclusion with faith and accept it, you cannot live any other way than naturally. 9. A person with reliable and certain information changes their actions after seeing the results. 10. Focus on Jesus. Always think of Him. 11. Are you lonely? Pray like this: Make me even lonelier. 12. This is the appearance of a spiritual strongman and the farewell journey of one who mocks fate and walks the Lord's path.
A challenge is to step out of your comfort zone and face your fears. It is allowing yourself to fail, and you will find that the more you try, the easier it becomes.

I fully understand being called ignorant and a fanatic. It is natural criticism. This is because they do not know the following verses. The resurrection of Christ resets and renews all value. If in Christ we have hope in this life only, we are of all people most to be pitied. -1 Corinthians 15:19
There is no reason to hesitate or waver. If the Lord has established it.

Reject evil and embrace good. Abraham, after winning a war, is greeted by two kings. He accepts the greeting of Melchizedek, who serves the same God he does, but rejects the king and culture of Sodom, which distorts the truth. Discern and accept or reject as appropriate.

A person of faith does not ignore common sense and reality. They simply transcend them through faith. Though I have forsaken worldly pleasures and the world has forsaken me, I do not cease to change the world from within it. I illuminate the world with excellent wisdom and faithfulness.

There is a saying, "a kite with a broken string." This refers to a situation where the person or power that supported you has fallen, and thus can no longer offer you favors. Simply put, you are separated. Such strings should be cut, and their severance is actually a blessing. As long as you are not separated from Jesus, the true vine, it is fine. Become a kite with a broken string, descend, and be a branch attached to the Lord.
Simply cast it. Cast it to the Lord. Then, trust the Lord and just go. The Lord is alive.

I see you. Humans are those who, finding nothing in a dry well, keep lingering, only to return empty-handed, suffering and feeling lonely. Life is unhappy because what should be there is absent, and what should not be there is present. What should be there is Jesus, and what should not be there are the many things that try to fill the emptiness, that is, trash. I am not trash. The Lord never creates trash.

God will never let you down. When you believe in Jesus, you will face tribulations in the world. But there is no need to fear. For Jesus has overcome the world. He has never failed me, and He never will. All His victories were already accomplished on the cross. That is the conclusion. My conclusion. It is the conclusion Jesus foresaw on the cross. This is my destiny.
Wherever you go, whenever you are always with You
Peace comes when I believe in the Lord's omnipotence. Because then miracles happen. Peace is a great grace given to those who believe. The Lord is greater than the problems.
In fear and anxiety, what is needed is not logic and persuasion. Even if understood, the anxiety does not disappear. What is truly essential is intimacy. Ah, the Father is with me! That is enough.

The words, "The Lord is my strength, and I love You," are not learned words. They are confessions that burst forth from a soul that has experienced God in the direst moments of life, unable to suppress them. The Lord is my strength, and I love You - Psalm 18:1.
It is the burning love of Jesus in my heart that gives me an unbearable sense of pity when I consider living a life of comfort and convenience.
Faith is giving your heart to the Lord.
Do not let your faith weaken while praying; instead, give glory.

Those who find faith too difficult have too many things they cannot let go of. Consider this: how difficult would it be to run the narrow path while carrying heavy burdens? Only after shedding easy sins and heavy loads can one run the race of faith.
Many people fall too easily even after receiving grace because they stop praying. Pray. Kneel down right now and ask for the Lord's new grace.
Faith is not clinging to what has not been given to you, but moving forward with gratitude and praise for what has already been given and what will be given. The Lord makes no mistakes.
What do you want me to do for you? Show me your desires. That is how faith can be known. What you truly desire is yourself.
Faith is the priority in life.
It is by the Lord's grace that I have had good things. It is also by the Lord's grace that I have been allowed to experience unexpected pain. What I am today is solely by the Lord's grace.

The source of faith is Jesus. As we know more deeply what Jesus has done and who Jesus is, we will have better faith. The strength to fight and overcome difficult hardships and unwanted tribulations comes from the fact that we have greater joy. We can know this when we look at Jesus. Let us fix our eyes on Jesus, the pioneer and perfecter of faith. For the joy set before him he endured the cross, scorning its shame, and sat down at the right hand of the throne of God. Hebrews 12:2.
I am always so insufficient. Therefore, I rely more on the Lord. Whether I live or die...
My value is solely the value of Jesus Christ.

You can trust even if you don't agree with everything. You can love even if you don't reach complete agreement. Even without rules like how to hang a bathroom towel or how to squeeze toothpaste, there is no major problem for a couple to love and live together. Love comes from the center and moves towards the center.

You cannot imagine how hopeful the saying "faith overcomes" is. It means that even though the problems are very difficult and I have no strength, faith overcomes, it overcomes for me, and it leads the weak me to victory. When I truly believe and rely on Him and go forward fearlessly, I overcome the whole world with the strength of believing in Jesus. Faith overcomes, faith overcomes. Believing in Jesus overcomes the whole world. - Hymn 357.
When my heart aches deeply, I rely on the precious blood. When I rely on the precious blood, pain transforms into praise.

Rather than relying on other things, I hope you will rely only on the Lord who enables you to bear it. Even if the situation looks difficult and those who trusted you turn their backs, you must look to and believe only in the Lord who allows only the trials you can bear and who, when you cannot bear them, makes a way of escape so that you can bear them.

The reason faith does not work is because it is not continuously believed. There is too much of a gap between moments of faith, creating a void of disbelief. That is why the Bible says to always look to Jesus. Sustainable faith is achieved when we fix our gaze on Jesus and contemplate deeply.

When you are afraid, rely on the Lord. God, who is your refuge, will hide and protect me. The Lord's peace is boundless, so those who trust in the Lord sing praises. When I am afraid, I rely on the Lord.

Those who like me do so as long as our conditions match. Liking means that the perishable conditions are satisfactory for the time being. However, love is unchanging and rather tolerates and covers up many faults and shortcomings. Loving is a gift poured out because we have decided to sacrifice for the soul given by the Lord, not for changing and perishable conditions. I love you.

The only reason a person of faith, who cannot muster courage, does so - David was in great distress, but he found strength in God his Lord. - 1 Samuel 30:6. He tells us to find strength in God. And then, to muster courage and turn things around.
In the end, it is about living eternally with my Jesus. That is what makes me smile and stand up again.
Faith allows us to trust in the Lord's sovereignty and rule beyond our sorrow, and it even gives us amazing peace that He answers. The Lord is still working. Not by your sight but by your faith.
I am not afraid because I love God. O Jehovah, my strength, I love you.

Sometimes in life, there are no answers. You could say it's a heart-breaking situation. What is needed then is faith. It's about ignoring everything else and relying on the Lord, and continuing on the path you were going. Since there is nothing that can be resolved without the Lord's help anyway, all I can do is rely on Him.

Rudeness is a weak person pretending to be strong and imitating the strong. A truly strong person finds themselves in the Lord, and even if they humble themselves, their pride is not hurt, and they acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty who governs the situation.
The problems I face are things that God must work out anyway, and God works miracles for His people.
I trust the God who hears my prayers. I believe that the One who enabled me to pray will also work. I intercede and bless.

If the purpose is clear, you become focused. You gain the insight to see core values, and you simply cut out the unnecessary to achieve your purpose. Therefore, the person of faith can do all things through Christ who strengthens them.

1. During the Imjin War, the Japanese forces captured Hanyang in two weeks, captured the crown prince, and advanced to Hamgyong Province. 2. What was even more astonishing than the Western firearms, the arquebus, which could pierce armor from 50 meters away, was the samurai sword, which became a source of terror for the Joseon army after experiencing many civil wars. Their ships were agile and fast, and they were a group of warriors with 200 years of training, capable of defeating ten men in close combat. (In reality, 1,600 Japanese soldiers annihilated 50,000 Joseon soldiers in Yongin.) 3. In that precarious situation, one general built a formation with Panokseon ships, which were slow but had excellent maneuverability, and used cannons as their main weapon for long-range attacks, fully utilizing the terrain. He fought 24 times and won 24 times, and in the final battle, he annihilated 333 ships with 13 ships. 4. Out of 160,000 Japanese soldiers, 110,000 were wounded or killed by him, and out of about 1,800 ships, 1,500 were sunk or wrecked, but friendly casualties were only about 200. 5. He was a leader who had prepared for a long time, was strong in details, and had a strategy to fight at a distance differently from his opponents who were strong in close combat. Above all, Admiral Yi Sun-sin had a strong affection for defeating the Japanese and protecting the land. 6. Wisdom and ability come from affection. If you truly love, you will save souls, and if you truly long for, you will see glory.

The handwriting I wrote can be recognized as mine even after a long time. When I hear testimonies, I recognize the message of truth embedded within them. It is clear that the Word is working in their lives as truth... that is transformation.
David prepared five smooth stones. Because if one missed, he was ready to throw the other four in succession. You just have to keep trying until it works. Just ignore everything else and go for it.
Faith is expecting and approaching the Lord. However, the most excellent stage is to lay down our will to God's will.
What is needed in a hardened heart and unchangeable despair is the wind of the Holy Spirit. It's not our quality that matters, but whether the wind of the Holy Spirit blows.

Faith looks forward to a better reward. When we pursue better values, choices are made, and destinies change within those decisions. Faith-based decisions fulfill the intentions of the sender and lead to the most meaningful life.

What I feel during prayer meetings is that as soon as I open my mouth to pray, it's not me praying, but God drawing the prayer in. By the grace of God I am what I am. May the Lord design the life He desires for me.

We are told to be bold. But when we are told to be bold when we cannot be, it's maddening. A student who hasn't studied is fundamentally anxious about doing well on an exam. A student who hasn't done their homework cannot be bold. If the Lord is not within me, and He is not my master (if the Lord's influence is not in my lifestyle), I cannot be bold. If you are bold on a path the Lord does not desire, that is not boldness, but ignorance. A life that has strayed from the Lord's path should be feared and dreaded. Only then will there be even a sliver of repentance, won't there?

The opposite of faith is not doubt. Doubt does not cause that much harm. What is truly dangerous is testing God. If you have no faith, you simply don't cross the Red Sea. But trying to cross the Red Sea without faith is testing God. It's not because you lack faith, but because you pretend to have faith that you fail greatly. By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if on dry land, but the Egyptians, who tried to do so, were drowned. - Hebrews 11:29
Faith is not seen with the eyes. However, proclamation and confession are revealed undeniably. Above all, obedience is like that.

I hope you do not struggle with emotional problems. When you lift your eyes of faith, it is just one piece in the midst of all the processes. Emotions are not reality; faith is reality. Circumstances are not evidence; faith is evidence. Be bold.

Faith, according to Hebrews 11, is seeking a better sacrifice, a better homeland, preferring suffering, a better resurrection, greater riches, and a better provision. Faith is having the discernment to compare and risking your life for what is 'more' important. If you cannot see 'better' or recognize its value, you cannot seek what is better and will only pursue what is perishable and visible. What a foolish thing. - Hebrews 11
Doing what I can and doing what I cannot is not faith. It is doing what the Lord has permitted and abandoning what He has forbidden. True power is obeying at the decisive moment.
The person of faith confronts. Not because they have no fear, but because they clearly know who they should fear the most.

The person of faith is not pessimistic about this sinful world. Criticism alone without solutions is meaningless. A thermometer only indicates the temperature, but a thermostat actually exerts influence and changes the temperature. Faith is not a thermometer; it is a thermostat.
Faith is about insight. It is about stopping time-wasting activities and pouring time and passion into valuable endeavors.
Faith is not an obligation or a burden to do something, but a joy that makes you unable to do otherwise.

A quack doctor explains things complicatedly, treats them complexly, costs a lot of money, and the illness rarely heals. An excellent doctor diagnoses accurately, explains simply at the patient's level, and treats boldly. I will tell you simply. The root of all my problems is my inability to love the Lord.
Grace is when I have to cry out to go a path that others easily walk. It is because I will see the Lord there.

* What is needed after immense grace: Mary received immense grace that an angel could confirm. Nevertheless, that grace turned into anxiety due to human circumstances and customs. When she met Elizabeth after a long and arduous journey, she received confirmation again along with unexpected hospitality. "The mother of my Lord...". What is needed after receiving grace is continuous confirmation through the Word by God's people.
I urge you to hold fast to your faith. The Lord desires it, and in the end, trials have an end, and at the end of that path is the glorious Lord.

Because the purpose is clear, life becomes simpler and more focused. When challenges and tribulations come, the focus becomes even clearer. It is to hold onto the cross, be filled with the Holy Spirit, and preach the gospel. Nothing in this world can block my path.

God, who spoke in many ways in the past through the prophets, is now speaking through His Son in these last days. Jesus is God's love and His Word. God speaks to those who look to Jesus through His Son. In these last days, God has spoken to us through His Son, whom He appointed heir of all things, and through whom He made the universe. - Hebrews 1:1-2
Many people are satisfied with claiming and agreeing with the truth. Not praying without paying the price is denying the truth, because life will eventually deny it. A tree is known by its fruit.

The most powerful manual of faith. Simple power messages for each topic. Tens of thousands of insights that penetrate the worries and dilemmas of the world. Indexing that powerfully equips you. - Faithbook by LFGC Youth
Give me a young man who loves the Lord, and I will give you the world.

Living by faith. Do not cling to what is beneficial to yourself; instead, quietly do what is right. Instead of seeking a position that matches your ability, seek the ability that matches your calling. worldly affairs are about investing little and gaining much, but heavenly affairs are about pouring everything out and going forward, looking only to the Lord. (John 1:48) Nathaniel asked, "How do you know me?" Jesus replied, "Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you."

What is precious is not protected because we have the ability and strength to protect it. It is protected because it is precious. It is loved, so it lasts until the end. Protect those you cherish with love.

The most pitiable people in the world. If our lives are only about seeking worldly things, we are truly pitiable. Because of the resurrection, we have the most glorious lives. However, if life is not that pitiable even without the hope of resurrection, there is a problem. It means we are Christians in name only. Without resurrection, our lives should be ruined. If our hope in Christ is only for this life, we are of all people most to be pitied. But Christ has indeed been raised from the dead, the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep. (1 Corinthians 15:19-20)

The basis of inner healing. Projection, repression, rationalization, denial, reaction – these are defense mechanisms heard in early morning prayer. They are representative of narcissism. Knowing these alone is helpful, but the Bible speaks more fundamentally. Please pay attention to the following words. This is the basis of our healing. For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death. - Romans 8:2 (In = indwelling, Law = power, Freedom = liberty) Trust in him at all times, O people; pour out your hearts to him, for God is our refuge. - Psalm 62:8 (Pour out = confront and confess, Refuge = healing and restoration)
Faith is like dominoes. If one falls, others fall in succession. Conversely, if one succeeds, others experience revival. Please add more whips to the running horse. Keep going~~~

There is no need to force commitment on someone whose heart is down. Instead, they should experience the cross. To love is to become indebted, and from that, commitment flows, and intensity arises from that affection.

The power to rise again. The power to rise again from brokenness and weakness comes only from seeking Jesus. It is by relying on the power of His precious blood and receiving the fullness of the Holy Spirit. Seeking this is what those who will rise again must continuously pursue. It is because we have turned our gaze away from it that we have fallen.
When facing difficulties, pray. God, humble me further. Let me live by Your Word, not by my own strength and ability.

Prayers that are answered, problems that are solved. Despite much spiritual life and prayer, His work may not be evident in our lives. Miracles begin to happen when we make Jesus our Lord. When we acknowledge, believe, and confess Jesus as Lord, all disciples invariably experienced miracles and power. A powerless Christianity is when we dilute the Lord's lordship. Amazingly, all of Jesus' questions in the four Gospels can be summarized as, "Who is your Lord?" The fallen wine, the feeding of the five thousand, the boat in the storm, the sick daughter, the demon-possessed child, the thirsty life – who is truly Lord? Therefore, do not cling to the problem, but check who is the Lord of the problem. That is how you can have peace. His mother said to the servants, "Do whatever he tells you." - John 2:5
When I am utterly unable to live and surrender everything to the Lord, the kingdom of God begins to revive. My problems are no longer mine; this war belongs to God.

Faith is about your gaze. Due to the grandeur of the sunrise at the beach, other surrounding things do not catch your eye. Having strong faith means looking at the Lord with exceptional frequency, absolute duration, and depth. It was said that looking at the bronze serpent (not touching it) would save you. If you only look at the Lord, other things will not catch your eye. That is why you win. All human problems arise from where you look. If you turn away from the cross, you have already sinned. Let us fix our eyes on Jesus, the pioneer and perfecter of faith. - Hebrews 12:2

How to overcome fear. The opposite of fear is not courage. It is companionship. If you walk with the Lord, fear will turn into song. To the timid and uncharitable Joshua, the Lord said, "Meditate on the Word (faith)" and "Observe and do it (holiness)." Pursue peace with all people, and holiness, without which no one will see the Lord. - Hebrews 12:14 By faith Enoch was taken up so that he did not see death: "He could not be found, because God had taken him up." Now before he was taken up, he was commended for having pleased God. - Hebrews 11:5

When people come to church and diligently serve and sing, it may seem like they are living well without any issues, but when they return to their own places, they face various problems and live in anxiety and restlessness. This is because they lack assurance of God's love. For those God foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son, that he might be the firstborn among many brothers. Romans 8:29 God is a God of impartiality. That means God loves us, His younger brothers, just as He loves His Son Jesus Christ. God cannot be unfair.

Sometimes friends ask if they should use Facebook or not. Pray before you use it. Everything done without faith is sin anyway (Romans 14:23). Therefore, the right way to live is to pray and conquer, so that God's good influence is realized through Facebook. If it is not done with faith, it can become a sin even in a righteous place. Canaan was not a sinful place; rather, the people who lived there were not righteous. But the one who doubts is condemned if he eats, because his eating is not from faith; for whatever does not proceed from faith is sin. - Romans 14:23

Paul called legalists and those who possessed self-righteousness "dogs." (Philippians 3:2) In simpler terms, this is like saying "if you try hard enough, heaven will be moved." No matter how sincere you are, you cannot move God. And without faith it is impossible to please God, because anyone who comes to him must believe that he exists and that he rewards those who earnestly seek him. Hebrews 11:6

The reason people who have faith stay away from sin is not because they are ethical or strong-willed, but because the glory and love of God they have experienced are so immense. Under the blazing sun, a candle must be extinguished. The more you love the Lord, the more you stay away from sin. This is because it becomes too difficult to sin. ㅠㅠ
Exalting myself does not bring recognition, nor does lowering myself make me humble. What truly matters is not how I see myself, but how God sees me and what plans He has for me.

The 600,000 Israelites who witnessed the ten plagues and great miracles died in the wilderness due to their grumbling. However, their descendants, who heard and believed the Word at Mount Gerizim and Mount Ebal, entered Canaan.

The dark clouds before me are frustrating until you enter them, but after passing through, they become testimonies. A dangerous challenge with the Lord is far more grateful than a safe surrender without the Lord. At the end of life, we always regret the things we "haven't done" more than the things we "have done."
I am infinitely lacking. I am lacking to the point of having no excuse. That is precisely why I need Jesus even more. Without Him, I cannot take a single step. And that is also the greatest blessing.

Living by faith means roaring against the reality that is contrary to and distant from my vision, and continuing to move forward. The reality is their own circumstances, but I am moved by the heart poured out by the Lord and persevere.
If you fall into sin despite your resolve, you do not yet know the truth. If you know the truth, you will be freed from the bondage of sin.
I am not a great person. However, I walk this path with people I respect and who are beautiful.

Do not try to provide solutions for everyone else's problems. There is a high possibility that the pain will deepen rather than be resolved. Try entrusting them to the Lord and sharing the same heart. Then, the precious things you ignored when you couldn't stop will begin to appear.
Faith is not about pursuing one's own plans, but about responding to the Lord's calling and following it.
The secret to being in God and God being in me is because of the precious blood of the cross. That impossible thing became possible in that noble blood. Hallelujah.
A long time with Sharon, just the two of us for dinner.

In the face of problems, I am powerless. What I need most is not a capable person to back me up or good luck. It is His voice that turns my fear into peace and makes me know that the Lord is God. He says to me, "My grace is sufficient for you" - 2 Corinthians 12:9. If the Lord is sufficient, then it is sufficient.
Why worry when you can pray?
Do not fear, only believe. This means to entrust everything. Cast all your anxieties on him, because he cares for you. 1 Peter 5:7

If you only look at the results and the parts, you will despair. But the Lord makes you see the whole. I hope you will have the perspective of faith to see glory in suffering, in the face of the world's request to settle and maintain the status quo. He is moving us to where He is building, in ways I do not know. Joseph -> Egypt <Low cost, high efficiency, shortest distance travel>
Once again, I pray to be consumed by offering the life of the least of these.

When Abraham was asked to offer his only son Isaac, he fell into a dilemma like a contradiction. The actions he took were two... 1. To obey, and 2. To believe that He would bring him back to life even if he died. That reckless obedience overthrew idols and brought him immense, uncalculated blessings.

After praying, there should be no worry on your face. If you are still afraid after praying, you must implore more to gain faith and assurance. Trust in the Lord who governs all entangled situations. Use your faith. And declare that it will be as you believe, and be at peace.

Most people go towards higher probabilities. Chasing probabilities means delaying the time of ruin. Rather, it is a blessing to be broken early. Because that person realizes that they cannot live by bread alone, but must live by the Word.
Do not be afraid, only believe. Do not be crushed by problems, break through with prayer. Jesus heard him and said, "Do not be afraid, only believe." Luke 8:50
Through you, all nations...

What holds me is not my plausible eloquence or good reputation. Nor is it because I have managed my image well. It is solely the precious blood of the Lord. That is why I am loved despite my weakness and forgiven despite my shortcomings.
It is the Lord's grace that I keenly feel my weakness. From that weakness, the Lord begins to work. The evidence of the Lord's work is when my weakness becomes my boast.

In the wilderness, there are no extras. One set of clothes, one pair of shoes, even food requires faith to look to the daily manna. It is grace that basic survival for a day is not permitted without God's help. Without the Lord, you only do things that will lead to ruin.

Therefore, the Lord says not to remember the past and not to think about what was done before. For what the Lord does is to make rivers in the desert of life and water in the wilderness of the heart. He alone within me can do a new thing. Therefore, you must look only to Him. "Forget the former things; do not dwell on the past. See, I am doing a new thing! Now it springs up; do you not perceive it? I am making a way in the wilderness and rivers in the desert. The wild animals honor me, the jackals and the owls, because I provide water in the wilderness and rivers in the desert, to give drink to my people, my chosen, whom I formed for myself that they may declare my praise." - Isaiah 43:18-21
Those who trust in His guidance are not flustered even when storms rage and there are rumors of war. To be bold is not a matter of personality, but of 'what' you believe.

Pharaoh oppressed his people by increasing their labor. King Nebuchadnezzar, with his absolute power, forced an impossible standard – idol worship. The world tries to bind and drag people with worry and fear. Thus, many people become slaves to injustice and death. However, this does not work on those who have 'faith that looks to the Lord for help' and 'even if He does not,' relinquishing their rights and clinging to the cross. They have mistaken the people. Those who stand before the gospel, though they appear weakest, are stronger than death. (Daniel 3:17) "If that is the case, our God whom we serve is able to save us, even from the fiery furnace, and he will save us from your hand, O king. But even if he does not, we will not serve your gods or worship the image of gold that you have set up."
Someone asks, "How do you live like that?" It is love, the love the Lord gives. Will you follow? As it has been, I will not stop for a moment, treating each moment as the last.
The reason I believe and rely on Him is that there is a Lord who knows all my circumstances. Therefore, what precedes faith is knowing the Lord. Then, naturally, you will trust.

It is true that Christ was crucified for me. But if you only stay there, you will be in a cycle of repeated sin and repentance. You must accept and declare by faith that you too were crucified with Him.

Wherever I am, my heart is always at peace. The peace the Lord Jesus gives is always abundant. My heart is always at peace, my heart is always comfortable. Though there are many waves of evil sin, my heart is always at peace. - Hymn 408 Do not let circumstances and people rule me. All those things will wear out like clothes, and they will not be my true joy, nor will they be a threat.
People of faith do not meditate on circumstances or people. People of faith meditate only on God.

When you are at a loss about what to do, you must hold onto only what is important and essential. Even if you have no decision-making power, you must humble yourself and look only to the Lord. That is faith, and it is acknowledging the Lord's sovereignty, and it is doing very well in this situation. Opportunity will surely come. Like Jesus, like Joseph, prepare your whole life for a moment.

The greatest danger is not being outnumbered. It is boasting that you can handle it yourself. Only when the 35,000 who think they can handle it disappear will the salvation of the 300 men of faith bloom. 35,000 is a much more dangerous situation than 300. "The Lord said to Gideon, 'You have too many men for me to deliver Midian into their hands, or Israel would boast, 'My own strength has saved me.'' " - Judges 7:2

If you try to get rid of fear and worry, you will focus more on the problem and face greater difficulties. Instead of trying to get rid of them, just hold onto Jesus. If you hold onto a greater value, other values become refuse. It is not training to discard refuse without regret; it happens naturally. If I die to myself and live by Jesus, it is a victorious life.

1. In sports, the player who controls the flow and situation is the ace. 2. They turn a defensive situation into an offensive one and change defeat into victory. 3. The Lord wants us to be aces who control the situation where He has called us. 4. This is not by brute force, but by the 'authority of gratitude.' To raise your hands in gratitude in a broken place requires faith, and such a person controls the situation and wins. 5. The expression of a grateful person carries an air of victorious ease, regardless of their circumstances.

What I want to give to the youth is not the convenient achievements of life, but the immeasurable love of Jesus' cross. Before that cross, all wrong configurations are reset. Anything becomes possible and obedience follows. "As God has promised, this is the word of truth that we have spoken to you. For all the promises of God find their Yes in him. That is why it is through him that we utter our Amen to God for his glory. And it is God who establishes us with you in Christ and has anointed us, by putting his seal on us and giving us his Spirit in our hearts as a guarantee." - 2 Corinthians 1:18-22
To be bold is to be someone who tries to eliminate the object of fear. Such things rarely happen in life. When you discover a greater object of reverence, small fears disappear. Fear the Lord.

All water flows to the sea. It is not the sea because it is the sea, but because the sea was the lowest land. Though He was the Most High, the Lord, who became the lowest, loves with a sea-like love. You too, love like the sea. If you become extremely low, you will flow.
Boldness is not a state of no fear, but a choice to hold onto something more precious. "Jesus is my strength and my hope." - Hymn 93
Many Happy Returns of the day, Hope you have a lot of fun.

A person of faith has余裕 (yoyu). Yoyu is not idleness, but gratitude in times of crisis and mental breakdown. Then the Lord's intervention, which the world cannot take away, will come as peace. The Lord reigns.

There are times when things don't work out despite effort, and when the results are not good despite working hard. What is needed then is to hold onto one thing: the fact that God loves me. While heaven and earth may change, this truth will not.
I have trusted in your love; my heart will rejoice in your salvation. - Psalm 13:5

Therefore, the small things I have are truly precious. Chasing only flashy and big things happens because we don't know who we are. If you don't know Him, you don't know yourself, and a person who doesn't know themselves can never be happy.

Even when I say I can't do it, that I'm not good enough, and turn away, if it is the Lord's will, I will readily say, "I will receive and serve," and set out on a long journey. Faith is not of me, but of the Lord.

If I don't let go of the swing I'm holding, I can't grab the next one. If I let go with both hands, for a very brief moment, I might be in a state where I can't rely on any swing. But soon my partner will catch me. It's a circus with the Lord.
Faith is giving up something less good to obtain something better.

I have a dream, but I have fear. The fear is, what if God doesn't want it? This worry is real, but it arises because I haven't truly met the Lord. It's not because it's truly hard to let go of the world and too difficult to hold onto the mission, but because I haven't discovered the real thing. Those who have seen glory do not struggle or fear anything.

Sometimes there are children in orphanages who are not discouraged. Children who play joyfully and energetically, unbothered by bullying or ostracism, are all children who have received a promise. They are children whose parents have promised to come pick them up. A person who looks at God's promise with good faith is like occupying a high ground in spiritual warfare. They never lose. Hold onto the promise. (Hebrews 11:33) By faith they conquered kingdoms, administered justice, and received what was promised; they shut the mouths of lions;
Moses' request was to show him God's glory. The reason we easily fall and become slaves to sin is because we don't know glory. A person who has seen God's glory can never live that way.

Let's make a bet. I'll turn over a cup, and a marble is inside one of them. I found it's not on the right. Where is it? Of course, it's on the left. Since it's not on the right... If you go to church but are not happy, it means Christ, the treasure, is not filling your vessel. Are you happy even amidst tribulation? Of course, I am happy even then, even happier as it gets worse. Because of Jesus... Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. - Matthew 5:3

Some want the fastest way to their destination. Others want the most beautiful way to their destination. But the Lord says He is 'the way.' He also says that without that way, you can never reach your destination. If Jesus is the way, then it is the most beautiful and righteous way. Jesus said, "I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me." - John 14:6
I love you too. Jesus...

You must fight the good fight of faith. Believers cannot avoid this fight; they must fight it until they enter heaven. This fight can only be won by faith, and it is not a wicked struggle to pursue the perishable self, hence it is a 'good fight.' The 'good fight' is dying to self by faith and letting the Lord live instead. Fight the good fight of faith; take hold of the eternal life to which you were called and about which you made a good confession in the presence of many witnesses. - 1 Timothy 6:12

The Pharisees hated Jesus and wanted to kill Him. This is because they loved themselves more than God. Their failure to recognize Jesus was not an intellectual problem, but a heart problem. "How can you believe, when you accept praise from one another, but do not seek the praise that comes from the only God?" John 5:44 A self-centered person can never reach the truth.
Come to me, all you who are thirsty.
If the final test comes out well, St. George will also say goodbye.
The most foolish person is one who opposes God because of their own strengths. A humble weakness is far better than arrogant strength.

It is not because I have something left that I can help others. That is absolutely not the case. Those who have more tend to give more. It is because we decide to share that we are given the blessing to share.

The Holy Spirit whom the Father will send in my name... When the Holy Spirit comes, He will cause you to call on the name of Jesus, and through the power of that name, works will be accomplished. Whoever calls on that name will be saved. If you have problems, I hope you will call on the name of Jesus. John 14:26 But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you all things and will remind you of everything I have said to you.
Do not wander searching for a way; seek Jesus. Jesus is the way. He is our purpose, our all, and our destination.
When faith is inherent and overflows, it is expressed as joy. Romans 5:2 Therefore, there is heavenly joy in worship.

When the Word is proclaimed, believe and obey immediately. Simple obedience leads to progress in life. If you advance a little, the next path will also become a little clearer. Even a journey of a thousand miles begins with one step at a time.
Faith is not insurance that enhances my value and expands my happiness. When I face rejection and failure, my reaction reveals what I have truly pursued my mission for.
The turtle may be eagerly awaiting the hare's sleep, but a hare that is focused and faithful to absolute time is an invincible and powerful hare. Infinite loyalty, absolute faith.

If I entrust my path to the Lord with my whole being, I receive it back as faith refined like gold. (Job 23:10) Then, I, who only knew others' testimonies and experiences theoretically, will directly encounter the living God and become a glorious person. Stepping away from the values and paths the world speaks of is itself suffering. However, suffering is beneficial to me. (Psalm 119:67) "Before I was afflicted I went astray, but now I obey your word." - Psalm 119:67 Because suffering is another revelation of God.

(Mark 16:17-18) And these signs will accompany those who believe: In my name they will drive out demons; they will speak in new tongues; they will pick up snakes with their hands; and when they drink anything deadly, it will not hurt them; they will lay their hands on sick people, and they will get well. No matter how powerful the challenge, if you respond powerfully with faith, you will always win.
Pride must die for the vessel of humility to be filled with God's grace. I hope we become servants who know how to bow in humility, no matter how much we receive.
If I am shipwrecked in my faith, all other successes are merely splendid failures. I trust in the faithful and almighty God. He will sustain me.

In the last days, Satan will roar like a lion seeking whom he may devour. He only imitates a roaring lion; he is not a lion. The true Lion is Jesus, the Lion of the tribe of Judah (Revelation 5:5). Trust in the Lord's name and be courageous.
Can you praise the Lord in all circumstances? That is faith.
It's time to start our Saturday gathering, which is open to everyone. I long for the Lord's anointing. There is no one but the Lord.

The Parable of the Wicked Tenants (Mark 12:1-9) Jesus revealed the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, hidden from the foundation of the world, through parables. Most of Jesus' parables about the kingdom of heaven invariably feature Jesus as the Messiah. Ultimately, the secret cannot be unlocked without believing Jesus is the Messiah. The truth that Jesus is Christ is the key to unlocking all these parables. Words requiring explanation Vineyard: The representative fruit of Israel, essential for preserving water in Israel's dry climate, was the grape. Unlike other plants, grapes were a crop that absolutely required the farmer's help. Hedge: A fence to protect crops. Renting out: It was common practice for someone to lease out farmland and receive a certain rent. From this, we can see that the farmer is not the owner. Text Analysis 1. What did Jesus say was built when the vineyard was made in His parables? 2. In what business model did a man entrust his vineyard to the tenants? ① Hostile Takeover (M&A) ② Franchise (Deposit and 5:5 split) ③ Subcontracting ④ Partnership ⑤ Renting out 3. What did the owner demand from the tenants? ① Company ownership ② Stock options ③ Exclusive contract ④ A portion of the produce Entrusting the vineyard after building a hedge around it signifies it was set apart in a very special way. God set Israel apart from all nations and peoples, desiring to give them a mission, His word, salvation, and even His own Son, Jesus Christ. He gave them the temple and Jerusalem. Entrusting it to the tenants when He went on a long journey shows how much faith and expectation He had. What God wanted from them was a portion of the produce. However, the wicked tenants found it difficult to give even that. 4. What did the tenants do to the servants the owner sent? (e.g., business trip care, karaoke, etc.) 5. Who was the last person the owner sent? ① Debt collector ② Police Man ③ Thugs with buzz cuts ④ New Malden gangsters ( ) 6. What was the owner's expectation when he sent his son? (Verse 6) 7. Why did the tenants kill the son? (Verse 7) Surprisingly, the tenants killed the son because he was the heir. They mistakenly thought that if they killed the heir, the inheritance of the vineyard would become theirs. They were complete thieves. They should have been grateful just to have rented the vineyard, but they didn't even pay the rent and killed the son. We can see that the owner's response was lukewarm even to the point of the son's death. This shows how much God did not want to destroy Israel through judgment. For the tenants, the greatest wickedness lay in mistaking what was not theirs for their own. Sin entered humanity when Adam and Eve sought independence, and the Pharisees, seeking glory for themselves, crucified God, whom they claimed to love so much. 8. Where was the son thrown after being killed? (Verse 8) 9. Who do you think the owner gave the vineyard to after destroying the tenants in his anger? (Verse 9) Primary Meaning of Parable | Secondary Meaning of Parable | Tertiary Meaning of Parable Subject | Jews | Gentiles | Me Vineyard | Israel | Church | My Life Winepress | Jerusalem Temple | | | Watchtower | Temple | Worship | Spiritual ( ) Renting out | Mission of Jews, Stewards | | | Journey | Jesus' First Coming | Second Coming | Death Further Considerations 1. What aspects of self or self-love cause you to reject Jesus? (e.g., religious zeal, sense of entitlement, ambition) 2. What do you think God desires from us when He gives us the vineyard (our lives)? 3. God is the owner of the vineyard (our lives). Do I let God be the owner and the vineyard be a vineyard?
To take another step, I must risk my life. But I have no regrets. My dream is love.
If pain and conflict linger in my heart, it means I must look to the Lord alone in this moment.
I must know the truth. I must surely know and experience the cross. This is because the power of transformation does not lie within me but depends on what I am captivated by.

In the 1960s, the national income was $80. Now it's over $20,000. In just a few decades, it has jumped 250 times. Back then, people starved to death from absolute poverty, but now they don't starve from relative poverty. If income increases 250 times, we should be ecstatically happy, but it doesn't seem so. Does possession truly bring such happiness? Not at all. It's because we understand people as mere lumps of flesh. Humans have spirits and seek meaning. If we cannot be happy by existing, we cannot be happy by possessing either.

I have called you friends (John 15:15). In the Gospel of John, Jesus said He would be our friend. The premise for becoming our friend is explained in the preceding verse: love. This is my commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you (John 15:12).
Tell me what you want. I will tell you whom you serve.
Do you like Free coffee for everlasting life? Yes, membership has its privileges. -LFGC Youth Group
If you don't like the way you were born..., try being born again.

'A sense of entitlement' leads to excessive pride, a monopoly on righteousness, and an attitude of "only we are right, only I am right." We can fall if we cling to and boast about what we do well. However, if we have a 'sense of grace,' the 'grace that saved a sinner like me' becomes astonishing.

Refusal is faith. Not everyone can refuse, but when you hold onto Jesus, everything else is reduced to the level of refuse. It's strange not to discard it. "By faith Moses, when he was grown up, refused to be called a son of Pharaoh's daughter, choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God than to enjoy the fleeting pleasure of sin, esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches than all the treasures of Egypt, for he was looking to the reward." (Hebrews 11:24-26)

The devil's message about money is "I earned the money" and "I can buy anything with money." If God does not give it, you receive nothing, and the truly important things in life cannot be bought with money. Eternal values are unseen, and unseen values are lived by unseen faith, hope, and love.

Everyone focuses on Moses being the leader who led 2 million people. However, they overlook that he was a leader who prostrated himself before God for 40 days and nights. Prayer is power, and prayer is true leadership.
You love God because He first loved you.
If you have nothing and receive nothing but criticism, can you not at least give love? A true love that cannot be embellished because there is nothing.
The person who sees the holiday as holy is truly a person of faith.

"Buy the truth, and do not sell it; buy wisdom, instruction, and understanding." (Proverbs 23:23) Buying truth is a blessing. It is achieved by meeting good leaders, belonging to a good community, and faithfully serving that community. However, more important than buying the truth is not selling it. Disobeying the truth for worldly gain, or deceiving one's conscience and following desires, is selling the truth. Distorting the Word for the purpose of self-defense and rationalization is also selling the truth. God's truth includes practical wisdom, instruction, and understanding for living a successful and meaningful life. We must prioritize meditating on and studying the truth above all else to live life according to the Creator's intent. A new day promises success only for those armed with the truth to live out the specific times of a new life.

"Everyone on the side of truth listens to me." (John 18:37) This is what we have continuously taught through meetings and sharing. Truth does not bind or threaten. Rather, it sets free. If you love the Father and the Son, you will respond to the truth and oppose coercion and falsehood. If you seek the glory of man, a person belonging to the truth will fundamentally feel uncomfortable. We pray for the anointing of the 'ability to interpret' and the 'wisdom of revelation' to know and discern whether this is the Lord's grace or not.

The Bible says not to boast about bitter envy in your heart (James 3:14). If there is hatred, envy, jealousy, and unforgivingness in your heart, your prayers will not be heard. Even if great history unfolds, it is meaningless. James sounds a warning to the so-called elite of faith, who have great faith, to wake up. Faith achievements cannot prevent such envy and jealousy, nor can they be recognized by God. Therefore, if such bitter envy is stirring within you, it is not a cause for boasting but a reason to fall on your face on the ground. The primary enemy of self-satisfaction within me is envy and jealousy. Only through the one who loves can restoration and miracles occur.
I believe in the power of prayer. I believe in the faithful God. I believe in the almighty God. I believe He is the One who changes hearts. I believe He is the One who performs miracles.

Many people confess with their lips that they fear the Lord, but sin with their bodies. Even if the sin is due to the weakness of the flesh, repentance is necessary, but it is more serious if the conscience is seared and one does not know it is a sin and does not repent. I hope you do not despise the Lord's patience and forbearance until you repent. God will surely judge.

How can they believe in one of whom they have not heard? And how can they hear without someone preaching? (Romans 10:14) You cannot believe in what you do not know. If there is no channel (understanding) to enter the grace God gives, His promised word will be of no benefit to me.

Will my God supply all your needs according to Wall Street, the banking system, the economic situation, and the unemployment rate? No. My God will supply all your needs according to the riches in glory in Christ Jesus (Philippians 4:19). Any faith or thought that prevents God from being God is sin. God alone is God.

To believe is to change masters. When the master changes, values, worldview, perception of reason, and all evaluations change. Specifically, the 'authority' and 'power' bestowed are viewed differently. In the past, I focused only on achieving much, rising high, efficiency, and effectiveness, but ultimately, I could win or lose. Only following the Lord's will is true victory.

Anyone in the world can say, "Everything will be alright." But what a person of faith says is different. "Everything will be alright. Because..." That "because" is not a fragile twig but a mighty tree with roots reaching deep into the earth. "Do not be afraid or dismayed because of this great multitude. For the battle is not yours, but God's!" (2 Chronicles 20:15)

Much love is not obtained through begging or effort. And it certainly does not bring complete happiness even if obtained. If I have received any small comfort or love from someone, it is from the Lord's

What the devil most guards against and fears is the body of Christ, that is, our unity and oneness. He will do anything to prevent it. But we already know. That situation is very frustrating for him. When we become one, the body of Christ will be made whole, and we will do the works of Jesus and even greater works.
Walk in the Word without fear. Let the Word be my conclusion, rather than what my senses and the world's rumors say. I will not be shaken.
I trust the Lord, regardless of what happens to me. I entrust my life to Him alone and desire only His faithfulness. Blessed is the one who trusts in the Lord.
To hope and rely solely on the Lord's faithfulness in seemingly impossible situations, that is faith.
If I call, will You come? When I cry, do You hear? I believe every tear is caught up by a faithful God.
I can do all things through Christ Jesus who strengthens me.
There is a confession that continues to push and drive me forward, even after my youthful days have passed. I love Jesus.

David composed the Psalms after two great experiences. One was defeating Goliath, where he received courage and strength. The other was escaping from King Achish by pretending to be mad and drooling. (Psalm 34) First, he received help for a dramatic victory, and second, he received help in his weakness. In the Psalms, David proudly testifies that both were God's grace. There is no need to be ashamed of not being strong or lacking faith now. It is not shameful to be a sinner, but it is shameful to hide it. If you are truthful, God will help you.
I dislike tears, and I dislike bloodshed even more, but if the Lord needs them, I will gladly offer them. If dying is necessary to save, then dying is also a blessing.
An increase in cockroaches is not revival. Revival is when they transform into people.
On the sixth day, after creating man, the first command was to rest. Rest is not doing nothing, but believing in the Lord and abiding in Him. The Lord does it. The Almighty Lord does it.

I miss everyone so much, and I long to see them. I have tried to be humble and loved with excellence. But as I walk the journey of faith, I realize that the Lord is love, and that is how I love. To be stingy with the words "I love you" is a great mistake. If you confess your love to those close to you, darkness will recede, and the kingdom of heaven will come. Really, try it right now.
I hear that I cannot take another step without the prayers of my co-workers and the Lord's grace, not as a limitation, but as comfort.

Sometimes, after staying up for days preparing, you are not used. That is natural. It is because the intention was to save lives and souls, not to satisfy people. Faith is the ability to let go of precious things.

If you look at what is close, you will waver. You cannot proceed straight. This is why beginner drivers zigzag on the road. They are looking at things too close... If you keep your gaze on the Lord, you will not waver. There is no reason to waver.
A person who has been drenched in a downpour no longer fears a drizzle. A person who is satisfied in the Lord is someone who has nothing more to lose. Holding onto Jesus is enough.
Pride leads to wrong judgments, maintains wrong emotional states, deceives, and is deceived.
Do not fear, just believe.

The only power for all believers is to return to the gospel. It is not to react according to one's own thoughts and feelings, but to return to the gospel that shed blood. The gospel means returning to Jesus, who is outside of self-centered thinking. There is no salvation except in Jesus.

Many of God's people are needed in the last days. Through people who react with faith and pray with love, the Lord will perform miracles and power. I earnestly desire that you become such a designer of God, an engineer of God, a teacher of God, a son of God, a daughter of God.
When going through difficult times, I hope you will offer this prayer: Place your face on the ground. "Humble me more, make me lower." Face down.
Living by faith does not mean giving up your life in the end. It means being dead from the start, having no more life to give. What more can you desire in heaven except the Lord?

When passing through the wilderness, it is actually easier to entrust yourself to the Lord. This is because you personally experience that you cannot handle it anyway, and that the Lord handling it is completely safe.

Hardship and poverty cannot hinder faith. If your desire is the Lord, you desire that "whether I live or die, only the Lord be glorified within me." Those who seek the Lord's will cannot be stopped by the world.

Faith is believing two things: the assurance of His presence, "that He is surely there," and the assurance of trust and response, "that He rewards those who seek Him." God chooses the detailed options, and we enjoy the joy.
To deploy the parachute of faith, you must fall from the cliff. From St. Martin.
If I had a drop of mercy and tears, it came from the Lord.
Sorry, simple, surprise, sweet, smile. Attitude precedes qualifications.
Be courageous. Be courageous as if you have conquered the world. The Lord has overcome the world.

Look to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of our faith. Faith is not believing in my own convictions. It is not believing that I will get married this year or buy a house. True faith is believing in Jesus. It means believing that the Jesus I look to is with me.

Only prayers of faith receive answers. Faith is looking. Then it becomes reality and fact. If you hope for, believe in, and confess what you do not have as if you do, you can break a rock by throwing an egg.
Take risks by faith, and you will see miracles.
You become happier when you humble yourself. Please humble yourself.
Mercy triumphs over judgment and boasts. What is the evidence of God's work and daily miracles? It is the forgiveness that happens within me daily.

Nelson Mandela, after enduring an unjust imprisonment for 27 years, embraced the white man who had put him in prison as the first thing he did upon release. Why? 'Because otherwise, I could never have left prison for my entire life.'

Unknown comets approaching at terrifying speeds from distant galaxies, or news that dominates the headlines upon waking, do not pose a crisis for us. The true crisis is when 'Jesus alone is not enough.' When Jesus is not enough, we resort to our own strength and use power we shouldn't. Then we lose friends, fellow believers, and even family.
Though He slay me, yet will I trust Him.
Faith conquers all.
There will be suffering. If you do not look to heaven...
When you are in extreme emotional pain, please look only to God. Just as the Lord bore the cross, which the Jews so vehemently hated and cursed, I hope you will also go to that place of the cross.
We won through prayer. I believe in the Lord's omnipotence.
Therefore humble yourselves under God's mighty hand, 1 Peter 5:6.
Even a person falling without wings can soar again at the speed of their fall if they rely on the Lord. - The first law of Spiritual Thermodynamics.
It is not I who hold onto the Lord, but the Lord who holds onto me, so I do not worry excessively. The only reason I have been able to leap through the past years consistently is 'Jesus.'
I am not ashamed of the gospel, for it is the power of God for salvation to everyone who believes!
Trying to achieve something is overstepping. The Lord accomplished everything on the cross, so what dare I attempt to achieve further?
99 is definitely a larger number than 1. Mathematically, that is true. However, when the concept of a soul enters, what is important is life, not size.

Please read only if you do not want to repeat failures. Those belonging to the flesh can never please God, but if you belong to the Lord, you become His joy. Position, not actions, determines me. Acting has never overcome positioning. I am under the precious blood of the Lord.

And also said, As the Lord lives... - 1 Samuel 26:10 If the Lord truly lives in my life, I can cast aside impatience and look only to Him. David was tempted to take human actions many times, but he relied on the 'living God' and ultimately triumphed. Hallelujah~ Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever.

God who makes us gather and pray, God who makes us proclaim the grace of His precious blood for three hours, God had much to say and much grace to give. The moments of life are 감격 (deep emotion/thrill) and boundless grace.
Understand the reason behind the season. Jesus is the same yesterday and today and forever. I trust in Him.
Do not let my thoughts, my abilities, my calculations be the conclusion. Let faith be the conclusion and truth be the conclusion.
To love my Savior Jesus more.

When fear comes to believers, it is perfectly normal, so God permits it. Jesus crushed the head of Satan with the cross, so Satan cannot harm us with poison. He can only strike with his tail. It will hurt if it hits, but it cannot kill like poison. Remember your relationship with the Lord and overcome.
Do not try to confirm everything to find peace, but rather be at peace by being assured only of 'that love.' The Lord's love is enough.

Most people are frustrated because what they need and what they want do not align. What they want is not what they need, and what they truly need is not what they want. When these two align, amazing things happen. Only Jesus can fulfill both my needs and my wants simultaneously.

Faith is surrendering to the Lord amidst all impossible and desolate situations. And rejoicing in Him forever. This morning, we cover our fellow members with His precious blood. I bless you in the precious name of the Lord.

Do not be satisfied with reactions; be assured by faith. Faith is the 'substance' of things hoped for, things not yet seen because they have not happened, things that do not manifest as reactions. Those who live by reaction will suffer.
I hope you will stand before the gospel again. Jesus is the gospel. I hope you will have a life where Jesus is the answer, and a life where Jesus is enough.
Use faith. Lay down your spears and swords...
What I can do is not important. What is important is who I believe in and how I believe. It is believing in God the Father and the one He sent, Jesus.
Do not be afraid of disappointing people. Instead, look to the Lord who loved me when I was a sinner, bore the cross for me, and never disappoints me, and find peace.

"I will spend what is mine and be spent for you." - 2 Corinthians 12:15 Paul's way of loving the Corinthian church, who misunderstood him about money, was to give himself and spend himself. The reason we can love with persistent and compassionate hearts is to have a clear life attitude of being determined to spend ourselves. It means throwing away our pride for all misunderstandings. Who is the one who loves enough to spend themselves?
You will hear of wars and rumors of wars, but do not be alarmed. The Lord said not to fear. We are in the Lord's hands. May the Lord shine His light.
[Psalm 62:8] Trust in Him at all times, O people; pour out your hearts to Him, for God is our refuge.
When He asks us to entrust something, it is not to make things difficult for us, but to increase our faith.

If you have faith even as small as a mustard seed, you will keenly feel that nothing can be accomplished by your own strength. When you try to handle relationships directly, they do not go as you wish. Entrust them. The level, frequency, and outcome are in the Lord's hands. The Lord is faithful.
Setting aside all thoughts of measuring up and all selfish ways, letting Him become your all. You will begin to see His manifold blessing.

The dualistic thinking seen in the Old Testament: those who seek God and those who do not. Life's happiness changes based on that boundary. It is wisdom to know that disobeying God is much more difficult and has a greater cost in the long run than obeying Him.
The only way to be free from fear is to have a heart that reveres God. The more you are aware of God, the less you will be aware of yourself.
We listen to God, we do not listen out for God.

Fear is also a kind of faith. It is an unlucky but persistent belief that something bad will surely happen to me. To believe is not to calculate circumstances, but to go to the Lord and entrust everything, come what may.

It is not suffering itself that is miserable, but collapsing in the face of suffering that is miserable. Since He clearly said He would give us trials we can bear, the truth is to be confident that we can bear them.
Meaning is far more important than money. A simple life in the fear of God is better than a rich life with a ton of headaches.
