A leader does not coerce but inspires, does not dominate but serves.

If I have no love for others, no desire to serve for others, and I'm only concerned about my needs, I should question whether Christ is really in my life. A saved heart is one that wants to serve. Apostle John said "Our love for each other proves that we have gone from death to life."

Many strive to be remembered and honored, but believers fundamentally live to be forgotten. Clinging only to promises, possessing only faith, and living by grace alone, believers are hidden in Christ, forgotten from the perspective of worldly desires and eyes. Colossians 3:2-3

Many think they will be free if they have a lot of information, but that is not the case. The freest thing is to set aside unnecessary desires and concerns and look only to the Lord. The Lord Himself will counsel you.
Comfort my beloved servant.
Whether good or bad things happen to me, it is God's will that all circumstances work together and harmonize to conclude beautifully. To achieve this, we must love God and be within His calling.
I would like to ask for answers from those who know the rock band Gukakasten. I want to survey how many fans there are. Please vote as "I am a fan," "I don't know," or "I've heard of them."
All things work together for good for those who love God and are called according to His purpose. Our God is a good God, and He has the amazing ability to turn evil into good.
The most important things above all else are God's presence and His voice. If you have these two, you are heading in the right direction.
True happiness is being content with what you should be content with and longing for what you should long for.
Trying too hard to do well is greed. What God desires is faithfulness.
We can overcome suffering when our trust in obeying the Lord's will is greater than the suffering we endure.
The more so, the more I rely only on the Lord.
Many people of faith testify. What they thought was fate turned out to be refinement.
Not just anyone can carelessly say that suffering has a purpose. However, that is the truth.
A Christian on their knees sees farther than an astronomer standing on tiptoe.
I will walk with you wherever you go. Through tears and joy, I will trust in you, Lord.

Can you let go of the most important thing? If you do, the Lord will become fully my Lord. If you give up one most important thing rather than a million trivial things, the Lord will rule over the rest.
The relationship between a leader and Barnabas is like placing an apple on one's head and shooting an arrow. It is a relationship of trust, come what may, because the Lord has called us.
The more I hide my faults and weaknesses, the worse they become. Only when I bring them to the Lord am I covered. This is called a blessed person.
True peace comes when I let go of judging myself.
Envy and self-deprecation will not cease until genuine gratitude for what I have received from God overflows. What God has given us is so great that it cannot be fully expressed in human words.
It was not Abraham, the father of faith, who was great, but the God he believed in was great.
Emotions are the fruit of thoughts. What is different is not necessarily wrong. If you change your values, anger can transform into fresh ideas.

Pray about all your problems. Then, it is not you who solves the problem, but God. If you offer up problems from your ignorant childhood, even problems that existed before you were born, He will solve them all.

Demons know our weaknesses like demons. No matter how plausible those weaknesses seem, they are not truth. Only the Word of God is truth. Do not accept your weaknesses; believe the truth. You will be victorious.
...But for this reason I came to the present time. John 12:27
What is important in faith is not to become accustomed to it. The Lord's love must not become accustomed; it must always be new. Otherwise, it becomes a business from that point on.
Christ does not frustrate ardent pursuers. From Acts 6 and answered cries.
Changed hearts lead to a changed society.
Believe that what you have asked for has been received.
It is foolish to try to surgically remove all your wounds. Be led and entrust yourself only to the Lord. The Lord will take responsibility.

[1 Timothy 3:10] Paul's advice is to first test these people, and if they are found blameless, then let them serve as deacons. He urges us to test them by giving them small tasks, how they handle situations where their opinions differ, and how they serve when no one is watching. When a flight is canceled, when the last train is missed, in situations where resources must be shared and suffering endured in limited circumstances, people inevitably reveal themselves.
Joy is an attitude, not a reaction. "Rejoice because your names are written in heaven."

There was a man trying to escape from prison. It was to seek revenge. He eventually escaped and became a free man, then went searching for his enemy. However, he was not free. He had escaped the prison, but he was still trapped in the prison of hatred.
Faith is looking at what you should look at. Greed is looking at what you should not look at. Everything not done in faith is sin.

The less valuable the jewels, the more ornate the jewelry box. Those who possess true jewels do not compare or slander their own or others' boxes. Those who have Jesus in an earthen vessel are already precious and have no need to prove it.
Pride is a choice. It is choosing to deceive the true 'self.' This destroys self-esteem, makes one anxious, easily wounded, and prone to hiding.
Humility is not self-deprecation. The more humble a person is, the greater their self-worth.

Those who honor me I will honor, and those who despise me I will treat with contempt. Not reacting in faith is despising God. No one who despises God is happy or successful. Those with wrong priorities may seem advantageous, but they are the most disadvantaged.
Suddenly wanting to eat without ingredients... It's been a while for family.
No one can stop someone who is prepared for loneliness and solitude while looking to God in faith. In times of deep solitude, the Lord meets us deeply.
What kept me from getting tired? That is where my eternity lies.

Thirst is not simply quenched; you must dig for it. And diligent digging alone won't suffice. If you dig where the Lord tells you to dig, you will find living water. Sometimes it may be bedrock, or land that people have driven you away from, but if Jesus is there, the living water will continue. I hope we are servants of faith who can give up everything to gain Jesus.
Doing what I want and believing what I want is not faith. Seeking the face of God is true faith. People wish God would change, but God never changes and cannot be persuaded, for He is truth.
When everything collapses, when the last hope fades, when everyone says it is despair, if I am still looking at the cross, the mission continues, and the game is not yet over.

When we guess, we make terrible mistakes. Therefore, we must be careful. Be quick to listen, slow to speak. A letter written sentimentally in the evening has a chance to be reconsidered in the morning, but an email cannot be retrieved.
Starting this week, we begin the relay fast for the retreat. We hope for God's great grace in anticipation and prayer.
Many people live believing that diligence leads to a better life. And they blame themselves for laziness. In reality, it's not laziness, but a lack of fire in their hearts.
Even if everyone is in darkness, if you don't give up on a small ember and hold onto it, it becomes the sun that illuminates the whole world.
You need to relax and be yourself. God loves us as He created us.

There are no worried birds. God said He loves us more than the birds of the air. You will experience that almost all problems are solved with just faith and confidence in God's love. Why are you afraid?

A leader is not someone who handles copies, but someone who stakes their life on the original. If you consider the influence your life will have, you must live a life that is a precious original. We are constantly delivering messages through our lives.
A leader is someone who has two things: grace and humor. If you catch both rabbits, communication happens. That's where affinity comes from.
The words I say to you are not my own, but the Father who lives in me does his work. John 14:10. Do not strive to accomplish things, but surrender so that the Lord may be revealed.

When you acknowledge Him, God's sovereignty is revealed. Please acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty in finances, relationships, studies, work, and spiritual warfare. If I cannot believe in the Lord's sovereignty, I will be deceived by Satan and condemned. Let us proclaim that the land we tread is the land of God's sovereignty.

Paul, seeing the armor of Roman soldiers, strongly urges in Ephesians 6 to put on the full armor of God. The purpose is to defeat the devil's schemes and stand firm on the last day. He advises us to use the shield of faith, trusting in the Lord, to block the fiery arrows that cause doubt in God's good purpose. The most effective shield against spiritual attack is trust and faith.

Therefore, fellowship of faith, worship with presence, and the living gospel are needed daily. No, they are needed every moment. The devil challenges us every day. We cling to the Lord every moment. Forgetting the gospel and grace is why the Israelites who left Egypt perished.
All members, receive blessings in Jesus' name today. Those who honor me, I will honor, and those who despise me, I will despise. If we honor the Lord with our firstfruits, God will honor us.

H + C + N = potassium cyanide becomes what is called potassium cyanide. 0.15g can kill a person in 3 seconds. We think it kills by poisoning the body, but it is actually asphyxiation. The bond between carbon C and nitrogen N is triple. Simply put, there is such a strong preference that it creates a strong hunger (instability) in the opposite area. When a person ingests it, it reacts with hydrochloric acid, the stomach acid in the esophagus or stomach, within seconds. Then, due to the stomach acid, a gas called HCN is produced, and this is what causes death. Simply put, the mechanism of potassium cyanide, in Christian terms, is preference and reaction. Especially, if there is no reaction, no harm is done. Some people who have not produced stomach acid have survived after eating potassium cyanide. This is because there was no reaction.

If you had calculated, you shouldn't have come this way. This is the path I came because I walked by faith, not by overthinking. I used to think people lived by bread alone, by money alone, but I realized that people live by the Lord's word. Furthermore, you must entrust yourself to that word.
Before asking to be shown the way, the first thing to do is to confess, "Speak, Lord, I will listen. I will obey."

Don't just live day by day; live by painting a magnificent self-portrait of yourself. That is the difference between ordinary and extraordinary. Resolve and rely on living according to how God sees you, not how you or others see you. Then miracles will happen.

Many people I've met were originally greedy and tough young people. But now they have become gentle and greedy for good things. They seemed like they would live proudly on their own, but now they have become fools who cannot live without the Lord. That is doing well. Peter was like that, John was like that, and the Apostle Paul was like that. And you will become like that too.
since I've been in Jesus, even though there have been lots of toils and snares I always believe God is good, God is love, God is faithful, I never doubt those, never...

You must have assurance in your heart. Is Jesus truly within me? If not, you must come before the Lord as soon as possible. You may need to consult with a minister and ask for prayer. Because this is the most important matter.

Even if a person has everything they lack, they are not necessarily happy. Behind every smile, there are tears, and behind the bright lights, there are shadows. However, under the cross, there is true rest and happiness in being satisfied with the Lord alone.
Faith is thinking about and looking at the Lord even amidst indescribable busyness and activity.
I prayed for grace, and God gave me the grace He had prepared. Grace always brings about change. That change makes us resemble Jesus.
The most basic, yet most fundamental, faith is not to be conscious of people's gaze and to fix all your attention solely on God who is present in that moment.

Jacob's blessing, I love and bless you all. I bless you with the most precious thing I have. I pray that God hears your prayers, that His face shines upon you, and that you may have a life of deep emotion and inspiration.

When Shawshank Redemption first came out in 1994, there was a phrase under the poster of a man standing with his hands raised in the rain: "Fear can hold you prisoner, Hope can set you free." As you can tell from the movie title, it has a strong Christian structure. However, it only borrows the structure; the content is humanism. (It portrays Christianity negatively.) The original Bible says, "The truth will set you free." Not just hope, but THE Truth. I hope we become those who hold onto that truth.
A person who is not afraid when threatened, a person who becomes bolder when threatened. Those who fully trust the Lord say this: "I'll just lie back and relax!"

When eating manna in the wilderness, you must have strong faith. It means you will starve without faith. However, when we entered Canaan, we could live by storing grain even without faith. When it's not necessary to live by faith, prayer, the Word, and worship can easily disappear.

A thirsty person seeks living water. Do you know what a person who drinks living water desires? To become even thirstier. To be more thirsty so that the living water does not cease. It is an unceasing thirst for the Lord.
Faith is the ability to do the most ignorant thing in the most noble way.

The author of the renowned book "Christians Who Live on Their Knees," an anonymous Christian, stated in the preface that the root of all problems for Christians is not encountering God sufficiently, meaning not praying enough. Worship deeply, preach the word concisely, and pray abundantly.
If you cannot be moved, you cannot be bold. Acting boldly without emotion is tormenting those you belong to. Return to the Lord, remember Him, and be moved.
The strongest and most handsome worm was scouted by an entertainment agency to debut. When he returned, his unremarkable friends had become brilliant butterflies. He had failed.
Beware of the one who says, "I will take care of everything." God does not work so carelessly that we should rely on people. If you rely on people, you will surely fall.
You are not blessed because you are harsh. God's blessings are poured out by God. Wake up from your deep sleep, stand up, and lift your holy hands to pray.
The reason I live is because of the Lord.

If you avoid fear, fear grows larger. If you remember the One you should truly fear, you will gain the strength to fight. Do not fear them, but remember the Lord, who is great and fearsome, and fight for your brothers, your children, your wives, and your homes. Nehemiah 4:14
If you pray with piercing eyes, God will hear. I must go to the Saturday meeting to pray.

As mentioned, volunteers, please let me know your available times (Tue-Thu) within the mission conference schedule. We need to know the number of people by date in advance for meal preparation. The 7th 2010 London Mission Conference Venue: London Steward Church Date: October 5-8, 2010 (Tue-Fri) 6:30 PM - 9:30 PM Target: Pastors, missionaries, lay leaders 577 Kingston Road - Raynes Park - London - SW20 8SA
Gospel_and_Cross: * Never tear down the altar. You have torn down the altars of the Lord and killed your prophets with the sword. - 1 Kings 19:14 1. It was because of the altar that sinful humans could approach God. 2. Furthermore, the holy God spoke to imperfect humans through the prophets. 3. Tearing down the altar and killing the prophets was a satanic strategy and intelligent sin that sought a complete (two-way) severance from God. 4. Satan's desire is to prevent us from coming to the throne of grace through the cross and receiving mercy and grace. 5. Therefore, all ministry and pursuits lacking the blood of the cross are own goals.
* Reset & Reloaded Before the cross, for any reason or story, we are reset by Jesus' love. This is because nothing is more important than knowing and proclaiming Jesus.

* The Power of Change Change begins with the change of the person in front. My repentance must come first. 'Change' is not difficult; it is 'impossible.' It is a fruit that only the Lord can produce. Therefore, where there is change and testimony, willing hearts are built up. I planted, and Apollos watered, but God gave the growth. So neither he who plants nor he who waters is anything, but only God who gives the growth. He who plants and he who waters are one, but each will receive his wages according to his labor. For we are God's fellow workers; you are God's field, God's building. - 1 Corinthians 3:6-9

* The Suffering of the Cross The cross was particularly difficult because, historically, prisoners were stripped naked. However, Jesus did not mind. Therefore, true love bears shame. Let us fix our eyes on Jesus, the pioneer and perfecter of faith. For the joy set before him he endured the cross, disregarding its shame, and has sat down at the right hand of the throne of God. - Hebrews 12:2
* The Eternal Gospel We can live gloriously and unchangingly only by continually hearing the eternal, glorious, and universal gospel of the cross.

* Addiction and the Gospel A person addicted to games cannot quit no matter how hard they try, but they can quit if they play a game that is more addictive than games. Why can't people quit futile addictions? It's because they haven't found something more fun and interesting. The only thing that is more powerful than worldly addictions and provides true satisfaction is 'Jesus Christ.' He alone is the way, the truth, and the life for this broken generation. Call on Jesus, and you will live. Jesus said to him, "I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me." - John 14:6

* Go and Make Disciples (Matthew 28:19) The model shown in the book of Acts was to equip and release farmers in rural areas, teachers in schools, bankers in finance, students on campuses, and lawyers in the legal profession to minister without hesitation. "Go and make disciples" does not mean bringing a Bible to church on Sunday and filling a seat, but rather becoming a moving church that goes to barren lands, plants the cross, and prays for the Holy Spirit to descend. The churches of Asia send you greetings. Aquila and Priscilla, together with the church in their house, send you hearty greetings in the Lord. - 1 Corinthians 16:19 And to the brothers in Laodicea and to Nympha and the church in her house. - Colossians 4:15

* A Father Who Cannot Be Common Knowledge and titles may be splendid, but it is difficult to sacrifice. The gospel bears fruit only when life gives birth to life and blood is shed. That is why such things do not happen commonly. For though you have countless guides in Christ, you do not have many fathers. - 1 Corinthians 4:15

* Basic The reason we cannot remain faithful only to the essence of the cross is that there are too many systems. Pastor Ko Yong-jo said, "To maintain a large organization, you must have systems and structures, and then you inevitably lose sight of the essence." This is because from the beginning, the object and purpose we try to maintain besides the cross are wrong.
* Essential Subject Faith is not lived alone. There must be a community led by God that conceives the gospel within me. This applies to all believers.

* The Moving Church On a flight to New Orleans, due to an unexpected delay, four elderly gentlemen, as if performing a scheduled concert, moved the audience beyond singing with their a cappella. The ability to create God's throne and worship Him wholeheartedly anytime, anywhere, is power and the moving church. This is why I stand here now, and it is also the life of being true light and salt. If we are awake and prepared with the gospel for a lifetime for the moment, then in every era and space where such people are present, we will see God's glory, and it will become a moving church.

* The Power of Loss The gospel that cannot move me is not the gospel. If greed, people, recognition, or relationships move me, then those are my own gospel and the objects of my worship. When I lose them, I see my true self and realize what my true gospel was.

* Simple When we hold onto the cross and pray, the gospel becomes glorious. The reason the originally glorious gospel is not glorious is that we retreat from the front lines of prayer, and that is because we give up holding onto the cross.
* When the Gospel Becomes Power If we live according to the gospel, the power of the gospel works. The gospel becomes more like the gospel to the extent that we live it out.
* The True Gospel Do not try to imitate the gospel; come to the true gospel that can change you.

* Do Not Be Discouraged. In the end, the sinful world flows in one direction, and there is only one path for the soul to live. Therefore, if you glorify the gospel with your life, it is a power superior to all things.
* The Reason Prayer and Worship Don't Work A gospel that cannot change me can no longer be the gospel. Only the gospel can move me to kneel. Worship with the gospel.

* The Greatest Gift What God wanted to give more than anything else was Himself. Therefore, He reveals Himself to us and gives us the cross. For humans, God's greatest gift is 'presence,' and the greatest gift humans can give is 'fellowship.'
* The Greatest Cry God's love, spoken in perfect silence on the cross.

* Peace Not being at peace means not being at rest, and consequently, it means indulging in sin. God has granted unique peace today. Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace among those with whom he is pleased! - Luke 2:14

* An Appeal to Sinners For those who repeatedly fall into the futile sin of never being satisfied, I recommend Jesus, who is eternal joy. It is a higher, absolute peace that can uniquely break the chains of sin.

The reason for not being corrupted is that Paul preached the cross in all his writings, words, and life. He was not corrupted even at the moment his head was cut off. It is because he was close to the cross.
A life without regret. Even if I lose or lose more, if I go back to that place, I will make the same choice. There is nothing more glorious than the gospel of the cross.

To you who have lost courage. We need someone who can shake their head and say that the cross is not like that, in a world that says, "The world is just like that." It is not about living by faith and finding no difference, but about holding onto the cross with the resolve to die.

Mountains are mountains, and water is water. The ability to see mountains as mountains and water as water is not a small thing. Even those who boast of knowing the gospel often fail to see believers as believers and the church as the church. This is because believers and the church are, by their very nature, new creatures that are only truly seen when "I die and live by Jesus."

Youth Sunday 2nd Service 2014.11.23 - We are the Lord's Moving Church (Infinite Challenge). Just as singers are people who breathe through song and climbers are people who break through to the summit, a person of God is someone who, wherever they go, reveals God's glory that cannot be imitated through the unashamed gospel they carry.

You are the Lord's moving church. I hope you will always be awake and prepared with the gospel for a lifetime for the moment. I hope you will carry your rightful cross and bloom and fade without hesitation. In the era and space where such people are present, we will see God's glory and it will become a moving church.

Blessings and curses. When revelation is heard, one lives, but when revelation ceases, people quickly corrupt. The person of the gospel and the proclamation of the gospel are blessings. Zechariah, who clearly knew God's revelation, sought God in his days, and as long as he sought the Lord, God made him prosper. 2 Chronicles 26:5
The disease of perfectionism. It is not because we cannot be corrected that we cannot return to God. Only the cross can correct me. When I return to the Father, everything finally falls into place.

Spiritual Matrix - Reformed since AD 30 every 500 years. Experience - Truth = Mysticism Greed + Law = Authoritarianism Greed + Comparison = Envy, Jealousy Faith - Grace = Legalism Faith - Belief = Humanism Model + Inspiration = Change Instruction + Management = Labor Company = Management + Investment Church = Spirit + Sacrifice Bank = Money + Time Church = Jesus + Time Tear down, build up - John 2:19
Radical Gospel, Radical Jjajangmyeon
The power of the precious blood is not a one-time license. It is something that must always be proclaimed, drunk, sprinkled, and applied.
A strong person is one who has decided to suffer with the gospel.

Please give what is needed. If you are eager to give what the souls want, it will inevitably become an awkward ball where everyone is dressed in outdated clothes. We must supply what is needed, not what is wanted. Only Jesus can fill the thirst and emptiness that they themselves do not understand. Jesus said, "You give them something to eat." Matthew 14:16

Proclaiming the gospel is not difficult. It is impossible. Proclaiming the gospel is not difficult. It is impossible. It is not an area that can be done with my own strength and wisdom. It is a matter of revelation to proclaim what God has commanded us to do and to proclaim.

A sad masquerade wearing a mask of holiness. As long as one does not admit that they are an existential sinner and that merely plucking a few fruits of sin does not remove the root, the laborious and sad masquerade will not cease. It is not a matter of 'will,' but a matter of dying before the 'gospel.'

If you miss the essence, you will suffer. If the nation, company, or family misses the essence, everyone suffers. The same is true for the church. If you miss the cross, there is nothing more unbearable.
Choose the cross. You must choose to respond with the cross. For the cross is the conception and completion of salvation.

Struggle and the cross. God did not struggle but ruled with sovereignty. The pinnacle of that rule is the cross. Therefore, believers live by the cross and die by the cross. If you live and die like that, there will be resurrection.
I have nothing to give. That is why I am happy. I have no silver or gold, but what I have I give you. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk. Acts 3:6

Do not confirm yourself through ministry. You become a servant of man, not a servant of God. And you will often experience emptiness and pride. My existence is by God's grace. This means that the cross is my identity, which saved a sinner like me.

Do not envy their league. Jesus came as an insignificant branch in this dry land to bring life to the marginalized who were excluded from 'their league.' True influence that breaks down the gates of hell and rescues souls comes from relinquishing rights and acknowledging sovereignty. It means carrying the cross and going to the dry land.

Ministry is Delivery. It is delivering God's grace, that is, Himself, His presence. It is being with Him, not information or usefulness about Him. Therefore, the responsibility of the deliverer is to faithfully go back and forth between God's post office and the soul's house.
The reason for praise is not because I received an answer or grace, but because of Jesus. It is because Jesus is the One who gave me the life of His only begotten Son. That is enough.
Prayer that is sucked up to heaven. Prayer rises as if sucked up by a vacuum, without falling short of the ceiling. The Lord wants to save souls. Ah, I'm looking forward to the retreat.
The gospel and sales are similar but different: proclaim it coolly, but pray with tears.

The reason of moving on. When I feel worthless, what I need is to receive the Lord's forgiveness. The fastest way to get rid of "a sense of unworthiness" is to trust in Christ's forgiveness. And He is ready to forgive at all times.

True victory is to give your heart to the Lord and not compromise, rather than focusing on the adversary. The clear thing is that the adversary will not gain anything they desire. It is because Jesus bore the cross.
Proclaim, do not be intimidated, proclaim easily. Evangelism and mission are not begging, but giving the gift of privilege.
A true leader is not someone who gives solutions. They are someone who conveys the Lord, who is the only way.

Do not postpone it any longer. Repent and be filled with the Holy Spirit. Cast away all wickedness, call on the name of the Lord Jesus, and you will be declared not guilty and receive the Holy Spirit as a gift, just as it is written in the Bible. Do it right now.

The fundamental sin is not "fruit theft" from eating the fruit of the tree of knowledge of good and evil, but "selfishness" that makes me want to be God and not have God in my heart, and "idolatry" that serves the self. He died for all so that those who live might no longer live for themselves but for him who for their sake died and was raised again. 2 Corinthians 5:15
The reason the Lord came was to be rejected, abandoned, and crucified. So, isn't it more glorious to follow Jesus and live that way than to be welcomed?
Cain's desire for sin is not accepted as an offering if the wound is hidden. Such desires for sin must be confessed to the Lord and ruled by grace. It is not disappearing but being covered.

Do not fear the testimony. A community with strong faith means there are influential believers within that community. If we all follow the world, the gospel will not flow. Do not fear rejection and disregard; strive to testify and love Jesus.

It may seem very religious, but it is not. It's just that you liked the people. Faith is not about liking people, but about liking Jesus, and it is good when Jesus is revealed in fellowship because of Him. An easy way to know is to ask who you are testifying about.
Try living according to the gospel for once. You will know what true victory is without complaining anymore.
Without the gospel, you will suffer disgrace. Suffering disgrace is not because you don't know the gospel. It is because you do not live according to the gospel.
The reason you are called is not to prolong your life but to live eternally. Eternal life means victory with the fullness of life in all areas.
It is truth, not suggestion. Do not react to suggestions; please conclude with the cross. Suggestions are lies and greed.

Being cleansed by blood is dwelling in the light. Even if you fail and fall today, please never give up on being God's good people. It is not because you have little sin, but because the one who brings their sin has fellowship in the light. If we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus his Son cleanses us from all sin. 1 John 1:7

Jesus suffered outside the gate because He came as the Lamb of God to grant forgiveness of sins and salvation. Therefore, we too must carry Jesus' cross to gain life and go out, rejecting worldly glory and human popularity. So let us also go to him outside the camp, bearing his reproach. Hebrews 13:13
The grace we enjoy now is not a given. Someone shed blood for it. The completion of grace bears fruit only when we cherish and give thanks for the shedding of blood.
Those who know how much God has loved people like this cannot easily disregard or hate others. It is because God loved them specially.

The amazing secret of the precious blood. A characteristic of the few who are good with language is that they acquire language within the situation. Jesus is the one who shed His precious blood, but we must become like Moses and the Israelites who observed the Passover, the ones who sprinkle the precious blood. I will tell you a secret. Confessing to the Lord in detail what temptations of sin you feel and how you fall is sprinkling the precious blood. Sprinkle it well. You will be washed clean.

The true Passover is fulfilled as the Lamb of God, who was to come at the end of the Passover, arrives. Since atonement is received through that final Passover, we now commemorate and proclaim life through the Lord's Supper until He comes. That is why the place of the last Passover became the first Lord's Supper. Now the place of repentance will be a place of victory and life.
Life becomes difficult because you try to receive love. Even though you were sent to give love...

If a Christian is to be hated, it should be for the truth. Jesus was too. When you receive such hatred, do not be ashamed, but rather rejoice. The world hates you not because you are evil, but because I testify that its works are evil. John 7:7

Radical Gospel. It is nonsense to say you can change the world while your own body is not free from the world's sin. Succumbing to fleshly desires and falling into worldly temptations is because the gospel has been diluted. The gospel is that the Son of God bore a difficult cross. Living according to the price of that blood is faith.
Complexity is evil. Paul warns against evil and false teachers who corrupt the gospel. Faith is simple. Complexity that prevents you from holding onto the cross is evil that has lost its essence.
To surrender to the vain... The gospel is too glorious, Jesus is supremely beautiful, and God's glory is infinite. I will choose this side.
Life is not romantic. To blossom life from death, there must be a grain of love, devotion, and sacrifice that dies. If you lack the heart for it, it is right to feel moved and sorry for such a state.

The power to cast off heavy burdens. Even if you earnestly try to cast off heavy burdens, they will not be cast off. Heavy burdens are not cast off but exchanged for light and easy burdens. If success is to labor and bear heavy burdens to avoid becoming miserable, the cross gives the courage to be okay with being a worthless being in the world. The person who has the courage not to fear even when they become a miserable being also receives the grace of being embraced in the Lord's bosom. Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light. Matthew 11:28, 30

The reason for anxiety even after success. Everyone rejoices when they win a silver medal, but Korean athletes cry. In a society that only remembers first place, everyone tries to succeed. However, even if some of what I and my family want is achieved, we are still anxious. It is because we are not victors. That is why carrying the cross truly brings peace.

He makes a way of escape. God does not allow trials that you cannot bear. When you are about to be unable to bear it, He makes a way of escape. Usually, this is thought of as a relief from hardship, but it is more about the path to Jesus, the unique refuge, the Way.

True salvation is needed. Salvation is not about suddenly getting money or having your problems solved. Salvation comes when you receive forgiveness of sins. Therefore, in your brokenness, you turn to God, not to people.

The voice crying in the wilderness. John the Baptist, when asked "Who are you?", said he was "the voice of one crying out." He is truly a great man. He could disappear as a voice while introducing the Lord. He said, "I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness: ‘Make straight the way of the Lord,’ as the prophet Isaiah said." John 1:23

The Gospel of the Cross. When you testify about the cross, you will be criticized. In that suffering, the gospel of the cross is testified more strongly and exerts influence. However, compromise to avoid criticism will bring the death of the church.

You receive grace because you are a sinner. Sinners are freed from guilt and justified freely by believing in Jesus. However, the sinful nature does not disappear. Therefore, we rely on the precious blood of the cross and move towards a holy life. When we acknowledge that we are sinners who have been justified, we realize that we are beings in desperate need of mercy and grace. What is needed is not "improvement" but "boundless grace."
On the day he achieved the greatest success of his life, Peter left everything and followed Jesus.

The reason for going to the hospital is to remove the root of a serious illness, even if it requires surgery. Going to the hospital cafeteria, having tea with the doctor, and visiting other patients are not fundamental. We are saved only by going onto the operating table and undergoing major surgery as if dead. We must pass through the cross. In the church, we must continue the work of being washed by the precious blood and receiving the Holy Spirit.
The Gospel 6, God's Original Design, Young People, Go Out into the World and Become Fire

The Gospel is perfect, and God is perfect, but I say I cannot do it. Rather, because of the perfection of the Gospel and the perfection of God, I become insufficient. It is because the Lord has chosen me that it is possible.
There is no discrimination in the Gospel. Whether Jew or Greek, or even someone whose life is at the bottom according to people's evaluation.
The Gospel must be proclaimed to me every moment. That is the way to live.

Leaving the Gospel is not only about great corruption and debauchery. If Jesus is not in your heart and there is no fervor, you have already left, no matter how outstanding your religious appearance may be. Regarding this essential question, the Lord does not give up on the youth department, and the Lord will use young people who humble themselves before this question.

The most basic characteristics of the Gospel are grace, not my merit, and transformation. A Gospel that cannot transform me is not the Gospel. If the Son of God gave His life to save me, such a transformation must follow.

What the prodigal sons who left because they disliked the Gospel need is the Gospel again. There can be no restoration or salvation apart from Jesus. That is why Christians are persistent. When it comes to Jesus.
If it were not for the Gospel, it would truly be just an unrelated event, but because of the souls who stand before that Gospel, we become family.

Evangelical Complex - Preaching the Gospel and giving bread. Finding comfort in balanced ministry. True Gospel is total salvation and is quite practical. It is about believing in Jesus as Savior and having the opportunity to cultivate wheat with wisdom and holiness. The only competitiveness of Christians is holiness and wisdom. Like a dove and a serpent.
Changes in appearance without changes in essence are of two types. One is plastic surgery, and the other is hypocrisy. Changes in essence are only possible through the Gospel of blood. That is grace.
In the insufficient disciples, we eventually see the image of Jesus. This is because Jesus loved them by shedding His blood.

* The Christmas spirit came for two reasons, He said. To serve and to give as a ransom. Then there will be peace, and glory in heaven. That is why it is said that on Christmas Eve during World War II, German and Allied soldiers put down their guns and sang carols together.

The Gospel must be continually preached not only to unbelievers but to everyone. It is natural for anyone to fall into stagnation if they fall away from the Gospel. The mistake of powerlessness is treating the Gospel merely as a ticket to heaven. Therefore, as much as possible, I want to preach the Gospel to you who are in Rome as well. - Romans 1:15

Fire overcomes fire. The fire of revival overcomes the fire of war, and the fire of faith overcomes the fire of the flesh. The fire that falls on the altar stays only on the sacrifice, and the wrath poured out on the cross was borne by Jesus alone. Let us become lives that are lightning rods.
When you criticize, you become more evil than the person being criticized. Jesus never rebuked the weak. When you love with the Lord's love, not your own strength, miracles happen.

People love in a way that pursues value. It ends endlessly, never reaching a conclusion. However, the love that the Father has given us is a love that bestows value. It lifts up the unworthy, heals the sick, and enriches the poor. I hope you become lovers who bestow value, rather than chasing after value.

People want to be moved. This is because the world is busy, dry, and lacks truth. Jesus is an eternal source of inspiration for everyone. When you are inspired by Jesus, you become a life that inspires others.
* Words of true love Speak the words that Jesus, who lives within me, wants to say to that person.

People never change by being pointed out their faults. Rather, true change comes when they are forgiven. Jesus came to forgive, and He soaked us, like dry nails in His heart, with His precious blood. The reason I can live today is because of the people who accepted me, and especially because of Jesus.
Do not become a person who circulates information about others. You will get caught up in causeless disputes. The Lord has permitted us only to circulate the Gospel.
Love inevitably involves sacrifice.
Ministry is not about coercion or squeezing. It flows abundantly, just as the Lord always did with His disciples. The Lord's love compels us.
When the Lord shines the light of His truth on the dark shadows of life, people tend to hurt and hide. God is not interfering, but seeking to forgive sins and make them whole.

* The grace of acceptance "Does the Lord love even that kind of person?" Yes, of course, He loves them. I know this because He loves even someone like me. And I know this because He bore the cross not only for me but also for that person.

* If you avoid it, you cannot have peace. There is a life lived by greed and a life lived by grace. If you live by your self-centered ego, the collapse will be severe when the storm comes, and you will fall into ruin by retreating. This is because you seek peace but are prescribed a counterfeit, a painkiller, offered by the world that avoids and flees from the situation due to the wind and rain. However, the peace that Jesus gives is fundamentally different from the peace the world gives. Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid. - John 14:27
* Types of Battles Christians fight the good fight. The good fight is a fight that saves lives, looks to the glory of soul conversion, and restores. All other fights are evil fights.

What appears in a person who has sinned with the fruit of the knowledge of good and evil is 'self-pursuit.' Because of this, we live with envy, quarrels, and even murder. (James 4:2) To kill the self is not to practice asceticism or attain enlightenment. If the death of the self were possible from the human side, then Jesus' cross would be in vain. It is believing in what God has predetermined on the cross. It is not about 'doing,' but about 'taking,' 'enjoying,' and 'considering.' In the Lord, you just need to 'consider' and 'believe.' Please look upon, reckon, must consider, count. For we know that our old self was crucified with him so that the body ruled by sin might be done away with, that we should no longer be slaves to sin. So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus. - Romans 6:6, 11
When the Holy Spirit comes, He helps us to know Jesus more and also to testify about the world. The true work of the Holy Spirit leads us to deep knowledge and understanding of Jesus.

* The difference between angels and demons An angel became a demon when it wanted to be worshipped from the position of a worshiper. When I avoid the Gospel for the sake of a suitable relationship, it is my nature, seeking to be worshipped without being criticized, that is at work. Open your mouth and just preach!

The world boasts of perishable things, but there are truly two kinds of strong people. One is a strong person who has been humbled in Jesus, and the second is a weak person who is bold in Jesus. Let the brother in humility boast of his exaltation, and the rich brother of his humility, because like the flower of the grass he will pass away. (James 1:9, 10 KRV)

The powerful people of this world are weak. Those outside the gospel are extremely weak. They are weak people who are terribly worried about traffic accidents, cancer, and their families. The gospel is a lifelong rider that covers this world and the next.

Regarding the retreat, the church (organizer) is ready. Are you? There are things in life that must not be missed. We must seize the opportunity given by the Lord in the time of grace and the day of salvation.

Humiliation is an emotion that only humans feel. A dog can be happy, angry, sad, and joyful, but it doesn't feel embarrassed. This is because humiliation came as a result of sin. Those who suffer this humiliation are inwardly wounded, leading them to become violent or so lethargic that they give up on life. Only the Lamb has the power to remove this humiliation and instead make us dance in a crown of glory. Through the blood of Jesus, all my shame has vanished. Hymn 282.
If the gospel of the blood of the cross is preached and the Holy Spirit descends, whether we live or die, it is for the glory of the Lord.
The cross, the cross, beneath its shade, there is life.

The cross takes precedence over all emotions and precedes all reactions. The cross is the Lord's only and eternal conclusion. Those who live by the cross and die by the cross are truly happy, free from pain and anguish. The cross is not a game. It is more of everything than what I thought was everything. Before the cross, all desires are rejected, and all requests of the heart are deprioritized. I cannot sleep because I am overwhelmed by the fact that the Almighty Holy One "cannot bear to give me up." "I will save you no matter what it takes." Before that cross, all clumsy pride, futile human expectations, and rotten acceptance become refuse. By holding onto the low and humble, difficult cross, we are lifted higher, and the power of sin cannot reach us and retreats.

The cross is the best conclusion the Lord has given. If a trial cannot be overcome by the cross, then the Lord would not have been defeated from the beginning. The spirit of the cross is, "I cannot bear to see you die; I will save you, no matter the cost."

* My purpose is my calling. It is God who sent me to this place. What God expects is not the fulfillment of ambition or ostentatious success. I am not only imperfect in my faith, character, and disposition, but I also deserve to be criticized, yet I can accomplish what I am called to do by the merit of Jesus' blood. That is to plant the cross in this land, baptize in the Lord's name, and cry out for the Holy Spirit to descend. Then I will be truly happy.

Evil must be overcome. But if evil is overcome with evil, it is not victory but defeat. Evil must be overcome with good. The greatest victory is compassion and forgiveness. That is why the cross is the greatest victory. Do not be defeated by evil, but overcome evil with good. Romans 12:21.

* True Love. It is truly good to be loved. However, we don't truly know it when we only receive. We come to know it when we love. We come to know how much God the Father loved us by seeing the price He paid. Behold, what manner of love the Father has bestowed on us, that we should be called children of God! And so we are. Therefore the world does not know us, because it did not know Him. 1 John 3:1.

* The Absolute Gospel. No matter how strong and organized the opponent is, and no matter how difficult our circumstances may seem, the gospel has never been defeated. The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, for he has anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim freedom for the prisoners and to give sight to the blind, to set the oppressed free, and to proclaim the year of the Lord’s favor. Luke 4:18, 19.

An Englishman was crossing the Atlantic to America in difficult circumstances. This poor man, whenever it was mealtime, would take his cheese and biscuits and eat alone on deck. Soon, even these ran out, and he was so hungry he couldn't get up. The captain asked why he went up on deck for every meal. The man explained he had no money. Captain: Your passage fare includes meals. The Englishman was so overjoyed that he ran to the dining hall and ate. It is said that this meal was his last before arriving in America. Jesus paid it all on the cross.
The strongholds of darkness have fallen. We have won. The great dragon was hurled down—that ancient serpent called the devil, or Satan, who leads the whole world astray. Revelation 12:9.

No matter what disappointment or frustration comes, and no matter how much time passes with worldly difficulties, the "power of the blood that brings freedom from sin" is an "eternal, unchanging truth."
* Fellow workers in the gospel must love and be united with each other. Otherwise, all the messages preached will be mere empty echoes.
There is no one without passion. The problem is not having passion, but not seeing it through to the end. Jesus never stopped. He pressed on to the very end and said, "It is finished" on the cross.

Jesus lived as a carpenter until he was 30 years old. He must have been a good carpenter. He was likely kind, diligent, and didn't overcharge his customers. Because He came to serve in humility, He lived that way as an employee as well.

Jesus' death followed a public and prolonged method of execution. This is because the death of the Son of God was not something to be hidden or contained, but a tremendous declaration against darkness and death, and a power that brings freedom from sin.
Jesus came to destroy the works of the devil. We are called to save lives and crush darkness, and paradoxically, this work becomes most powerful when we give up ourselves.

In the past, sacrifices of oxen, sheep, or even goats had to be made for offerings. However, for us, who lived in complete darkness and could offer none of these, the light has come. Not by my own merit, which is unworthy, but solely through the blood of Jesus...

* The True Gospel. The world is not impressed by church buildings, high status, or power. It wants to hear the true gospel. A pastor's wife shares the gospel with a taxi driver. He is indifferent. Then she adds, "I am on my way back from the hospital after being diagnosed with cancer. Driver, please believe in Jesus. There is definitely heaven after death. You must believe in Jesus." The taxi driver listens. The brighter glory and influence of believers is not "material gain" but "overcoming." Making up one's mind... (Daniel 1:8).

More important than outward appearance is the comforting embrace of a mother. The ability to see value deeply leads us to glorious places. We buy rice based on its price, but a farmer looks at a single grain of rice and reads its story—whether it's from a specific region, grown in drought-resistant conditions. When we understand the essence of the gospel, we read the beginning of salvation in places we thought were the end. The gospel rejects palaces and leads us to the wilderness.

It is impossible for us to drive out darkness. Greed and sin are not discarded through our own effort and resolve, but are discarded when we draw near to Jesus. When we seek the Lord who is the light, darkness is chased away at the speed of light. This will be the strength to rise again from our own failures.

We are not called to this earth to prove our own righteousness. We are called only to testify to His cross. The Pharisees, in their attempt to prove their own righteousness, clung to human approval and lived by its standards, but the Lord silently bore His cross and fulfilled His Father's will. Do not seek to prove yourself and be approved; rather, humble yourself and testify. It will not be easy to resist the temptation of proving yourself in your youth.

Our spiritual ancestors, unable to deny the power of the blood, lived in caves and were imprisoned, and had to give up their lives. Proclaim again that cross, that blood. The problem of humanity is the problem of sin, and only the blood can solve it. That gospel was delivered with life.
Jesus, who is the Lion of the tribe of Judah and also the Lamb, heard all the accusations against Him but was resolutely angry at the challenges to His Father.

Have you ever seen a music critic who can sing a song properly? We are not called to evaluate and criticize, but to sing of life. Regarding the sin that existed from the Garden of Eden and the evil that will continue in the future, what we must hold onto is the "gospel." The gospel is enough.

Popularity does not necessarily mean truth. Action movies are very, very entertaining, but no one rewatches them as masterpieces. What remains over time is not popularity, but life. Life is reborn when blood is shed.

Life is always full of misunderstandings. Among them, malicious rumors are unbearable. Such baseless rumors disappear in a few months. If I don't stir them up and explain them... The reason we shouldn't is that Jesus did not bear the cross because of such baseless rumors. Jesus' cross was for me, who am incomparably more wicked than those malicious rumors.
Life is not about accumulating, but about scattering and giving away. Giving away is making a path. Do not defend, but love. Jesus' life will flow along that path.

Whether you admit it or not, when you stand before the Lord, you are immediately a 'sinner.' Furthermore, it doesn't end there; the Lord calls such a wicked sinner a 'fisher of men' and personally leads and transforms them.

When you feel like you are insignificant, rejoice. Now is the time for the Lord to work. The beginning of the gospel is that there is no goodness or righteousness within us, and we must still hold onto that gospel today.

Please do not be troubled by wrong outcomes. Instead, the Lord does not condemn but heals the causes, wounds, anger, loneliness, and attachments behind wrong outcomes. He did not come to call the righteous, but sinners. If you were healthy, why would you need Jesus? Matthew 9:12
If you went to do business and returned with nothing left, you did excellent business. If you are on the path of faith, proclaiming only Jesus, and have nothing left, you are on the right path.

I see the unfortunate reality today that many believers are living lives without the power of Christ or life. This is because they lack the power of life. Why do they lack the power of life? Life comes from the power of resurrection, and there is no resurrection without the cross. If you refuse to bear the cross, the work of life will not happen.

In my 25 years of faith and ministry, I have seen many young people who were in heavenly grace but lost their faith and wandered. And I have heard the Lord's tearful cry for such young people. In a dormitory room at Seoul Theological Seminary in 1991 and at a prayer mountain in Gyeongnam in 1990. His heart has always been the same: "Did the world give you true happiness? I do not want to lose you."
My heart overflows. It is because the gospel of the cross is glorious. Because it is the power of God that brings salvation to everyone who believes, I am not ashamed to proclaim that gospel.
The reason yesterday's fear turns into my prayer and deep sighs turn into my song is that I have laid down all my burdens at the foot of the cross. Hallelujah~♥

I will proclaim and pray, and be a witness every day. From Hymn 505, today at work we will have Lesson 3, 'Invitation to Happiness,' and Luke 5:1-11, 'A Confession More Precious Than a Full Net.' Please pray that I may live a life of proclaiming, praying, and being a witness.

True faith is looking to the Lord in the midst of the storm. The reason proclaiming the cross faces intense resistance from darkness is that the gospel of the cross is the most precious and valuable thing. I hope you become servants of life who do not waver in your hearts and cling only to the cross.
Jesus, who washed the disciples' feet, told us to do the same for our brothers and sisters. It is not because their feet were dirty, but to wash away their sins and shortcomings.

It is not that Jesus washed the disciples' feet because their feet were dirty. Bathing signifies salvation, and washing feet signifies repentance for personal sins. Those who have received salvation do not need to discuss salvation again. It was the Lord's last ministry. Repentance is important.

It is meaningless to judge, evaluate, and predict by mixing imagination with fragmented information. Who liked whom, how is that person's child, what about that family's groom and in-laws... We are not called to circulate unsubstantiated (defective) information, but only to proclaim the gospel.

Liking and loving are different. Liking is what brings joy when you are together, while loving means sacrificing and expending for that person. Do not remain in liking; love. God loved with His Son, the Holy Spirit with companionship, and Jesus with the cross.

We cannot find ourselves in good cars or good houses. Possessing useless things or receiving envious glances does not change the value of our essence. Those things are all jewel boxes. But a jewel is a jewel even if it is wrapped in tissue paper. It is a jewel even if it falls into mud. That is why Paul said we have the jewel of Jesus in jars of clay. Do not prove; testify. You will be happy.

It is impossible for a sinful person to dwell in God and for that holy One to dwell in us. However, the only way is to be crucified with Him who died for me. When we are crucified with Jesus, an impossible union occurs.

Knowing that you are a sinner is the first gateway to the doctrine of the cross. But you must not stop there. If you lament, "What shall I do?" because you are a sinner, believe in Jesus, and your old self dies on the cross, and you are resurrected as a new person. This means that your essence is not changed by evaluation, but you become a completely new creature. By the Lord's grace.

The heart that wants to give but cannot is deep love. Many fools have much but cannot love. It is because they have no heart. You just need to feel compassion. Love heals. Love makes us believe. Love makes us hear. That is why, if you love, you are happy even if you have nothing. I hope all young people feel deep love and practice deep love.

The saying that the giver is more blessed than the receiver is the principle that what I give to others, I receive first. If you intend to show mercy, God will first show mercy to you, and only when it overflows can you give it to others. This also applies to wounds. To inflict a wound, that wound must first dominate you. What will you give and what will you receive?
How can I remain silent when the Lord has saved me?
I sow seeds according to the Lord's word, but it is God who makes them grow.

I held onto a fellow believer who was rapidly drifting away from the Lord and proclaimed the gospel. "You who were so moved and wept in God's love, saying you loved the Lord, why have you become like this?" My friend replied, "What has God done for me? He has blocked my life at every turn." From my perspective, what that friend desires is ambition and a desire for relationships not led by the Lord, or rather, the success they want. Poet Song Myung-hee one day writes a poem with the Lord's voice: How painful it must be for God's heart when humans complain of insufficiency, even though He gave His only Son. How painful it must be for the Lord's spirit when people deny and revile Him, even though He gave Himself.

We do not need to spend time contemplating the works of Satan and impurity when it is already difficult to draw near to the Lord. The Jews ask for signs, and the Greeks seek wisdom, but we proclaim and focus only on Jesus crucified. If you know Jesus, you already know and discern everything.

Since this life is only temporarily entrusted to us by the Lord, we must live it meaningfully. Humbly and passionately... Jesus gave Himself for us. He died for us to rescue us from all evil and make us pure, a people eager to do good works. - Titus 2:14
The Lord is the One who transforms moments of longing and distress into salvation at His level.
Proclaiming the Lord's gospel is not difficult. It is impossible. Fundamentally, it cannot be done without the Lord's guidance and help.

A person of the Lord can never be inherently selfish. This is because the gospel is entirely altruistic. Even when an apple tree bears apples, the tree does not eat them. Others eat and rejoice. However, the owner cherishes that tree greatly and cleanses it further.

When you acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty, you will see the Lord's work. God, who gave His Son through the cross, gives us precious people to love and be loved by, work to serve, and a longing heart that I cannot even understand. That is why we offer our bodies.
Serving the people the Lord has entrusted to us is pouring out sweat, tears, and blood. Then many seeds will return to the Lord.
Being called by the Lord is infinitely precious in itself. It is immensely glorious to live with faith alongside precious people. Let us glorify the gospel.
Though Sunday ministry may seem over, it is not. We continue to seek the Lord, and to share and be witnesses without stopping. We will go as far as we can.

To call the unimportant important and the important unimportant is falsehood and error. In such places, the important thing is truth. The authority lies in not diluting the 'original power' of Jesus' cross. Holding onto the potent original (the cross), not a watered-down copy, is God's ace in this dark world. For me, there is nothing to boast about except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. <Galatians 6:14>

The reason I have been allowed to proclaim the Lord's name, despite my unworthiness, is due to His burning compassion. If I am allowed to proclaim that most exalted name tomorrow as well, it will be due to His boundless grace.

Deep love makes the walls of distance and time fade away. It is because of Jesus that what should be broken becomes a meaning that connects. Who shall separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus?

Because it's youth ministry, I try to be a bit friendly when I preach. I don't think being serious and being cheerful are opposites. However, the problem is that I clearly think it's humor, but I see many friends crying when I preach. It seems the Lord is not using my jokes. Instead, I see Him working directly. Whatever the means, Christ is proclaimed, so I will rejoice and be glad.

It is not a hardship but an extreme honor for a soccer player to run on the field without resting on the bench. I do not consider my life valuable to myself, if only I may finish my race and the ministry which I received from the Lord Jesus, to testify the gospel of the grace of God. Acts 20:24

They say even a whale dances when praised, right? If you want to see a miracle greater than a whale dancing, give yourself and love. In His prayer the night before His crucifixion, Jesus asked that they also be one, just as He and the Father are one. If we have become one through loving and forgiving, the Lord's miracle has already begun.

Quo vadis Domine, Lord, where are you going? This phrase appears twice. Once to the Lord carrying the cross, and another time, though not in scripture, it was asked to the Lord carrying the cross who appeared before Peter as he fled from Roman persecution. Jesus took all our sins through the great surgery of the cross and poured out God's grace. There is nothing more we can do for Him. Because the redemption was perfect through the cross alone.

Christians are people who cannot be dealt with. They cannot be swayed by money, honor, greed, or lust. This is because the Lord has already finished all dealings on the cross. I have died with the Lord, and I live again in the Lord.

The holy fellowship of Christians is not like salesmanship that tries to please everyone. It is walking in truth in the Holy Spirit. To some, it is the aroma of death, and to others, it is the fragrance of Christ that gives life. Share, pray, and be a witness every day.

The prodigal son lives among pigs. However, the fundamental difference between the prodigal son and the pigs is that the prodigal son has a father to return to, a father to call. Christians can experience the worst despair. Nevertheless, the truth declares, 'There is therefore now no condemnation to those who are in Christ Jesus.'
Without weeping and transformation, we will be cast out and trampled. Jesus died for my sins, God's love was confirmed by the cross, and the Holy Spirit dwells within us.

The gospel Paul truly wanted to convey to the Roman believers, in today's terms, would be like this: At a reunion after a long time, standing next to a classmate bragging that her husband's company is in the top 100, or next to a classmate congratulated by everyone because her husband entered politics and became a minister, or next to a classmate beaming because her son or daughter graduated from a prestigious university and received a doctorate from Harvard, or next to a seminary wife thanking God because her husband became the senior pastor of a large church, to publicly boast that you have staked your entire life on Jesus who died on the cross. There is nothing to boast about except the cross of Christ.
If we do not stand before God in our honest state, as sinners and the sick, we cannot meet Jesus. - Mark 2:17

Those who do not have regular fellowship with God are, for the time being, all living as slaves to their flesh. If you receive the gospel as life and fully rely on the power of His blood, it becomes much easier to overcome sin.

When God called me, He knew I was foolish and ignorant, but He did not despise me; instead, He transformed me with wisdom and power. This is so that I may be complete in Jesus, be clothed in Christ, manifest Christ, and live by Christ. "But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, to fulfill its lusts." - Romans 13:14.

God gives unimaginable conflict and anguish to those He dearly loves. ㅠㅠ This is because He wants us to completely let go of everything, look only to the Lord, and hold onto His word. Be of good cheer. I have overcome the world. Jesus has overcome the world.

Catching flies while garbage piles up in the living room is not a fundamental solution. Covering it with a nice blanket or spraying perfume is not a solution either. The garbage must be removed (repentance) and filled with something precious (Jesus). Do not strive to eliminate the consequences of sin (insects); instead, be filled with the good (Jesus).
I wish to be a companion on the journey where God's love and grace become life.
There are those who have received God's grace but abuse it in vain. What is the response to the abuse? It is not punishment and law, but overflowing grace.
God's grace and love do not allow us to be hurt. To be hurt often, well, and more excellently than others is because the unpayable love has become exceptionally thin.
"Behold, I have come to do your will, O God, as it is written of me in the scroll of the book." - Hebrews 10:7

Jesus received all authority in heaven and on earth, and through the cross, He reclaimed all the rights that Adam lost. However, since the Lord is at the right hand of the throne of majesty, we, His body, must naturally exercise dominion and rule in this land. Rise up boldly and exercise the power to reign in life through Jesus. When I call, the Father is bound to answer. That is the covenant, that is the promise, and that is the power of the gospel.

Jesus brought all the blessings of heaven. When His body was broken, the blessings of heaven poured out. The sign of the Son of God piercing the darkness and destroying the works of the devil was His humility, wrapped in swaddling clothes and lying in a manger. The sign by which Christians overcome the world and disarm the devil is humility.

The ability to not stop is power. Instead of focusing on the many reasons to stop, we simply run towards the goal. This is because the goal is Jesus, who did not stop from His heavenly throne to sinful me.
Every soul I encounter stands precariously at a critical moment. I strongly feel the need for the gospel and prayer. And I see them being restored.

When you feel utterly insufficient, look to the cross. It will give you the strength to start again. You must descend to rise again. You must be emptied to fly again. When you hit rock bottom, you can rise again.

The only reason for becoming utterly insufficient is the fact that only the Lord can save us and save our limbs. Please be humble and lower yourselves so that all may reach salvation and forgiveness of sins.

One piece of good news is that the Lord does not condemn us. However, He also does not want us to remain in that sin and the sickness of our souls. Do not be a spectator to a fire across the river; you must be renewed. Jesus said, "Neither do I condemn you; go, and sin no more." - John 8:11
This is Brother Hanseok's last visit to Geumcheol with tears. He returns tomorrow. My last words were, 'Jesus saved you!' There is nothing but Jesus. Only Jesus...
Core values, where passion and effort are focused, we run without stopping for the gospel.
I did not believe in Jesus to be happy. Rather, I had to give up and abandon many things. But I was truly happy. Because after losing, the true value became clearer. There is no one but Jesus.

When the hairstyle changes, it becomes a completely different person. Among facial features, the eyes occupy 90%. However, such artificial changes quickly become tiresome. True change is an evaluation where other values become trash due to the gospel and the preciousness of Jesus.

The taste of a persimmon cannot be imitated, nor can it be forced. If I explain the taste of a persimmon that I haven't tasted and cannot give, and then pressure someone by asking, "Why can't you feel the taste?", they have to act forcefully and live tired and listless lives. Even though they haven't experienced the taste because they haven't received the supply, they blame themselves and fall into condemnation. Just as the taste cannot be imitated, so is the gospel. Clearly, when life is supplied, the game is over. We cannot help speaking about what we have seen and heard - Acts 4:20 https://youtu.be/WzJad3R29r8

If what is lost becomes what is gained, and even death cannot take away my happiness and excitement, it is because the one living within me is not 'me' but 'Jesus Christ'. I hope you will lose it simply. You will gain the Lord.

Repentance is a change of direction, turning back, and abandoning self-reliance. The essence of repentance is the same, but there is repentance that obtains salvation and repentance that restores the joy of salvation. When I repent not for success and achievement in the dark land where I stand, but for repentance, the Lord is glorified, and it becomes a holy land covered by His blood. If you repent, believe in Jesus (be baptized), and receive forgiveness of sins, you will receive the Holy Spirit as a gift. - Sunday Leader Meeting Summary

In all his epistles, Paul frequently uses the expression 'to pursue'. Personally, this arises because there are two things: direction and passion. When the direction of Jesus and passion are combined, you cannot help but pursue. Also, considering everything as loss because the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord is most excellent... I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which Christ Jesus has called me upward. Philippians 3:8-12 If you know Jesus, you will run. It's that simple, right?
On very difficult days, rather than asking the reason for the hardship, it is enough to know that we can return to Jesus and that He embraces us just as we are. Jesus has given us everything.

"For he whom God has sent utters the words of God, since he gives the Spirit without measure." - John 3:34 What is needed now is not earthly logic but the word of God, and what I need is the Holy Spirit poured out without limit.

The worst thing to do is to advertise and sell a product you are not confident in. There is nothing worse than proclaiming Jesus without being filled with Him. Therefore, being filled with Jesus is the beginning of missions.
My fullness is missions. It starts from there. We must be filled with the fire that can save perishing souls. At the center of the problem is me.

The Apostle Paul, while in prison, had an opportunity to be released, but he turned that opportunity into a chance to preach the gospel before Caesar in Rome. (Acts 25) As he sailed toward Rome, adverse winds blew. Those who live by the gospel face powerful hellish winds. However, when the wind of the Spirit blows, everything will be devastated. "The winds were against us" (Acts 27:4)

"Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid." (John 14:27). Only Jesus can give fundamental peace. This is because only Jesus washes away sins with His blood and reconciles us with God. Those who do not fear God and are not reconciled with Him should naturally be anxious. We should not call a disease without symptoms a blessing. We must pray that those who have left God can never sleep soundly.

* Self-diagnosis Instead of 'Who am I?', we should look at 'What the Lord did on the cross.' Instead of 'What have I done?', we should focus more on 'Who is He?'. Those who focus on 'themselves' encounter problems in 'relationships'. They become aggressive due to inferiority complex or avoid due to a victim mentality. If the lens is defective, the picture of the subject will be distorted.

There is no other gospel. Especially in the last days, people want to receive special, miraculous, and immediately working secret methods. There were none, and there can be none. There is only the gospel of the cross. The gospel that faith that moves mountains and the words of angels ought to testify is Jesus who was crucified. (Galatians 1:6-8) I am astonished that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you to the grace of Christ and are turning to a different gospel— which is really no gospel at all. Evidently some people are throwing your community into confusion and wanting to pervert the gospel of Christ. But even if we or an angel from heaven should preach a gospel other than the one we preached to you, let them be under God's curse!
The greatest work, bearing the cross.

I ignored the Lord's call several times until I was in my teens, but one day I met the Lord and returned. The reason I returned was that I, a sinner, had crucified Jesus, yet He loved me and died on the cross for me. Even at my young age, I knew this was precious. I intuitively knew that there would be many challenges in holding onto that truth. However, at the moment of my conversion, I confessed that I did not want to do that. 'I will follow only the Lord.'
When your heart is dark and troubled, please think of the Lord. The Lord, who had nowhere to lay His head, bore our suffering in our place.
* The Price of Love To love, you must pay a price. Therefore, when you truly love, you face various challenges. Just as Jesus came to this earth.
Tears that only the saved can shed: gratitude and repentance.
Nevertheless, healing must continue, the cross must be held onto, we must continue to look only to the Lord, and the word of the gospel must be constantly proclaimed.

Preaching the word is a gift. Those who have received a gift do it exceptionally well. Just as someone with the gift of music plays music well. However, the Lord does not praise someone for preaching the word well. It is a gift. Ability and eloquent speech do not guarantee a life of obedience to the word, or who becomes the master when you are alone. For even when I preach the gospel, I cannot boast about it... What then is my reward? That when I preach the gospel, I may offer it free of charge, not availing myself of my right in the gospel. 1 Corinthians 9:16-18
When the word flows, we live. Therefore, we must pray and strive for the word to flow. When it flows in and flows out, the work of life occurs.

Following the pillar of cloud is not a fanciful story. The Exodus and entry into Canaan are not speculative investments in a bad land. It is His true grace, the cross, the grace of the cross where He nailed Himself and me to a tree to be one with sinners. Therefore, the journey of faith is not the achievement of any status or state, but the training of holiness, presence, companionship, and fellowship.

The reason prayer does not come out when you want to pray is because you have forgotten. You must remember. Only then will prayer be opened. And when he had taken a cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave it to them, saying, "Drink from it, all of you. This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins. I tell you, I will not drink again of this fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it anew with you in my Father's kingdom." - Matthew 26:27-29 Remember and do not forget the Lord, His death, and His coming again.

A few things that praise me, I mistakenly think that my strengths, which I find commendable, hold me up this much. No. Rather, it is the things I have dismissed as insignificant, the things I have confessed as truly weak, that hold me up. (2 Corinthians 12:10) Therefore, I will gladly boast about my weaknesses, that the power of Christ may rest on me. That is why, for Christ's sake, I delight in weaknesses, in insults, in hardships, in persecutions, in difficulties. For when I am weak, then I am strong.

That I fall into sin proves something. It is the surest proof that I have lived by my own strength. If my will, my strength is strong, I will eventually fall a little later. Do not try to delay failure; instead, acknowledge your downfall through the cross and accept living with Jesus.

That I fall into sin proves something. It is the surest proof that I have lived by my own strength. If my will, my strength is strong, I will eventually fall a little later. Do not try to delay failure; instead, acknowledge your downfall through the cross and accept living with Jesus.

Everything I receive, visible and invisible (whether tangible or intangible), is given by the Lord. These are things He gives because He loves me. On Christmas Eve, I hope you will be thankful, even if only for two minutes. To the Father who gave His only begotten Son, who is in the bosom of glory, for me, a sinner.

Those who come to make a deal, bargain, or struggle become tense. Because they have to get more. But those who come to love are at peace. Because their initial purpose is to give generously and even lose. The Lord gave His life, but He only obeyed by carrying the cross and dying without any other reward. The reason power and authority come to those who discard greed and consume everything without leaving anything behind, leaving only their glasses case and the clothes they wore, is because they are at peace.
