When one value is superior, other things become mere waste, and one feels deep pity for those who cling to that waste.
Catch up me if you can. Ideas and methods can be copied, but passion cannot. Ministry can be imitated, but dying for souls cannot be immediately imitated.
What God desires is not plastic surgery. "O man, what is good; and what does the Lord require of you, but to do justice, and to love kindness, and to walk humbly with your God?" Micah 6:8

Human joy, God's joy. Even if you seek human joy in the church, you are not a servant of God. Even if you seek God's joy in the world, you are a servant of God. What moves you is your master. If it is not the Lord that moves you, then such idols must be broken and burned. Until they no longer tempt me...
The reason for losing the cross. It is because there are many things to do. Especially, there are many useless things. It is enough to do two or three things, or even just one.
The scariest movie. A person of God appears, but without love.

Only when you love can you truly hear. At the end of Jesus' ministry, facing a moment of crisis, the words needed were not the assurance of help, "I will help you." It was the assurance of love, "This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased." In a crisis, the most needed thing is the assurance of love.

True holiness. It is a great mistake to think that it is about praying a little and reading the Bible. True holiness is a change in the object of one's desire. Often, people think that if the method of desire is Christian, it is holiness, rather than the object of desire.
The reason for boldness. It is because I know what I want, and I also know what I can afford to lose. Those who long for eternity cannot be stopped by anything.

When one cannot be humble. If one looks down on and hates others because they don't have much or didn't attend a famous school, it is because they have much internal inferiority, with no other way to prove their own worth than by criticizing and comparing weaknesses. People tend to vehemently deny, criticize, and impute their own weaknesses when they discover them in others.

True investment. What the world so desperately craves are things that cannot ultimately be held onto until the end. However, on the other hand, there are things that can never be let go of. A person who lays down fleeting pleasures, self-satisfaction, and material possessions for the sake of what can never be let go of is by no means a foolish person.
The reason for not being drunk. It is not to obtain salvation. It is because salvation has already been obtained, so it is no longer needed.

What you desire determines quality. If all you desire is this life, you are truly pitiable, for it will all pass away. Long for what is eternal. Only Jesus and what is given to Jesus are eternal. If in Christ our hope is only for this life, then we are of all people most to be pitied. - 1 Corinthians 15:19
If you cannot let go, you will miss what is important. Good faith means abandoning lesser desires before the Lord. When you focus simply on the Lord, sin decreases and holy influence strengthens.
The reality of an angry person. An angry person is not particularly looking into the apple of the Lord's eye. For one fixed on the Lord does not react to the desires of the self.

Absolute happiness. Paul says it is beneficial even if there is loss, because the original purpose was not to gain. If Christ, who lives within me according to the original purpose, is the one, then loss does not become pity, and gain does not become pride. "For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain." Philippians 1:21
The present. You are not being ignored for the welcome and gifts of people, but you are a person waiting for a truly precious gift. You do not need "a present," you need "The present."

The best gift and a plot twist. If friends remembered my birthday and sang "Happy Birthday" unexpectedly, I would be touched. But if shortly after, I blessed someone celebrating their birthday on the same day with a beautiful cake and candles, it would immediately turn into hell. (Comparing) My heart would be so hurt, I'd rush to the restroom, tears welling up, when a brother as handsome as Jung Woo-sung slips a ring into my hand so only I can see. (Identity) The tears that were welling up would turn into a storm. Because I would experience heaven. (Happiness) Happiness is not about quantity, but quality. Do not expect many superficial, soulless welcomes; instead, expect the Lord who is truly important to me.
Hypocrisy. It is like boasting of the eternal, but inserting and packaging one's own decay within it.

The opposite of faithfulness is not laziness. The opposite of faithfulness is trying to do many things well on your own. God is the one who makes things excellent, so when He calls us, He expects us to be faithful and loyal. Adam tried to become like God, Saul tried to be recognized by people, and Israel wanted a quick settlement in Canaan. When you try to be recognized by doing well, much worry pierces you.

If you boast of vain things, you will succumb to emptiness. The irony is that you fear what you boast about so much. If you fear it, you serve it. If you boast of vain things, you will eventually be unable to bear the disappointment and fear, and your heart and life will crumble. You should fear only the Lord.

The burning bush is not consumed. The fire was on the burning bush, but it did not consume it. God does not use us as a source. He simply wants to be with us and reveal His glory Himself. Therefore, when we take off our sandals of rights and doubts before the Lord, God's holiness and glory are revealed.

Prophesy. 1. My small flutter of wings becomes a tsunami in heaven, covering the world. Do not be discouraged. 2. The One who gave me femininity and charm is God, and when I believe that, I become most peaceful and beautiful. Do not console yourself by calling it a humble wish; instead, long for the most beautiful wish God gives you. 3. My weakness does not cancel God's promise. Do not know yourself through others; if you see yourself through the Lord's eyes, you do not have to hide in a cave. 4. The God of peace, who led me to Him through pain, makes me comfort those whose hearts are broken. 5. The 'leisure' you have now is to be thankful and to serve. It becomes the most glorious life where the community is blessed because of me. 6. What I am is by God's grace and guidance, and the Lord's intention is peace, not disaster. Do not be impatient; entrust it. In the collapse where there is no answer, ask, "Holy Spirit, descend."
The core. To bear good fruit, you just need to be a good tree. That is the most important thing.
He notices the insignificant me. Even among the crowds, even among VIPs, the Lord notices the insignificant me. Because God is interested in the anointed.

It is not a choice, but a lifeline. Gratitude is not a generous option to be exercised when you feel like it. It is a lifeline; if you don't, you die. The moment you miss gratitude and complain, you cannot see the truth and your heart is stolen by lies. Gratitude is the perspective that makes God God and makes me me.

The recovery of a struggling father. No matter how difficult family support is or how challenging company work is, a bright child's words, "Dad, I love you," can make all worries disappear and allow him to stand up again. What cannot be denied is the unfeigned confession of these young people that they love Jesus. I think this is enough.

A person who lives not by self, but only by Jesus. It is not that there are no conflicts in life. Having conflict is not evil. However, it is evil for a servant who should seek God's pleasure to seek human pleasure. "Whether then we eat, or drink, or whatsoever ye do, do it unto the glory of God. Give none offence, neither to the Jews, nor to the Gentiles, nor to the church of God: Even as I please all men in all things, not seeking mine own profit, but the profit of many, that they may be saved." - 1 Corinthians 10:31-33 (Note: The provided Korean text seems to be a misquote or a paraphrase of Galatians 1:10, which is about not pleasing men. The English translation provided here is a common interpretation of the sentiment.)

The reason, not the method. We cling to "How," but God asks "Why." We must honestly ask ourselves why we are doing it, why we must do it, why we are happy, why we are hurt. Because behind that reason, we know who is king and who is master. I truly hope that God alone is the reason and motive. "For none of us lives to himself alone and none of us dies to himself alone. The life that we live, we live to the Lord, and the death that we die, we die to the Lord. So whether we live or die, we belong to the Lord." - Romans 14:8
Grace. Just as what I am is by grace, so is my obedience. It is not by me, but by the Lord's grace.

If you look at the motive, you won't be deceived. Looking at the outward appearance is sensory; looking at the results is logical. But looking at the motive is love. Therefore, God looks at the motive. "But the Lord said to Samuel, 'Do not consider his appearance or his height, for I have rejected him. The Lord does not look at the things people look at. People look at the outward appearance, but the Lord looks at the heart.'" - 1 Samuel 16:7
The most important thing. You must ask. If you ask for the Lord's face, you have asked for the most important thing.
Why suffering is suffering. Because you are not walking your own path, walking becomes suffering. If you set a clear goal and adjust your course, it will not be difficult, but enjoyable.
The reason for repeated stumbling. It is because you harbor a sliver of hope that you can be happy through people and glance at vain things. If the goal is clear, the path will be brightly visible.
Lions do not eat rotten meat. Do not be satisfied with decay. Have unashamed expectations and hopes; let Jesus be glorified within me. There will be a joy that even death cannot stop.
Who knows? Ultimately, it is God who knows me and calls me. Only He knows me and calls me. Those who respond to that calling will shine like the stars in the sky.

If you dislike rice, you can eat ramen; if you dislike that, you can eat bread. However, if you become too lazy to breathe and bored with your heart beating, that is a truly serious problem that needs to be resolved. The symptoms themselves, like breathing and heartbeat, are not problems that can be covered up with something else. A baby gains the ability to walk even a few steps after falling thousands of times. If a baby is given a wheelchair as an alternative because walking is too difficult, such a person deserves to be cursed. It should be a structure where those whose spirits are alive are moved, and those who are not are pitied. Conversely, if those who are moved are restricted and cynical people gain power, the church suffocates. There are things to compromise on and things not to. If a baby cannot drink milk, juice should not be an alternative.
When you want to defend yourself, when you want to make excuses, please just remain silent. Just as Jesus entrusted His soul to the Father and was completely silent.

Do not live a life of defense, but a life of dying on the cross. Saul lived a life of defense and destroyed himself, but David lived a life of surrender and became the good shepherd of Israel. When you give up protecting yourself, the Lord begins to protect you.
When betrayed, insulted, or treated unfairly, it hurts. But what hurts more is when we do not look directly at the Lord. When such anguish comes, confess this: "I am a person who loves the Lord."
In the hearts of the people, there was King Saul. But in God's heart, there was David. Do not seek to be supported; instead, long to receive grace.

There are things we should let go of but cannot. Pride, greed, lust, envy, jealousy, inferiority complex, anger. These are ingrained throughout life, have a strange charm, and parasitize the flesh, remaining unreleased. However, before the knowledge of Jesus, all become refuse. Refuse is bound to be discarded. If you hold only onto Jesus, faith becomes not 'asceticism' but 'romance.'
Going to Virginia Water is not mandatory, so feel free to do so according to your own schedule without feeling burdened. You have already carried many yokes in the world, so I hope you will be free.
When you stand before the Word as if naked, you experience the Bible not as just a thick book, but as 'God' Himself.

Even if criminals gather, there is truth there, and even if only saints gather, there can be pettiness. Cutting in line at church is evil, but caring for new families is good, and encouraging and building up brothers and sisters is good, but it is evil if others feel excluded through it. Good faith is not extreme. That is why we are not judges. Only the Lord judges.

Peter, realizing he was a sinner before the Lord, asked Him to leave. But Jesus stayed with him instead. Even in the disappointing moment when his top disciple denied Him, and in his weakened body at the end of his life, He never left. The Lord does not leave us. Never.

* A sufficiently happy way. Being misunderstood while quietly serving is much happier than being welcomed, thinking you are Santa Claus while trying to steal. This is because happiness comes from existence, not from reaction.

Walking alone through a starless winter forest, with a thirst for a long journey and poverty, yet still happy, is because of a beautiful star buried in my heart. Do you have that one beautiful star that turns all problems into mere waste and allows you to overcome abundantly in the sea of life? I understand the friends who came to the Saturday meeting.
The characteristic of a sick person is that they try hard to prove themselves and always try to condemn. When afflicted with the deadly disease of sin, one inevitably becomes self-centered.
It is foolish to boast when you go to the hospital. All patients are just patients. If we all stand before the Lord as people who need healing, there will be no pride or comparison.

The reason for going to the hospital is not to do what you want, but to cure an evil disease and save your life, even at the cost of pain. The reason for coming to the Lord's community is to be cut down and humbled so that the Lord's life may abound. It is not a place where the stories you want to hear and the situations you want to see are staged, but a place where you repent, shed tears, humble yourself, and turn to the Lord.
* Card of unknown sender. Regarding the thank-you note in the Christmas card, I reflect, 'Is this really fitting for me?' but it feels awkward and doesn't suit me. It is simply grace.

Potiphar's wife instigated his wife to put Joseph in prison to harm him. However, even if it wasn't Potiphar's wife, Joseph would have gone to prison. The people and circumstances that tried to harm me ultimately could not harm me, and they even helped fulfill God's providence. By the grace of God I am what I am.

* Following greed leads to anxiety. To satisfy greed, you will be anxious. This is because you are stealing what was not given to you. If you try to fulfill your calling, you will be joyful. This is because you are pleasing the One who gave you the calling.

Even if a jewel falls into the mud, it does not tarnish the jewel's value. A person with a strong essence is not shaken by any attack. No matter how much you attack a diamond with a piece of glass, the diamond does not become glass. You are a jewel born of the Lord.

* What is good is not necessarily good. There is a saying that what is good is good. Following this saying can be convenient, avoid criticism, and evade relational risks. However, what is good is not good. What is right is good. To hold onto what is right, you may not be able to conform and may become an outcast. But the Lord is with those who do what is right.
Even seemingly insignificant talents, if continuously pursued, will be used as great abilities. Persistence is faithfulness.

A forced change in outward appearance without a change in essence is called 'hypocrisy.' Change your inner self. Then you will serve the Lord in a transformed manner without external pressure. There is pure gold, but there is also gold plating. I hope you will have a faith of pure gold, not a plated faith.
Becoming His servant is easy enough, but being one can be very rough.

I see young people confessing that clinking glasses, dazzling lights, and intense beats no longer satisfy them. Congratulations. You have experienced God's glory. Now, you will always be hungry and thirsty for other things.
If your parents run a mart, you don't worry about snacks. If your parents are billionaires, you don't worry about money for school supplies. A person whose Father is God does not fear the future.
I will do it until the day God tells me to stand, even if I am broken.
* The reason for looking at the Lord and feeling sad is that you have not looked only at the Lord.

* The reason it is difficult is that you try too hard to do well. However, that area was not given to you. What was given to you is the realm of faithfulness. Faithfulness is keeping your place. To those who are faithful, the grace to do well and succeed is also given.

Clearly, there were countless women at the ball showcasing their utmost beauty, but in the prince's eyes, only Cinderella was visible. The ability to live in the world yet look only to Jesus, the ability of sight, comes to those who have deeply experienced the Lord's beauty.

The ability to discern. The most basic thing is that ability recognizes ability. Elisha's excellence did not start from doing great things, but from recognizing Elijah. The most fundamental beginning starts from respecting those who are worthy of respect. Discernment is ability.

* Being called and being used. All are called, but not all respond. And not all who respond are used. To be used as instruments of righteousness in God's kingdom, there must be a redirection of self-denial and self-renunciation. Carrying the cross without self-denial...? There is no such falsehood.

* Calling. My task is not to get the most votes in a popularity contest. That is why I was not called to be a celebrity. Carry contempt, neglect, and the cross. The Lord alone should receive honor, glory, and all authority.

* Deviating from the purpose is corruption. I hope you will hold onto the original purpose for which you were called. If you know the reason, hold onto it, and strive to achieve it, you will mature, become glorious, and gain life. Fulfilling what you were called to do is your calling.

* Do not be conscious of people. If you are conscious of people, you will 'act hypocritically,' but if you are conscious of God, you will be 'faithful.' If I become conscious of God, people will also become conscious of God.

* If the foundation of your life is possessions, pride, or relationships, it will shake. But if it is the Lord, you will not be shaken even if other losses come. Trials will show you what you have lived based on. Stand on the rock. The Lord gave, and the Lord has taken away; blessed be the name of the Lord. - Job 1:21

If you are uncomfortable but do not change, you will remain in the same place. Even if you avoid people you are uncomfortable with and don't want to face, you will meet other similar people. This is because your spiritual level is fixed. If you know God and have experienced Him, but do not obey at critical moments, such awkward relationships will not cease. If you have gloomy and upsetting relationships, the core of the problem is your un-dead ego. Even an earthworm squirms when stepped on. This is because it was not stepped on properly. I cannot kill my ego by myself. The only way is to invite Jesus to the throne of my life. Then Jesus will carry me, who is troubled, to the cross. There, you can feel His love flowing towards you.
Do not be disheartened by comparison, but count the grace you have received. What you have received and enjoyed to this point is God's boundless grace.

To live a life without comparison, you must discard the wrong plumb line of the heart. I am a person who lives by receiving answers. It is entirely God's prerogative to exalt and humble people. Trials come to dismantle all emotions that do not originate from God. They come by God's permission and providence. God dismantles everything and rebuilds us to heal us. - From Saturday Service

Whether I am criticized or praised, it is difficult to say that it is my essence. Conversely, the judgments I make about others are the same. We know in part and speak in part. Only in the Lord do I know who I am. I am loved by God and cleansed by the blood of His Son.

Even more difficult than criticism is praise. Many valiant warriors of faith have fallen there. That is why the graves of many servants of God are lined up before the course of praise and flattery. Beginners struggle to overcome criticism, but masters overcome praise. "Stop regarding man, whose breath of life is in his nostrils; For why should he be esteemed?" - Isaiah 2:22
Though I may be foolish, I am not so foolish as to exchange the glory of the Lord for corruptible glory. If you know the Lord, you know what is truly important, even if you know nothing else.

* Attitude is more important than problems. When facing situations with a human heart, we try to prove ourselves, which leads to hurting and being hurt. This is because we do not know the object of the fight, the method of the fight, or God's intention. Our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, authorities, and the powers of this dark world and against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly realms. - Ephesians 6:12
Ambition disguised as vision and calculated kindness... that is Absalom.

If you are criticized, admit it. The more you see your own shortcomings and weaknesses, the more you should say you need the Lord. We are beings who need the Lord's grace with every beat of our hearts.

I came with empty hands, and I will leave with empty hands. I will only take with me what I have loved with tears and labor. It is foolish to risk your life for things you will not even remember later.
It is not important how fast you run, but for whom you run. If the Lord has given you talent, it can be most beautiful or most futile when deciding for whom you use that talent.

Do not be intimidated by the dazzling power, money, and beauty of the world. Pharaoh held a scepter made of gold and jewels, but it was shattered before Moses' dry staff. The power God gives is not always adorned, but it is incomparably glorious, far more so than Gandalf's staff. It is not the material that matters, but who holds it.

It is correct that we have no control over events. However, we can control our own reactions. If you react with the Holy Spirit, you are doing very well. If you approach events and people with love, joy, peace, patience, and self-control, you get 100 points. If you are reacting instinctively and maintain the emotional state determined immediately after the event, you are at 0 points and continue to stay at 0 points. If you keep getting the same wrong problem wrong on the next test, the teacher will continue to give you that problem.
* A beautiful life begins. Everyone wants to be beautiful. It is like a wedding. However, it is truly beautiful when the choices of the process and the outcome are beautiful.
The Apostle Paul suffered for the Lord and died for the Lord. This is because he chose what was most important. What is important to you right now?

The Apostle Paul's spirit: I am not very concerned about what people think of me. I don't even feel the need to blame myself or fall into self-pity. This is because the true evaluation will be made by God on that day. "But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged by you or by a human court. I do not even judge myself. For though I am not aware of anything against myself, yet I am not thereby justified. But he who judges me is the Lord." - 1 Corinthians 4:3,4
* The real problem: In front of God, problems are not real problems. The real problem is my attitude towards the problems.
Do not stand before people, but stand before God. Then you will become a noble person.
* The need for & the right to be angry: If I am not wrong, there is no need to be angry, and if I am wrong, I have no right to be angry.

Living as a supporting character by observing people's expressions is not the life God designed. In your own life, only the Lord is the Lord, and you live as His masterpiece. The world's concern is popularity and recognition, but the Lord's concern is 'relationship.'
Do not be heartbroken by people and circumstances, but rather desire that God shares your wounded heart over the things that hurt you. Our purpose is not people and circumstances.
It is not important whether you are recognized by people, but whether you are being used by the Lord.
Meetings where people wear masks and act in plays cannot lead to eternity, because plays eventually end. A good meeting is one where I become most myself.
* Strong love: A mother becomes strong. This is because she protects and nurtures life with love. Become strong. And love.

While people live in an era of infinite competition, living by the rules of the jungle, devouring and being devoured, Christians live by bearing fruit. That is the purpose of life and what He intended.

People long for worldly dreams. That is a tragedy. A dream should not be to have your heart slowly harden while surrounded by good environments, wealth, comfort, and guaranteed security. Is the death of Stephen and the devotion of Paul a tragedy? They lived not a tragedy, but glory, by dedicating their entire lives to ensuring that the name of Jesus Christ would be praised among people.
* The basis of joy: If it is about me and myself, it would be very sad, but if it is about Him and Himself, it would be joyful.
My sincere confession to the Lord is more important than people's imperfect evaluations.
Do not become a leader among people, but rather become a servant of the Lord.

The suffering assigned to people is constant. The law of the conservation of suffering. If the suffering is the same, what determines life is the love with which one responds to suffering. The amount of love for the Lord varies for each person.
Being misunderstood by people is not very important. If I do not misunderstand to the end, the devil will go away empty-handed, and we will gain one more child of God. Overcome evil with good.
People are bound to have flaws, and trying to forcefully hide them makes one a stifled person. Flaws in life can make us free and relaxed, and can also make us approachable.

People lie for their hidden desires. They deceive others, deceive themselves, and even try to deceive God. When we reveal ourselves with sincerity, we see our true selves, and begin to distinguish what we should ask for and what we should let go of.
People become like what they boast about.
Do not fear not being a joy to people, but strive to be a joy to the Lord. Jesus endured the cross for the joy set before him, and sat down at the right hand of God.
Human life is crumpled by the pursuit of self. The ironing that can unfold it is 'obedience'.
Whatever a person sows, they will reap. This means the result is the same whether you sow where others can see or where they cannot. Who are you when you are alone?
When a person pours their soul into a work, a masterpiece emerges. When God's Spirit is poured into a person's body, it becomes a masterpiece of God's level. You are God's Masterpiece.
If you have sorrow and pain in your heart because you are not loved, you will have little happiness in your life. Love is given by the Lord, and I love and serve with that love.

* Living a faith life in fellowship with the Lord means living with the Lord as our guest. The reason we live as we please is because we do not know Jesus well. Those who live with Jesus as their guest cannot live according to their own will and desires.
When you fail in ministry, you can truly know whom you have pursued. It becomes clear for whom you have sought honor, glory, and all power.

Do not rejoice in ministry; do not rejoice in successful ministry and visible results, but rejoice in eternal relationships and love. Rather than rejoicing that we were able to do something for God, we should rejoice more in what God has done for us. However, do not rejoice that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven. - Luke 10:20

Ministry is not a livelihood. When the Lord says to stop, that is the extent of your calling. The 'me' that I evaluate, and the 'me' that others evaluate, offer no justification. Only He knows my path and sets the time. Moses, who completed his mission but did not enter Canaan, was a truly magnificent person.
After his victory, Saul erected his memorial stone at Mount Carmel, but Samuel set up Ebenezer (The Lord helped us this far) between Mizpah and Sen. Whom you face tells the whole story of your life.

The most effective way to resist Satan is to stay in your place. Because it is a fallen spirit that originally fell by not staying in its place, it fears believers who faithfully keep their place. No matter how splendid or enviable a position may be, if it is not the place given to me, it is far better to just go home and eat ramen. Also, the angels who did not keep their own position but left their proper dwelling have been kept in eternal chains in darkness until the judgment of the great day. - Jude 1:6

In life, there are often times when we strongly desire to please people's hearts. In the Kingdom of God, the main player is a person after His own heart. We need a time like this, a time that needs people like David, who are trusted to fulfill the Lord's will by being pleasing to His heart, and who, despite enduring great hardship, ultimately abandon their own will and self-centered thinking to fulfill God's will.
* How to change the world Do what I must do and entrust what the Lord must do. What I must do is repent and be transformed. Revival begins from my spirit.
You are the reason for my life. You are the song of my life. With all my heart.
Samson was physically strong but morally weak. We too have times when we are physically strong and morally weak. This is during our youth. Strength without self-control leads to ruin.
When you love the other person, you can see the true you. But when you hate, you only see a fierce, wounded person.
When there is no understanding of the other person, people can become extremely cruel. This is especially true towards themselves.

Wounds hurt our hearts. A wounded heart, an unhealthy heart, bears the fruit of sin. Try to recall your memories. When do you become weak towards sin? Is it not when you are angry, hurt, lonely, or caught up in wrong emotions?

* Those who approve of slavery Slavery is a barbaric prejudice that determines a person's status based solely on their preferred or disliked skin color, regardless of their personality and the image and character endowed by God. If you judge souls carelessly based on prejudice, it is not much different from approving of slavery. All souls are lives for whom Jesus died on the cross.

When you become a new creation, new desires arise. One of these is the desire to gather together. Do you, as a Christian, dislike gathering? You need to be restored. Earnestly strive to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace. Eph 4:3
* The reason we cannot stop Tribulation, trials of the heart, wounds, harsh words, and cynicism cannot stop believers. It is because Jesus, who lives within me, advances, not I.

Thoughtless people choose gifts based on price. A gift is my heart for the other person. Therefore, the gift reflects the other person's appearance. A single rose means the other person is beautiful, and a small keychain means I will accompany you. It means, 'This is the luncheon meat spam you liked. It's quite expensive compared to other things. Good, right?' It means, 'You are my budae jjigae.'
* Jesus does not cling. Clinging comes from fear and anxiety. Jesus loves without clinging. This is because He loves our core, not our outward appearance or image.

* Gratitude changes destiny. King Saul was tested by David's fame and the people's cheers. Praise is what refines people. If, truly, if Saul had thanked God at that moment for giving him such an outstanding talent as David, the course of his life, indeed history, would have changed.

* The definition of being built up The Lord has exalted you and will continue to exalt you. The true meaning of exaltation is to build up souls in a manner like Jesus, who was lower. For the height you are raised, there will be a fall of humility.
* Who lives? For to me, to live is Christ, and to die is gain. - Phil 1:20

* The outstanding ministry, gifts, and status that I respect and love are acknowledged with a 'I see.' However, my heart naturally bows in respect and admiration for the love of Jesus. They are so lovely that their flaws are hidden.

They must be offered with all your heart, as if every moment were the last, as if every meeting were a farewell, burning like a flame and scattering. So that there is no regret before the Lord, because what I am is by the grace of God, and He alone is the eternal judge. "For I am already being poured out as a drink offering, and the time of my departure has come. I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith. Henceforth there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will award to me on that day, and not only to me but also to all who have loved his appearing." - 2 Timothy 4:6,7,8
Heart! Conquer. Ask yourself. Are you afraid? If you are afraid, you must conquer. If you are not afraid, you have already won.
Do not live by achievement, but by response. Victory in the world makes people arrogant, but the response given by the Lord makes us spread the fragrance of the Lord.

Worldly people avoid challenges and fears and make choices by preferring personal gain. However, people of faith stand in the place where the Lord told them to be, the place they deem right despite disadvantages. Those who are prepared to die have nothing to fear. Yet, we are people who have already died in the Lord and are living a new life. Crossing the line of death, we are not just bold, but peaceful.

The challenges of the world and darkness disappear with the passage of time. This is because they are not truth and lack the power to endure. However, the Lord's love is the same yesterday, today, and forever.

While discarding 'ambition' for the world, do not discard 'calling'. We do not study and work solely for our own enjoyment. It is for service. We must refuse positions, material possessions, and qualifications for enjoyment, and obtain them for service.

There is something impossible in the world that many people constantly attempt: serving two masters. It is impossible. While there can be sincerity in serving both, there can be no wholeheartedness. Ultimately, you will serve the one that causes you to fall.

The hardest thing in the world is imitation. Those who are good at it are called comedians. Living as if you are something you are not at your core is tiring, and it is a life that the Lord hates the most. "Beware of practicing your righteousness before other people in order to be seen by them." - Matthew 6:1

I hope you become missionaries who choose victory in the Lord over success in the world. Do not envy those who build up their "specs" through self-improvement; instead, become young people who overcome evil with good through true "ability" that the Lord is with. The era of competing with mere appearances is over; in a world like live broadcast, compete with substance, not lip-syncing or marketing.

Mission over efficiency. Rather than striving to make the best use of my time, I must do what I am supposed to do. Just as breathing and the heart beating are natural, those who love God and have received His heart stand where they should, do what they should, and are with those they should be with. However, such a life and decisions often face criticism from lust and worldly values.

We must not ignore more important things for the sake of worldly success. We should not be held captive by success, but be captivated by the light within us. Though we may go slowly, we will not retreat, and eventually, we will become the image God dreams of.
Become salt and melt away. Deny yourself and take up your cross daily. You may disappear and be forgotten, but the Lord will surely find you.

He was a man who trusted God and was loved by God, a king after God's own heart, even though he was marginalized, threatened, despaired when hope of life was cut off, and stumbled into sin. It is David. Rise up like David.

"Just a moment, let's think about my sister for a bit~~." What you want to think about now is not a negative situation that contaminates me. Think and look forward to good things happening. Imagine and declare that you will embrace God's heart and that its influence will flow out, spreading the kingdom of heaven. Surprisingly, I see Christians resolving difficult matches from the smallest things. Instead of thinking, "I'll have a hard time meeting that person!" think, "In me, Christ will be glorified, whether I live or die. God will surely help me!"

A retreat is about transforming myself with the power of godliness, not about providing a changed environment. The world is vastly different from a place with no signals and no corners to sin. To minimize the difference between that place and this place, inner transformation must occur. Coming back to Korea and attending early morning worship is not because I am diligent, but temporarily due to jet lag. Inner transformation and being with people of faith are what give power. Also, flee from youthful passions and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace, along with those who call on the Lord from a pure heart. - 2 Timothy 2:22
What did I so desperately want to see, the passion that burned me up in my youth and drove me to run without ever stopping? Jesus, only Jesus.
Tailor-made. Who will order you? Who will stop you? The mission is individual.
Victory is determined by how you fill your time and empty spaces. Will you fill it with Jesus? Or will you surrender it to laziness and meaninglessness?
To avoid regret later, you must live well. Living well means focusing on the Lord and not fearing people.

Even if you encounter something you don't know during an exam, you must move on, leaving it behind. We are not called to be perfect, but to be obedient. God does not delight in those who shrink back and fall into destruction. Problems that were difficult in elementary school become easy in middle school.

A judge, an audience, and a player cannot perform an excellent game if they repeatedly focus on the audience's gaze or rely on their own feelings. Only the judge makes the decision. There is nothing more foolish than judging one's existence by one's feelings derived from the audience's reaction, and there is nothing more foolish than giving up the match and running away because of it. "Henceforth there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will award to me on that day, and not only to me but also to all who have loved his appearing." - 2 Timothy 4:8

Knowing myself. David was the eighth child, not even introduced to honored guests, not trusted enough by his father to be asked for proof when sent on errands, and habitually heard harsh words from his elder brother Eliab. He even confessed that his mother was conceived in sin. Nevertheless, he was faithful, steadfast, and bold because he discovered himself in his relationship with the Lord. "Abide in me, and I in you." - John 15:7 "Though my father and mother forsake me, the Lord will take me in." - Psalm 27:10
It is a blessing to have something to offer, no matter how weak or poor you are. It is not that you cannot help because you are weak, nor that you cannot offer because you have nothing.
If there is no peace... the problem is not the situation or the people, but that I am not giving thanks. Heaven-like life and hell-like life are divided by a single act of gratitude.
If you are pulsing, you have a mission. If you are given time and life, you have a mission from God, and that is love. Serve with what the Lord has given you.

If you leave your father's embrace and worship idols, you lose your sonship and live like a pig in a sty. Those who lose themselves and live confined boast about having gathered carob pods, the food of pigs. Do not merely survive; soar like an eagle towards your goal. "Rend your hearts, and not your garments, and turn to the Lord your God." - Joel 2:13 Do not try to appease the Lord with changes in your actions; instead, return to the Lord and experience a change in your being by turning your heart.

A father does not necessarily have to be a high-ranking person in society or wealthy. Sometimes, they are more limited and weaker than we are. Yet, to me, he is a good father. This is because he became limited and weak for my sake. Be good to your parents.

It is love, not calculation. You don't understand life when you're calculating. Life seems like riding a roller coaster. However, the more you love, the more you realize how short life truly is. Especially, life is too short even if you only live by loving. Do not regret wasting (calculating) your life.
Redesign your painful youth with God's dreams and passion. You were not meant to be that way. The unchanging truth is that you are a person of God, personally called by God.
I do not want to be an evil minister. Nor do I wish to be called a decent minister. Apart from the Lord, I am an empty can, becoming nothing.
To look toward the goal, ponder deeply, pursue, and run means that the goal one aims for is so precious that one does not care about other things.

A change in affection is not living by law. Enduring and enjoying are fundamentally different. Giving by force and giving with joy are different. Struggling to forgive and being in a state of hatred are fundamentally difficult. Suppressing sinful impulses and hating sin and evil are different. It is not easy to distinguish between someone who does things by force and someone who does not. As a tip, someone who does things by force lacks grace and condemns others if they do not meet their standards. Jesus never condemned anyone.

Obtaining the promised land is not about entering Canaan and registering it or making a good investment in prime real estate. The sole ownership of that land comes from your relationship with God. Canaan is a region where rain plays an absolute role. Your relationship with God determines everything about you. Relationship is authority and power.

We hide our weaknesses and exaggerate our strengths. That is the principle of marketing. However, we boast in our weaknesses. Through our weaknesses, the Lord's power flows. Therefore, in weakness, we become strong.

God's gaze and hand rest upon the weak. Do not boast in what is perishable; rather, boast in your weakness. Then Christ Himself will visit and dwell with you. "And He said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for My power is made perfect in weakness.' Therefore I will boast all the more gladly of my weaknesses, so that the power of Christ may rest upon me. For the sake of Christ, then, I am content with weaknesses, insults, hardships, persecutions, and calamities. For when I am weak, then I am strong." 2 Corinthians 12:9
Testimony of Suffering The reason your heart aches as if pierced by a sword is because God created you precious and works within you.

Someone asks, "Even if I can't reach high, can't I take off one piece of clothing and give it to someone who is naked?" The answer is this: That is why the Lord wants us to live diligently. Not just to receive help when we are naked, but to give to those who need help. And if you are going to give, give multiple pieces to help. The Lord does not quench a faintly burning wick or break a bruised reed. However, the Lord also does not want us to become a faintly burning wick or a bruised reed. He wants us to be like a torch and a cedar tree.

Two Choices If you must give something up, the things you love and cherish must be abandoned without regret to hold onto the Lord. A person who truly loves souls will try to love them with the highest love. This is choosing 'voluntary self-isolation' called solitude to draw living water from a deep well. Being called a channel of grace is because holiness is life. Truth is solitary but grows bolder daily. - Pastor Choi Chun-sun
An adult can match a child's stride, but a child cannot match an adult's. Therefore, the mature person is the one who serves and understands.

Encounters and fellowship where manners were kept toward the other person, even if imperfectly, and where we acted truthfully before the Lord, leave no regrets even after parting. Even if we meet again by chance on a certain street or in a certain place after many years, the path I must take becomes clearer than my heart beating again.

How successful do you want to be and how much praise do you want to receive? Why you did it is more important. If you did it out of love for God, after finishing the work, you will fall prostrate and give thanks and worship God. Then you will not envy kings. Meeting God simply and directly means looking only to God in everyday life.
A woman's heart is easily broken. This is because it is precious.

The Lord said to him, "What is in your hand?" He said, "A staff." - Exodus 4:2 1. God does not ask how much you have achieved or if you are recognized. 2. He asks what you have, even if it is insignificant. 3. Moses was a failed old man with a dry stick. 4. Having much and rising high does not manifest the Lord's glory. 5. There is no need to pretend to have what you don't have or to boast. 6. Even in a barren life, if you obey the Lord, power and glory will be manifested. Moses took the staff of God in his hand - Exodus 4:20 A dry stick becomes the staff of God when held in the Lord's hand. A ring in a department store and a ring given by a loved one are not the same ring. It is a ring that 'you' gave me. When a meaningless stick that I used as I pleased becomes the staff God holds, there we see glory. Exodus 4:17 "Take this staff in your hand, and with it you shall do signs."

The Lord favored you and chose you, not because you were more numerous than other peoples, for you were the fewest of all peoples. Deuteronomy 7:7 I bless you to be the weakest. Then the Lord will be more manifested.

Until we are refined, even if we receive grace, we are closer to rough stones than gemstones. Angels cannot refine us. Therefore, God refines us through people. When the interpretation of life changes, the 'enemy' transforms into my 'life teacher.'
Rise high so that inferiority and victim mentality cannot touch you. At 350km upto 800m.

If you worked hard but feel empty, it is not zeal for the Lord. If you loved the Lord, you should have let go of other expectations long ago. If you sought the approval of people, you are not a servant of Christ and will be ashamed.
Immortal Purpose It is not the world or people that hold me. It is only the Lord. That is why life can be beautiful and glorious.

Transformation of the Soul If you do not live out the Word, your soul will not be transformed. Living out the Word is not a matter of will or compulsion, but only through union with Christ. Then it becomes clear where to start again. The prodigal son returning to be united with the Father, repentance is the beginning.

If you do not meet God in worship, you will have endless tribulations like Israel. When people worship God, they can also see themselves. If they cannot see themselves, everything appears distorted, and a cycle of misunderstanding continues. Worship is what breaks that cycle. If worship collapses due to busyness, even a passing dog will attack you.
Those who believe in Jesus are not impatient. If it is not the Lord's will, they deny their own emotions and will, and rejoice because their fleshly desires have been broken.
Those who believe in Jesus can be a little lonely. This is because even in conversation, the focus is on the Lord.
In Jesus, there is nothing in vain. All the mistakes I thought were trial and error will be used for important purposes, and all things will work together for good. If you are in Jesus...

If you are satisfied with Jesus alone, you will have grace and fear will recede. In Jesus, all evaluations will be reinterpreted. It is a life of fullness and there is no lack. A person captivated by Jesus is the most beautiful person, and He will vouch for such a person.
True Humility Humility is not focusing on oneself but exalting only the Lord.
When you ponder and love Jesus deeply, you will not be conscious of how others see you. In worship and in life, the world and I disappear, and only the Redeeming Lord is seen.

Believing in Jesus does not immediately make you a saint. That is the cause of mental breakdown. When Israel defeated Egypt through the ten plagues and crossed the Red Sea, they became 'free people' from 'slaves.' However, the 'slave mentality' remained in their spirit. The 'wicked self' of the old man is still alive and wriggling. That is why the wilderness journey begins. The Lord calls this (wilderness) 'church life.' - Acts 7:38

Tears with the Same Composition but Different Reasons People feel pain because of what is within them. If you hurt because of people, it means people are present; if you hurt because of worldly conditions, the world fills you. A person of God feels pain because of God. That is why His tears become my tears.

The biggest problem with the elder son in Jesus' parable of the prodigal son is that he lives in his father's house. He lost his father but did not realize it. He is in his father's house, doing his father's work, yet he has lost his father and his father's heart without knowing it. After comparing, he became angry, and after judging carelessly, he asserted his rights. If you plant beans in the spring, you cannot harvest red beans in the fall. If something starts from a fundamentally wrong place, no matter how similar it looks, the fruit it bears will be different, and you cannot find the Father.
I am ready Lord, I came to do what I should in the book it is written of me.

The infinite loyalty of a finite being means being limited by time and space. We can only work within our limits, in the time and place given to us. Conversely, the Lord will work in other realms. If we are faithful unto death, His omnipotence will be realized beyond our limitations.

Your 'awe' that I am only now coming to know. Abraham was a worshiper who built altars everywhere, discussed God's judgment through prayer, and had already earned the title of a man of faith (Genesis 15:6). However, it was only when he did not withhold even his 'only son Isaac,' whom he received in his old age, from God, that he was confirmed to be in awe of God, 'at last,' 'now.' To fear God is not to receive something, but to offer up whatever He tells you to let go of. The angel said, "Do not lay a hand on the boy. Do not do anything to him. Now I know that you fear God, because you have not withheld your son, your only son, from me." - Genesis 22:12
Faith is not a show; it is sacrificing unconditionally, not by considering people, but by focusing solely on the Lord's gaze.

Love is looking at people through a telescope, and hate is looking through a microscope. When you love, you become blind. Therefore, you can cover sins. When you give everything away, you become the richest person. God commands us to love God and love our neighbor before all other missions. He says this is what a prophet is, and this is what a person of God is. Peter, an old man with white hair who followed Jesus in that way, experienced failure, and blamed others more than himself, earnestly implores the saints living their final days: Above all, love each other deeply, because love covers over a multitude of sins. - 1 Peter 4:8
A person who speaks by the results of their life. "Are you not the result of my work in the Lord?" - Paul in Corinthians - 1 Corinthians 9:1

Faith like Herod's. Herod listened to John the Baptist's sharp sermons with distress, yet there was no change in his life because he did not want to lose his pleasures. If you do not lose, you will not change. You will surely perish. It is merely religious satisfaction. It is like reading a prayer book instead of praying, or undergoing evangelism training and being satisfied with that. Herod knew that John was a righteous and holy man, and he was alarmed. He protected him and, when he heard him speak, was greatly disturbed, yet he listened to him gladly. - Mark 6:20
A life focused solely on honoring Christ is peaceful. Living and dying are not such big issues because one is overwhelmed by more important values.
Without gentleness, you cannot lead a team. That is why God trains us.
We are not called to be perfect. We stand to serve the perfect Lord.

The king was deeply distressed and went up to the room over the gate and wept. As he went, he said: "O my son Absalom, my son, my son Absalom! Would that I had died instead of you—O Absalom, my son, my son!" - 2 Samuel 18:33 Those who have the heart of the Lord, join in His lament.
Do not be lonely; become a comforter. If you look up, there are many more people who need you.

Joseph went before the king in office at the age of 30 and lived to be 110 years old before he died. If Moses had benchmarked Joseph, he would not have left the palace even at 40. Moses was called at 80 after 40 years in the wilderness. Do not envy the lives of others; live the life God has given you, even if it is not clearly visible.
Repent of greed and lust, and become strong. Become the strongest person who hopes only in Jesus.

We mistake the effective fulfillment of greed for happiness. That is not happiness; it is sin and death. True happiness is how free you are from sin, which is the same as how successfully you have emptied yourself of greed. When the Holy Spirit came, there were no longer any who "claimed anything as their own" in the book of Acts. Without the power of the Holy Spirit, there is not even a 1% chance of solving the problems we face.

Not zeal, but class. There are two ways for an elementary school student to solve a problem: by working hard. If you work hard, you might move up to about 10th place in the midterm exam. The other way is to solve it as a college student. Revival does not come from relative zeal alone. It comes when you live a completely different life, pray differently, and worship differently, focused on the goal, so great is its glory that you disregard everything else. Discard the trivial and dwell in a different class. Brothers, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which Christ Jesus has called me heavenward. - Philippians 3:13, 14

Life begets life. There are people who save lives, like police officers, firefighters, and coast guards. Unlike jobs that only involve phone calls and computers in an office, they may sometimes die in the line of duty. To save a life, you must risk a life. There are some believers who inspire respect and awe even without looking at the minister. As the disciple is, so is the teacher.

What we dislike hearing is people criticizing us. But surprisingly, we see and hear it because, in another part of our heart, we desire it. These contradictory feelings stem from hurt and rejection. When negative emotions creep in, the way to cast them out as waste in one fell swoop is to know Jesus. When you know and experience Jesus, the evaluations of others become meaningless, and you see yourself, your relationships, and the Lord more clearly. You are approaching true reality.

Being loved does not mean monopolizing all attention and affection. It means that God's love, which is irreplaceable by anything, is poured into us, like clay pots, making us jewels, and sometimes we meet people who recognize and respect those jewels.

Do you know why we fight with the people we love? It is because of selfish desires. When we try to satisfy our ego, wars in our homes, friendships, and relationships never cease. To end this, we must die, and dying means accepting that we have already died. In fact, we have already died with the Lord.
Love is.... Love is not a decision. That is why we cannot do it by our own strength. Love is what fills me up inside. Therefore, it is what I always think about, and ultimately, it is what moves me.

For we are God's handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do. - Ephesians 2:10 We are God's masterpiece, His one and only creation, His poem, and His letter. When I become myself, the abilities God has given me flow out. Do not be someone else; be yourself. That is the freedom that comes when you discover yourself in God.

Every day, we hear Satan's whisper to prove ourselves. Even when Jesus came, He was tempted to turn stones into bread and prove He was the Son of God. What would you do? If I had such power... If it were me, I would pick up a stone, pretend to pray, and then smash Satan's head with it. The Apostle Paul said in his letters that he did not proclaim himself but the Lord. Then the Lord said He would also testify about us before the angels.
We cannot satisfy everyone. That is greed. Even Jesus could not satisfy everyone. Do your best, accept the results, and be thankful.

We are called to be the light and salt of the world. But we are light and salt that serve without light and without name. There is an overwhelming anointing upon those who have removed their own greed and personality.

We are the body of Christ. The head and the body can never be separated. Just as my head is Kim Hwaseong, my body is also Kim Hwaseong. We are participants in the divine nature, in the fullness of Jesus. Therefore, the power to rule dwells within us. I hope you will never give up that authority.

Why do fortune tellers ask for payment but not repentance? Fortune tellers and shamans do not tell people to repent. The characteristic of idolatry is its "immediacy," the short-term effect and temptation of fulfilling my desires for a certain price. However, it cannot satisfy or fill me, and its end is death. Please distinguish between bait and food.

It does not matter if there are the schemes of the enemy, fierce challenges, darkness that obscures vision, or if we are surrounded and feel suffocated; we can walk in regardless because we rejected comfort and ease from the very beginning. Believing in Jesus means being saved from other relative purposes, reputation, and success. If we can plant the cross in that land and pray for the Holy Spirit to descend... then it doesn't matter much whether we live or die. For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain. My earnest expectation and hope are that in no way I will be ashamed, but that with all boldness, now as always, Christ will be magnified in my body, whether by life or by death. - Philippians 1:20, 21
The common trait of great leaders is character. Image and reputation are the 'me' that others see, while character is 'me' myself. If you cannot inspire trust, you cannot lead others.

Whether you are a hypocrite deceiving people or a saint inspiring them, both exert powerful influence. However, there is no need to be a hypocrite or a saint. You just need to know the answer. If you know the truth, you cannot live any other way.
Those who receive grace as a gift will flow with wisdom and powerful ruling ability (Romans 5:17, to reign) that influences people in the Lord.

Food is more appealing the more harmful it is. Inferiority, comparison, pride, and rejection are harmful but never let go. However, knowing Christ allows us to let these things go. In the face of the Lord's preciousness and beauty, the value of everything is reset to refuse.
No one in this world confesses to being jealous. They only claim to be executing justice. Jealousy is grieving over another's joy. The two solutions to this are...

Isaiah 14:13-14 speaks of the devil's original sin: pride. In those short verses, the pronoun "I" appears five times. Have you ever met the devil? Those going in the same direction as the devil are not blocked by him. When the words "I" come out of my mouth, the devil is at work. When I say, "I deserve this treatment," "How dare they do this to me," or "I can't stand to see that," I am getting closer to the devil's original sin. It should be "He" instead of "I." Especially for ladies, if you keep emphasizing "I," you become "Gaga," Lady Gaga.
Now, I hope you will become someone to whom the Lord can entrust authority with peace of mind. Even when gaining power, you must be someone who understands where that authority comes from.
I understand, but you ask how one can live like that. How can one live a different life after receiving that love that is incomprehensible?

Popularity is fleeting and disappears as quickly as it comes, and money is the same. Only what belongs to Jesus Christ is eternal. Nothing is as uncontrollable as human relationships. Instead of grieving and rejoicing in fleeting moments, lift your eyes to the Lord and move forward, acknowledging His sovereignty and rule. The Lord is eternal.

I saw a human chef find great joy and rest by visiting the marginalized and shadowed people on his day off and personally cooking for them. Escaping those who judge and value you, and becoming a joy to those who truly need you, is also a form of good rest. The truly difficult thing is doing nothing without meaning. Resting is not doing nothing, but being filled with grace and meaning.
The happiest moment in life is not when you are recognized. It is when you lay that recognition at the Lord's feet.
Life opens up to a different path suddenly from a small incident. Therefore, every moment is like a jewel that cannot be treated carelessly.
Life is a battle of priorities. The cause of success or failure lies in who is the object of love and affection. What I do first tells me about my future.
Life is compared to a marathon. I think it's an appropriate analogy, provided that not everyone runs the same course... Live with your own strengths on your own course. Stop comparing...

Thinking of life in terms of speed leads to unhappiness. However, thinking of it in terms of direction leads to interpretation. If the path I am on is the path of truth, I will not be shaken even if many people run on the path of greed that others pursue. The meaning of life comes not from speed, but from direction.
The purpose of life is to fear God and enjoy Him forever (Westminster Shorter Catechism). Forcing enjoyment is a disaster.
Even if I spend my entire life alone in loneliness, if I can live for the Lord, that is an unbearable glory and a great blessing to me.

Do not be satisfied with where you belong, for God is the one who made me. Do not find meaning in your splendid relationships, for God is the one who called me. Do not fear the world or pursue glory, for God is the one who sent me.

What you boast about will be taken away. All boasting will be taken away. However, if you boast in Jesus, Jesus will be shared. (2 Kings 20:13) When Hezekiah heard the words of the messengers, he showed them all his treasures—his silver, his gold, his spices, his precious oil, and all his armory—everything that was found in his storehouses. There was nothing in his whole palace or in all his kingdom that Hezekiah did not show them.
What is more important than position is posture. With persistence and sincerity, not only will people recognize you, but God will also take notice.

Those who see themselves in the mirror of comparison with others become proud or frustrated. However, before God's mirror, people become humble. One who has the wisdom not to sin and the wisdom to be humble becomes precious. Refer to Acts 1:2.

If you reveal yourself, show yourself, appeal for yourself, and elevate yourself, there will only be profound emptiness. However, when I die on the cross, and Christ lives within me, and I live by faith in Christ alone, those who judged me or tried to ignore me will focus on the Lord. This is because the Lord cannot be hidden. - Galatians 2:20, Psalm 40:3

If you cannot let go of yourself, you will let go of the Lord. If you hold onto the Lord, you can let go of and deny yourself. We cannot serve two masters, nor can we straddle the fence. This is because the gap is absolutely unshareable.

Those who see themselves with their own eyes become fixated on people and the world. This means being obsessive and demanding. This is because they are anxious and chased by their own shortcomings. However, those who abide in the Lord fear nothing. Where you set your gaze determines all success or failure. Indeed, I have calmed and quieted my soul like a weaned child on its mother's lap; my soul is like a weaned child. Psalm 131:2.
If you work with your own mind and popularity, you can do it quickly, but you can only move a few. However, if you work from the heart, you win everyone's heart and manifest God's work.
Three things reflect your state: 1. What do you boast about? 2. What causes you pain? 3. What are you obsessed with? Here lies the answer that dominates you and what you serve.
Examine your desires. Then you will know for whom you are living. Those who live for what perishes will be ashamed, and those who live for the eternal One will receive glory.

When you confess that you cannot go further due to your weakness and shortcomings, the Lord instead tells you to feed my sheep with His love. What holds you is not your will, your zeal, or your determination; it is simply because I hold you. <1 Corinthians 15:58> Be more devoted to the work of the Lord... for you know that your labor in the Lord is not in vain.

Not defending your position is sitting in the seat of the arrogant. David says in Psalm 1 that he is extremely unfortunate. When you cannot maintain the position given by the Lord, shame and defilement will come. Please defend your position.

No matter how plausible and convincing your judgments and thoughts may be, the conclusion is that the Lord has chosen me. The Lord's conclusion will render all evaluations and attacks meaningless. Be bold.

By looking at your thoughts and words, you can know where your life is heading. Especially, the first thought when you open your eyes in the morning is important. It is because it is your core. People bring forth good from the good they have stored up, and bring forth evil from the evil they have stored up. What is within me?

You don't need to fear becoming a dropout. God has prepared things for His good work long ago. Even if someone more successful prepares something, we like it, but God's preparation is the most beautiful and perfect. For we are God's handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do. Ephesians 2:10.
The biggest reason you get hurt is not people, their expressions, or words of rejection. The true reason is selfishness, the desire to protect yourself.
You are still precious even if you are not recognized. Therefore, there is no need to prove or confirm it. Recognition of preciousness comes only from God.

Those who cling to themselves greatly fear being rejected, having relationships severed, and becoming lonely. From the moment we are born into this world, there is almost nothing that happens by our own will. What needs to be clarified is that the One who holds me is God, and He always gives me the best. When we know and have faith in this, 'forcing' disappears, and we are freed from 'obsession.' Of course, life becomes more abundant.
You complain that you are undervalued or that people do not respect you. The problem is not the people around you who have many issues, but that you have not been emptied.
It is easy to stumble in the very thing you do best.
The true goal, the destination you genuinely desire, tells us who you are. Who are you?

Natural persons have many things to discard. Foolish things, things that react sensitively, and things that trouble me and others must be discarded, but it is not easy. However, when one matures, they put away the things of childhood. Love makes us complete as children and gives us the same perspective as the Lord. 1 Corinthians 13:11-12 KRV When I was a child, I spoke like a child, I understood like a child, I thought like a child. But when I became a man, I put away childish things. For now we see in a mirror dimly, but then face to face. Now I know in part; then I shall know fully, even as I have been fully known.
Be free. Subjects failed due to lack of freedom are subject to infinite retakes.

My abilities, surrounding circumstances, rumors, and crises are not significant problems. What is important is that because the Lord sent me, His hand protects me. Therefore, the focus of my aim changes. It is to testify of the cross in that land and to see the glory of God.

Animals that cannot lie down even when sleeping are all herbivores. On the other hand, strong predators have a sense of ease. Become a spiritual strongman, and you will have a sense of ease. We are not predators, but like Psalm 23 says, we will experience lying down in green pastures with God as our shepherd. Note here that the sheep are made to lie down. The Lord's rest is gracious and sometimes even semi-compulsory. Experience illness; there will be days when you are forced to lie down and rest.
Living well does not mean gaining wealth and honor, but living according to the plan and will of the Lord.
After maturing, you will put away childish things and see dimly perceived things clearly, as if face to face. To grow is to change, and this is achieved when perfect love is realized.
Wasting talent and money is foolish. Among them, wasting time is the most foolish thing because time is life. When time decreases, life decreases.
Talent is remembered in the mind. Consideration remains in the heart.

Where your treasure is, there your heart will be also. God sees our motives, so He is very interested in matters of people and money. Earning money is a skill, and spending that money is an art. God wants us to be artists who know beauty.

Even warships sometimes return to port and anchor. They receive supplies and undergo repairs because they will go out and fight again. If you rest in port and rest at sea, you are not a warship but a cruise ship. The Apostle Paul said there is a 'good fight' throughout his life. We are called to be combative beings. If you choose to believe, you will fight, and if you fight with faith, you will surely win.
The transformation of desperate situations does not change with seasons and time like a weather forecast. I must change. Repent and correct yourself. That is the only way.

In my youth, when I served with dedication and loyalty comparable to others and was recognized, one day I was given a heart to simply wait for the Lord. Unexpectedly, the Lord came. Repentance poured out. Although the Lord's presence has been gone for a long time, a life that wonders why without knowing shame, that does not grieve for failing to uphold holiness but instead is shameless and rationalizes everything with the power of absolute positivity that everything will be fine, and that the cross is the restoration of faith and holiness, is revival.
In my youth, I believed there were things I could not lose. However, any person or thing I rely on besides God can be lost. Therefore, we must rely only on the Lord and be humble.

The biggest illusion young people have is that they will be happy if someone gives them wonderful and perfect love. People become unhappy from the moment they start wanting to be loved. You become happy by existing in God's love and by loving others. Do not cling to receiving love and be lonely; instead, receive God's great love and love others.

Come forth as pure gold. God, the only One who knows the way I take, refines me, and only then do I become pure gold. Pure gold is valuable not for any use, but because its essence itself is precious. The purpose of refinement is to obtain pure gold. When impurities are removed from me, only Jesus remains. More important than the problems of the Korean church or the demands of the times is "You do your part well." The owner of pure gold is only God, and one cannot dictate terms just because they have become pure gold. But he knows the way that I take, when he has tested me, I will come forth as gold - Job 23:10.

If you truly love, there is no need to hide or exaggerate. Coughs, poverty, and love cannot be hidden. Especially love, the more you try to hide it, the more clearly it is revealed. That is why one sheds tears and pours perfume on Jesus' feet.
In my long experience with youth ministry, I have deeply realized a simple truth: young people change as much as they are loved.
The disciples rejoiced when they were persecuted because being resisted by the world meant becoming worthy of heaven.

To be impatient means to have my standards, or in other words, my desires. We must let go of the standards by which we seek recognition from people and God. When we are willing to be relinquished so that God can be revealed through our lives, rather than achieving any particular goal, we become known by God. Otherwise, even after doing countless things, we can become irrelevant before the Lord.
No one respects a gangster just because they drive a luxury car. A person's true dignity comes not from school, reputation, or class, but from the value of their soul.
Do not try to be respected; be trusted. However, even this is not done by our own strength. It is achieved only by looking to the Lord and loving souls.

If you grieve because you are not respected or recognized, you will not see the glory of God. The agony for the kingdom of God and the community is evidence that you are a participant in God's glory. Do you become angry or disappointed for human glory? Or do you grieve for the Lord's glory? May the Lord lead us to the place He calls us.
To be more honest, you are incredibly weak. However, in the Lord, you are strong. Infinitely so, even...

Ultimately, life is a battle of the final destination. Will you live immersed in the world and go where the world goes, or will you be madly devoted to Jesus and live with Him? I run towards the Lord.

Anyone can like something. However, a professional does what they do with passion until they like it. Therefore, they do not dwell on or care about the obstacles and challenges that block the path of faith; they simply ignore them. The ability to run forward while ignoring them is power.

What you like is 'because of what,' and what you love is 'in spite of it.' If you just like something, your heart flutters, but if you love it, tears well up. Many people 'like' someone to be happy. However, a lover makes you happy because of me.
If you like someone, you make calculated investments, but if you love them, you are happy even while sacrificing.
Good is good, but anything good that prevents you from approaching the Lord is evil.

There is no special way to gain a good motivator. If I had to say, it is the boundless, total grace that the Lord pours out despite my lack of goodness. The next step is for me to become a good motivator. The Lord gives motivators, but remaining a good motivator is my part.
Good morning. You all know I love you, right? Be loved. You were born to be loved.
A good mother is not a capable mother who can solve everything. She is a mother who feels regret because she cannot do everything. That is where love comes from.

Eagles become bolder when storms come. This is because they have the vision and wings to fly higher. That is why they do not react to chicks. So, when I see someone I react to, I know who I am. Chicks react to chicks.

Do not try to overcome the limits of your sinful nature, the weakness of your flesh, the limits of your personality, or the power of spiritual trials. These are things that humans have tried to overcome for thousands of years but could not. Instead, look to the Lord and follow Him. Where the Lord is, if I am there, I will suddenly see myself having overcome the limits. My goal is not achievement, but His presence and fellowship. I have been captured by the Lord Jesus.

Do not fall and lie in sin, but rise again through love for the Lord. This is what the Lord truly desires and what the devil truly hates. Sin cannot be overcome by determination. It can only be overcome by the love of Jesus. The sacrifice God requires is a broken spirit. God, you will not despise a broken and contrite heart. - Psalm 51:17

The decisive secret to relationship failure is surprisingly trying too hard. Your voice becomes strained, and tension is felt in your eyes. It is human nature to want to eat a bowl of ramen comfortably. A play must eventually end. Life cannot be covered by acting. Touching a masterpiece is damaging the original. Instead, by valuing yourself and seeking to help the person God has given you, you will enter into the wonderful blessings of relationships that God has prepared. If you crave many things but cannot love yourself, just let go and walk away. From that hell...

The reason the Lord called me is to do His work. His work is to believe and to become holy like Him. The fact that the apostles devoted themselves to prayer and the Word means that strengthening the essentials is the true solution.

In my youth, the Lord enabled me to run even though my heart was deeply wounded. The Lord allows me to wipe away my tears again and run for Him again. I go forth because I am called. The Lord alone is enough. My desire is to love the Lord more.

If you have done what the Lord told you to do, you have done very well. While obeying, you may have made mistakes and lacked ability, so you could not be perfect in everything, but standing where the Lord told you to stand is the beginning of carrying the cross.
Walking the Lord's path means not expecting anyone to follow or support me, but rather that I follow the Lord. Even if that path is narrow and lonely, I must walk it with all my life.
The good thing about abiding in the Lord is that you have nothing more to lose. You who have nothing to lose will continue to be filled.
Re-establishing myself before the Lord and looking only to the Lord is what I have learned so far and the path I must continue to live.
Do not grieve over the paths not taken, but be content by walking the path you must take. Everyone walks, but each has their own path.
If you rely on or boast in anything other than the Lord, it will be taken away. You will waste time and have to start again from zero. Only the Lord is true satisfaction.

For the Lord, for the Lord's glory, I run forth. Even if everything collapses, if that is in the eyes of the youth, that is the blessing of the young and the hope for this nation and church. May the Lord alone receive all honor, glory, and power.
For the Lord, I will live, for the Lord, I will work, for the Lord, I will suffer, and for the Lord, I will die.
For the Lord, I will live, for the Lord, I will work, for the Lord, I will suffer, and for the Lord, I will die~
