Risk your life for the Lord. Shoddy sin and temptation will be no match. A person who is willing to die for the Lord cannot be moved by petty worldly pleasures. To love Jesus is to die for Jesus.
Wanting too much is called greed. If you remain faithful to the original purpose, you will find rest. For the One who came to bear the cross, every moment was a gift and a thrill.

The Lord has never commanded us to read the Bible or pray daily. Of course, daily devotion is undoubtedly very beneficial. He only commands us to take up our cross daily. The evidence of taking up the cross daily is that there is a change of life around me. Paul's daily practice was a life of the cross, giving himself up. Luke 9:23 Then he said to all the people, "If any of you want to follow me, you must forget about yourself, take up your cross daily, and follow me." 1 Corinthians 15:31, Brothers, I swear by the joy that I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, that I die daily.

The Lord is omnipresent. This means that He is with me now and works in the place I have surrendered in my heart. Joseph, who lived consciously of the Lord, overcame the temptation of sin, did not despair, and did not seek revenge.

The Lord humbled Himself and rejoiced, and with that joy, He came into the world, became a grain of wheat, served to the point of death, and left many fruits. The biblical definition of pride is not keeping one's place, as Satan did. Unless I deeply root humility and become utterly humbled with the Lord as my only all, I can never exalt the Lord.
The Lord seeks. And He asks. Who am I? Respond to that. Show me through your earnest desires, affections, and choices. As a person thinks, so is their life.
The Lord seeks. He calls me. The Lord is my Lord and the only One worthy of praise.

Knowing that I should look to the Lord but being unable to do so is a great pain in my youth. The reason is not complicated. It is because I do not regard the values I believe in and cannot have taken away as 'dung.' Unless I regard the world's approval, people's attention, the pride of being successful in relationships, and the sinful entertainment that briefly pleases me as filth and hate them, I cannot serve both these desires and Christ. ...I consider everything a loss for the sake of gaining him, and I count it as rubbish, in order that I may gain Christ and be found in him... - This is the crystallization of the faith confession of one who lived humbly with the Lord his whole life.
When I cannot look to the Lord, I have much but feel empty, and I am capable but frustrated. I hope you will let go of everything and look to the beautiful Lord.

I am the kingdom of God where the Lord's reign prevails. Through me, my community will be blessed, and I am the source of blessing that blesses the world. When I move forward, the kingdom of God comes there.
The true meaning of leadership is having learned obedience.
Knowledge puffs up, but love covers a multitude of sins.

When the water pressure disappears from an underground water pipe that has been cut off, groundwater seeps into the corroded cracks of the pipe. Therefore, when tap water is supplied again after the cutoff, muddy water comes out. If you are not filled with Jesus and take no action, your life will be like supplying muddy water to those who expect living water. Maintain holy water pressure. Therefore, holy brothers, who share in the heavenly calling, let us consider Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession, deeply. -Hebrews 3:1

My position, title, and past experience do not define me. More than knowledge and zeal for truth, or much activity, Jesus valued 'first love' above all else. My priorities and motives reveal who I am. "But I have this against you, that you have abandoned the love you had at first. Remember therefore from where you have fallen; repent, and do the works you did at first. If not, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place, unless you repent." -Rev. 2:4-5
It may feel overwhelming to parallel the Lord's work while working and studying. However, later you realize that it is harder to enjoy the world without living that way.

If you truly want to save lives, you must pay the price and unite with the Lord. John the Baptist, the greatest man in the Old Testament, was focused in one direction in terms of his possessions and lifestyle. John wore a garment of camel's hair, and a leather belt around his waist, and ate locusts and wild honey. -Mark 1:6
True happiness is not human satisfaction, wealth, fame, or the envious glances of others. Happiness comes from heaven. It is the grace that makes me who I am.

The genuine and the fake are revealed when they are damaged or tested. A pine tree adds fragrance when it is cut. Harmony may seem plausible, but it has no honey to give from the start. The genuine does not fade, and its subtle lingering scent continues.

The characteristic of an earthenware vessel is that it has no value in itself, is easily broken, and crucially, has no other option besides its sole function of holding. When it is filled abundantly with treasure, that is victory. The rest can be entrusted to His sovereignty.
If you are attached, you cannot discern. True discernment comes only when you die to yourself. Everything done before that is not discernment, but merely noble criticism and prejudice.
Do not be attached; instead, focus. Abandon idols and love the Lord.
Attachment means dissatisfaction. It is frustration. It stems from not being filled. It is a common symptom that arises when we are not satisfied with the Lord and do not live in Him.
Some people are attached and try to fulfill their desires, while others surrender themselves to be crucified, thus achieving salvation and life.

Do not be chased. We are called not to be chased by the devil, but rather to chase them. We will expose all lies and schemes and burn them in Jesus' name. [Matt. 16:18] "So I tell you, you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of Hades (hell) will not overcome (prevail against) it." * Hades: the realm of the dead, * Gates: the entrance, * Prevail against: to conquer. In the original language, it means to aggressively break down the gates of demonic forces. It means to attack, not defend.

If you hire a car, you must refill it as much as you use it. Of course, since there is no need to add more, you fill it just enough. But with your own car, you just fill it up. You're not going to drive it for just a day or two, are you? Your soul should be filled to overflowing with grace. Since it's for a lifetime anyway...

Just as a car's warning light comes on when it's out of gas, an emotion warning light comes on when the Lord becomes dim in your heart. Do not ignore or remove these warnings; instead, when pain comes, you must receive the Lord. Faith is loving Jesus with all your heart. Open your heart and confirm. Is Jesus at the center of your heart...?

When you become a true worshiper, God responds with a question: "What do you want me to do for you?" The first worshiper, Solomon, made an amazing request because his purpose had been purified through worship.

The art of dealing with people teaches you to highlight strengths and hide weaknesses. However, faith challenges you to boast in your weaknesses and testify to God's power that makes you perfect in them.
When you first follow Jesus, you will fear nothing, and nothing will stop you on the path toward the Lord.

The first thing I learned when I believed in Jesus was that I must keep moving forward toward Jesus, who is the goal. Even if I don't have many choices and experience some setbacks, I must keep getting up and running. If I fall, I will fall forward and be able to grasp Jesus' feet...
You will be surprised when you get to heaven. You will realize that you are not a victim, but rather that you are going there by the Lord's mercy.

If ten million people call me an angel, who am I? I am a person. If ten million people call me a devil, who am I? I am still a person. Then why am I so shaken by others' evaluations? It is simply because I am not looking at the Lord.

If you try to receive the love of ten thousand people alone, you will be lonely and empty. However, if you love ten thousand people even when you are alone, you will be noble and happy. Life is not about accumulating, but about scattering and giving.
Genius can ruin you. People who succeed by luck, who prosper without paying a price, are not people to be envied, but pitied.

Youth will surely pass away. However, the emotions and tears of that time that remain in your heart will not disappear. Therefore, youth must sow. Even if you cry... even if you fall, stumble, and are pushed aside... you must crawl and sow. In youth, success is not success, and failure is not failure. Those who sow in tears will reap with joy. Those who go out weeping, bearing seed for sowing, shall doubtless come again with rejoicing, bringing their sheaves with them. -Psalm 126:5-6

Just because someone calls me an angel doesn't make me an angel, and just because someone calls me a devil doesn't turn me into a devil. The reason Paul didn't care about people's evaluations was that he looked to the only Judge. In fact, the reason people often get angry is not justice, but inferiority within themselves. If the standards are different, there is no reaction. "To me it is a very small thing that I be judged by you or by a human court. I do not even judge myself." -1 Cor. 4:3 -> Mind your own business. "Therefore do not pronounce judgment before the time, before the Lord comes, who will bring to light the things now hidden in darkness and will disclose the purposes of the hearts. Then each one will receive his commendation from God." -1 Cor. 4:5 -> Don't overdo it.
Youth, stand at the forefront. Dedicate yourselves to the mission of breaking through to prepare the way for the Lord's coming.

Not fearing criticism and not rejoicing in praise, I forget what is behind and press on toward the goal for the prize of the upward call of God in Christ Jesus. I press on toward the goal for the prize of the upward call of God in Christ Jesus. -Phil. 3:14
For the minister, the greatest gift to the flock is personal holiness before the Lord.
When you do your best, you gain confidence. That's why someone who has read everything can do better than someone in a good environment.
Loyalty is not about choosing and keeping by your side those who please you. Because you are called to love, you look only to the Lord and cover everyone with the heart given by the Lord.
Praise tests people. They are tested whether they will become arrogant by receiving more praise or jealous by receiving less praise.
Praise refines people. God is looking for those who can be humbled when they are exalted and serve.

Whether holding the hilt or the blade in a desperate situation, a Christian's attitude is always consistent. They do not become arrogant in favorable situations, nor do they despair in unfavorable situations. Only the Lord establishes me and leads me.

After performing miracles and wonders, Kathryn Kuhlman struggled and wept backstage. It was to fight against pride. After becoming busy and famous, one can become insignificant in heaven. Please be humbled.

Toddlers brag about getting stickers or Pokémon cards. As they grow, they brag about computers, mobile phones, and pretty things. Strangely, as adults, they brag about drinking well, mixing drinks well, and having miscellaneous information about alcohol. Bragging reveals one's level. Those who brag about alcohol do so to compensate for their inferiority complex regarding power and status. Woe to those who are brave enough to drink wine and skillfully brew strong liquor (bomb shots) - Isaiah 5:22. The essence of humanity is the pursuit of power. (The Will to Power) - Nietzsche

No matter how much you praise, confess your love, and train well, if your heart is not continuously drawn to the presence of Jesus, you have lost 'that love.' You must realize that loving Jesus is not just about doing something. Even with much effort, it is all in vain without a burning love for the Lord. Moving towards a deep relationship with the Lord every moment demands a certain payment from me.

The greatest comfort for a Christian is that the Lord knows their heart. If you do not have the heart to lose everything to gain Him alone, everything becomes a problem and a test. Clarify your purpose. It will be determined who your master is.
The true and complete power of a Christian is not to point fingers but to bear the cross where they stand. God's solution is always people. Revival and repentance begin with me.

The way Christians live in the world is not the old way. It is not the way of unbelievers. It means not living in crisis where being pushed once means being pushed to the end. We live as sheep among wolves. Then you will experience the comfort of the Lord's rod and staff. Do not become a wolf. You are a sheep walking with the Lord.
God gives Christians two simple ways to exert influence: 'holiness' and 'truth.' Even if 'holiness' is broken in any area, facing it with at least 'truth' will lead to repentance.
Living truthfully in your own way is far more meaningful and happy than succeeding and living well by imitating others. It is blessed to have Him increase and me decrease.

The prodigal son leaves his home and father for a better life. Perhaps he rationalized that he could strike a balance between the freedom of deviation and a compromised faith. Only when he enters the pigsty does he realize that from the point of compromise, which began in pursuit of happiness, he becomes even more unhappy. Faith that has lost its holiness and savor becomes emptier and more distant the more it pursues something. The more you rationalize, the farther the path back becomes. The Lord does not compromise.

A transparent life has nothing to worry about. There is nothing to calculate. Because you can be beautiful just as you are, you do not need to add or subtract anything, but focus on the goal, running the race set by the Lord.

The fervent love given especially to mothers for their children is because raising a life requires such a great price and sacrifice. To willingly shed tears of great sacrifice and not grow weary, 'that love' is necessary. Those who receive the Father's heart release heavenly miracles on this earth.

The Lord's will and help are more important than reputation. Worldly reputation is not important; rather, history changes because of one prepared person like Hezekiah. The Lord prepares and expects people.

Exercising violence is because one is weak. Therefore, they try to overpower the other person to overcome that inferiority. All abusive language and violence stem from a desire to prove oneself. It means one lacks confidence. Those who truly possess authority and power are gentle. This is because they no longer need to prove themselves. In the Lord, there is no need for further proof; we only testify of the Lord.

David, who was pleasing to God's heart, is promised that God will fulfill all His will through him. (Acts 13:22) Isn't that wonderful? A person of faith who is pleasing to God's heart and fulfills His will, can I also become such a person? Why not me? David was a man of repentance. The two things he asked for in repentance in Psalm 51... show what kind of person is pleasing to God's heart. 1. Do not cast me away from your presence. I do not want my honor, money, status, or vested interests. I only want the Lord. 2. Do not take your Holy Spirit from me. Your presence is my life. The greatest judgment is the absence of the Lord, so he is desperate to hold onto His presence. Have you fallen? Become this kind of person. The cross is the conclusion.
I always ask myself a question every moment: What do I want, and why am I here? The answer is always the same: 'I want to see the glory of the Lord!!'

I am here solely because of the Lord. Therefore, I can overcome everything and do not care. I can smile in the face of human voices, spiritual challenges, and sometimes overwhelming situations. Because the Lord is my only vision.

What is important before God is who you are and how you live. Your worship must be worship that pursues truth in your spirit. The Father is looking for people like that, people who worship Him simply and honestly as they are before Him. (John 4:23 Message Bible)

The only authenticity of belonging to God is 'loving.' When you believe in Jesus, God dwells within you, and the most powerful characteristic of this 'indwelling' is loving. This is how the authenticity of faith is revealed. Jesus teaches neighborly love to those who ask about eternal life. Loving belongs to God. 1 John 4:7 ... Love is from God,
To live a life approved by God, one must form distinct habits from a young age. Daniel resolves not to drink wine from his youth.
To know what I react to, I must dig into the roots of my distress. When I discover them, they are truly shameful to reveal. People do not get jealous of dogs because they are not on the same level.

When God designed me, He did not make me 'able to do well on my own' from the beginning. I am made to reach my highest potential when I become one in the Lord, pray for others, and receive prayer. This happens when I become a being who can do nothing without 'grace.'

If God were to paint us on a canvas, He would paint us beautifully. He does not love us because we have no flaws, but because He loves us, He covers our other aspects. I hope you will put down the self-portrait you have drawn and hang the portrait that the Lord has drawn. The Lord does not see the iniquities of Jacob, nor does He behold the perverseness of Israel. The Lord his God is with him, and the sound of the trumpet is among them. - Numbers 23:21 The Lord says, "Come now, let us reason together, though your sins are like scarlet, they shall be as white as snow; though they are red like crimson, they shall be like wool." - Isaiah 1:18
What God desires is not 'success' but my 'transformation.'

The reason God has set us apart from all nations and given us special grace is that He Himself dwells with His people. Walking with God, His presence, is the greatest sign and blessing. Become holy. By the power of His precious blood...
If you only look to God, human evaluation becomes meaningless. With the simple words, 'God knows,' even life is not too precious to give.

When you hope in God alone, people's evaluations become meaningless, and you are freed from all gazes. You are not elevated by being praised, nor lowered by being despised. It all comes from the Lord alone.

God reveals our hidden darkness through suffering and pain. It is difficult for what is hidden to be revealed, but it is unavoidable when we approach the Lord in holiness and completeness. We become whole in proportion to the depth of our pain.

God finds me when I am at my lowest and awakens me to 'my existence.' Then, He renews me. Knowing who I am, how I was saved, who paid what price, and what love I received gives me the strength to rise again.

God does not immediately demand that I become a perfect person. That will take a long time. He only wants honesty. Honesty requires only a decision. Tax collectors and prostitutes can be honest. I am a prostitute. I am a tax collector. But the Pharisees were not like that; they were hypocritical. That is where Jesus' evaluation differs.

God sees David differently because David's purpose was different from others. Even while being chased by enemies, and even when he had to step down from the throne and face the threat of death from his son, what he truly missed was worship in the temple, that is, seeing God. If the subject of your longing changes, His grace will not be withdrawn even after thousands of years.

Before meeting David, God had no particular intention of establishing a king or sending the Messiah through his family. Abraham's blessing reached David, and through the 'covenant made with David,' He used his descendants as kings, and eventually, the Messiah was born. I hope you become a person whose heart is so aligned that it creates thoughts that were not there.

God does not evaluate people by the importance of their assigned tasks. He does not ask where they belong or what their title is. He only desires how they respond before God and what they look like standing there.
When God uses people, He does not entrust them with current tasks based on their past achievements. David was young and inexperienced, but he loved the Lord and confessed that He was his strength.
God does not want us to remain people whose hearts are broken by the expressions of others. He wants us to be more sensitive to the joy of the Lord.

God created us 'absolutely.' We are not relative beings. Therefore, we should not compare ourselves. Comparing ourselves at work or school makes us incredibly unhappy, and suddenly so. Only the Lord determines my path. Be courageous.

God wants us to rule the earth (Gen 1), cultivate the earth (Ch 2), and bless the earth (Ch 12). He also wants us to make disciples and change the world (Matt 28). We are called to enter deeply into the world, not to be corrupted, but to participate with the power of the Holy Spirit, to participate as experts with biblical principles, and to change the world as God's representatives. Become an expert filled with the Holy Spirit and wisdom.
God has given us people to love and resources to use. However, people often love resources and use people.

God is not one who fulfills our greed. Jesus came as a suffering servant, but He came as a servant suffering in my place, though He was sinless, not to be a servant we can command at will, but to cleanse sins and make us holy. Rather, we must stand as holy servants following Jesus. Like Jesus, we must be transformed into a holy life with anointing and purity.
God has already given us something precious, though small. If we are not satisfied with what we have, we become depressed or angry. Eventually, we fall into sin and suffer great shame.

God is not bewildered by the corruption in established institutions. He leaves 7,000 who have not bowed to Baal and 300 who pray. God will use many professionals as missionaries. All are royal priests. If the Lord has called you, do not limit yourself by being a layperson.

God's rule came to the Egyptian court, which did not know God, and to the Babylonian offices that worshipped idols, because there were Joseph and Daniel, who were inspired by God's spirit and walked with the Lord. They are not of the world but are not separated from it; they do not become corrupted but rather bring about change. - John 17:14

Those who truly worship God in spirit become new people. When we see God and His glory, we cast aside our sinful perspective and gain the heart with which the Lord sees. Therefore, those who fail in 'worship' will inevitably fail in 'relationships.' Only through worship can we discover our true selves and be free from hatred and misunderstanding.

If you have a burning love for God, you have already built an invisible temple, even without building a physical one. You are the temple. Eph 2:22. You can see God when you go to the temple. The light of God shines on those who meet me.
God's concern is not our skill and ability to handle problems, but our attitude towards problems.
Those filled with God's power do not rush around in a panic. Rather, they are people who give challenges and pursue them.
When you understand God's will, your life, like a dry stick in my hand, becomes God's staff. Joseph secured the shortest distance to Pharaoh's palace rather than becoming a prisoner.

God's word prepares me, purifies me, comforts me, transforms me, and gives me power. Thus, it makes me 'God's letter sent to the world.' You are a letter from Christ, revealed through us, written not with ink but with the Spirit of the living God, not on stone tablets but on tablets of human hearts. - 2 Cor 3:3. You need no introduction or recommendation. Look at the dew of the morning that has been with us.

A strong person is one who can lose everything. When you are willing to lose everything except Jesus, everything suddenly becomes easy. If you cling, you will be robbed, but if you let go and gain the Lord, He will give you all that you need.
When you try to live by faith, you truly experience death. It is not pretending to die, but completely dying and being buried. And then you are reborn.

God's individuals are never mass-produced. The fact that young Mary readily said Amen to the angel's words without saying 'I will consult my mother' means she was a person who had undergone immense training. Do not be amazed only by the conclusion, but receive grace in the process.

God's work can never be done by position, seniority in faith, or background. Just as animals live by instinct and wild beasts survive by their wildness, Christians live solely by the spirituality of gratitude and awe.

Trying to do God's work yourself is pride, and leaving your own work to God is licentiousness. The characteristic of such people is that they do not repent. Without repentance, they cannot meet God. Repentance means laying oneself down before the cross.

Those whom God calls have respect in their relationships and honor those who deserve respect. However, this is not to seek the pleasure of people. If you sought the pleasure of people, you are not a servant of God. Will you please people? Or will you please God?
God's standard for using people is not usefulness or competence, but 'surrender.'

If God were to give you a great reward and comfort one day, your expectations would be immense. You would let your imagination run wild. A nice car and house, a wonderful spouse, and so on. But if that great reward and comfort were to be God Himself giving Himself to you, would you be disappointed? Or would you be even more joyful? If your exclamation is 'Oh~' when He says He will give Himself, you are far away.
What God wants is not competence. He alone is ineffably competent. He wants loyalty. He considers laziness evil. Fulfill your mission. The mission will carry me.
God gave it. He lent it. If you mistake a temporary 'right to use' for eternal 'ownership,' you will immediately become unhappy. Possession makes us compare, while use makes us wise.

'Pride' is impersonation, stealing glory as if one did it oneself in God's stead. 'Frustration' is a false report, denying the living God and declaring Him dead. *Violation of laws regarding false claims of qualification, violation of laws regarding false reporting.
The heavens above the heavens, the Lord who is greater than them, desires my small and lowly heart.

Those who know the secrets of heaven never compete. "Let the one who is in humble circumstances boast of their exaltation. But let the rich boast of their humiliation, because like a wild flower, the rich will fade away." James 1:9-10

If the president or king of a nation feels unhappy because they don't have a lollipop, it means they are not a king but a snot-nosed child playing king in the alley. The Bible says I am a royal priesthood. I hope that lack does not become unhappiness, but that the level (standard) becomes the solution.

I see one person's change not only changing their family and friends but also being used as a pillar in the body of the church. I will also realize that the worship and encounters I once had were of the highest value to me. That means I will regret later that the worship and companions who were measured out to me now were truly the worship and beloved people of my dreams, and that I could have given more. Therefore, we must love so passionately that we cannot give more. Now.
Being called as a single grain of wheat means you were not sent to judge or analyze others. The calling of a grain of wheat is to perish. Deeply~

When you are afraid, you defend yourself, and in that process, defense is achieved through competition, avoidance, jealousy, victim mentality, obsession, anger, and guilt, but instead, it causes deep harm. When you die with Christ on the cross, you no longer need to protect yourself, and conversely, weakness and death become beneficial. This happens when you always believe and hope in the Lord.

When you feel endlessly shabby, or when you can only sigh, thinking 'What can I do?', you must look to the glorious Lord and that you are in Him. If Satan reminds you of your past, I want you to remind Satan of his future.
One soul, one soul, is not made just like that. I am proud of you.

There was an office worker who worked at an insurance company. Due to harsh wage cuts and restructuring, his mind was troubled with anxiety and worry about the future. The world's standard of recession had broken his peace. A mother named 'Ria Miller' left a note for her son like this: 'There is nothing more precious than the Lord Jesus, nothing that can be exchanged for worldly wealth.' Her son, 'Beverly Share,' was greatly shocked and turned to compose a hymn. 'There is nothing more precious than the Lord Jesus, nothing that can be exchanged for worldly wealth.' ... 'I cast away all worldly pleasures, I cast away all worldly boasts. There is nothing more precious than the Lord Jesus, there is nothing outside of Jesus.' - Hymn 102 If the order (Priority) of GOD changes, it becomes DOG.

Koreans don't hesitate to immigrate for their children's education. However, they are so busy supporting them that they are stingy with spending time with their children and building relationships. It's like cooking rice and giving it to dogs. More precious than academic achievement is the child as a life, the child itself.

It was just yesterday that the Korean church had 12 million members, but now we see 6.5 million. That seems to be a slight exaggeration. Large churches can still hold on, but they are often targets in the internet and media. That's why people say the Korean church is in crisis. Nevertheless, I say there is hope. Because God is preparing diaspora youth who have not bowed down to Baal. I see the blood of the cross in your eyes. That means I see hope for the church in my homeland. From the slavery in Egypt, I see a Moses. Do not just lament and criticize, but become Moses yourselves.
As I came to Korea, I think this: I will not stop, and I will pursue what I was seized by Christ Jesus toward the goal. Everything else can be controlled and restrained. If the purpose is clear...

In traditional Korean weddings, there is something called 'Napchae.' When both families unite through marriage, it is a ceremony to first inform the spirits of both families. The Israelites were competitive, worthless transients after 400 years of slavery and 40 years in the wilderness. To live in Canaan, like Korean weddings, they had to exchange idols of the gods they believed in for trade and exchange, and to gain know-how in farming. However, God told them not to live that way. He wanted them to hold onto God's word stubbornly, even if it meant reaping less, receiving less recognition, and being ignored. The God who delivered them from Egypt and the wilderness would deliver them in Canaan and use them as a channel of blessing to all nations. Israel perished in Canaan because they feared people, flattered them, and even tolerated the idols they worshipped. We must truly believe that there is no one who can lower whom the Lord exalts, and no one who can exalt whom He lowers. The Lord alone is my strength.
Instead of trying to overcome my limitations, I should try to remain in Jesus. Jesus is the One who is not bound by limitations. It is His presence, not achievement.
Chocolate melts in the sun, but mud hardens. Some people become stronger when they face the same trials. Shine on me a little longer, until my edge is razor-sharp...

It seems that if you shed the burden of dedication, much productivity will be guaranteed. Soon you will see how meaninglessly the leisure gained by abandoning loyalty disappears. If performance and efficiency were the goal, God would have made us terminators or livestock. Faith is not efficiency, but relationship. Please, stay with the Lord.

In modern times, there are countless teachings and many expressions. They offer their own interpretations and solutions to many difficult problems. How did the saints confess and move forward in the past? "I will lay down my life for the Lord too." There is nothing to boast about. Without suffering, there is nothing to fear. In Jesus. There are no problems before a martyr. For those who cherish their lives, everything is a problem.
Hold on. It is holy to guard your position.

The world's best female figure skater currently is Kim Yuna. Mao Asada is also a good skater, but she cannot be the best. This is because her dream is the same as Kim Yuna, the world's best. "Let's beat Kim Yuna!" - Kim Yuna "Let's beat Kim Yuna!" - Mao Asada "Each of you should examine your own conduct. Then you can be proud of yourself alone, and not in comparison to someone else. For each of you will have to bear your own load." Galatians 6:4

When his brothers perceived Joseph as merely their father's son, not their brother, they tried to kill him. However, when Joseph forgave his hurt brothers as family, God's salvation came upon everyone. The people beside me, my co-workers, are like angels sent by God Himself to love. When they become my brother, not 'your son,' restoration, healing, and salvation occur. Love leads to forgiveness, not forgiveness to love. Love the people God has placed beside me with all your heart. This is what God earnestly desires and asks of me at this very moment. But when this son of yours who has squandered your property with prostitutes comes home, you kill the fattened calf for him!' - Luke 15:30 / The brother's perception of the prodigal son. They took the ornamented robe back to their father and said, "We found this. Examine it to see whether it is your son's robe." - Genesis 37:32 / The brothers' perception of Joseph. "He is not ashamed to call them brothers." - Hebrews 2:11 / Jesus' perception of us.

I am not grateful and joyful because my circumstances are good. The key is who I live for. Some people find that precious life burdensome, while for others, even death becomes a gain. "For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain." - Philippians 1:21 Because my purpose is to live for Christ, even death is beneficial to me. - Easy-to-Read Version Philippians 1:21
Do not be afraid that you no longer have dazzling human relationships. Rather, you should fear that your sincere relationship with God is weakening.

I do not want to give false hope in the name of faith. The Lord presents the same level of challenges to those who follow Him from the beginning and to those who have followed Him for a long time. 'Who is your master? Whom do you serve? Who lives within you? Where will your allegiance lie?' Faith is surprisingly simple. People just make it complicated. This happens when what you pursue is not the goal. "Sell your possessions and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me." - Matthew 19:21
If you are not validated, your heart will feel like it's about to collapse. Do not be afraid; rather, let yourself collapse and become dust. God gives a new heart in that broken place.
If you are swayed by your environment, the world, or people, you will live a life without peace. If you seek the approval of people, you are at least not a servant of Christ. - Galatians 1:10
There are people who want to be welcomed and people who welcome. They seem similar, but the results are worlds apart. Welcome others, and you will be welcomed.

The Lord's first sign that repentance and a change of direction are needed is the disappearance of joy in worship and a willing spirit. A person who realizes their state and feels that the true heavenly joy given by the Lord cannot be exchanged for anything else is a person who loves the Lord.
Companies move in the direction of profit. However, believers must make decisions that bring them closer to the Lord. Woe to those who move and expect to please people.

In the company's contract template, there is a section for "Party's Bargaining Position." It asks who is the "갑" (the superior party). In the world, this is extremely important. Daniel also faced a king who held his life and death in his hands, and rivals who constantly sought his life and were organized against him. They even resorted to racist attacks against Daniel, who, at 89 years old, had served four kings and been prime minister for 75 years, calling him a "captive from Judah." Daniel's method of confronting this was to "completely ignore them" and continue serving the Lord as he always did. "I serve only the Lord!"
A life lived without regret is a life well-lived. To stand before the Lord without regret, two things must be clear: one is the goal, and the other is to run towards it with passion.

Often, when people try to prove their love, they give excessive gifts or plan events to evoke emotion. However, all of these can be imitated momentarily. But sharing in "pain" and "continuously empathizing with that pain without change" can never be imitated. Only when we become one does his pain become my pain.

People often overreact excessively when the other person responds less than usual or is friendly with someone else. This leads to the conclusion that they are not flying high. When the altitude is low, they are affected by all sorts of flies, mosquitoes, and sparrows. Those whose conclusion is the Lord fly in the heavens. Therefore, they do not cling to earthly matters.

People often like to divide into factions: my side and the enemy, good people and problematic people. However, this way of thinking is wrong from the start. In the Bible, the people Jesus welcomed and befriended had serious problems with their personalities and characters, and their professions were not well-received. Among them, the fact that "I" am the chief among sinners is particularly true. If I do not see myself as a sinner, I have not passed through the cross.
You came from dust, and to dust you shall return. There is no reason to cling tightly. Only He knows and remembers me.
When the spirit of selfishness is released, it is ashamed and flees.

If you boarded a bus marked for Heathrow, you must go to Heathrow. You cannot go to Gatwick, which is in the opposite direction. If you have decided to look to the Lord, you must run towards Him alone. To aim for anything else is deceit and theft.

The principle of the world is that the strong prey on the weak. Therefore, many people strive to have sharper claws and stronger fangs. However, those who follow the Lord do not live by fighting others. They live because they hear the Shepherd's voice.

Thoughts on Parting. When I was in college, a renowned psychology professor suddenly asked me a personal question: "Mr. Hwaseong, what kind of person do you want to become?" Without a moment's hesitation, my reflexive answer surprised even me: "I want to be a person who inspires." I realize how quickly five years have passed. It was a bold statement, but I deeply realize it is not as easy as it sounds. Forget inspiring; I would be grateful just not to hurt anyone and not to block the Lord's path. I wanted them to be happy. So, when I was called, I thought it would be grateful and meaningful to be used as a mere 6-month broom for God's glory among the youth, even though I lacked skill and qualification. I cooked, drove, led worship, and visited, but more than serving, I loved them, and it was ecstatic. We laughed together, cried together, and even burned with longing, yet I saw the glory of heaven. It felt less like ministry and more like love. Before being a minister, I was happy to be just one of the beautiful young people. I tried hard to give, but I received nothing; instead, I was richly blessed. Walking away without looking back, I found myself unexpectedly wealthy. My daily life has returned, but today, my hands cannot focus on work. Jesus' cross takes precedence over everything. When breathing, when the heart beats, when storms and waves arise, when climbing brilliantly to a high place, we can think of and look at the cross. It was happy and truly glorious to be together. Missionary Kim Hwaseong.

There is the concept of "walking with two legs," meaning balance. It refers to legs straddling both the Lord and the world. The intention is to move to the side that incurs no damage if things go wrong. This is a truly wrong way of thinking. How can one think they can straddle two legs when the distance between the Lord and the world is so vast? My condolences.

If Noah had managed his drinking well, and if Samson had handled his relationships with women well, history would have truly changed. People are not tested by their strengths. A person of character is one who handles their weaknesses well. Try to overcome the sins you constantly fall into. You will become a Samson without relationship problems. Faith is surprisingly simple: simple, intense, flexible.

The question of "what I have become" must be nailed to the cross. The truth and conclusion are that it has become so by God's grace. It should not be brought up again for debate but should be a moment of gratitude that is never enough. Foolish people forget grace and compare themselves. Instead of judging yourself by comparison, you should worship with gratitude.

All meaning and value in my life come solely from the Lord. When I view others and my circumstances through that truth, I rejoice in the meaning given by the Lord and give Him glory. The Lord alone is my reason. "Unless one has been given from above, no one can receive anything." - John 3:27
Oh happy day, I will never be the same.

*Don't try to be better rather than to be you, for you are his workmanship. Attempts to appear as a slightly better person are futile. Life becomes wasted and empty, and secret sins enter when you think too deeply about what others think of you. There is only one word I need to hear: "Good and faithful servant."

*God never let you down nor you can let Him down. The greatest pain for a person is disappointing someone they love. Especially when they disappoint God, they hurt immensely and become angry with themselves. They want to disappear into the ground, abandoning everything. However, fundamentally, I cannot disappoint God. I think I disappoint God by holding onto Him incorrectly. Rather, it is because God holds me with His righteous right hand that I am here. The Lord knew my weakness from the beginning, which is why He bore the cross, and therefore, He is not disappointed nor will He ever let me fail.

Paul was a man of power, yet he asked the Thessalonians to manage and deliver the pledged relief funds in an orderly and transparent manner. Why don't you pray to open the windows of heaven and pour down money? Because another undeniable expression of miracles is the transformation of the heart. The kingdom of God must advance through sacrifice and love.
I will not collapse. Because I cannot collapse. As long as Jesus, who is my soul and life, is with me, I pursue the goal.
What I have done must be forgotten because there is an eternal goal. I strive to be grasped by Christ, but I have not fully attained it. Therefore, I relentlessly pursue Him, who is that goal.
The happiest moments for me are when the Lord makes me weep.

Moses departs beautifully, not shamefully. This is because he lived each day as if it were his last, faithfully fulfilling his mission with all his might. One who has fully accomplished their mission is pure.

Blessed are those who change churches, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven; blessed are those who persistently purify the hearts of others, for they shall see God; blessed are those who pass exams and get promoted, or are recognized for their true worth, for they shall inherit the earth – such words do not exist. It may seem like happiness will come if the environment changes or the other person changes, but without my poor spirit, my mourning, my peacemaking, and my holiness, it is all in vain.
Also, we become like what we love.

Animals all boast of physical attributes. However, humans, God's greatest masterpiece, should not boast of such things. We should boast not of ourselves but of Him. The essence of social media is, in a word, boasting: what we wear, whom we meet, what we eat, what we see. However, we boast only of Jesus. That is the difference between us and animals.

The reason I get hurt is not because the other person is wicked, but because of my selfishness. Selfishness is self-deception, a delusion, where I try to satisfy myself with illusions rather than truth to protect myself. If you understand the Father's heart, you choose healing over hurt. You realize that he and she, including myself, are ultimately objects of salvation.

Whether the south wind blows or the northwest wind blows, we just need to go on, saying Hallelujah and Amen, spreading the fragrance of Jesus, who became a lily among thorns. No matter what anyone says, no matter what misunderstandings arise, no matter the world's evaluation, we just need to live that way.

A head of a household does not sit idly and spread out. It is because there are dependents. A person with a mission does not lie fallen. It is because they are saviors of lives. We will rest eternally in heaven anyway. I urge you to run for glory.
As always, and as if this were the last time, I stake my life.
If I have too much of myself within me, I wander. Do not be distressed, but do what the Lord desires. Knowing alone is not enough; it requires obedient faith.

There is no better place of ministry for me. There is no better youth group in the world for me. There is no better opportunity for me. This is because the Almighty Lord has already given me all of these.

Many people blame their circumstances and others. They seek the cause of their unhappiness in others. However, this is the result of letting go of what they should hold onto and grasping what they should not. It is unhappy to be a victim. Do not be a victim; become a wounded healer.

One of the most foolish things is to shift one's own shortcomings onto others. It is not a matter of throwing stones at others, but of holding onto the cross and breaking one's own heart. I am an unfaithful servant living with a heart that is so broken it might disappear.

Do not carry too many burdens. That too is greed. Carry the burdens the Lord has given you. Your heart will become gentle and humble, and those burdens will be light. To boldly let go of what has not been given is called 'peace,' and to hold onto what has been given until the end is called 'courage.' "Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy and my burden is light." - Matthew 11:29

I complain that my colleagues do not cooperate, the system does not support me, and people misunderstand me for no reason. No! It is only my worship that has collapsed. Worship is the beginning of all restoration.

A new employee who just joined a large corporation after working part-time would be extremely offended if asked to clean the trash can and mop the floor. However, the chairman of that company receives respect and glory when he does such things. A strong and noble person can easily humble themselves to serve, and that becomes glory, but a weak and defensive person cannot humble themselves and considers it shame. True strength is serving without anger and being able to humble oneself. Jesus humbled himself infinitely and bore the cross.

He sent away 20,000 people and, with only 300, defeated an allied army of 135,000. To be more precise, it was a war that could only be won if 20,000 were sent back. God used Gideon, who was like barley bread. To be exact, He used him precisely because he was like barley bread. Many people boast of vain, perishable things. They boast because of their numbers or their high status, but in reality, it is precisely because of these things that they are not used. I had a dream, and in my dream, a piece of barley bread rolled into the Midianite camp and reached a tent, and it struck and overthrew it, and it fell down. - Judges 7:13

The me that others evaluate is not my reality. Whether they praise or criticize, they are speaking without truly knowing my essence. It is like calling a heinous criminal who has committed a thousand unforgivable atrocities a thief who stole a needle and expecting them to become enraged. The groundless rumors and criticisms of people are foolish and narrow-minded, but my sinful nature is more wicked than that. No progress will be made without breaking my idols. I am the chief of sinners.

A proud person is truly unhappy. This is because they look to others for the reason why things are not working out. Being discouraged does not mean being humble. The more proud and self-centered one is, the lower their self-esteem and the easier it is to be frustrated. This is because of comparison. One whose center is not the Lord tries to confirm themselves through creation. Trying to muster strength and courage without having died is running in the opposite direction of the destination. Look at yourself in the Lord. You will become humble. "Pride goes before destruction, and an arrogant spirit before a fall." - Proverbs 18:12

If you are disappointed that there is nothing good within you, it means you are proud. A humble person does not expect anything from themselves; rather, they desire only to die to themselves and live only by Jesus. Therefore, they are not frustrated by their own state but are captivated by the Lord and rejoice.

Whether in a peaceful bed where everything is forgotten or in a pressing situation of immense pressure, God wants to confirm that He is the center of my being and that the Lord is the center of my heart. "So whether we live or die, we belong to the Lord." - Romans 14:8

*The Calling There is a most meaningful and happy life to which the Lord calls us. The conclusion is simple. Either walk that path simply, or be blinded on the road to Damascus, kicking against the goads, and suffer greatly... Ultimately, the ball must return to the goal.

It is not a large number that is needed. The Bible says boasting about numbers is foolish. Gideon's 300 devoted men are warriors, but tens of thousands are simply called a crowd. Do not be a crowd; be a warrior. That is the kind of person the Lord uses.

We mistakenly think that we can give glory to God when we achieve something. God is already supremely exalted and possesses all authority; why would He need anything from us? The only way to glorify God is for us to be glorified. This is achieved only by grace, by denying the self, and by relinquishing rights. Only then will the Lord receive glory. He has never received glory through my self-realization. "Jesus looked toward heaven and prayed, 'Father, the hour has come. Glorify your Son, that your Son may glorify you.'" - John 17:1

Picasso's 1943 sculpture Bull's Head is Picasso's famous Bull's Head. One day, Picasso saw a bicycle in a junkyard. What others could not see, Picasso saw, and by attaching the handlebars and the seat, he created this work. Picasso was from Spain, the 'land of bullfighting.' When people are 'immersed,' they draw closer to their origin. From there, with a unique perspective that others cannot see, he saw the bull from his childhood. When there is 'immersion' where I pour my heart out to the Lord, I discover the Lord there. Immersion: "I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which Christ Jesus has called me heavenward. Not that I have already obtained all this, or have been made perfect, but I press on so that I may make it my own, because Christ Jesus has made me his own." - Philippians 3:12

Because I know better than anyone else what is more important, I can let go of less important things better than anyone else. Without counting the things I have, I run toward the prize for which Christ Jesus has called me heavenward. Those who have discovered the highest value live simply.

In the parable Nathan told King David, the compensation for a stolen sheep was legally fourfold. However, David was enraged and declared that the perpetrator must be put to death. David, who avoided repentance for a year after the Bathsheba incident, was insensitive to himself but enraged by others. The characteristic of hardness of heart is always finding the cause in others.
If you truly desire something, you will pursue it. If you desire something but do not pursue it, it means you did not truly desire it. You will continuously pursue only what you desire.
Instead of blaming others, I must repent and confess. The problem is in the first person. If I change, it will become a good community.
A simple way to become a leader. It is to resolve to have an all-weather faith that is not easily offended in any situation. A leader has no vacation.

*The Reason for Shedding Blood We do not understand why God chose to love us. Of course, we cannot understand. It is not that He 'chose to love us based on evaluation,' but rather 'He chose us because He loves us.' This is why we often fail to understand even our mother's heart. In our teens, our mother's interference, such as our accessories, celebrity gossip, favorite hoodies, friends' opinions, and the frequency and hidden meanings of words in texts and messages, sometimes feels much more bothersome than things we cherish. However, when we ourselves give birth to a new life that we would give our lives to love, we stay up all night to feed and nurse it. At that time, we realize that love is sacrifice.

The reason flowers are beautiful is because there are flowers within me. However, sometimes we must walk toward where the flowers are blooming. This is because the blooming flowers cause the flowers within me to bloom as well. Within people, there are both flowers and filth. What reacts depends on what we encounter. Be with good people.

*True Repentance A confession after committing a wrong, saying, "I will never do that again," is not repentance. We are that kind of beings; how can we not live that way? Rather, it is: "If the Lord does not grant me forgiveness and help, I have no choice but to live this way. This is because I am inherently this kind of person." After a desperate recognition of oneself, a new beginning comes through the Lord's forgiveness.
The devil says, 'I will give you what you so desire.' Tell the devil, 'How can you possibly give me Jesus?' If your longing is for the Lord, people and the world cannot control you.

I always ask myself a question: "What are you doing right now?" Each time, a confession reflexively comes out: "I want to see the glory of God!" Behind that question and answer, there is a warmth in my heart. It is a blessing to know what I want and what alone satisfies me.
The reason David was strong and recovered quickly can be seen by looking at the Psalms. He constantly confessed and proclaimed to himself, to God, and to the world.
*A Life Well-Lived If my daughters ask me in the distant future, I may not have much to show them. However, I will not be ashamed. If nothing remains because I burned it all for the Lord...

*True Purpose There is no need to become great in this life. Doing so leads to strain and side effects. Moreover, since there is already glory in the Lord, there is no reason to seek worldly glory. In the face of the Lord's glory, worldly things are merely like fireflies.

*The Lord Being Lord King Saul lived obsessively, conscious of others' gazes, but David lived desiring and letting go of the Lord's presence. Though he had more, he was always anxious about not having more, but he has peace through the grace that pours out, even though he has nothing. This is because the master they served was different. What am I chasing, and what are my motivations? "Saul was very angry at this saying. 'David has struck down ten thousand, but I have struck down only thousands. What more can he get but the kingdom?'" - 1 Samuel 18:8

Though it hurts, there is a story you must hear. When you are in a relationship, change inevitably occurs. It's a change you know, and those around you know it too. The Lord says He has something to rebuke the church in Ephesus for, even as they endure persecution, holding fast to the gospel without compromise. It is that they have abandoned their first love. This was most important to the Lord. It is a serious illness to live a faith life pretending to believe and pretending to love. We must be honest and sincere. I hope you will reflect on how you use your time, your interests, and what you truly desire at your core. It is possible that you do not truly, passionately love the Lord. If you do not have intimate time with the Lord and do not pay the price of sacrifice, you must admit that your love has grown cold. What marriage in the world exists without private times of intimacy? This is a grave insult to Jesus, our bridegroom.

Relationships are important in life. However, when relationships replace the Lord's place, it becomes idolatry. Relationships that the Lord has not given, or an obsession with relationships, is evidence of this. The Lord is not a mere bystander, and we must gain insight through the joy of the bridegroom's friend that John the Baptist possessed. When the bride is taken, the bridegroom and his friends celebrate and rejoice.

Victory over trials. Difficult, extreme trials cease when the self is crucified. When you carry your cross and die, the trials end there. When Judas, who had sold his brother, took responsibility for Benjamin to save his family, all the tests ended beautifully. "Now, therefore, let your servant remain instead of the boy as a slave to my lord, and let the boy go up with his brothers. For how can I go up to my master without the boy? I fear to see the grief that would overcome my father." Genesis 44:33
God's sacrifice. God's sacrifice is not loud or resentful. It becomes a perfect sacrifice, like the Lord who was silent. He made no excuses.

The purpose of the wilderness and the tabernacle is to make a prepared people for God. Preparation means seeking the Lord's face and becoming a life that obeys, not one that lives by bread alone, with God as Lord.

The importance of encounters. Encounters are truly important. Through those encounters, we are influenced in what we will value in life, and if our life's purpose is corrected, our entire life and our eternal dwelling place are determined. For what is our hope, or joy, or crown of boasting? Is it not even you in the presence of our Lord Jesus at his coming? For you are our glory and joy. 1 Thessalonians 2:19-20
One of my missions is to raise up people who can never be satisfied with imitations or substitutes. It is to become Christians who, with that holy dissatisfaction, run ceaselessly for Jesus alone.

More important than having more than others or living longer than others is how we use our lives. What the world calls blessings may not be blessings, and what are called difficulties can become blessings that draw us closer to God.
All true ministry comes out of intimacy with Jesus.

When you love, you are not afraid, and you overcome even things that should be feared. Ministry and love are synonymous. A minister must be a loving person. A loving person does not grudgingly squeeze out something from dryness. Rather, with overflowing emotion, passion, and the tears of God, they approach souls, shedding their blood. Loving God and loving souls is the most powerful ministry. "Simon, son of John, do you love me?"... "You know that I love you." Jesus said to him, "Feed my sheep." John 21:17

The design of time and life. Life's priorities determine the quality of my life. Time is fair to everyone, but the content that fills that time is different for each person, and the results also differ. When a person's affections change, the quality of their life changes.

Demonic possession uses the word "possession" because Satan compels. However, the Holy Spirit inspires. From there comes a willing spirit. Only humans, made in the image of God, can offer freedom and love the Lord.

I am not a chef, but I cook. I am not a professional minister, but I minister. I am not a driver, but I drive. I am clumsy at love, but I love. Why? Because the Lord loved me first, a chief of sinners. Ah, one more thing... I am not a writer, but I write. I am not a poet, but poetry flows from me. ^^ "I am not a prophet, nor a prophet's son, but I am a herdsman and a dresser of sycamore trees." Amos 7:14

When we realize the faults of others, it is because we have the same flaws within us. Jacob met Laban, and the younger brother who had deceived his brother and stolen his blessing met Leah, who pretended to be his sister and married him.

I will not regret it even if I perish unknown, upholding the Lord's name where I stand. For He knows me, and I know Him. If I can seek only His glory, with all vain glory vanishing, that is the eternal inheritance and blessing for this 부족한 자 (insufficient one).

When Park Ji-sung played against Japan for the Olympic bronze medal, he probably didn't feel it was difficult or want to sit on the bench and rest. Because the Lord is with me, everything that happens to me is a love story. Only He, that one person, makes all interpretations excellent.

On a cold day, close to 0 degrees, I walk hunched over. Then, a British person passes by wearing only a reflective safe jacket and a short-sleeved t-shirt. What the...? The most foolish thing is to imitate what others do. Please dress warmly. And do not live the way the world wants you to. Living someone else's life is a tiring life.
The most childish thing is to boast about money, academic background, or honor. Instead, boast about your weaknesses. The Lord will be revealed. His grace is sufficient for us.

While serving in a position at church is a noble thing, God's fundamental purpose is the transformation of our character, a change in the fundamental disposition of our hearts. If that doesn't happen, no matter how much you serve or dedicate yourself to the church, you will still be "scolded." The purpose is "Repent!"

If I have not yet become a "glorious person of the Lord," it is because I have not yet clearly encountered the "glory of the Lord" or the "glorious Lord." Even with strong self-control and free will, if you do not encounter and see the glory of the Lord, it will ultimately end in a three-day resolution. For one who has seen glory, a life lived without glory is even more difficult.

1. There are those who "leave to remain" and those who are eternal "citizens." 2. Those who remain depart when their purpose is fulfilled, but family has love as its purpose, so there is no departure. 3. We love heaven not to go to heaven, but because we love, we go to heaven. Therefore, I dislike heaven without Jesus. 4. Those whose purpose is Jesus are not disappointed and do not crumble. We are by no means beings who seek blessings to live a little better than others and then act haughty. 5. Moses said to the Lord, "If you yourself will not go, then do not carry us up from here. How will it be known that your people and I have found favor in your sight, if you will not go with us? For what else will distinguish your people and me from all other peoples upon the face of the earth?" Exodus 33:15-16

Many people would jump up if you said they had treated the Lord lightly. Few realize that what is done to the least of these and what is done during worship is done to the Lord. "The Lord of hosts says: Will man rob God? Yet you have robbed me. But you say, ‘How have we robbed you?’ In your tithes and offerings. You are cursed with a curse, for you rob me, the whole nation of you. Bring the full tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house. And thereby put me to the test, says the Lord of hosts, if I will not open the windows of heaven for you and pour down for you a blessing until there is no more need. I will rebuke the devourer for you, so that it will not destroy the fruits of your field, and none of you shall be barren in your land. I will not let you be barren," says the Lord of hosts. Malachi 1:8-9. I hope you will honor the Lord.

If you received a bright red rose from someone, your heart would flutter. But if the person who gave you the flower was wearing only a tattered trench coat, your mind would race. It's not that good things exist, but that a good Person exists.

When you feel that others are moving faster than you, consider God who rescues you every moment of your life. Instead of being distressed by what others have that you don't, you should lament that you are not living more for the Lord. At least David, who was chased by his own son, felt that pain. "Blessed are those whose strength is in you, in whose heart are the highways to Zion." Psalm 84:5

Moses was called at 80, Isaac married in his 40s, and Joseph faced the threat of patricide at 13 and became a politician at 30. Judging myself by my current circumstances is truly incomprehensible. I must interpret myself through the Lord's eyes. To those who discern His will, He makes them a true light without wasting their years. The time given to me now is the best opportunity and a place of service. "O Lord, grant me to know my end and what is the measure of my days; let me know how fleeting I am! Behold, you have made my days a few handbreadths, and my lifetime is as nothing before you. Surely all mankind stands as nothing!" Psalm 39:4-5

There are times when you are brought down to the bottom. It means to repent and be humble. It is not a new disappointment, but a temporary forgetting of our inherently sinful and flawed human nature, and then a reconfirmation. That is why we need the Lord more. It is absolutely impossible without Jesus. For that reason, He bore the cross. Repent, pray, and rise again to run.

Everything given to the poor tastes delicious. For the poor in spirit, they feel the Lord's love and gratitude in everything. That is why they are blessed. How can life not be happy when it is delicious?

It is truly precious for someone high and mighty to clean with a broom. However, someone whose profession is cleaning in the world also cleans at church. No one notices them, and it is taken for granted. They are even more precious. There are often precious people in the world who are not recognized and excluded from praise.
Sometimes, trials that are difficult to understand are permitted. This happens when love becomes an idol. Faith's obedience overcomes trials.

When you strip away the fluff from everyone's words, the essence of their complex statements is as follows: 1. Upon closer inspection, the other person's behavior is undesirable. 2. Nevertheless, I responded quite coolly. -> Conclusion: I am a pretty decent person. What can be understood: Most conversations without God are 1. putting others down, 2. lifting oneself up, and 3. ultimately craving "recognition."

If you are afraid and in shock, your purpose is 'survival'. However, those whose purpose is 'the glory of God' are not pursued. This is because whether they live or die, they are interested in Christ being glorified in them. Whether you eat or drink, study or work, do it for the Lord's glory.
If you don't go crazy, you can't reach.

The difference between a bouquet and a potted plant is that a bouquet changes with the environment and time, while a potted plant changes with its roots. A life rooted in God may not be glamorous at first, but it is the strongest life.

My longing reflects my purpose. Whether it is a perishable purpose or a purpose of high calling can be known by looking at what you long for. My longing is to know the Lord fully, just as the Lord knows me.

Many people want to have more. This is because in today's world, possession and accumulation are believed to be power. However, true strength is rather the ability to let go. The strongest person is the one who can let go without regret.
He must increase, but I must decrease. -John 3:30

Many people want their weaknesses to disappear, or at least try to hide them. However, even in my weakness, if I look to the Lord alone for hope, the Lord receives glory through my inadequacy. Despite my imperfect character and expression, clumsy relationships, and unstable emotions, the Lord wants to be glorified through me. Only when I look to the Lord.
It takes about 25 years to understand a little of my mother's heart that gave birth to me. Even if the people I meet do not understand my heart, I should not resent or complain.

If the things I did with joy suddenly feel like a loss or a one-sided sacrifice, it is because I am not looking at the one who truly suffered loss and sacrificed. He, who was in the very nature of God, became a servant and obeyed even unto death. When the focus shifts, you start thinking about getting your money's worth. Please fix all your gaze on the Lord.

Walking the same path as others, at a faster speed, does not necessarily lead to happiness. Only 'He' knows the path I am taking. 'He' alone can refine me like pure gold, like a jewel, making me truly 'me'.
If you wait with patience and focus until the end, without acting on emotions or being hasty, opportunities will surely come. Preparing diligently for a lifetime for that opportunity is called skill.

Three years ago, when I returned after a long time and started ministry again, I saw a heart within me that I had not initiated. It was a heart given by the Lord, not my own nature. It is 'love'. The original author of that heart was God. It is grace that a sinner can have God's heart, and by that boundless grace, I can run today.

I am loved not because I have no mistakes, but rather because I am lacking and in great need of mercy. Therefore, Christians are not beings who hide their flaws and wear masks, but transparent people, even if their expressions are clumsy. All is God's grace.

Be free from the concern of who is watching you. Also, do not strive to be seen. Our entire focus should be on God alone. That is worship. If I look only to the Lord, others will not see me but will see the God I am looking at. Do not be conscious of people; look to God. You will be free from suffering.

Even for David, the most painful thing after his downfall and losing everything was not being able to go to the house of the Lord. That is why he envied pilgrims so much. (Psalm 84) This is the one who is after God's own heart.

Paul was a man whose handicap became his boast. This is because through that weakness, the power of the Lord is made strong. The one who is infinitely weak in the Lord becomes the most powerful person.

The very mindset of thinking I can give something is pride and sin. Peter mistakenly thought he could hold onto his own faith. He thought he could do it if he risked his life. However, after a terrible failure, he was filled with the Holy Spirit and began to look only to Jesus and live by Jesus alone. "Silver and gold I do not have, but what I do have I give to you..." -Acts 3:6 Woe to the one who has nothing to boast of but silver and gold. For he does not have the most important thing, the Lord.

1. The beginning of all sin is pride. Pride can also be called 'terrible self-centeredness'. Like the Pharisees, it is subtly evaluating oneself with satisfaction, thinking, "I am not like so-and-so..." (Luke 18:11). 2. Do not be satisfied with the 'first class' of the world you belong to, but rejoice in being an unnamed doorkeeper in the Lord's tent. The moment you do not look to the Lord, you have already taken the place of sin. 3. If you seek satisfaction in things other than the Lord, you will become fearful, and a fearful person is evidence of a proud person. 4. You should gladly stand in the place of repentance, humbling yourself. 5. The world is vain and its glory perishes. 6. May you have the greatest blessing of loving the Lord the most.

Everyone lives a different life, but if you truly believe that Jesus' blood saved you from death, the weight of that grace is the same for you and for others. If our faith and calling originate from Him, not from us, we should ask, "What other path would I take besides this one?" rather than "Do I really have to do this much?" Better is one day in your courts than a thousand elsewhere. I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God than dwell in the tents of wickedness. For the Lord God is a sun and shield; the Lord bestows favor and honor. No good thing does he withhold from those who walk uprightly. Psalm 84. For my eager expectation and hope that in no way I will be ashamed, but with all boldness, Christ will be magnified in my body, whether by life or by death. For to me, to live is Christ, and to die is gain. Philippians 1:20-21. God, my heart is steadfast, my heart is steadfast; I will sing and make music. Psalm 57:7
If my tears dry up, the rainbow of my soul will not appear. Without a rainbow, I cannot reach beyond dreams and remain in greed.
Those who seek the kingdom and its righteousness are excellent people. However, the trap for those who seek righteousness is to expect profit in return for righteousness.

The most important character of a Christian is to resemble the character of God. God's character is 'humility', who 'emptied himself, taking the form of a servant, and became obedient to death'. The Lord taught the humility of a servant to His proud disciples by 'washing their feet'. However, merely knowing this fact does not make one humble. Only when we always stand before the Lord, who washed my feet as the Son of God, can we be humble. When you stand before the Great Master, you naturally become humble. That Master is Jesus. "Have this mind among yourselves, which is yours in Christ Jesus." Philippians 2:5

I succeed as many times as the number of people I respect. That is why a generation where authority has collapsed is unhappy. Even if they leave, if they remain with respect, both those who stay and those who leave are blessed.

Lack is not inferiority, but rather the ability to achieve dreams. Choosing to make your parents and others laugh is the wrong choice. Even if the whole world tries to dissuade you, the choice that you cannot deny for a second, that is your dream.
I shall be telling this with a sigh Somewhere ages and ages hence; Two roads diverged in a wood, and I…… I took the one less travelled by, And that has made all the difference. -The Road Not Taken
Whether I did my best is not told by the result. The process bears silent witness.
The refinement and pain that are demanded, even of David, are because the Lord desires that he not repeat even David's mistakes. This is love.
The devil makes you imagine, and the Lord makes you love.

If you look up, there are many things to lament. This is because we are called to be servants. When David saw the angel striking the people, he immediately said to the Lord, "I have sinned, and I have done wrong; but these sheep, what have they done? Let your hand be against me and against my father's house." -2 Samuel 24:17

The furnace of affliction is not meant to kill me, but rather to set me free and allow me to walk. Though that fire may seem terrible, it harms nothing good; it only burns the bonds that tie me, the chains of sin, and my terribly corrupt self. Walk through the fire. You will not die, but will see the glory of God, Jesus. And the king commanded, and they threw Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego into the furnace of blazing fire. -Daniel 3:20 Then the king said, "Look! I see four men unbound, walking in the midst of the fire, and they are not hurt; and the appearance of the fourth is like a son of the gods." -Daniel 3:25

While I may seem like a decent person to others, the more accurate assessment is that I am full of problems that I cannot even protect my own heart. My weakness, which cannot be sustained for a moment without the Lord, is my complete reliance, and that is power and authority. We must rely only on the Lord.
If I dare to speak of who I am, I have no other way to express it than to say it is by the grace of God.
If you expect to be treated, you will surely be distressed. Instead, if you treat and serve others, you will be joyful. That joy comes from heaven.

He is more important than what is most important to me, Jesus. No matter how many blessings I receive or how good they are, they cannot be better than the Lord. Without the Lord, it is better to be lonely, and if the Lord moves away, it is better for everything to stop.

When hardship comes, do not dwell on the depth of the suffering, but focus on its meaning. The devil tempts us by making us feel meaningless, while the Lord holds us by making us realize our mission. We are spiritual beings. Therefore, we are beings who seek meaning.

The opposite of fear is not boldness, but love. Originally, I am timid, weak, and trembling, but when I love, I become bold. I am weak, but when I love, I can give everything. Women are weak, but leaders are strong. 1 Timothy 1:7 For God has not given us a spirit of fear, but of power and of love and of a sound mind.

Various people come to church. And many people with hurting hearts come. Therefore, church members sometimes exhibit unvirtuous speech and actions like worldly people. Because people are changed by believing, not by believing and then changing, we must accept and wait for such people. However, leaders are different. If a leader or a worship volunteer acts that way, it is difficult for them to continue in that position. It causes many people to stumble. Those who are confirmed to be holy are required to have more dedication and holiness than those who have newly come to church.
It is not important what I have done. What is important is in whom I have done it. Everything done in Christ works together for good.
Hardship makes people dry, and success makes people arrogant. But the Lord makes people abundant and humble.
My spirituality is revealed through even the smallest action. However, this does not mean you should focus on actions. It means to guard the heart that is the root of those actions.
I did not ask for a task that matched my ability, but for ability that matched the task. Phillips Brooks (1835-1893)
Trying to do everything well is greed and it moves you away from the cross. You should do your best for your mission, and it is good enough to be third in other things.

Hardship makes me a person with a mission, and hardship makes me bold. Hardship makes me discover 'my value' and makes me an 'eagle' that shakes off the temptation to stay in the nest like a chick. May you soar high amidst hardship.
After David was anointed, what happened was not that he immediately wore a crown, was exalted, and recognized, but rather he was driven into the wilderness and underwent training in 'failure.'

No matter how much a vessel tries, it cannot become a treasure. It becomes a treasure when jewels are placed in it. Become a person who holds Jesus. You will have a glory that is on a different level from those adorned with luxury goods.

The strength and ability I possess should not be used for myself, but invested in caring for the weak and vulnerable. Especially when I serve and bear those who have been entrusted to me with an unchanging attitude, regardless of their response, true power and ability are revealed.
