The most powerful person is not someone with many means, methods, or specs, but rather a life with a powerful purpose. That purpose is the glory of God. An absolute strong purpose becomes an absolutely strong life. May you become strong by embracing Jesus.

As an eagle stirs up its nest, hovers over its young, spreads its wings, takes them up, and carries them on its wings... Deuteronomy 32:11 If the nest is stable, you will stay there forever, and then the eagle's chick will become a chicken. I hope you become a magnificent eagle, not a chicken.
Mary chose the good part, and it was not taken away from her. The gifts and opportunities given by the Lord must never be taken away. A life that does not let go is a victorious life.

How to break a rock with an egg – Why not me? 1. Motivation is ministry. 2. Wise ministry is made of passion, wind, momentum, flexibility, and elasticity. – Do not stop. 3. Fight a winning war. – Admiral Yi Sun-sin (The Battle of Myeongnyang and Hansan Island – Utilization of asymmetric forces, the tortoise and the hare). 4. Even a nuclear bomb starts with a small button. The fuse – Jasmine Revolution. 5. Systems are clothes. – Bathe them, and the clothes will change. 6. Love is study. The art of love; ignorant love is rude love and becomes a wound, not love – e.g., Erich Fromm. 7. The world is fast, but the church is real-time. 8. He stirred up the people – were they motivated? 9. We love the church. – What is the alternative to criticism? 10. Believe in the Lord's power, His omnipotence. 11. The power of Saturday meetings – comes from prayer. 12. Pray without ceasing. – A war in which the Almighty intervenes. 13. When we boast, we always give negative wounds – the success of shepherd David, the failure of King David. Now, to conclude...

The goal is to go to college, after admission it's employment, after employment it's marriage, and after marriage it's to live above others. It's like being a machine. If you live with such goals, you will live dryly like an empty can. If you prick it with a needle, not a drop of blood will come out. I wish you would shed tears as if it were your own problem when you see someone being pricked.

Whether I am real or fake is evaluated in hardship. Some people become stronger through hardship, while others become negative and dry. A dry person only exists for 'my reaction' and 'my wounds,' with no one else around. If you go to a psychiatric hospital, everyone talks only about themselves. It's not that the people around you have become cold. It's not that God has moved away. It's just that if you are dry, you feel that way. If you are struggling, you must cry out to God, saying you are struggling and in pain. Then you will discover God who comforts and helps the suffering you. That is what hardship does.

There are times when we want to please someone. It is a feeling that arises when we want to love someone and be loved. To love and be loved healthily, you must first become a person who pleases God's heart. He raised David as king and testified, saying, "I have found David the son of Jesse, a man after my own heart, who will do all my will." - Acts 13:22 God looks at the heart. The heart is revealed not by status and reputation, but by loyalty and confession.

As a shepherd, he defeats the world's greatest Goliath. But as a great king, he falls before Bathsheba. When I become the king of my life and am exalted, I seem to be the strongest, but that is when I am weakest. A solid and strong life is achieved when we serve God as king. * David, who defeated Goliath, was a person who was not even recognized in his own home.

The statement that a fish wants freedom from water is a statement that it wants to be bound to land. The term 'sexual freedom' is synonymous with 'bondage of sexual sin.' The sexual corruption that many people face is because they have not given themselves to God.

To discard something magnificently, you must have gained something. Without the premise of 'gaining,' there is no 'discarding.' It is noble and beautiful to have gained much and to be able to enjoy it, yet to discard it and give it to many people and share it. People earn, gather, and achieve to stay and enjoy it, but we must earn, gather, and achieve to share, discard, and leave more.

We must be free from the gaze of others. We should also not strive to be seen by them. Our entire attention and focus should be solely on God; that is the worship of life, true worship. If I look only at the Lord, people will not look at me, but at the God I am looking at. If you can see God through yourself, that is the highest glory.

David does not accept Goliath's evaluation. No evaluation is mine unless I accept it. No one can condemn me if I do not acknowledge it. Just as Eliab could not define David, no one can define me. Goliath: I will make you food for the beasts. David: Ridiculous, you be the food, I'm ignoring you. Conclusion: Goliath became food. King Saul was a person defined by the evaluations of women. That is why he is consumed by jealousy and envy in his life. This is the miserable state of a person whose relationships are shaken. Let us learn to work in good relationships. You will realize that solving problems is easier than you think.

If you live like the advertisements, and become the person in the advertisements by having what is shown in the advertisements, will you be happy? Then you will be harmed. You will probably die from being eaten by worms.

Values determine greed. It is so difficult for us to treat gold as if it were a stone. However, a dog treats gold as if it were a stone without any effort. This is not because the dog is mature or prays in tongues a lot. It is because it lacks perspective.
If you have much but cannot share, you become a miserable beggar, and if you are poor but empty yourself to become lower and can share, you become rich.
It is not the problem to have a lot of money. It is a serious problem if even a little money possesses me.

If the Landlord gets angry, they tell you to move out. If God truly got angry, He could take away our 'breath,' and people would simply go. God rejects sin, but He does not reject people because of it. Rather, He loves us immensely. Our conscience testifies to our wrongdoings, but the devil accuses us, highlighting that we are bad people, worthless people. When Cain felt rejected, he envied Abel and murdered him. We have not been rejected; rather, we have been welcomed. You have welcomed me with your wonderful blessings, and placed a crown of pure gold on his head. Psalm 21:3
What lies before me and what lies behind me are truly small matters compared to what lies within me.

When 4-year-old Grace helps her mom and dad set the table, my wife and I are truly joyful. It's not that we are happy because Grace has lessened the work for her mom and dad. It's not her labor that brings us joy, but her heart. Clinging to ministry or results is a sign that my soul is depleted. I try hard to impress someone, or even want to impress God. The Lord says this: "Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest." The Lord gave us a mission not because He lacks labor power. He is an Almighty God who can make Abraham's descendants from stones, so He doesn't need our insufficient help. He wants to do things 'with' us and 'fellowship' with us. Do not be a 'beggar,' but be a 'friend.' The Lord desires intimacy. Allowing me, in my clumsiness, into God's plan is like my desire to do chores with my children.

The reason church communities struggle is not because members lack motivation, but because those who are faithful demand rewards and recognition. When people go beyond having a 'sense of ownership' to becoming the 'owner,' and when 'useless servants' deem themselves 'useful servants,' wounds and divisions arise. When we have achieved something, we do not claim it as ours, and by not claiming it, we do not insist on staying, and by not insisting on staying, we do not insist on leaving.
you've turned my shame into joyful praise

Therefore, I tell you, by the grace given me, everyone among you not to think of himself more highly than he ought, but to think soberly, according to the measure of faith that God has assigned to each one. - Romans 12:3 In his letter to the Romans, Paul exhorts us to "think soberly." Without receiving God's grace, our thoughts themselves become sick and corrupted. That is why we fall and collapse. Please abandon the thoughts you want to have and think after receiving grace.

Fear exists because we have not given everything. Those who have given everything to Christ, even their own lives, have nothing left, and therefore have nothing to fear in life. We have already given everything, our lives are not our own, and we live the rest of our lives by the power the Lord gives us; what further fear can there be?
You are a person sent to love. Therefore, you will not be disappointed in anything, nor will you regret it when you leave.

Even with great power and wealth, it is useless without health. In that sense, a greater ability than any other gift is to delight in God. (Nehemiah 8:10) Delighting in God comes from relationship and worship. If the relationship is broken, it is sick, and if it is sick, what is the use even if you walked on water? Are you truly delighting in God at this moment?
Why not me? Lord, You do everything through me by your faithfulness.

Animals do not struggle because they are made of dust. Angels do not struggle because they are made only of spirit. Humans struggle because spirit has been breathed into dust. (By design...) When dust and spirit collide, where we fall determines the outcome of the battle. That is affection, and it manifests as our pride, attachment, and suffering.

I need to confirm my calling. If I consider why I was called, I can be truly free, happy, meaningful, and not be concerned with vain things. To be called to a mission is to realize that I have only done what a useless servant would do.
When I am weak and have no strength, and do not know what to do, at least what I can do is to love and forgive. No one can take this away from me in the Lord.

In every game, there is a player who ensures victory, and in every fight, there is a game maker who changes the tide. David, who was after God's own heart, was such a person. We must become people whom God can trust with His will, people who will always work according to His will.
He must become greater; I must become less. John 3:30 If you must become less, rejoice. Then there will be a joy that the world does not know.

Many people rush towards their goals. It is to prove that they are decent people. When you try to prove yourself, life does not open its doors. Only when you abandon yourself and seek only the glory of the Lord does the door of life finally open. God does not exalt the proud. For His sake, and for the sake of God's kingdom...

2 Timothy 2:4 No one serving as a soldier gets entangled in the affairs of life, he wants to please the one who enlisted him. The person of the cross is not preoccupied with their own problems. Their entire focus is on pleasing the One who called them. My pride, my reputation, my status, my emotions are not the main thing. Fulfilling the Lord's will becomes their desire.

[Philippians 1:20] According to my earnest expectation and hope, that in nothing I shall be ashamed, but with all boldness, as always, so now also Christ shall be magnified in my body, whether by life or by death. [Philippians 1:21] For to me, to live is Christ, and to die is gain. Paul's concern was not for himself but was entirely focused on the Lord. Even more than the issues of life and death, more than the limitations and urgency of his current reality, he confessed that his complete interest and passion were for Jesus Christ to be proclaimed and glorified, and His honor to be revealed in his decaying body. Who can stop such a person? Can the devil, can death, stop him? His confession states, 'I have no interest in fleshly desires. I don't even dream of being exalted worldly or being recognized as having great faith in the church. I look solely at the cross and run.' When the old soldier, who had crucified his self, offered his life like a flame before the altar as a drink offering for the Lord, the glory of Christ, which even angels admire, was revealed. - From the London to nation by Jacob Kim 11 pages

200 years ago, saying "abolish slavery" meant you were crazy. 100 years ago, giving women the right to vote would get you sent to prison. 50 years ago, shouting for liberation from colonies would get you killed as a terrorist. What the media says is not always the truth. Do not live as they want you to live, but live as He created you to be. That is who I am.

When asked about their dreams, people say they will become 'something.' That's why they become that desired 'something' and end up feeling empty and making a mess. Dreams should not be spoken as nouns, but as verbs. The dream is not to become president, but to liberate slaves.
We do not enter the land of Canaan because it is blessed. Because I, the source of blessing, enter, that land receives blessing. Therefore, those who welcome me are the ones who hit the jackpot.
By looking at what I pursue, I can know whom I serve. Should I do what is good for people? Or what is good for God? If you seek the pleasure of people, you are definitely not a servant of God.
Gold is the Lord's, and silver is the Lord's. If I have even a small tear or love, it all comes from the Lord.
If He is the purpose, everything is a joy.

To me, who wants to achieve everything easily, the Lord gives times of maturity and ripening to become a person of 'sincerity.' Those times are sometimes difficult, but after being 'refined,' one becomes like pure gold.

The circumstances I face are not my choice. However, my attitude is something I can decide. It is not the environment, but my attitude that determines my life. Changes in disposition take time, and the changes may not be felt, but in time, they will surely bear fruit.
In Him who gives me strength, I can bear everything with gratitude and praise. It is a contentment that is incomprehensible from the world's perspective. The world cannot handle such a person.
The person needed in a community is not someone with excellent qualifications, but someone with whom you can be at peace when trapped together in an elevator.
When everything changes and fades relatively, you must think of your True North. Knowing your true north makes other choices easier.
When the Lord's holiness overflows within me, it manifests outwardly as service. The most holy Daniel was also an excellent public servant.
From the first day of his work life, Daniel resolved in his heart and lived with self-control according to that resolve. Those who set their hearts on the Lord move forward with trust, unshaken.
Whatever you do, do it with excellence. Do it for the Lord, not for show, showing kindness to people and acting with integrity.
When you assert your rights and oppress others, you are not elevated but rather humbled. God is with us with the highest authority when we serve and give.

We call someone who can see through others' thoughts a "Pro." We Christians are the unique experts in reading God's thoughts. (Even angels admire this.) In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus explained the 'pure in heart' would see God. The only way to read God's thoughts is not through research or illegal surveillance, but by 'becoming pure.'
Moses prostrated himself before slander directed at him, but he became angry and threw down and broke the Ten Commandments in the face of idolatry. Become dead to yourself and alive to God.
Do not lose your simplicity. Hold it until the Lord comes.

David did not resist the baseless misunderstandings about himself. However, he never compromised with any challenge to God's name and risked his life. The Lord's people risk their lives for the Lord, not for themselves.

The tempter in the wilderness, 'Satan,' comes to Jesus with plausible suggestions. It is about using one's rights to finish things roughly without pain. The convenient option always opposes the great work (the cross).

Being your disciple means you are not perfect. You may take a few steps forward and then a few steps back. However, life is not about speed but about holding onto the right direction; that is a blessed life.

I have the image of the blood of the covenant in the gathering called Ebenezer, and the teaching of Pastor Kim Jong-han, who only looks to Jesus. And I follow the path of the grace-giver, Director Hong Nan-yi of the National Salvation Mission. And now, I see, learn from, and emulate the love of the Lord in our young people.
Unpolished, rough truth is stronger than smoothly told lies. An ugly face is far more beautiful than a life lived behind a mask.

There will inevitably be times when I disappoint people. This is not a cause for fear but an opportunity for growth. What is more frightening is fearing what you should not fear. Fear makes you discouraged, defensive, and paralyzed, preventing you from living life. Jesus did not satisfy everyone, and satisfaction does not always help.
A leader is someone who loves the Lord with all their life and loves souls with tears, wanting nothing more.
The strongest person is not the one who hides what they want, but the one who wants nothing.
Ephesians 6:10–18 It is about 'us', not 'me', and offers a picture of an army standing and fighting in hand-to-hand combat.

David was left outside with the sheep, excluded from the evening banquet where his family and honored guests were present. However, God does nothing until David arrives, as revealed through Samuel. Do not fall into rejection and discouragement; use lonely times to become a worshiper who intimately worships the Lord. The breakthrough in life flows from intimacy with the Lord. Do not look to people. Breakthrough flows out of intimacy with God.

Please go your own way. That path is neither about emotions nor about comforting your heart. It is about leaving the world behind and living with vigilance. When you cut off what you cling to, true value comes alive. Only those who have clung to it will know that the joy and meaning are far happier.
This is my highest calling, to love as I am loved.
Your attitude determines your altitude.

Do not let what Satan brought a year ago, or a month ago, or a week ago, to tempt you and distort the direction of your life, still have power today. Instead, resist and say, "I am not that kind of person..."

1. Do you think you are closer to God than others? 2. Do you feel you have superior spiritual maturity and self-control compared to others? 3. Do you believe you hold a very important position in God's work? 4. Are you encouraged by success in your endeavors? Are you disappointed by the opposite? If any of these apply to you, you are someone living in religious zeal of self-righteousness. The Bible calls such people enemies of the cross. As you read these questions, do you often think they apply to someone you know? If so, you are highly likely to be such a person. A humble person and a proud person do not truly know if they are that kind of person.
There is a power that is so strong, yet darkness can never possess it. What renders all attacks and challenges insignificant is 'love.' It is the motive behind all Christian actions and words.
If you give up what you love, you will discover true love.

This is my desire, to know as I am known, to love as I am loved. This is my ardent wish, to give my very life an offering of love, a living sacrifice. This is my reward to hear You say, ........ "well done."
What leaders seek is sincerity and persistence. They desire that you serve with your initial heart, even as time passes.
I am not called for such things. I am not called for these things either. I am called by the Lord to fulfill His will.
There is no such thing as a part-time Christian. There are only full-time Christians or non-Christians.
If every moment were the last, there would be no regrets when closing my eyes. If love were the only thing left in the world, I would be bold in the face of death.
My weakness will ultimately be a help to me and the community. Our disappointments are another language of God's calling.
What others have taken from me is not important; what remains with me now is more important.

Do not go to places that only fulfill your needs, but go to places that need you. Do not stand as a bystander in relationships where you are merely pleased and entertained, but stand in a position that brings the Lord joy. There you will find true satisfaction and joy that receives the Lord's gaze.
Maturity requires time. We are often focused on growing quickly, but the Lord is interested in growing strongly.

God, who was so pleased with David, declared that He would accomplish all His planned work through him. He was so pleased by David's three qualities: gentleness, faithfulness, and his attitude towards authority.
Please do not compare yourself to others. Moving forward does not mean being a few steps ahead of others, but rather walking further today than you did yesterday.
Do not listen to the enemy's accusations. When the devil reminds you of your past, just remind him of his future.
When I give up the rights I ought to exercise, God grants me authority, and the devil becomes powerless before the surrendered right.
When I fail to maintain my position, I mess things up and leave wounds. This is especially true when I act arrogant. Pride always leaves scars on the victim.
Pride is much deeper than we think of ourselves. It causes many wounds and bitter roots. Humility begins with acknowledging that we are not humble.

If we were to list our flaws and shortcomings, we could easily fill dozens of pages of A4 paper within minutes without needing the devil's help. It is God's will for us to be in the place He has appointed, even with our imperfections, and by enduring for the Lord's glory, He receives glory and is pleased. God did not desire achievement but obedience despite our shortcomings.
Do not be angry on my behalf, but be indignant for the sake of the Lord's name. That is gentleness, and God seeks out and meets such people to entrust them with His will. - From a Leaders' Meeting -
In the church, saying "I love you in the Lord's name" is not a mere pleasantry. It is loving with the heart of Christ.
A pure soul is pure, even if it is not recognized.

When David, living a life on the run, went before the Philistine king of Gath, David drooled, rubbed himself against the wall, pretended to be mad, barely escaped, and hid in the cave of Adullam. I believe that was the lowest point of David's life. Even the life of David, a great hero in human history, had such a miserable period when viewed in a single segment.
If you want to fly like an eagle, you cannot run like an ostrich.
If you base your self-esteem on the evaluations others make of you, you will not have a single joyful day.
total surrender
How unfortunate it would be if God were not present in the place where I spend most of my time.
Standing in the place where I am, in the way God desires, and confessing and acknowledging that the Lord is the owner of what I do, is the worship that most pleases God.
Even if my heart is completely broken and it is difficult to even breathe, if I can live for the Lord, that is my blessing.
I hope you become God's pride before the devil and the world, just as Job was.
For the Lord, I give my life, so what else would I hold dear?

From the perspective of an Account or Information Security Auditor, the approver and the requester cannot be the same person. This constitutes a violation. Judging matters related to oneself while the self has not died is itself a violation. Discernment is not achieved by good intentions alone; one must become a detached being, a person in whom Jesus lives. "For the one who is spiritual discerns all things, yet he himself is not to be discerned by anyone." - 1 Corinthians 2:15
The person is beautiful who, when they earnestly desire to worship and earnestly desire to kneel, can kneel before the Lord in their finest tuxedo or dress, even if the place is a mud pit.

When you expect a lot, your heart is uncomfortable. When you want to give a lot, your heart is happy. Love is not necessarily about giving many things. True love is felt when you regret not being able to give what you want to give.

Living as you please, you think that is freedom. You look down on believers walking the narrow path as pathetic. That is not freedom but slavery, dragged around by sin. When you become a servant obedient to the Lord, you will have true freedom. You are free and have no need to be a slave to sin.

Discouragement comes from our perspective. When we look at unchanging objects, at ourselves facing problems, or at the environment surrounding us, we are bound to be discouraged. I hope you will look to the Lord and what He has done.
If your heart is cold, you are wearing different clothes. A person clothed in Jesus is sufficiently warm with Jesus alone. If you chase after vain things, your heart will continue to be cold.
A foolish person looks for the cause of problems in others. It is like analyzing the cause of a burning house. You must first get out and then investigate later. There is hope if I change.

My psychology professor in college said the most childish things in the world are trying to be treated as if you are older, richer, or more educated. The world might tolerate such things to some extent, but the church does not. If the Lord does not acknowledge you, you are nothing. It is not important what people think, but how God evaluates you. You are not a mass-produced commodity, but an irreplaceable masterpiece.

It is not the person who does many things or has much experience who becomes a great leader. The leader is the one who loves the Lord and is exceedingly happy in Him. This is because a person happy in the Lord is already conveying a great message through their life.

The humble person sees the Lord. Humility is knowing your true nature before the Lord, so naturally, a humble person will envision, see, and clearly perceive the Lord in their heart. The fragrance of Jesus will emanate from their life.
No one can ruin my life unless I allow it.

Flower arrangements are beautiful, but they are dead. Living to show off is tiring, lacks vitality, is meaningless, and brings no happiness. Do not be conscious of others; look to the Lord. Being conscious of others requires performance, but looking to the Lord allows you to be boldly simple and truthful. A life of flower arrangements will pass; become a life planted by the stream.

Who you eat with is more important than what you eat. Who you travel with is more meaningful than where you travel. It is better to travel economy with a loved one than to fly first class alone. There are always people who say, "Why can't I fly first class with my loved one?" That's why people tend to keep their distance.
A leader is someone who influences others. In other words, they are charismatic. It means being a friendly person. When people see me, they should want to meet me.

It is not important what or how much I have done. That is the standard of the world. What God desires is not numbers, popularity, or recognition, but that the Lord truly reigns in me. Kneeling in my room might be no worldly achievement, but if the Lord is reigning, He will naturally be revealed through me.

Among weapons, a grenade has a greater radius of destruction than a gun. When I was in the military, I pulled the safety pin and threw one, and it had an explosive power beyond imagination. Comparison is like that. If you start comparing, you will reach a situation where you pull the pin on a grenade and it's "you die, I die." Do not compare; stand before the Lord. Then you will be transformed. And you will become happy. Self-discovery = transformation = happiness are the same words.

A person who tries to be happy based on their reputation, job, bank balance, or circumstances is truly pitiable. It is like being tossed about by the waves of the sea. Instead of those perishable things, where am I heading? Who am I running towards is more important. Life is not made happy by numbers; it is determined by direction. Only when life is understood by direction does my life begin to be interpreted.

Many kings fell because their reign was not long. They did not fall because they were weak, but because they became too strong. When one tries to defend and protect oneself, the fall is ultimately great. Those who are completely broken before the Lord seek nothing but the Lord. David did not try to protect himself, so the Lord protected him instead.

It is not a big deal if believers are misunderstood. What is more important is that I do not misunderstand. If I firmly hold onto this, I can avoid being deceived by darkness and giving cause for it. Tragedy occurs when the other person and I misunderstand simultaneously. Believers in the Lord have no need to misunderstand.

The reason David was strong was that he had nothing to desire. A person aiming for worldly goals is easily trapped. I hope you have nothing to desire except the Lord. On the other hand, Saul fears people and oppresses them to protect his kingship. The level of life is different for those who try to protect and those who let go.
Knowing how someone spends their holiday reveals what kind of person they are. Who are you?
If I am not broken, health, family, and the church will be broken. If the Lord is the Savior of my spirit, He must also be the King of my self. - From a European conference -
Do not react with emotion; react with truth. Then you can protect yourself and save others. God's righteousness will be revealed.
You are a one-of-a-kind creation in this world.

Lift your eyes and look at the world. What the world values is the fulfillment of desires. If you chase desires, you will inevitably fall into darkness. It is foolish to say you cannot see while dwelling in darkness. For those who look only to the Lord and accept the death on the cross and move forward, there is a preciousness that the world cannot comprehend. Therefore, there are two kinds of Christians: those who live to fulfill their desires and those who live by letting go of themselves.
Rise high. Evil thoughts cannot follow you to higher places.

In his greatest crisis, when he was being chased by his own son Absalom, David divided his army into three units and entrusted them with command. He even entrusted the last unit to Ittai the Gittite. Ittai was a Philistine, an enemy of the Israelites. His ability to deeply move even an enemy saved him in that difficult situation.

A Christian stirring coffee at Starbucks is like parting the Red Sea... (omitted)... I do not love only when I am strong or perfect. There is a saying, "Women leave when they have nothing to give a man, and men leave when they get nothing from a woman." Is true love really like that? When did you feel deep love for your parents? It is not when you give them something. When you want to give them something good but cannot because you have nothing, that is when you feel the greatest love.

The reason God lets me see others' shortcomings is not for me to point fingers, but rather to examine myself. I must remember that I too was once a sharp thorn to others, and I still am at times. May I be a source of counsel and comfort with gentleness and authority.
Embrace even thorny people with heartfelt warmth. I too was once a very sharp thorn to someone.
The biggest problem with leprosy is the inability to feel pain. If there is no hunger, thirst, or pain, one is severely ill.
The saying "even a thorn, if embraced with a heart-wrenching warmth, becomes a rose" means that while I do the embracing, God brings about the transformation.
The church can be a church and worship can be worship only through self-denial and the power of the Holy Spirit.
The purpose of advertising is marketing. If you claim to proclaim truth but have not experienced or practiced it, you will experience the most effective counter-effect.

Love ramen. What devoted people must be most careful about is having no expectations. They must seek only the glory of the Lord, know how to step back, and let only the fragrance of Jesus emanate where they have disappeared. I said this 14 years ago on the day I left Korea: "With just one ramen and a Bible a day, I can do anything..." Simply learn to love ramen; it is much simpler and more comfortable than being treated inconveniently or having elaborate parties. And focus everything on giving glory only to the Lord. Then you will see the glory of the Lord.

Next week: early morning service, Wednesday service, evening prayer meeting... Awesome. All the ministry leaders are gone, and I'm left alone. One of the gifts that shows up when I check my spiritual gifts is 'stand-in.'
Loyalty_and_Focus: Do not fall, but keep going. Joseph continuously faced adversity, but he persevered without any clear metrics or schedule. Human goals bring frustration, but God's character and promises bring hope and joy.

Speak what you hear. Trying to speak your own words makes you tired with performance and calculation. Just speak the word given to you, and simply obey. Jesus said, "When you lift up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he, and that I do nothing on my own authority, but speak just as the Father taught me." The undeniable fact is that you have never earnestly sought the Lord continuously. -2014 Aug 14 "Those who love me I will show my great love, and those who seek me find me." -Proverbs 8:17

* When annoyed at the movie theater: When I went to the movies as a child, the movie showed no signs of starting, and only advertisements played endlessly. Faith is similar; when we don't practice what we preach and act impoverished, we have nothing substantial to convey, like chasing clouds.

* More important than projects and preparation: If the Lord does not take the lead, humanly planned projects will yield no results, and if the Lord does not protect, even thorough human preparation will crumble. "Unless the Lord builds the house, those who build it labor in vain. Unless the Lord watches over the city, the watchman stays awake in vain." -Psalm 127:1
* Change: If change is truly needed, God will change the established authorities. If you refuse to be changed, He will exalt you who obey Him silently.

* The Lord's guarantee: The Lord will surely dismantle what you subtly expect and secure, and challenge you to rely solely on Him. Therefore, those who trust the Lord last will have nothing left. Like salvation obtained through fire.

* Prescription for injustice: The psalmist Asaph, in Psalm 73, stumbled by observing the circumstances around him and the prosperity of the wicked. God's prescription for the wronged psalmist is to endure for the 'end.' This is not easy, but through worship in the sanctuary, understanding and strength are gained, leading to a place of reliance solely on the Lord, resolving all trials and doubts. Though you face tribulation in school, work, and relationships, be bold. Jesus has overcome the world. "When I thought to understand this, it was too painful for me until I went into the sanctuary of God; then I understood their end. ... You hold me by my right hand. Whom have I in heaven but you? And there is nothing on earth that I desire besides you." -Psalm 73 "I have said these things to you, that in me you may have peace. In the world you will have tribulation. But take heart; I have overcome the world." -John 16:33

* Wisdom: When you focus on important matters, the rest will fall into place. Anger has a life cycle that lasts until sunset, but a mission lasts until the kingdom of heaven. Instead of fearing or getting angry, focus on your mission.

* The reason for not looking back: If you lack confidence in guidance, you should not start, and what you have obeyed, you must risk your life for until the end. If it is something to risk your life for, it must be more precious than self-satisfaction and proof, and that is seeing God's glory, and you go leaving everything else behind.

* Moving Worship Christian Organisation from where you stand: It is not important where or in what state you stand, but "Whom are you worshiping?" is important. I believe that wherever you exalt His name, you will see the glory of heaven. The reason the early church saints, wearing animal skins, becoming prey for beasts, being burned at the stake, and dying on the cross, could instead command death was because they saw the glory of heaven. See the glory of God. That is the only answer. "He is the radiance of the glory of God and the exact imprint of his nature, and he upholds the universe by the word of his power. After making purification for sins, he sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high." -Hebrews 1:3

* The reason for surviving: The reason for crossing the desert without dying is meeting an oasis when you are about to die. Since we are in God's hands, we must win with patience. "The Lord has chastened me severely, but he has not given me over to death." -Psalm 118:17

* Important factor: Whether good things happen or difficult things arise, I cannot know, but what is important is that I face them filled with the Holy Spirit. He is the defender of the weak, comfort those in need. Being with the Lord is power.

* The power of change: There has never been a history of change brought about by eloquent speech. Behind change, there has been someone's bloodshed. We are a generation that speaks and hears easily through social media. We mistakenly think that by proclaiming something, we give the impression that we live that way. If you truly want to see the glory of change, you must be willing to pay the price. You must speak of living it out. And you must live as you speak. Eliminating fakes is a surprising and great thing. That is why you deny yourself first.
* The most meaningful work: The seeds sown with tears are not in vain because the Lord notices them.

* How to break the rule: You must keep the rules, but there are times when you must break them to save a life. When you break a rule because you cannot avoid it, you risk your life to save a life. "Go and gather all the Jews who are in Susa and hold a fast on my behalf, and do not eat or drink for three days, by night or day. I and my young women will also fast as you do. Then I will go to the king, though it is against the law, and if I perish, I perish." -Esther 4:16

* Redeem the time: We want to redeem the time, but we do not have the ability. The only way for a person to redeem the time is to be filled with the Holy Spirit. Walking in the Spirit allows us to redeem the time and live a victorious life. "Look carefully then how you walk, not as unwise but as wise, redeeming the time, because the days are evil." -Ephesians 5:15-16

* Irony: If a bakery has no bread and you can't eat at a restaurant, you might take out loans and advertise, but soon such a business will be unable to operate normally. If you say believe in life but there is no change, and you say you have overcome death but you are defeated by material possessions, greed, envy, and jealousy, you are putting on a show, and God will not go along with such a show. Just as a company's collapse intensifies due to normal accounting and audits not being performed, faith will also inevitably collapse if it avoids reality and is filled with empty talk. The key is to stop the show and run to God for refuge. That is the essence. "The Lord redeems the life of his servants; none of those who take refuge in him will be guilty." -Psalm 34:22

* When resolutions abound but execution fails - Spiritual neutrality: It is because you try to maintain spiritual neutrality. People are bound to be used. Either as instruments of righteousness or as agents of darkness. Only when used righteously can you avoid being used by darkness.

* Golden hour: 'Golden hour' refers to the time when the survival and prognosis of critically ill emergency patients with circulatory and cardiopulmonary damage after an accident are determined. In this era, it has been 10 years since a generation of youth with different cultures rapidly began to leave the church. If we miss this last moment, we will lose a generation, and the decline will accelerate, impacting the church, families, and society. When there is an opportunity to take responsibility, if we listen to the voice of the Spirit with the authority given to us and obey, we will not only save everyone but also reclaim what has been lost.

* The clear reason: What makes a person complete is the word written by divine inspiration, and what builds and benefits the community are the positions and gifts poured out according to the will of the Spirit. You must discover the clear reason why you are not made complete and the community is not built. If the cause does not change, no matter how much you try, the result will not change.

* How to hear the word: If you only listen to the word and then forget it, Satan will snatch it away. Hearing the word begins with the ears, but it becomes power and manifests when you hear it with your life.

* Let the fearful turn back: Those who accompany are the bold. Those who hear His voice obey without calculation. "Then the people were told, 'Whoever is fearful and trembling may turn back and leave Mount Gilead.' So twenty-two thousand of the people turned back..." -Judges 7:3

* Do not try to keep, but worship: Those who try to keep will lose, and those who try to take will destroy themselves. Therefore, all the victors in the Bible were bloody worshippers and worshippers who let go.
Go, return on your way to the wilderness, you shall anoint -1 Kings 19:15
* A life without regret: Risk your life.
* I am the way: Do not envy what is not your path, but bring glory to God through the path given to you. The path the Lord has given is glorious and the best path. Hallelujah.

* Butterfly effect: Saul fell into the absurd situation of envying David because he did not obey completely in the war with Amalek, and Saul's incomplete obedience began with the foolish act of offering a sacrifice himself without waiting for Samuel. Present choices are closely related to future choices.

* A person people follow: It is a problem if foolish and ignorant crowds work hard, but it is useless if a devout and capable person works with resentment. When you fear God, you become a true leader. "And to Solomon his son, he said, 'Be strong and courageous and do it. Do not be afraid and do not be dismayed, for the Lord God, my God, will be with you. He will not leave you or forsake you, until all the work for the service of the house of the Lord is finished. Behold, the divisions of the priests and the Levites are ready for all the service of the house of God: and every willing man skilled in any craft will assist you in all the work, and the officials and all the people will be at your command.'" -1 Chronicles 28:20-21

* Last Christmas: The time the Lord called me, why He called me in that way, why He made me walk such a thorny path... I cannot understand, but God is good, truly good, faithful God. The happiest moment of my life was when God made me cry... That God is calling me again. The blood of Jesus has power. -May 2007
* Win like this: Strive to win in your respective fields. This is not for cheap comfort that prolongs a day or two, but to share eternal opportunities.

* How to live out your destiny: The situation I am experiencing now is something the Lord knew long ago. If it is the life He has allowed me, it is our part to live it faithfully and holy. We do not avoid suffering, nor do we ignore the joy given to us, but we respond with faith and move forward. "Your eyes saw my unformed substance; in your book were written, every one of them, the days that were formed for me, when as yet there were none of them." -Psalm 139:16
The greatest power is the power to be with the Lord. It is the power to be lonely for the Lord. Everyone retreats from this stage.
Be courageous. It doesn't mean the situation is okay, but that 'God is with you.'
A bee that comes to a flower attracted by its scent is the first to leave when the flower wilts.

Obey as much as you are called. Those who are called but do not come, or those who desperately try to go when not called, are both proud. God does not look at ability. He makes those who are called capable.

I never told you to do it perfectly. Trying to be perfect is trying to do it well. God never told you to do it perfectly. He wants you to diligently and faithfully do what you were asked to do, rather than seeking recognition for doing things you weren't asked to do. Perfectionism can be a burden to yourself and a wound to others.

If greed mixes with gifts, it becomes a disgrace. There are daughters of chairmen who, after living without lifting a finger, use their good abilities and privileges to merely reverse airplanes, and there are people of faith who faithfully support and advance the world in secret with little strength.
Change is frightening and difficult, but regardless of the superficial outcome, it is always valuable in the long run.

When the results betray me despite my best efforts in my youth, I become frustrated, powerless, and lose my motivation. At that time, I heard God's voice towards me: "It is not in vain. Much is prepared for you." - May 1990. Pusan. God never makes mistakes.
Do not be discouraged. Discouragement is like an umbrella in front of a typhoon, where you try to protect yourself. What God desires is His perspective and to look to Him. Be courageous.

Success apart from the Word is outstanding evil. Even if you graduate from a good school, have an enviable job, are recognized for achievements in your field, or are evaluated as successful in ministry, if you stray from God's Word, you are simply committing conspicuous evil.
Faith begins with responding to the promptings of the Holy Spirit. Today, do not harden your hearts when you hear His voice.
You must be addicted to the peace and joy that obedience brings, rather than the empty pleasure that sin and self-satisfaction offer.
Do the basics. The most important thing is to be faithful to the basics. This means starting by focusing solely on the Lord. If your focus wavers, you will become angry, dissolute, and regretful.
You can start again from the basics.
At first, you struggle to abandon your will and choose God's will. Later, your will becoming one with God's will is faith.

Trying to break your will and follow God's will is commendable, but you will still find yourself falling. Faith is not about breaking your own will, but about yielding yourself so that the Lord can turn your will. Therefore, spiritual maturity is not about managing your will through law, but about aligning with God's will through grace.
In the wilderness where I stand, God makes a path I do not expect.

It is not a lack of communication, but a lack of mission. Confusion arises because the person who should be responsible avoids making decisions, and the person who should not be responsible insists on making them. First, clarify the mission. Then, everything else will fall into order.
What to do in a rebellious generation is to exalt the Lord where you stand. Exalt the Lord higher than the wickedness of the world.
Since there is no self-realization that I clearly desire and must achieve, there is no fear of losing anything. The Lord's grace has always been sufficient and will continue to be sufficient.
Maturity does not happen on its own. Maturity and revival are always accompanied by bloodshed. Where there is God's glory, there is a leader who sheds blood.

To attribute glory to God through results, you inevitably cling to humanistic methods that spare no effort. God's glory is attributed through attitude, not results, and God oversees the results. "Well done, good and faithful servant, you have been faithful over a little..."
Whatever He has prepared, He has done so with love. Walk in peace.
Humble yourself and repent, love with all your life, and be faithful unto death. Then you have done well.
It is to acknowledge God as God and to fulfill God's will, not human will.
To those who look to the Lord as nameless and unknown, God gives faithful guidance.
It is because you are planning things that God has not given you to do. The things God tells you to do, He Himself prepares and provides helping hands, so you can anticipate them without worry.
If someone else drives or uses navigation, you won't know the way well even if you go many times. But when you drive yourself, it finally becomes your path.

Rather than asking what I can do, you should ask if you are entrusting yourself to the Lord's hands. If God has not given it, you have not received it, and if He has not permitted it, it will not be accomplished.

When He calls, He has already prepared. When He called the farmer, He had already prepared King Cyrus, Artaxerxes, Darius, and Ahasuerus. Even if rulers seem to act arbitrarily, the Lord is the master of history and revival.
Do not decide arbitrarily. I do not decide as I please. Because I do not want to be arrogant. If the Lord decides and leads, He will take responsibility.
The ability to overcome a recurring thief. If the enemy challenges me repeatedly with the same problem, I must hold onto the Lord's word even more persistently. Victory or defeat is determined there.
The most precious people. They are the motivators who remind me of God's glory and show me that the arduous tasks were not in vain.
A life without regret. Live the best moments for eternity.

The foundation of a believer. It is to honor God who acts and accomplishes, no matter how valid one's own opinion may be. It is to sing in the storm because of God's plan, which is greater than my thoughts.
Observe the Sabbath and attend the main broadcast on Saturday. This means observing the Sabbath and not missing the main broadcast on Saturday.

How to reach the summit without giving up. I once climbed Glen Navis, the highest mountain in Scotland, in the UK. I could never have climbed it alone, but my eldest daughter Sharon told me, "Don't give up," as we climbed for seven hours. Being together is the power to complete the journey.
The purpose of the wilderness is to humble me. Therefore, if I humble myself before God and authority and obey, my problems will no longer be problems as I fulfill the purpose.

It's not that I don't know the answer. I am not wandering because I don't know the answer. Storms and wildernesses always have a reason and a purpose, and when that purpose is fulfilled, they stop immediately. Wanting a solution while not wanting to change means you haven't been humbled enough yet. The greatest prayer of one who has encountered tribulation is, "Humble me further." The problems in life are not due to not knowing the answer, but are a matter of obedience. Remember that the Lord your God led you on this forty-year journey through the wilderness, to humble you and to test you, to know what was in your heart, whether you would keep his commandments or not. He humbled you and let you hunger, and fed you with manna, which neither you nor your fathers had known, that you might know that man does not live by bread alone, but that man lives by every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord. - Deuteronomy 8:2,3
The standard of recovery. In the Lord, with the Saints. Abide in the Lord and fellowship with the saints. Those who are 'in' and 'together' are the most powerful.

Spiritual warfare - How to make the adversary cook and feed the dogs. There is always a persistent intention behind what happens to us. The adversary's intention is to steal, kill, and destroy. He wants us to react with situations to complain about, relationships to hate, events to be discouraged by, and things to be angry about. We may not have the ability to control the situation immediately, but we can choose our reaction. Especially when we lay down our rights and declare the opposite of the situation with our hearts, the darkness begins to crumble.

What you like and what you should like. If you focus on what you like, which God has not given you, you will not receive the best that God gives. Let go of what prevents the best that God gives from coming to you.
Move in a straight line. The answer to life is immediate obedience. It is to lay down rationalizations and masks, abandon shortcuts, and run straight to the Lord.

Why they fail in the World Cup. The way to get good quickly in a short period is to change stamina and tactics. If you do that, you can manage to get through the preliminaries. What's really important is that soccer is played with a soccer ball. If the ball doesn't stick to your body, you'll be uneasy, and the spectators will always be uneasy. What's truly frustrating is trying to find the cause in non-essential matters when you've lost in the essentials. Holding onto the essentials requires a long time and a great deal of sacrifice (training). Foreign coaches (revival speakers) have a slight advantage because there are fewer distractions and it takes less time to focus on the essentials. In spiritual terms, if you become one with the ball (the Lord), you will have composure (boldness) even when defense (trials) presses you, and you will dominate the game (sovereignty). The essentials found in the book of Acts were the Holy Spirit and the Word, the cross and obedience. For emergency patients, the priority is to minimize all distractions and connect them with a specialist.
Do you think God is delaying? God is not delaying, but is slow to anger.
Do not worry. When you encounter things to worry about, do the simple things that God has prepared. You just need to say and do what you ought to say and do.

The fastest way. The fastest shortcut in the Exodus was the southern route. However, God made them enter from the east. As a result, it was a 'divine move' that provided the motif for Normandy and the Incheon Landing. When the path to your job and marriage seems unclear and life seems to be taking too many detours, you must believe that God's shortcut exists there. Those who walk with God will be overturned.

Mission over success. Mission is a concept that far surpasses the issue of success. In the eyes of the world, it would be considered success and completion for the refugee Esther to become queen, but from there Esther held onto her mission and went forward saying, "If I perish, I perish." The question is not "Is it difficult? Is it easy?" but "Is it God's will?"
It's always like this. I look only to the Lord who will do new things within me.
It is to stop the ministry, change direction, and run towards that eternal goal.
The reason for fear. It is because you are trying too hard to do well. If you try to be faithful, you will have peace.
The only way to keep the fire burning. It is to persevere. Perseverance is power. Do not let the fire on the altar go out. - Leviticus 6:13
Guard your precious freedom. When I try to be free as I please, I lose precious freedom and protection. Eden was lost because of disobedience.

When you want to break the ties. When you find various things bothersome and feel burdened by the community, it means you have been tempted, and the one tempting you is Satan. That tie you want to break is ultimately Jesus. God, who is pulling me from the cliff, has never once considered giving up with His blood-stained hands.

How God works. God works through people. Therefore, He reveals Himself through their obedience to His guidance and Word. Then, He sovereignly works even in those who do not obey His guidance and Word. Respond with obedience.

Dangerous familiarity. Familiarity is convenient, but it also means it becomes dangerous. Incidents always happen in places where you are most confident. Especially in faith, this happens when you lose your initial zeal.
When I am powerless. Now it is time for God to work.

To friends who are tempted. The purpose of temptation is unconditional destruction, and the one who causes it is Satan (Evoluon). Act contrary to what he desires. What he desires is for you to stop, separate, and not persevere.
How to focus and persevere. You will do so if you pursue what is high and eternal. Be captivated by the Lord. So much so that nothing else matters...
Obey, and you will hear the Lord's voice. If you obey the Word, you will see God's glory without regard for what others think.
The reason training is difficult is to save you from dying in a desperate battle situation.

You won't be happy if you're given 10 years' worth of birthday presents all at once. The reason we don't bother to wait until your birthday for a gift bought in advance is that a gift gains value when it is anticipated.
Don't be distressed by not achieving desires that God has not given you; instead, rejoice as you quietly walk the Lord's path.
Try to stop me. The devil will hinder me, but he cannot stop me. Relative hindrances cannot stop absolute pursuit.

The mentee already knows where they need to go. They are just imagining if there might be other options to not go down that path. The mentor's role is to prevent the mentee from turning away from the path they already know.
Letting go of the attempt to protect and defend myself and finding security in the Lord is the opposing spirit that crushes Satan's schemes. Do not protect yourself; obey Him.
The difference between a professional and an amateur is that an amateur wins by chance and talks a lot, but a professional wins when they need to win. Victory is a battle of priorities.
The Lord's work does not distinguish between night and day. The Lord's zeal will make you beautiful.

Do not worry excessively about making mistakes. The Lord's power and grace are greater than my flaws. Simply focus your motives on pleasing the Lord. If your motives are good, the Lord will take responsibility for the outcome.
The fact that good and bad things work together for good comes from His sovereignty. Become a person who fulfills God's will and sovereignty by being His hands and feet.

If spiritual supply is weak, your life will discharge. Eventually, it will be a matter of time before spiritual famine sets in, leaving you lethargic and unable to be happy. It is a cause for great gratitude if the community you belong to provides spiritual charging, but if not, it means you have a mission. If you are only experiencing spiritual loss (discharging) without a mission, it means you should move to a different community.

A good restaurant is one that serves food that is edible and should be eaten. However, these days, many places try to earn short-term profits by serving inedible food, using good interior design, poor ingredients, and excessive food additives, which is why TV shows go out to investigate good restaurants. I believe churches can be the same. Restaurants and churches are the same in that they provide something.

Worship with bloodshed. When the spirit of worship is poured out, worship becomes the greatest joy. Furthermore, the spirit of worship is poured out upon those who worship with all their heart. Therefore, a leader is someone who worships wholeheartedly and continuously in a place where no one else worships, so that the spirit of worship may be poured out.
You must be sensitive to whether you are currently charging or discharging. Charging only occurs when a current higher than that of the battery flows.

What you withhold and do not offer becomes a temptation later. Offer yourselves to righteousness and become instruments of righteousness, rather than offering yourselves to the flesh and becoming instruments of unrighteousness.
When it is difficult to find an answer, it is because 'the Lord is working.' At such times, all that is needed is to 'entrust yourself to Him.'
Letting go of the attempt to protect myself and finding security in the Lord is the opposing spirit that crushes Satan's schemes. Do not protect yourself; obey Him.

Sometimes God speaks through loss. From a human perspective, it may be regret and remorse, but in God's kingdom, He speaks of great works of God resulting from that loss. "Do not worry about your three-year-old female donkeys that were lost three days ago, for they have been found. And who is it in all Israel that is sought after? Is it not you and your father’s whole family?" - 1 Samuel 9:20
What I expect will not necessarily come to pass. God's methods surpass my expectations. Wisdom is to stop fantasizing, be faithful to the present mission, and entrust the rest.
After ministry, become a servant who serves even lower, and become like crushed grain, dead and ground to powder.
A life without regret is one where everything is poured out, leaving nothing left.

Why not give thanks? My friend was martyred in Iraq 10 years ago because he believed in Jesus. It means believing in Jesus even unto death, so why not give thanks for the unavoidable obstacles on that path?
A valuable endeavor is one that saves lives, and therefore, it tells us what to focus on.
Hold onto the truth that you have been given a burning mission and that God is almighty.
The purpose is not to test, but to save lives.
Draw near to God, and God will draw near to you.

If you complain, you become paralyzed. If you complain about the government, you cannot work diligently. If you complain about the teacher, you cannot study. If you complain about your parents, even food feels like a punishment. When you complain, your life becomes paralyzed. However, if you take responsibility, you become a leader, and if you become a leader, you can bring life.
The reason He humbles me is because God truly wants to use me. When you work hard, it's like swimming with splashes, but when you humble yourself, a tsunami occurs.
Love the souls entrusted to you as much as they hurt.
The reason the devil picks fights and tries to hurt and belittle you by any means necessary is for one reason: to make you stop looking at the Lord and to make you stop. Do not stop; that is victory.

The reason for peace even on a sinking ship. Whether the ship is sinking or sailing smoothly, the Lord is in all of it. If it sinks, He will make you walk on water, and if it sails, He will make you praise Him. Let not the flood overwhelm me, nor the deep swallow me up, nor the pit close its mouth over me. - Psalm 69:15
Do not worry about "what to do and how to do it." The Lord asks why you intend to do it. If the purpose is holy and clear, the method will naturally sharpen.

The reason for being called. You are not called to complain about circumstances and people. You are called to obey and live according to the Word. Then you will experience the God who lives and works in the midst of this situation that you find so difficult.
The condition for happiness. Blessed is the person who has a community where they can receive grace. If provision and healing happen through the community, it is truly a great blessing.

Battle for the high ground. The fact that the owner of the area changes several times a day, indicating fierce fighting, is proof of its importance. You are fighting an important battle. Trust in the Lord and rise up to fight again.
Do not be deceived, overcome evil with good. If you repay evil with evil, you are fighting and perishing. Do not be deceived.

A precious journey. I hope to go on a trip with loved ones. May the journey be filled with grace and beauty that the Lord provides, so that we become unforgettable meanings and names to each other, and the companionship of Jesus becomes captivating.

When disappointed. The disciples on the road to Emmaus were disappointed. Jesus made them fervent again. It was because He explained the Scriptures to them. Please come to the place of listening. If you do not listen and cannot hear, you will become scattered and stagnant, even if you serve a lot.
Decide the future now. If you want revival, you want the outcome to change. To change the outcome, the cause must change, which means changing your present.
God's alternative. For the severe seven-year famine, God prepared Joseph. God's alternative is one faithful person.
Do what you must do. Please do what you must do. Then what needs to happen will happen.

Give up the good for the best. One well-taken photo is better than hundreds of millions of poorly taken ones. A thousand keychains are not as useful as one key. If you have the Lord, it is good, and if you obey the Lord, it is truly good.

Relationships. It is impossible for me, who finds it difficult to fully obey God, to please imperfect and ever-changing people. If you decide and take responsibility in the Lord, you will have nothing but gratitude, without looking over your shoulder.
Revelation. The Lord's revelation shows only the one step that must be obeyed immediately. Because that is all you need to see.
The path to glory. Through repeated continuous obedience, you will eventually reach the place where you run your race and keep the faith.
Rollercoaster faith. It stops because it runs. Just keep running.
My way and God's way. Do not worry about what you cannot do; be faithful in what you can do. Then the Lord will add all these things.

Happiness does not come from the fulfillment of desires. David spent many of his younger days wandering in the wilderness and in enemy territory, being chased. If David had a choice, it would surely be to sleep at home with his children for at least one day. All the heroes of the Bible went forward not knowing their future, but when they looked only to the Lord and trusted Him, their lives were happy lives that fulfilled their purpose.

The necessity of helplessness. Only when you experience your own helplessness and hit rock bottom do you learn to trust the Lord. Only then do you realize that this war is futile with your own effort.
The reason God desires obedience. God sometimes makes us make difficult decisions. It is to let go and follow in obedience. Though you want to avoid it, God does not give up. He must use you.
The calm life of a sheep following the shepherd. The life of a sheep following the shepherd is serene. Because there is ultimate victory when following the shepherd.
How to do the Lord's work. The church suffers because of capable people. God works through useless servants because He is almighty.
A bold completion. If you start by letting go, you can be bold at the end.
Victory does not come to just anyone. Victory comes to those who win with the strength given by the Lord. Winning without the strength given by the Lord is not true victory, but merely a dispute.

If you cannot let go, you have no intention of letting go. If you are not prepared to lose, trials and tribulations will continue. To those who are willing to lose, the Lord alone will be their reward.
Be holy rather than successful. That is true victory.
When grace runs out, you return to what you used to enjoy.
It is not talent, but promise. If you try to work with talent, you will be impatient and frustrated, but if you hold onto the promise, you will be abundantly peaceful and whole.

Only by overcoming frustration does the real message begin. Even the faithful servants of God in the Bible felt deep frustration when their desire to be understood by others regarding their faith and deep spiritual communion was rejected. However, only by overcoming it can one participate in the Lord's main message and enjoy the Lord's glory rather than being recognized. "Though my father and mother forsake me, the Lord will take me in." - Psalm 27:10
Leadership is the self-control that clarifies what to focus on and the mobilization that moves all resources and strength towards that goal.

The evidence that a branch is not attached to the vine is that it bears no fruit and withers away powerlessly. The sole reason for a vine branch's existence is to bear fruit, and this happens when it is wholly attached to the vine and receives the farmer's cleansing.
A retreat is called that which can be simple yet great, simple yet intensive, and simple yet flexible.

Solutions do not bring happiness. If you have skillfully resolved every challenge, it means you have come to rely heavily on yourself. Seek the Lord rather than solutions. Through this blocked situation, may God be God, and may I die to myself and live for Jesus.

Right motives are more important than satisfactory results. When I try to take responsibility for the outcome, the Lord imposes limitations. This is because I do not know who the true master is. The Lord opens the door, and I seek the Lord who opens the door.

Advance of Sovereignty. Executing God's will is called faithfulness. Therefore, if you are faithful by faith, not looking at people, you will fulfill the Lord's will and experience His reign. The most advanced form of faithfulness is 'love.' Therefore, a loving person is called perfect. "And above all these put on love, which binds everything together in perfect harmony." - Colossians 3:14 "Whoever loves his neighbor has fulfilled the law." - Romans 13:8
A life without regret. If you are in the Lord, you should not regret it even if you have fallen a bit, because all things will work together for good.
The reason for repeatedly falling. It is not because the temptation is strong, but because you fear falling. If you do not fear, there is no need to fall.
The secret to victory. If the Lord does not help, you will only do things that lead to ruin.
The secret to sure victory. When you are angry, love. This is the surest way to overcome your adversary.
You will be tested. You will be tested. The reason it is a difficult test is that it is not a test to see if you can win, but a test to reveal that you have already won.

How thankful! He gives us situations that are difficult to be thankful for, so that we may be truly thankful. If the situation were good, why would faith be needed to be thankful, and what would be the point of acknowledging the Lord who governs the situation?
Everyday Special. Fresh events are sometimes necessary, but if you truly love, you can show a deeper love than events. Thriving communities are like retreats every week.

What should never be done, no matter what. You must not cause injustice. Whether it is the arrogance and prejudice of others, or my ignorance and bias, God will surely repay it. A more grievous sin than David's terrible adultery was killing a faithful righteous man. "You struck down the Hittite Uriah with the sword, and killed him, and took his wife to be your wife, and killed him with the sword of the Ammonites." - 2 Samuel 12:9

Foolishness. The devil's most cunning trick is the lie that tells us to solve problems with the flesh. The eternal adversary, Amalek, hides himself and attacks from behind. Rely only on the Lord. You will not make mistakes or regret it.
The most foolish thing. Worrying about what cannot be solved. Entrust it to the Lord and be free and at peace. Everything will work out well.

The secret to receiving power. It is not to feel, judge, and act by your own will, but to acknowledge and uphold the Lord's sovereignty. If you live according to your own will, you are a person of the flesh; if you live according to God's will, you are a person of the Spirit.
When you are thankful, you go beyond peace to boldness. Paul was not afraid of loneliness, tribulation, or death. It was because he was already sufficiently thankful.

A life without regret. When His will is fully accomplished, there is no regret. That will is not the fulfillment of my desires or the achievement of my wishes. His will is to take up the cross and go there. Therefore, those who uphold His will are fearless, bold, and without regret.

Repent through pain. The struggles of the young people deeply pierce the hearts of the ministers. Through that pain, we hear what He is saying. Be zealous! Repent! "For those whom I love I discipline..."

The precious blood. The only way to overcome sin and temptation is through the power of the precious blood. Without the precious blood, the Holy Spirit cannot dwell. The transformation that gives me praise every day is also the power of the precious blood. Never overlook the basics. Professionals are always faithful to the basics.

How to overcome situations and adversaries. For Paul, who ran solely towards the Lord, people could no longer be a source of suffering. Therefore, if your hope is only in this life, you are truly pitiable. Hope in the Lord and find your answer in the Lord.
The reason for being able to entrust. David did not seek revenge because he set his hope only in the living Lord.

The reason for not finishing by the Spirit. Many people start with the Spirit but finish with the flesh. This is because they lack the will to obey until death. Jesus emptied himself, took the form of a servant, and obeyed even to the point of death on the cross. If you cannot die, you cannot accomplish all things.

Closer than suffering. After entering into severe suffering, one realizes that Jesus is closer than the suffering. That is why hardship is a blessing. "Before I was afflicted I went astray, but now I keep your word." - Psalm 119:67

David's covenant. If only the leader can stand firm, God will give a lamp even in a collapsing situation. Become an ace like David. "The LORD your God, for David's sake, gave him a lamp in Jerusalem, to set up his son after him, and to establish Jerusalem, because the LORD had promised." - 1 Kings 15:4

In my opinion... I say my opinion is the best. That is why we fail. God is the one who turns the worst into the best, not the best into the best. David, for a moment losing faith and following the 'thoughts of his heart,' stayed in Philistine Ziklag for a year and four months, and experienced the 'trial of Ziklag,' which was as difficult as the Absalom incident. Decisions made without asking are usually easy decisions, and easy decisions always lead to ruin. Do not make easy decisions; take up your cross. "David thought in his heart, 'I shall now perish one day by the hand of Saul. It will be better for me to escape to the land of the Philistines.'" - 1 Samuel 27:1

No matter how much you rage, David's covenant is that even if everyone falls into temptation, if you and I remain unshaken and seek and obey God with all our hearts, He will save all the souls of the community. God remembered David and saved Israel.
Dedication is power. People say they cannot dedicate themselves because they lack power. No. It is because you do not dedicate yourself that you lack power.

Mentor and Mentee. The one who teaches is the one who learns. The one who learns will teach. In Hebrew, "to learn" is "lamad." But when it is in the intensive causative form, "limed," it means "to learn a lot" and also "to teach." The one who teaches is the one who learns more.
Spiritual malnutrition. There is nothing more dangerous than trying to live today on yesterday's grace, spiritual experiences, or anointing. It is like being malnourished but not feeling hungry.
What is love? If it ends with "Ah, it's just perfect," it will soon crumble. Love is based on understanding and fulfilling responsibility.

The reason for moving forward: If you cannot be bold, you cannot be obedient, and if you cannot be obedient, you cannot move forward. Boldness comes when He is with us, and therefore the greatest sin is to be unfulfilled. For sin begins there.
There is no frustration. If I relied on my own abilities, there would be frustration, but since I live by His guidance, I just go. The Lord is the conclusion.

True happiness: If you try to fulfill your own will, you will be anxious, and even if you achieve it, it will be empty, but if you try to fulfill the Lord's will, you will have no fear and will be happy.

It's not that there is no method, but because the purpose is not clear, we end up blaming the circumstances. For those who pursue the Lord and for communities, there are always wisdom and alternatives.
I wander. It's not that I don't know the answer. I just don't act on it. You have known how to act before the Lord for a long time.

Only when you entrust yourself to God do you become gentle. Gentleness is withholding judgment until God acts. It is not my thought but the Lord's heart, so I cannot fabricate it; it is given only to those who completely trust in the living God.
Peter explains the power of Satan's temptation by comparing it to a hungry lion. Here, Satan is called Diabolos, which originally means "accuser."

From Joseph's perspective, Potiphar's wife must have been someone he wanted to kill. At least, that was the case in Joseph's situation. However, without her, he would not have gone to prison or met the cupbearer. Potiphar's wife was truly a greatly used person. If you look with your own eyes, things just get complicated, but if you look with the Lord's eyes, all things work together for good. The Lord has made everything for its purpose, even the wicked for the day of trouble. Proverbs 16:4

The invisible roots determine the freshness of the visible tree. Receiving blessings through the parents and authorities given to me to serve is the first step in unlocking my interpersonal relationships, ministry, and vision. I hope all young people will be wise enough and that the doors of life will open for them so that parents and ministers can bless them abundantly. The success or failure of life lies in the basics.
Do not react with your nature; react with the Word. If you pause for a moment and receive the Word, which contains God's will and desire, you will feel that there is nothing you cannot receive.
Christianity that has lost its focus is more dangerous than immoral heresies. It is like a disease without symptoms being more dangerous.

The One who calls, the One who uses, and the One who prepares are one. If the Lord says He will do it, He will do it. He is the Master. Worry and anxiety are the master's portion, and obedience is the servant's portion. Say, "Let the Lord use me." - Mark 11:3
I want you to act according to the calling you have received. I have come for this purpose.
One case where a twist drama becomes a tragedy is when you drop out of the drama midway. The deeper the 고민 (worry/concern), the more glorious the twist will be.

What is clear is that there will always be a lack in every moment of our lives. God's glory is revealed when these shortcomings and slow progress are interpreted not as handicaps but as opportunities. Instead of the path not opening now, it means He has given us a little more time to serve the Lord and the saints before we become incredibly busy. We must become people who are more faithful and prepared when we have time now.
Opportunity in the worst situation: Only in the worst situations can we offer the greatest thanksgiving. The Lord is watching me even now. Be faithful.
Though there are many fiery trials, let us not be afraid. The Savior's power is great, so we shall overcome and have more than enough. Hymn 376

For Jonah, who was about to disobey, encountering a ship bound for Tarshish "just then" and the wind blowing favorably does not mean God is condoning his stubbornness, but rather it is a brief warm-up before encountering the storm and entering the belly of the fish.

The reason for faithfulness is to run towards the will of the One who called us without giving up. The reason this is possible is that He is watching. When you are conscious of God, it becomes faithfulness; when you are conscious of people, it becomes hypocrisy.
