What is a leader? It is not to show the countless arrows stuck in your back while smiling sheepishly at the whining children.

Visibility zero: When you can see nothing, you should not worry, but trust in the Lord who knows and prepares all things. If you imagine flying through a dark space, you must ultimately listen only to the Lord's voice and move forward. Life is a competition anyway. Go coolly. If God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the furnace, will He not much more clothe you, O you of little faith? - Matthew 6:30
Complaining and criticizing are strong, but sacrifice is little; this is our state today. Ananias and Sapphira were disciplined. It was not a matter of the amount but a sin of the heart and motive.

Unnecessary desires will be accompanied by confusion and unnecessary suffering. However, great missions will be accompanied by obedience and reward. For the desires of the flesh are against the Spirit, and the desires of the Spirit are against the flesh, for these are contrary to each other, so that you may not do what you desire. - Galatians 5:17
Although there are many painful things, the reason I smile is that I have a vision given to me by the Lord. - 1999 New Year Revival, Second Day Evening
Even if the authority is wrong, to ignore it or seek revenge is to be someone without God in their life. It means being a formal servant.

Even if I have nothing left, if the Lord looks upon me, His servant, and gives me the last pulse to burn, I will run again for the Lord's glory. If my strength gives out while running, I will call out Jesus with my last breath. Then I will be truly happy.

Those who stand firm against wind and rain are followed by a crowd. You are asked to be faithful to where the Word tells you to go, without expecting great fortune in your circumstances. The outcome must be fully entrusted to God.

If you set your priorities correctly, your sphere of influence becomes clear. Do not worry about areas you cannot handle, because they are not permitted to you. What I cannot do is not my concern, but the Lord's concern. I only need to seek His kingdom and His righteousness.
Criticizing may feel satisfying, but you lose people. If you persuade them, even if it takes time, they will understand, and spontaneity will arise from that.

Do not worry about the results you regret, but rather that the process and motives are not regrettable. Fearing the outcome will always lead to decisions of regret. If your heart and soul are in it, you have done well.

Philippians 1:29 "For it has been granted to you..." Reading this far makes my heart race with anticipation of what grace God will bestow. The latter half says, "...that you should suffer for His sake." ㅠㅠ Christians inevitably face battles. The greater the battle, the greater the victory. Hallelujah! "This is not a half-day sporting event that is easily won and quickly forgotten. This is a war of attrition, a fight for survival against the devil and his forces." (Ephesians 6:12, The Message)

Memories Practice In difficult moments, beautiful memories suddenly surface like faded photographs. "Can the beauty of sitting in a cafe on a cold winter day, sharing faith amidst the aroma of coffee, ever return?" Surely, it was God's grace that granted such happiness. And though no one can know, if that happiness is granted again, it will also be God's grace. The Lord's grace sustains me.
We will go forward as light into darkness. If I fall, another light will advance. Thus, we will finally proclaim the Lord's glory and witness all nations worship Him.
There will be trials throughout our lives. We will also see ourselves faltering and shaken by trials. However, though the wind blows and dogs bark, the train speeds on.

In Acts 21, many prophets and servants of the Lord, moved by the Holy Spirit, prophesy tribulation in Jerusalem and try to stop Paul's journey. However, Paul had already been enlightened by the Holy Spirit in chapter 20. While believers who only see the tribulation in Jerusalem try to stop Paul, Paul, who has seen even "to Rome," presses on. We can advance and be bold only as much as we can see. Do not just look at Jerusalem; look beyond to Rome.

In Acts 27, everyone was saved from a desperate situation where all hope of survival was lost. The soldiers thought it best to kill the prisoners lest they escape by swimming, but the centurion, wanting to save Paul, prevented them from carrying out their plan. Upon reaching the island, Paul was bitten by a viper but did not die. Paul was not afraid. He was convinced that the storm, the people who wanted to kill him, and the viper would not be harmed or die until the time appointed by God and His will was fulfilled. Discern the Lord's time and will. Discernment is power.
Stop worrying about what others think and look at God's word. He will make you wiser and more excellent than your enemies.
The process is more important than the outcome. God speaks many words in the process.
Do not react to people; react to God. Then you can walk the path the Lord leads. Do not seek the Lord's will in the reactions of people.
In moments of loss, the object of our reliance can truly be called our faith. When the Lord allows the pain of loss, I hope you will quietly look to Him.
People desire success, but success is achieved through repeated failures.

"If anyone wants to do His will, he shall know whether this teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own." (John 7:17) If you try to do His will and obey, you will hear the word and discern. But those who do not obey stumble in darkness, for they turn away from the light of truth and live "loving darkness rather than light." (John 3:19)
Beloved, there is no waste of time or futility in the kingdom of God. This moment I am with you is more meaningful than a thousand or ten thousand years.

What hinders a mission is not one's own weakness, persecution, or inferiority complex. It is not health or circumstances either. Only Satan hates and hinders missions. I hope you have the wisdom to ignore everything else and focus on that one enemy. I only fight Satan.

In truth, there is no luck or coincidence in life. There is always a purpose He has prepared. Life is walking that path, and sometimes we pass through a furnace seven times hotter, but His deep purpose does not fade away.
The best thing a minister can do is not to hinder or obstruct revival. I am reminded of the poem by Song Myung-hee: "The Lord has done it."

King Saul tried to kill David to protect what the Lord had not given him, and he eventually died himself. David entrusted what God had given him, sang praises in psalms during lonely nights, and the Lord eventually fulfilled it.

Ironically, after Saul achieved a great victory in the war against Amalek, he began a decisive path of decline. After this day, he never met the prophet Samuel again. The greatest crisis comes when things seem to be going very well and successfully. Life can crumble even after winning a war.
How thankful we are! How thankful we are to be able to repent! "And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers." (Luke 22:32)

Life is a battle for control. Who holds that control determines victory or defeat. The Lord does not help us take control of our lives; He is the One who rightfully governs my life in my stead. Victory comes when this battle becomes the Lord's battle, not mine.
It is not that life is becoming increasingly difficult, but rather that I am increasingly being prepared for the path the Lord desires.
It is not that the other person did wrong, but that I needed refinement. Look and interpret with eyes of faith, not wounded emotions. Interpretation is power.
Boldness is not based on observing the situation. When I am bold, the situation obeys.

The way to win. In a game, there is something called "momentum," meaning an opportunity to win. The moment when I am struggling to death is the golden opportunity. If you push hard in the last five minutes, you can lead the game to victory. The most foolish thing is to fail to push when you have the momentum. Then, a game that could end in five minutes must start all over again. When you have spiritual "momentum," you must go all the way.
Do not judge and despair according to the situation; rather, obey the Lord's leading and find peace. Do not look at the situation; interpret it with faith.
It is not that circumstances allow God's will to be done. When God's will is done, circumstances change.

The devil's enticement: "There are many problems, so let's take a break." "You've done enough; it's sufficient." "Let's stop for now, put aside what you're doing, and regroup." Paul preached the gospel even in prison to the guards and strengthened the church through his letters. Never stop. "So that it is clear to the whole palace guard and to all the others that my imprisonment is for Christ." (Philippians 1:13)
Do not live as you are; live as you have been trained.

Vitality is challenged. Maintaining and strengthening the grace received at the retreat requires a price. 1. You must hold onto the Lord with your life. The Lord strongly supports those who fear Him. 2. You must bless and forgive. When we bless and pray for each other, the Holy Spirit moves and is with us.

It is not about avoiding, but about overcoming. Storms and tempests are not to be avoided, but overcome. A life without turbulence does not exist. What matters is not the strength and height of the rough waves, but the grace that overwhelms the wind. When we believe in the Lord's sufficient grace, we will overcome every approaching trial without wavering. "My soul waits in silence for God alone, from him comes my hope. My soul waits in silence for God alone, from him comes my hope. He alone is my rock, my salvation; he alone is my fortress, I will not be shaken. My soul waits eagerly for the Lord, and my lips will praise the Lord. My soul eagerly follows the Lord, and I will bless the Lord all my life. He alone is my rock, my salvation; he alone is my fortress, I will not be shaken." Psalm 62
Grace that transcends limits. I dislike hardship, but I am not afraid. I am weak, but I see the Lord's grace working everywhere. The Lord's grace is not hindered by my human limitations.

Are you at peace? You must be at peace. Did not Jesus bear the cross? Whether my path is smooth and calm like a gentle river, or fearsome and difficult with great storms, my soul is always at peace. Though the devil rushes at us with open mouth to devour us, Jesus is our commander, and we will fight and win in the end. - Hymn 413

When everything collapses, what can the righteous do if the foundation collapses? The unrighteous flee to the mountains. But the righteous choose the 'righteous God' even when everything has collapsed. Because He is watching me. "How long will you all conspire against me, to kill me? You are all like a wall ready to fall, like a tottering fence. They all plot to bring me down from my vantage point; they deceive with their mouths, but curse with their inner mouths. For the Lord is righteous, he loves justice; the upright will see his face." Psalm 11:1, 3, 7

God's Crystal Terms and Conditions: Your sin stands between us, interrupting our communion. I can no longer bless you while you persist in it. Trust My Spirit to help you, My child! Do not reject God's word. Complete obedience is power. For when the king rejected the word of the Lord, the Lord rejected him as king over Israel. - 1 Samuel 15:26
No one gets married wanting to do dishes and garden cleanup. These actions follow as a result of love. Never work without grace. Whatever you do, you will be happy if you walk with Him.

God's Specialty. There is no such thing as a law that says you must die. If you go forward with the resolve to die, God will make a way you never imagined. God's specialty is making springs in the desert and paths in the wilderness.
When you succeed, you will feel envious glances from some. But when you obey, you will experience the power of the earth shaking and the heavens weeping. Obedience is more important than success.

Do not try to succeed, but try to obey. My fulfillment is not important; the Lord's will takes precedence. That is discipleship. "By this all people will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another." John 13:35

Though I have many shortcomings and regrets before the saints, I run today because of the Lord who called me. What I have lived until now is due to the Lord's burning compassion, and if I am to run for the Lord and serve souls in the future, it will be due to the Lord's boundless grace. Let us look only to the Lord.

The weapons of the Spirit include honesty, humility, truthfulness, grace, love, prayer, obedience, community, healthy dependence, God's Word, and following the Spirit's guidance. On the other hand, the weapons of the flesh include excessive anger, deceit, criticism, shame, hatred, pride, secretiveness, self-centeredness, defensiveness, and an unhealthy "I don't need you" attitude. The spiritual war we are fighting is not "a brief skirmish that is won easily and forgotten quickly. This is a war of attrition, a battle for life and death." (Ephesians 6:12, The Message)

In worldly sports, results are very important because of the audience's cheers. However, for us, the process and attitude are more important because there is Someone watching. Even if the path ahead seems entirely dark, God does not make mistakes. I can do all things through Him who strengthens me, and all things work together for good.

The excessive reactions of the world and people are not within my sphere. They are none of my concern. This is a problem that is solved by being filled with the Holy Spirit and receiving power. "It is not for you to know the times or the seasons that the Father has set by his own authority. But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses..." Acts 1:7-8

We must live fiercely and diligently enough to achieve worldly success. However, the purpose is not to claim rights over that achievement. Because we do not claim rights, we do not cling to it, and because we do not cling to it, we are not irresponsible either. When we walk the path He has given, all things work for good.

Somali pirates do not touch American merchant ships. This is because they refuse to negotiate with terrorists, making it impossible to receive money. Do not react to rumors and words. Then evil powers will no longer have power in relationships.

There are illusions that one can achieve greed by any means, find stability, and then invoke God's name. God is not someone who goes along with such success. He seeks and supports those who rely solely on His power and move forward boldly.
A student who has completed all homework and studied perfectly for the exam is not just at peace, but bold and joyful. Those who achieve their purpose are bold. That is why those we love are bold.

You don't need to send a reminder to the cupbearer to remember your favor. No matter how insignificant or meaningless your work may seem (like in prison), if you faithfully do your job, the day will come when Pharaoh will be so curious about his dreams that he will seek you out. Prepare your whole life for the moment.
Do not crumble easily. You must resist challenges fiercely, regardless of victory or defeat. This makes it easier to recover in the next step even if you fall.

We may be dragged along as defeated soldiers, barely alive, on the path we should be walking with songs of victory. The clear reason for this is that we cannot serve the Lord alone and harbor vain thoughts, and we are filled with the world rather than the Spirit. What is there to fear or find difficult for a life that offers itself to the Lord?

Trials and challenges will not cease. And none of them have ever been easy. But I am not afraid either. What more can a person who has already died on the cross cling to? Even if everything is taken away, the truth will live on and its kingdom will be eternal. Hope is power.

When you meet trials, you should be glad. This is because the testing of your faith produces perseverance. When perseverance is complete, you will be mature and complete, lacking nothing. It is difficult to persevere without reason. Therefore, a guide is needed. If you receive wisdom and insight that last month was an attack but this month will bear fruit, you can persevere through current suffering. Trials → Maturity; perseverance requires wisdom. If you are going through a tunnel, pass through it boldly. God will surely reward you. "Count it all joy, my brothers, when you meet trials of various kinds, for you know that the testing of your faith produces steadfastness. And let steadfastness have its full effect, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing. If any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask God, who gives generously to all without reproach, and it will be given him." James 1:2, 5

When you encounter trials, what the Lord commands is perseverance. Perseverance makes me complete. The wisdom of looking to the Lord's promise will help perseverance. The Lord is a rewarder of those who hope. "Count it all joy, my brothers, when you meet trials of various kinds, for you know that the testing of your faith produces steadfastness. And let steadfastness have its full effect, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing. If any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask God, who gives generously to all without reproach, and it will be given him." James 1:2, 5
If trials lead to hope, break through them with all your might. For we know that hardship produces perseverance, perseverance produces character, and character produces hope.

The word that has been chosen repeatedly since the New Year's conference is about faithfulness, labor, and dedication. And it is said that this labor and faithfulness will surely be remembered by God. Let us run towards that word, looking only to the Lord. Let us run for the Lord, leaving behind hardship and pain. "My beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that in the Lord your labor is not in vain." 1 Corinthians 15:58

Faith has various difficult factors and trials. There are two ways to walk that path: either go filled, or barely go. Environmental problems can be solved by being filled, but spiritual poverty cannot be solved by the environment. Simply be filled.

Faith is not over after hitting one home run. It progresses through choices made day by day. If we live by choosing according to the Spirit, we will not fulfill the lusts of the flesh or the worries caused by vain loss.

Believers must be flexible. There is nothing worth risking our lives for except the Lord, and within the Lord, all things work together for good. If you get easily excited, you will have many embarrassing moments later. The Almighty Father makes many beautiful conclusions even without my anger.

The reason believers need power is not to reign like tyrants and oppress. The world needs money and power to live comfortably and securely while discriminating, but we need power to face hardship and see the Lord's glory.
If you can overcome disappointing situations and endure criticism and ridicule for the sake of hope, you can receive the grace God intends to give.

Disappointment comes from greed. Greed means having human expectations. If the Lord has not given it, we have received nothing. If it is the Lord's gift, let us obey and wait. If we use human calculations and methods, our autobiography will become thick like Jacob's.
If you are disappointed, it means you were impatient. Impatience means you did not trust the Lord and, furthermore, sought your own glory. Remember this. The Lord will never give His glory to idols.
No matter how dark the situation looks and how critical the environment seems, we move towards the goal. We love and humble ourselves with the power to never stop ignoring... ♡♡♡♡

Abraham was commanded to leave the land of Ur of the Chaldeans and go to the land of Canaan. However, he wandered in Haran, an intermediate point. After receiving God's rebuke there, he finally entered the land of Canaan. That was at the age of 75. Please do not wander in Haran. The meaning of Haran is 'dryness'.
It is by the Lord's strong right arm that we are held up and not utterly cast down. Not utterly cast down... Ps 37

Never be overcome by evil. Even if you are overcome by evil, the Lord will restore you, but now you must overflow with the grace of leaping rather than the grace of restoration. Not being overcome by evil means not sinning, but rather overwhelming evil with good. Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good. Romans 12:21

Evil people may seem strong and prosperous, but their lives are like chaff blown away before the day of tribulation and judgment. This is because they have no roots. No matter how lush their leaves may be, they will be blown away at the decisive moment. Winnowing in farming means blowing away both grain and chaff together. Then the grain remains, and the chaff is blown away. The condition of the righteous is in God's word.
If the one who went ahead is faithful, the one who follows will also become faithful. Let's be faithful, not faint.

If ambition is disguised as a calling, competition arises. If there is competition, obedience is impossible. This is because the means and the end are reversed. If you cannot obey, the Lord is not your master.

* Schedule A Christian renowned for his piety was found to be involved in a crime. He was even caught in the act of attempted rape and received a heavy sentence, going to prison. While he was serving his sentence, some said that people's hearts are unfathomable, and others said that the God he believed in so much was fake. His name was Joseph. (Gen 39) Be patient. God's timetable and my timetable may differ somewhat.

Sometimes we encounter terrible, hell-like situations. It would never happen, but if we were sent to a hell-like place, I would find a guitar as much as allowed, form a worship group, and worship the Lord. And after the meeting, since there is a lot of sulfur fire there, it wouldn't be difficult to prepare food. Remember that the Lord's grace is sufficient, that He gives us what we can bear, and that it is clear what we must do wherever we are.
If you endure when things are difficult, you will surely win. Foolishness is giving up a game that is sure to be won. Giving up is the work of the devil, and enduring is the work of the Holy Spirit.
The foolish person thinks of each moment meaninglessly. This means not cherishing the time. This moment we are together now is more precious than gold. It is because it is life.

* Life comes with hardship as a default option. When God does not work according to my will, it is easy to blame others. This is because we try to walk in the air, detached from reality. Life is not a vacation; it is paying a price to hold onto holiness.

To the one who faithfully tends his small flock, the Lord of glory will appear and reveal Himself 'suddenly, unexpectedly, at a time not anticipated.' Now there were in the same country shepherds out in the field, keeping watch over their flock by night. And behold, an angel of the Lord stood before them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were greatly afraid. Luke 2:8,9

How much you have is not very important. What is important is whose hand you are held by. The two small fish and five loaves of a little child were nothing, but in Jesus' hands, they became a miracle that fed 5,000 people. Please entrust your 부족한 life to the Lord. The Lord's grace will be sufficient.

When a mother goes into surgery, the children cry. But there is nothing they can do to take her place. The mother tells her children to listen well and take care of each other, and she will return healthy. Jesus had one request the day before He was crucified. While washing their feet, He asked them to cover each other's shortcomings and love one another. "By this all people will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another." This is the Lord's request. The only way to follow the Lord now is to love one another.
* The Final Match Our match is not in this world. The true match is decided when we breathe our last breath and stand before the Lord.

If you hold onto summer because you like it, wanting not to let it go, the Lord cannot give you a bountiful autumn. If you let go of earthly things, you receive better things from heaven. That is walking with the Lord.
Leisure does not come automatically, nor is it given by favorable circumstances. Leisure is the spoils of diligent 'spiritual warfare' and the result of 'prioritizing' looking only to the Lord.

The Lord was with Joseph, so he became a successful man in the house of his Egyptian master. - Gen 39:2 Joseph was a slave. Yet the Bible calls him a successful man. He was betrayed, hated, and lost his status and much more, yet he is called successful. Conversely, his brothers who sold Joseph are free and seem to have nothing to envy, but they became unsuccessful. The reason for his success was that God was with him. Immanuel itself is a blessing. If God is with us, it does not mean our trials and storms are exempted, but rather that we experience His living presence in the midst of them.
The Lord blessed the Egyptian's house for Joseph's sake, and the blessing of the Lord was on all that he had, in the house and in the field... Potiphar was blessed because of Joseph.
* Ministry is Motivation Ministry is Motivation. If you are not motivated, instead of forcing others, it is something for which you must humble yourself and repent.

* Small Obedience, Miraculous Change When a church is not going according to the Bible, pouring passion into criticism will accelerate its collapse and you will not escape judgment. If you examine yourself and live by thoroughly obeying the Word, you will experience the miracle of the community coming alive.

In English, the word "crazy" is sometimes used to describe continuing in a field with passion. It's like being absorbed... However, the word "insane" feels like going to a fake well where you can't get living water. If you are insane about something futile, turn around and be crazy about the true goal. If you are not crazy, you cannot reach.

When engaging in spiritual warfare, persistence regardless of circumstances is the key to victory or defeat. Love is patient. If you persist with love, strongholds will crumble. Keep going. They can never keep going.

There are two ways to go to the next house: one is to walk straight, and the other is to go around the earth in the opposite direction. I will run to you. Yes, I will run the race until I see your face, not by might, not by power, but by the Spirit of God.

* When facing unfairness, you will face unfairness anyway. You may be misunderstood about things you haven't done and your intentions. There are two choices: either you explode in anger, and without resolution, you die and kill others, or you think of the Lord and glory within it, endure, and become beautiful. Great glory will be to the one who considers the Lord who allowed all this. It is good for me that I was afflicted, that I may learn Your statutes. - Psalm 119:71 Though unjustly afflicted, if you endure with thoughts of God, it is beautiful. - 1 Peter 2:19

* The Fate of One Who Looks to People Trying to satisfy people is truly selfish. Pilate knew Jesus was innocent, but to satisfy the Jews, he handed Him over to be crucified. It wasn't because he loved the Jews, but solely for his own comfort that he tried to satisfy them. Stop worrying about what people think and give E.F.X to the Lord.

Jesus becomes stronger as He is attacked. This is because faith, from the beginning, relies on the Lord, not on our own strength. This is why we become stronger when things are difficult, and the Lord's power becomes stronger when we are weak. Hit me a little more, until the edge is sharp.
Jesus does not force storms upon us. Nor will He ever lead us into one without giving us the strength to overcome it.

There are those who stubbornly walk a path Jesus forbids. They are foolish. There are those who seek Jesus' help on their own path. They are shrewd. There are those who walk a path they would not have taken without Jesus. They are people of faith. They will see God's glory.

From the past until now, the Lord has helped us and continues to help us. Samuel took a stone and set it up between Mizpah and Shen, saying, "The Lord has helped us this far," and named it Ebenezer. - 1 Samuel 7:12

In the past, at 20 years old, with a broken and torn heart, I went to an all-night prayer service, gritting my teeth and staying up all night. Around 2 AM, Pastor Kim Jong-han, who was 73, said to me through an unexpected word: 1. I understand all the pain in your heart. 2. And you, walk the Lord's path. God understands me better than anyone. And He encourages me to walk the Lord's path. A person of faith does not fail in God's work due to personal circumstances. It is a rule for me, who has kept it for the past 24 years, and the minimum courtesy to the Lord. Only by faith! I can do all things through Him who gives me strength.
* Philippians Deep Dive (Intense and Powerful) Bible Study - Preparing fellowship materials

* The Power of Refusal In a wicked age, by refusing peace and comfort and embracing our calling, we can live a life obedient to God's intention in calling us to this place. We mistakenly believe we can walk the Lord's path and fulfill our own desires simultaneously. We receive blessings when we refuse our desires. If your desire is greed, the Lord's desire is your calling.

* Reasons for Not Changing Even When Agreeing We may nod in agreement and listen sweetly while agonizing, but ultimately, when we see our choices, we cannot give up on ourselves. This is because we have no intention of giving up the pleasures we enjoy from the beginning. If you do not obey, you will not hear the voice.
* Powerful Provision For God's work that aligns with God's will, His faithful provision is never lacking.

* The Secret to Handling Life To achieve a blessed life, many things must be removed. Among them, if possible, we would try to remove the causes of suffering and be free. One of the greatest sufferings is the pain of childbirth, yet no one tries to discard the cause of that pain (life). Suffering is not a problem to be avoided but a work to be completed.

* Winning Hearts Winning people's hearts is not achieved by effort alone. It is grace. Therefore, two things must be done: contentment and gratitude. If you have more than one thing given to you, it means there are things to serve and do.

* Limitation is Excellence If you are swayed by circumstances and situations, you cannot follow God's will. You must discern the Lord's path for you and draw a line of what you must not tolerate. Limitation is excellence.

* What to Do When Humbling Yourself There is something you must do when you humble yourself. Your destiny changes there. In your unknown days, you must possess skill. It is difficult to develop sharp spirituality when you are well-fed and treated with respect. Abide in His presence, from which you cannot be robbed even if you give everything.

Only the Lord holds me. Even when I stumble, He will still hold me. If your law had not been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction. - Psalm 119:92 This word is my comfort in my distress, for your word has preserved my life. - Psalm 119:50

A person who sits on five thousand loaves and eats alone is not living well. They are merely a foolish rich person, not living well. A person who shares even a single bean is living well. You must become a person of faith who rushes into fire and water to save many people.
If you have sought only God's glory and the salvation of souls, you are already a champion. Do not be shaken; boldly look only to the Lord.

* Impatience Will I grow old like this? Will I become a failure, a loser in life? Should I take some action? Worrying that I might become someone I don't want to be is impatience. Impatience is natural if you want to achieve your desires, but those who fulfill God's will are bold and peaceful because He accomplishes it.

Joseph refuses sexual temptation and willingly goes to prison instead. He then forgives his wicked brothers who tried to kill him and wasted most of his teens and twenties in slavery and prison. If he had lived a life of revenge, he would have become miserable himself and made others miserable too. After being released from prison at 30, he lived for another 81 years, fulfilling God's amazing plan. This is the ability to interpret, discern the Lord's will, redeem the time, live wisely until the end, seize opportunities, and walk with the Lord. (Ephesians 5:15-17)

* Bad Habits Even when busy with work and exams are imminent, and your mind is so preoccupied that you don't think, "Ah, I've forgotten to breathe for the past few days! I need to breathe quickly!" You don't skip meals, let alone breathing. If you miss a place of grace because of work, it becomes a habit. You are nurturing a terrible habit.
Since it is God who works in us, I too must walk by the Spirit. This means self-control and focus. Then the desires of the flesh are rejected, and God's will is executed.

* Why Storms Are Fierce If a test is too difficult or too easy, its ability to gauge skill is diminished. Everyone can take it, but it is never easy, and the higher the level of the test, the higher the difficulty. Therefore, the wicked cannot stand judgment and fall away, but the blessed, the righteous, endure. The reason the storms are fierce is that not just anyone is allowed to go that way, and that place is prepared solely for me. But it is not so with the wicked; they are like chaff that the wind blows away. Therefore, the wicked will not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous. - Psalm 1:4,5

* We Win Even in Darkness All the figures in the Bible did not walk by seeing their success. They were people who, though in darkness, ran striving to live the life God desired. Those who stand in that place are already victors and blessed. Blessed is the one who does not walk in step with the wicked, who does not stand in the way of sinners, nor sit in the seat of mockers. But whose delight is in the law of the Lord, and on his law he meditates day and night. He is like a tree planted by streams of water, which yields its fruit in season and whose leaves do not wither; whatever he does prospers. - Psalm 1:1-3

We are shocked when famous people suddenly pass away. It is not that trials killed them. Most of them 'gave up' in the face of trials and fear. Trials and fear have never voluntarily or actively destroyed or killed people.
When we fall into sin, there is something that is inevitably proven: the fact that we have lost sight of the Lord. As long as we look to the Lord, even if the enemy comes against me, I will not fall.
When we pass through a tunnel, the most necessary thing is humility. For He humbles you, tests you, and knows what is in your heart, whether you keep His commandments or not. (Deuteronomy 8:2)
The reason we give up a day, and then a week, is not because the challenge is fierce, but because it seems difficult. Get up even if you fall and keep going. Never give up looking to the Lord.

The reason we must unhesitatingly continue to hold onto truth and advance even when facing tribulation is that no matter how dark the situation may seem, God is the One who gives a home run in the bottom of the ninth inning with His great wisdom and power. Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up. (Galatians 6:9)

* Meddling Even to Help God Trying to help God yourself because the situation is frustrating (?) is a lack of faith that severely limits God's sovereignty and work. Abraham helping God is the birth of Ishmael, the seed of conflict, through Hagar, and Peter acting with a sword in Gethsemane. (The Lord must have been very flustered in that urgent moment, having to find and reattach the ear that fell off in the middle of the night. ㅠㅜ)

We react easily, get angry easily, and also let go easily. If we had remained still in the Lord, we would have returned to our original place, but in that brief moment, a whole drama unfolds. Entrust it. The Lord takes responsibility.

Sometimes we cannot give a precise answer as to why we are in this place. We fight on the front lines even though we did not volunteer. This is because it is the Lord's calling, not our decision. If He has called us, He has certainly given us the ability to bear it.

We cannot arbitrarily dictate or refuse God's methods of shaping us. However, within those circumstances, we can decide our heart and attitude towards God. Do not try to do everything; do what you can. What more do you have to worry about?

We are God's creation and in His hands. Therefore, we do not act by force. We believe that entrusting ourselves to Him is the most righteous and best path. The Lord always gives me the best. If He doesn't give it, it's because I don't deserve it. It is more beneficial not to have it. The Lord boasted of Job's faith to Satan. Because of that, Job's trials begin. Thus, people know that suffering is the subject of courage. The conclusion is that Job changes. A decent believer becomes a person of faith who personally encounters the Lord. It seems like the trials began with God and Satan making a bet. No. It is the Lord's providence that leads us to move from the good to the best. Therefore, the theme of the book of Job is grace. He changes Satan's accusations and makes us like refined gold. He has the power to work all things for good.

Suffering is discernment. When the college entrance exam is too easy, everyone gets high scores, so evaluation is not possible. The reason suffering is difficult is that it narrows the entrance, and it is a place only for me who has received grace, not for just anyone. If it is difficult, it is prepared.

We endure tribulations and overcome challenges. However, enduring and overcoming is not the purpose. Through it, we release God's reign and rule into our sphere. You will be slandered and attacked (Luke 6:26). However, the suffering we are now enduring cannot be compared to the glory, rule, and dominion we will enjoy in the future (Rom 8:18). To release the Lord's reign in healing, prophecy, love, restoration, politics, economics, and sports, we must fight and endure. We must not give up. Then God will use us as a light to the nations (Acts 13:47).

Restoration Vs. Avoidance. There are people who avoid problems when they encounter them. The easiest decision is the most wicked decision. Instead of leaving my place, I can make the place where I am full and a place of repentance. If you face the Lord instead of turning away from the problem, it will be solved.
Most of our failures or mistakes can be forgiven in our lives. As long as we do not give up, which is the most fatal and biggest mistake for a person.

The Power of Giving Everything. Why is there no power? Because we give only a part. Simply put, you just have to give your whole life. Everyone is afraid when asked to give everything. They say they can't even go on trips and it's not fun. That's right. The trips you take won't be fun either. Instead, there will be journeys and joys with co-workers that the Lord has prepared. It is more important who you go with, even if you go to the same Scotland.
Command fate to come. Those who have entrusted everything to the Lord have nothing to fear.
Shout towards your destiny. Come, destiny, I will not fear and will entrust all things to the Lord.

Do not live as you wish, but live by asking for what you need. Running away and quitting everything is what you wish for, but receiving God's power and authority where you stand is what you need. I hope you become young people who overcome darkness with the sword of the Word.

The Most Powerful Restoration. The Word of God must flow. It must become overflowing Word. If God's Word flows into my life, I am happy enough without filling it with anything else. This is because peace in relationships and fulfillment of mission are achieved automatically. The person who fulfills the Lord's will is the person who experiences the most powerful restoration. It is not that I fulfill my mission, but that the mission fulfills me.
The Biggest Mistake. The biggest mistake is doing nothing for fear of making a mistake. If you avoid the competition, you will regret it deeply.
If you know the meaning, you can endure. Rather, you can rejoice. Meaning gives the strength to love. You endure all things, bear all things.

Doctors tell patients how serious the situation is before treating them. It is not because they lack the skill to cure, but because they want the patient to manage their condition long-term without returning to harmful habits. God allows us to experience the bottom before He resolves it. This is because the purpose is transformation, not resolution.
May you become powerful young people who awaken souls in spiritual lethargy by seeking devotion without entrustment and a crown without suffering, and by compromising with reality. - Winter of 1995
In this last generation, those who do not waste time, are not deceived by schemes, and truly accomplish the Lord's work are those who discern the Lord's will. - Eph 5:17

No one can force me to give up my dream just because it is difficult to achieve. Do not fear a fight you cannot win, nor worry about pain you cannot bear. Also, dream even if you cannot achieve it. Miracles happen where no one has looked.

The Israelites experienced the miracle of the Exodus, drank water from the rock, were baptized in the Red Sea, and ate the spiritual food of manna, but they perished. Experience is not everything. Do not grumble. For some of them, when they grumbled, they perished by the destroyer, so do not grumble like them. - 1 Corinthians 10
When you are in an unfamiliar environment, you will experience God's work more concretely.

Human will is greed. The Lord's will is mission. If you live according to your heart's desires, you will chase after what you have not been given, but if you live according to the Spirit's desires, you will live with gratitude for what you have been given.

It is not difficult to realize that life does not go according to your efforts. Unexpected lives and encounters await you from the beginning, and you feel yourself drifting further from the life path you have drawn. Behind that is the Lord's guidance. Many figures in the Bible did not live the lives they desired or could have foreseen, but lived according to the will God intended to fulfill.

The reason the storms of life are intensifying is because we do not turn to God. The storm does not stop until Jonah repents. Even if you decide to be thrown into the sea, a fish is prepared in the sea. God never negotiates.
When going through the storms of life, the most important thing is to anchor yourself in Jesus.

Life does not become wonderful in an instant. Just as there is a time and season for everything, wrinkles and white hair appear, and we become wise. True wisdom and understanding lie in making room for the Lord to act rather than intervening ourselves and entrusting it to Him.

When life twists contrary to your desires, there is the Lord's amazing providence. Augustine aspired to be a rhetorician, Paul a famous rabbi, and Joseph merely a nobleman of the Near East. When their lives twisted, they were bewildered, but they realized that lives intervened by God were within a glorious and amazing plan. Even if you fail an exam, business, or lose your home, the Lord is faithful to you who confess Him. Even in situations you cannot understand, the Lord remains faithful.
The reason life is difficult is because we do not shed blood. Rather, it is most happy and glorious when we willingly shed blood for Jesus.

Elementary and middle school students spread things widely on the internet. Those who know better refrain from doing so due to defamation and legal regulations. The Gospel seems similar. Those who insist on perfection can do nothing their whole lives. However, if you return to your initial mindset, you will preach and people will return.

Among animals and insects that defend themselves, those with camouflage change their colors according to the season and environment to protect themselves. However, most of them die within a year. But turtles, cranes, and cranes, which have their own colors regardless of rain or snow, live long without changing their clothes. If you have faith that can be revealed by shedding camouflage before God and brothers, problems will begin to melt away. Therefore, confess your sins to each other and pray for healing. The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective. - James 5:16

Instead of trying to change your position, try to change your attitude. If your attitude is pleasing to the Lord, your position will change on its own. If you worship the Lord as you are in the place where you are called, the Lord will see you.
You cannot escape the wilderness if you do not give up yourself and your greed.

If you have sought only the Lord wholeheartedly, not for yourself, it is okay to be criticized. Rather than being criticized for sinning, if you have wholeheartedly done what you ought to do, there is no regret regardless of the outcome or reaction.
Dedicating yourself is not achieved with great resolve, but simply by loving the Lord and remaining in His presence, it becomes the path of life that follows.

Those who rely on themselves seem confident and assured. What they do well is walking a tightrope. They must have been confident to walk it. However, their downfall and shame will be great. The claim that one can attain both holiness and carnal satisfaction is a lie. Such things do not exist. Stop walking the tightrope, simply give up and walk the narrow path. The prodigal sons who were cast out walk the wide path, and disciples walk the narrow path, but the prodigal sons who have returned half-heartedly tend to walk like tightrope walkers on the fence.
Do not prove yourself, but testify of the Lord. Then He will show you how to act.
If you try to fulfill your own will, you will have to struggle a lot and leave many wounds. If you try to fulfill the Lord's will, you must become truly humble and completely entrust yourself.
Do not be disappointed when your expectations and desires are not met, but rejoice that the Lord's sovereignty is acknowledged. You are not the master; the Lord is the master and He is responsible.
Stay in your place, do not leave, and be sober. The Lord is almighty.
If you have sought only the Lord's glory, act with boldness. No one can bring you down. Those who hope in the Lord alone are the ones who truly win.

Even a self-proclaimed God-fearing person like Jonah, who dodged and weaved, eventually preached the word in Nineveh in a perfunctory manner after three days of severe water torture, and the people repented. Open your mouth and preach the word, whether the time is right or not. Then the Lord will take care of it.
If you are not faithful in small things, in the affairs of others, and in unrighteous wealth, God cannot use you with His power.

If you are affected by small things, you are essentially covering your eyes and pretending when it comes to truly important matters. Do not just imitate; possess true skill. That comes from repeated presence through repentance.
Do not be too exhausted trying to achieve perfection in small things. Even the smallest task is something the Lord must do.
Those who listen well can proclaim well. Listening and humbling yourself takes precedence over speaking and proclaiming.

You rested well, didn't you? After a retreat where I used to sleep only two hours, I rested yesterday according to my usual sleep pattern for the first time in a while. Now, let's run for something better, something higher. Here we go~, One way, Jesus + a bottle of living water ^^// The youth group continues.

When you try too hard, you put in too much effort. Relationships, ministry, work – everything becomes stress. God did not call us to do everything well and satisfy everyone; He said to do one or two things, or even just one thing is enough. Doing the one thing God called you to do without greed is truly doing well. Trying to go too fast, trying to get everything is greed. Miracles happen when you let go of greed.

A rabbit that doesn't sleep never loses a race against a tortoise. A rabbit that stays awake and runs (an insomniac rabbit) is invincible. Today, we'll catch up on what we missed last week and have a "fire breathing" Saturday night.

Loud noises prevent us from hearing. If you are too busy to hear the Lord's voice, or if your attention is elsewhere, He will call you to a place where the sound is loud. That is the wilderness. A place with no water, dry, and where no one is. There, for the first time, you will hear the word, see it, and be trained to live by faith, not by what is advantageous. We walk by faith, not by sight. 2 Corinthians 5:7
It is said that those who sow in tears will reap with joy. But when you sow with beautiful people, tears become pearls, and labor becomes joy. When we are together, there is nothing to fear.

Even with disabilities and storms, the heart towards the Lord cannot stop. I run towards the Lord, towards His word. As time passes, I will run even more, and at the end of this path, I will see the Lord. God never turns away a "passionate pursuer." - From Acts 6 and Cries That Received Answers
It is only when my decisions and plans fail and I am discouraged that the Lord truly works. When I am weak, I am made perfect, and that is my strength.
People keep asking if there's an evening prayer meeting. You know, why do you keep asking? Even if nuclear war breaks out tomorrow or a tsunami occurs, it will continue as usual.

I understand that our friends are living in a time when it is very difficult to maintain their faith. There is weakness of the flesh, sin nature, and Satan's work. But you can overcome. Because you must overcome. Therefore, you will overcome, and you will be made to overcome.
We cook with craftsmanship, with a missionary spirit.
Even if the first half was not to your liking, do not be discouraged. Be sober in the second half.
Do not take off your armor. The enemy gives good things and satisfaction to make you take off your best. Do not give up the only way to fight the unseen opponent, the opponent not of flesh and blood.

Be an all-weather believer. Regardless of spiritual storms, look only to the Lord, independent of worldly glory, relationships, reactions, circumstances, and environments... a faith with the power to ignore, a faith that makes the devil depressed, a person who melts the dark world with love even in difficult positions where they can't stand up straight. You, who love the Lord, are the solution for this era. Acts 13:2 While they were ministering to the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, "Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them."
Without regret in my youth, only for the Lord~~
Will you truly serve, love, and worship only Him?

If we are truly to avoid bringing shame upon God's name, it means that the community needs many changes, and this implies a greater presence. We must seek the Lord's face more. This is especially required of those who minister.
Do you truly want to speak and act according to God's word? Then change your timing. The one whom God sent speaks the words of God, because he gives the Spirit without measure. John 3:34
If you live honestly, you will definitely face hardship and loss in normal times. However, in moments of crisis, in desperate moments, God always sides with the honest.
Do not let the fire on the altar go out - Lev 6:12,13
The most valuable life is one that runs for the Lord, leaving not a drop behind through holy consumption. To be used up completely is a truly glorious life.

The disciples' betrayal and flight from Jesus was not because they were 'evil' but because they were 'weak'. Not being filled with the Holy Spirit, abandoning the place of prayer, and forsaking the place of grace is already a heinous sin. Peter's boldness after Pentecost was not due to favorable circumstances but to being filled.

What we often call success in our national church is frequently expressed in numbers. However, many souls gathered in other churches are not receiving Christ, and even nominal existing members do not fully surrender to God nor dedicate themselves wholeheartedly. We should grieve over such success. And we deserve to be wounded by it.

A life of anxiety and impatience forced me to grasp my brother's heel and deceive my father to steal his blessing, bringing only suffering. True obedience is achieved when we let go of attempts to make ourselves happy, to satisfy ourselves, and to defend ourselves. The person who obeys God is the most beautiful person.
We want to sit next to someone we like. But a person who loves wants to give up their own seat.
Good choices lead to victory in battle. When we choose the cross and crucify the flesh, we win this battle victoriously.
The reason why those who had a good start fall is simple: they become complacent and compromise. It is the way of adding a whip to a running horse.

I hope you do not do the ministry and love you want to do, but do it in the way God desires. For God's foolishness is wiser than human wisdom, and God's weakness is stronger than human strength. 1 Cor 1:25

The only way to overcome sin is to surrender myself to the Lord. Then I become His servant, and He becomes my Lord, and I am under grace. If you win the battle of who is the master, you have won everything. Do you not know that if you surrender yourselves as slaves to obey someone, you are slaves of the one whom you obey, whether of sin leading to death, or of obedience leading to righteousness? - Rom 6:16

Before lamenting that you have fallen into sin, you must repent for not looking fully to the Lord. Our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against spirits. Therefore, faith is a battle of perspective.

Trying to overcome sin and temptation is futile. You can never win. It's like the idea of keeping garbage and spraying perfume on it. Garbage must be thrown away, and our nature must be crucified daily.
If you compromise with sin, you will be subject to it even after ten years, but if you start fighting against sin, there will come a day when you will rule over it.

Believing what you want to believe is not faith. It is simply superstition. Faith is believing in God's absolute sovereignty and providence and moving forward in obedience today. When you trust in the Lord's omnipotence, sorrow will turn into praise. Joseph named his firstborn Manasseh, saying, "God has made me forget all my hardship and all my father's house." He named the second Ephraim, saying, "God has made me fruitful in the land of my suffering." Gen 41:51,52
The Lord is at my right hand.
Entrust it to the Lord's hands. His promises are eternal. He is my reason, so I worship Him in all circumstances.
I hope you will be faithful before the Lord and gentle before the saints. God seeks such people, observes them as if recording them in a ledger, and pours out His power upon them.

There were times when I could do big and small things as I pleased without hearing the Lord's voice. But now is the time to listen to that voice and obey. God rejoices in a heart that seeks to ask and hear, and through His good pleasure, He leads us on a wonderful path. That is why when a faithful person buys an airplane ticket, even an unbeliever will try to board the same plane.
How do you know if a vision is given by the Lord? Dreams not given by the Lord will rot and disappear. Tesco eggs rot if incubated, but a fertilized egg hatches life.

Lord, I am not afraid to run towards You. I am also happy because I will live for You and die for You. As I look to You with all my heart, sin cannot overcome me. Being able to run for You is an overwhelming glory for me.
When you walk with the Lord, two representative powers emerge: 1. You bear much fruit. 2. You can do all things through Him. - John 15:5
Drawing near to the Lord is power. To do so, you must give something up. The most critical challenge right now is media. Exercise self-control. It is the price to pay.

There is time that must be given to the Lord. There is passion that must be given to the Lord. When absolute time and concentration are given, that life will soar. The greatest peak of life will come. Run over 300 kilometers, over 1800 meters. Revival begins with me.
Entrust the steering wheel of your life to the Lord. It is never too late. God is the only one who can bring the best results from the worst situations.

What the Lord wanted from Peter at Galilee was not an explanation of the circumstances or a convincing excuse. It was the confession, "I love you, Lord," spoken in a voice that crept in, so lacking in confidence and hesitant.

There are times when the Lord asks us to pour water into a leaky jar. Remember this: it is not the end. Even though it seems like nothing happens by pouring water, during that time, muscles grow and your constitution changes. At the Lord's appointed time, explosive revival, restoration, and the power of life will manifest.

The Lord does not solve everything at once in my way. He gives gradual restoration through timely grace. Just because He does not work according to my methods and schedule does not mean the Lord is not working. That is why we are thankful. You shall not fear them, for the Lord your God, the great and awesome God, is among you. The Lord your God will drive out these nations before you little by little; you must not quickly destroy them, lest the wild animals multiply and attack you. Deut 7:21,22
Look forward to the Lord. And rejoice in Him. Then He will grant you the desires of your heart.

Following the Lord, especially in ministry, means anticipating loss beforehand. Therefore, spiritual loss, material expenditure, and the pain of breaking relationships are not feared but considered payments that must be made. Those who have decided to follow the Lord do not consider these as terrible suffering. Why? Because they have seen the glory of the Lord. Personally, I dislike poverty and loneliness, but I do not fear them.

If there is no stumbling in speech, one is truly perfect. A person who does not stumble in speech is at peace. Because they are a thankful person. Gratitude and peace are the same word. If you have a thankful tongue, you are truly a perfected person. We all stumble in many ways. If anyone does not stumble in what he says, he is a perfect man, able to bridle his whole body. James 3:2

Those who do not fully trust in the Lord and cling to their own strength are all oppressed. Whether they know it or not, they are not free from spiritual oppression, lack peace, and suffer from illness. Let us cling to the truth with our lives. So that darkness may recede and light may shine...

Trusting in the Lord and rising again is true strength. Never give up! When the people, their hearts grieved because of their children, wanted to stone David, David was greatly distressed, but he found strength in the Lord his God. - 1 Samuel 30:6
Those who realize the Lord's grace, abandon the world, and return to Him have one true regret: returning too late, having wasted their years, when they were going to return anyway.

When doing the Lord's work, the most important thing is not financial support, gifts, or organization. It is guarding your heart. When you guard your heart, you will not be discouraged and will persevere, and when you continuously press on, God's glory will be revealed. Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up. Galatians 6:9

There are two ways to receive the Lord's blessings and transformation: it is best to understand through His Word. If not, do not worry too much. There is the wilderness. If you are thoroughly seasoned in the wilderness for about ten years, you will understand well. Of course, the former is recommended.
No one can harm me without the Lord's permission. However, when I react and respond to every instance, that very wickedness will harm me. Persecution, on the contrary, makes me a precious believer.
The reason the Lord postpones the conclusion is to truly give Himself.
It is suffering when I decide what the Lord must decide. After being disciplined, I will be faithful unto death in the small things given to me. The Lord is watching.

When the Lord raised Lazarus and the widow's son in Nain, He not only restored their lives but also healed the illnesses they had suffered during their lives. (There is no mention that Lazarus was resurrected but still had his chronic illness.) God's healing, God's restoration, and God's life are complete and faithful, transcending the conditions of worry and anxiety. Please trust the Lord. For I know the plans I have for you," declares the Lord, "plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future. Jeremiah 29:11

The Lord has sent me. - Even if the narrow path is one that many have turned away from, He has sent me to say that it is the true path, that it is a meaningful path. It is the path that the Apostle Paul walked, even if he was never treated with respect his whole life, even if he had no one to cook him a warm meal. It is the path to which he was called from Damascus, where God showed him His glory, and he poured himself out like a drink offering on the altar without regret. It is the path he gladly took, even with much loss and suffering, to align with God's will. I pray that you walk the path that will be remembered on your tombstone as someone who truly loved souls and God.

The Lord calls. - Calling. The Lord calls us to love, to serve, and to humble ourselves. Even if all things scatter in the distant future and no name or memory remains, He has called us to love eternally with a heart for Him, shedding tears. Even today, as we sow seeds and anxiously await sprouts, He has called us to be able to give thanks even if we are not the ones to reap.
Do what the Lord desires. The present suffering and cost of payment are incomparable to the glory you will receive.
We must not hesitate in doing what the Lord desires.

If the Lord does not give it, we have received nothing. Do not suffer and cling to what you cannot receive, hoping for a miracle; instead, be faithful to what He gives, even if no one else notices. The Lord will notice. This is God's powerful request this morning. John answered, "A person cannot receive anything unless it is given him from heaven." - John 3:27
Striving for what the Lord has not given is a waste of time. It is holy to pour your energy into where you should stand and what you should do. Focus on what the Lord desires.
Rather than suffering to obtain what the Lord has not given, please fulfill His will with what He has given. Then all these things will be added to you.

The path the Lord gives is the most blessed path. Forcing yourself onto a path He has not given is called greed, and though fulfilling greed may seem joyful, it leads to the greatest misery. The Lord is my path, and that is why I do not grow weary even when walking through a parched land all day.

If the Lord does not protect us, the vigilance of the watchman is in vain. This means that all efforts in relationships, careers, and social recognition become wasted effort. We must strive diligently, but effort alone is not enough. We must be entrusted to the Lord's protection.

Blessed are those whose lives are opened only by the Lord's blessing, whose lives are not swayed by cunning tricks or human schemes, and whose lives are such that even luck and probability, so easy for others, do not follow, leaving them no choice but to entrust themselves and move forward.
If we do what the Lord has commanded, there is nothing to fear or regret. If it is permitted by the Lord, even loneliness is precious.
Do not be content with what the Lord allows, but rather dedicate yourselves and volunteer for what He leads. That is a life of joy.
Those who hope in the Lord will be given new strength.

Trying to obtain what has not been given, or trying to hasten a time He has not led, is all greed. This means there are significant side effects. Such people will have thick autobiographies. Let us be people whose wells spring forth from digging our own land, not by stealing from others' wells. God does not abandon us.
To those who faithfully live out the time given to them, another path will be shown.

If your law had not been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction. Your statutes have been my songs in the house of my pilgrimage. - Psalm 119:92-93 The only reason that flattens all the valleys of difficult situations and mountains of suffering is God's Word. It is the source of life that sustains me.
Go not as you wish, but as God pours out and the Lord fills.
God fills those who confess, "If I perish, I perish."
Dead tradition is more evil than heresy. - John Stott

Paul worked daily in Ephesus for three years, teaching at night with tears (Acts 20:31). He also served the Lord with fervor, possessing burning truth (Romans 12:11). For decades of ministry, he continuously served the Lord with a burning heart and unceasing tears. Why? Simply put, he spent decades in the field as if it were one day. Remember that for three years I did not cease to admonish everyone night and day with tears. - Acts 20:31 Be diligent, do not be lazy, but serve the Lord with fervor (fervent in spirit - NASB). - Romans 12:11 Fervent in spirit, with a heart burning in the Spirit, day by day... We gather, pray, and proclaim the truth of the cross. 7:30 as usual.

Lifting dumbbells against gravity is exercise. Muscles are built by overcoming the resistance of gravity. What is built by avoiding gravity are lumps of yellow fat. Do not fear or avoid hardship. Instead, you must find and obtain the hidden treasure within it.

Please act immediately. You must do anything immediately when God moves you. Otherwise, you will quench the Holy Spirit and ultimately be defeated. Pray immediately. Read the Word immediately. Evangelize immediately. Forgive immediately. And always look to the Lord.
The evidence of 'giving thanks' now is 'victory'. Also, the evidence of 'achieving victory' is 'giving thanks'.

He says that being there now is the grace of the Lord. He says that being there in the first place was His intervention and grace, and that He sustains me by that grace even now. He sustains me not by my own choice or luck, but by God's sufficient grace. Faith is walking the path that I cannot walk without the Lord. Become a worshiper so that this place where I stand may be dyed with the Lord's precious blood.

The reason I can be bold in all these circumstances right now is because I believe in the Lord's will. Therefore, I am not sad even if I lose something, nor do I become arrogant if I gain a little. Those who walk the Lord's path are bold.
Those who walk with the Lord now will stand in the midst of the Lord's will tomorrow.
Even if you shed tears now, please sow seeds. You will surely reap with joy.
Paul was not lonely. It is because he kept His will and His timing in mind rather than hoping for luck.
Now is the time to seek the Lord; plow your fallow ground, and it is time to seek the Lord. He will come and rain righteousness upon you. - Hosea 10:12 - A word given on Christmas 1990
I will not remember the past, nor will I think of all the things I have done before. I look only to the Lord who makes rivers and paths in the desert and who will do new things within me.

I will not remember the past, nor will I think of all the things I have done before. I will look only to the Lord who makes rivers and paths in the desert and who will do new things within me. The Lord will protect me and hold me up. I know this, believe it, and praise Him.

I give thanks for the presence and worshipful spirit granted for the past three nights and four days. What I earnestly desire now as I return is the Lord's greater presence. "Not that I have already obtained all this, or have been made perfect, but I press on to take hold of that for which Christ Jesus also took hold of me." Philippians 3:12
True faithfulness is bringing my unfaithfulness to the Lord honestly.

If you persuade the intellect, you are convinced. If you empathize with emotions, you are moved. If you act on the will (specifically), it becomes a decision. Requesting without doing this is 'coercion'. In the church, instead of coercion, there is spontaneity, and ministry is motivation.

Only persistence bears fruit. Not persisting is just a waste of time and consumption. Do not throw away your hard work; hold onto the Lord until the end and bear fruit. Fruit is beautiful only when there are trials.
If you try to keep it, it will be taken away. If you try to give it, He will give you something better.

Wisdom does not fall from the sky; it is about effectively utilizing the abilities you possess. Using the sling you have (like David) is the greatest wisdom. God strengthens what you have rather than giving you something new. "The Lord asked him, 'What is in your hand?' He said, 'A staff.'" - Exodus 4:2 When a dry staff is held by power, it parts the Red Sea and draws water from a rock.

How important is it to preach truth like fire, to be blameless in doctrine, and to risk your life in ministry... Is my heart beating because of Jesus' love? If you cannot be moved, it will inevitably be difficult.

True love is accompanied by three things: a longing to see, a desire to hear his words, and a desire to follow his will. David possessed all three. He longed for God, wanted to hear God's word, and set aside his own will to seek God's will.
I will love you enough to tell you the truth. "Faithful are the wounds of a friend; but the kisses of an enemy are deceitful" (Proverbs 27:6)

True wisdom comes from affection. If you truly desire and love, life-giving wisdom emerges. It is an era that requires earnestness and an anointing that finds a way to live even in the most difficult and challenging moments.
There are countless signals in the air, but we only receive and respond to the frequencies that match our own hair. It is good to respond only to the Lord.
You must concentrate. Because the Lord tells us what is important. If you hold onto that, you will win.

The hymn says, 'Before you finish all the paths you walk, do not put down the sword in your hand.' You must push when it's time to push. Otherwise, you won't be able to push when you want to later. That's why I'm pushing harder this week than last week.

Defending the community with the fiery truth of first love, enduring persecution in Jesus' name, and possessing unparalleled loyalty and faithfulness cannot replace 'love'. More than excellent ministry and faithful devotion, He asks us to restore our 'first love'. This is regaining the most important lost thing, and it can only be restored through repentance, not through events or changes in atmosphere. Jesus says that overcoming it is repentance. "I know your deeds, your hard work and your perseverance. I know that you cannot tolerate wicked people, that you have tested those who claim to be apostles but are not, and have found them false. You have persevered and have endured hardships for my name, and have not grown weary. Yet I hold this against you: You have forsaken the love you had at first. Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first. If you do not repent, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place." Revelation 2:3, 4 "Whoever has ears, let them hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who is victorious, I will give the right to eat from the tree of life, which is in the paradise of God." Revelation 2:7

What must be done in youth is to be refined and disciplined through fire. Then, you can crucify ambition disguised as calling and offer it to the Lord. This is one of the most crucial disciplines. What I most keenly reflect on at my age is that I need to be disciplined and corrected much more than I think. I ask for the Lord's mercy.

When disruptions occur in their lives, young people are bewildered. This is because they only look at reality. Reality says, 'If it doesn't work, it doesn't work,' but what is needed then is to clearly reconfirm 'where I must go.' That is the mission. If the mission is clearer than the 'disruption in life,' it becomes the strength to rise again, and God never disappoints those who rise.
Even if young people cry, they must sow. That is God's will.

In youth, it is easy to mistake emotional guidance for God's will, even when praying. God's guidance is not hasty. It is because the Almighty answers and is confident that He will fulfill it. However, emotional guidance and self-rationalization originate from oneself, so one is rushed, wants to see immediate results, and resorts to human tricks. Follow the Lord.

When I was in the 6th grade of elementary school, I lied about my age and attended the middle and high school youth group service. The middle school girls thought I was their age and treated me so well... I had no faith, so the service was just a formality, and the fellowship time was more enjoyable. One Sunday, when my friend and I were, as usual, very late for the youth group service, a student who was retaking his college entrance exams, about 7 years older than me, grabbed my friend and sternly rebuked us. "You are attending a service to God so carelessly..." That day, my friend and I sneered, "He's retaking exams, so he should go to a good university... Hmph." That brother is probably almost 50 now. I think he must have worried a lot back then. "That red-faced kid needs to believe in Jesus and become a human being..." "Brother, I'm sorry. Although I am lacking now, I live yearning to live for the Lord and die for the Lord. I will see you in heaven."

Even if 60 elementary school students pray in unison, gather wisdom, encourage each other, and declare they can do it, or even write in blood, they cannot solve a calculus problem. Only a high school senior can solve it. Do not try to solve the problem; raise your level. The Lord wants our level to rise rather than solving our problems directly. This difference in perspective causes hardship. Be patient. All problems will be solved after graduating from the wilderness school.

Those who have seen the highest glory are not tempted by lesser glory. Those who have experienced the best are not shaken by anything inferior. Experience the glory of the Lord, and you will not be shaken by emotional reasons, comparisons, or worldly boasting. "And that the word of the Lord may spread rapidly and be glorified, just as it is with you" (2 Thessalonians 3:1). When God's word runs, you will experience glory. Let the word of the Lord run in your heart.

There are things that do not work out even when you do your best. To be precise, it's not that they didn't work out, but they didn't work out according to your desires. This means they were permitted under the Lord's sovereignty, and if your intentions and best efforts were combined, it means something even better.

David did not overcome while fleeing from Saul by holding onto the vision of a shining throne in the future. He simply endured. Holding onto the Lord, he praised with tears, as if each day were his last.
It is not great power, but rather not being rude and enduring patiently, that will heal most of our problems. 1 Corinthians 13.
Do not fear, but be faithful to the end. This is the word of the Lord.

The Christian's battle is not against flesh and blood, so it is not resolved by worldly methods. The powerful weapons given as we advance against all these battles are 'relinquishing rights' and 'the spirit of opposition.' When you relinquish your rights, you gain a stronger spiritual position and obtain what the Lord desires. When stinginess, hatred, and anger attack, by advancing with the spirit of opposition of giving, love, and gentleness, the kingdom of God is released in that darkness. We are asked to be helping hands with the heart of the Lord. - Acts 16:10
Do not worry; do what you can do. Then you will experience what the Lord will do.
There is the sound of a great rain. It is the sound of a great rain being accomplished through our young people.

After great spiritual victory, strong spiritual challenges and stagnation follow. Peter chose to return to his former life as a sailor from that stagnation, but gained nothing. Trials are overcome by perseverance. (James 1:3) Perseverance is thinking of the Lord and waiting, and in that waiting, it is most important to wait not only for others but also for yourself. Especially if you can endure patiently until you can change, you will gain life through the pains of childbirth. "By your endurance you will gain your lives." (Luke 21:19)

When a large car and a small car meet, the small car has to back down. The admiral of a large warship can order a ship blocking the way to move aside. However, the lowest-ranking soldier guarding the lighthouse is the only naval officer who can refuse this.

Before his fifth visit to Jerusalem, Paul was repeatedly warned and dissuaded by spiritual servants of the Lord and co-workers due to great tribulation and bonds. On the ship from Alexandria, he faced situations where all hope of survival was lost due to the storm, yet he remained calm and bold to the end. This was because he knew two secrets: the Lord's timing and the Lord's will. Faith is never complicated.

Going through a tunnel is a painful experience. It is dark, and you don't know how long it will last. However, refusing and avoiding the tunnel leads to greater pain. We do not welcome tunnels, but tunnels are also the fastest shortcuts. Do not refuse the shortcut; run diligently.
Hebrews 12:1-3. The life God desires for us is to run the race of faith, looking to Jesus, and thinking deeply about Him. Then we are doing well. Such a life has His presence and bears fruit.

There are people who have sawed wood, but no one has sawed sawdust. This is because it was already sawed when the wood was cut. Those who suffer because of the past or lose courage are like those trying to saw sawdust. Do not saw sawdust; saw wood.
I hope you walk the path of becoming strong rather than the easy path.

It will be truly blessed to cultivate fertile ground while walking on a smooth road. However, even if you fall after exhausting yourself cultivating stony ground, it will be a great glory. What the Lord has told you to do, and where He has told you to stand, is the promised land, the holy land.
In the policies of the Kingdom of God, the requests of Satan are never granted. There is no intention of giving even an inch. Instead, we advance forward.
When we stand before God with purity and honesty, not presuming to go ahead, we will enjoy what God has prepared in His time.
Rather than offering something that looks quite good to God, offer yourself. This is what the Lord desires, and it is a living sacrifice.
After receiving a vision from God, the next step is to pay the price for it.
