North Korea's Kim Jong-un is in a similar situation. So Pilate, wanting to satisfy the crowd, released Barabbas and handed Jesus over to be whipped and crucified. - Mark 15:15

If you are driven into the wilderness instead of being used as you expected, it is because you try too hard to teach others. The scope of the wilderness will extend to being quick to listen and slow to speak.

December War? 1. When a defect is found on a factory line, all other products are thoroughly investigated. 2. Whatever useless words you speak, if what you confidently uttered and acted upon was wrong, you must thoroughly examine yourself and repent. 3. Errors are tendencies, and these tendencies are likely to continue to occur. 4. If there is no repentance for being more sensitive to the voice of emotion, for being a prisoner of prejudice, for self-centered thinking and greed, it means that the same errors will be fatally repeated in the future. 5. If there is no introspection about erroneous eschatology, the December war theory, wrong judgments and divisions, and wounds caused by exaggerated expressions and self-centered language, as if nothing happened, you will remain in the same place even after decades. 6. David's greatness lies not in defeating Goliath, but in his ability to recover.

I once saw someone who had ministered wonderfully being put on trial. Profanity and violence erupted through the mouths and bodies of believers, and the police were called. The victim shed tears, but the perpetrator claimed to have caused no harm. The greatest discipline is to be left alone. It is a shameful thing, a fearful thing, what is being done to the kingdom of God and the Korean church, a truly fearful thing when greed masquerades as a mission.
Wash and burn. The Holy Spirit purifies our thoughts. He washes and burns.

I will devote myself to the Word and prayer. The idea of replacing the Holy Spirit, clinging to means given without losing their true essence, and being rushed is the cause of stagnation, and mobilizing other non-essentials to solve it is a way to produce nominal Christians who have not changed.

Two beings that remind us. The Comforter, the Holy Spirit, reminds us of the true meaning of the grace we received and the worship we offered, but the devil's role is to make us deny and forget. Do not be used by the deceiver, but by Him who is the truth. The Comforter, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you all things and remind you of everything I have said to you. - John 14:26
Two foolish acts. Being foolishly deceived by the same methods as the devil, who tried to torment us last year.
Make sure there is nothing to gain from the adversary. Just focus on the Lord.
Storms are to be overcome. Jesus did not walk on water to pull me out of the storm. He came to break through it with me.
The reason for being evil and lazy is being possessed by an evil and lazy spirit. When the Word is not heard and criticism within you intensifies, receive a serious warning and repent.

The reason for spirituality. A doctor washing their hands thoroughly before surgery is not to become holy on their own. It is to save lives. Remain in the Lord and be spiritual. Only then will life and transformation come, won't they?
Do not complain, but repent. Those who complain have no repentance one hundred percent of the time. That is why they perish. They look for the cause in others, so they cannot repent fundamentally.

Give thanks simply. After preaching last Saturday night about conquering the gates of the enemy with gratitude and being prepared to lose everything, the glass of my car parked in the parking lot was broken, and I lost my LG G2 phone. God has a sense of humor. If you think simply, 1. He wants to replace my car window, and 2. He wants me to turn off my phone for a while and pray more and hold onto the Word. The new car window is refreshing.

What we must truly overcome is our subtle, rationalized desires. It is not because temptation is strong, but because we cannot love Jesus, we are deceived and cannot abandon ourselves. However, we cannot deceive the Lord. If we truly love the Lord, we will vomit blood and repent.

The flesh constantly demands peace and comfort. However, the desires of the Spirit make me obey and become holy before the Lord. What we choose when these two desires clash tells us how we will live. It is like the flesh wanting worldly pleasures, but my spirit wanting to pray with my face on the cold church floor.

If you give thanks and praise with all your might, you will surely win. Revelation is filled with much persecution and tribulation. It also contains the most praise and worship. Acknowledging and exalting God in the face of trials and challenges is the most powerful response. Rejoicing in God is our strength. Rejoicing in the Lord is your strength. - Nehemiah 8:10
I hope you do not try to defend yourselves. I hope you give way for the Lord alone to protect us. Our calling is not to defend ourselves, but to lay everything down and know the Lord more.

Focusing on problems allows evil spirits to work. You have probably experienced all sorts of negative thoughts when you dwell on problems. The moment you try to solve the problem, you become bound to it and think as the evil spirit dictates. The Lord solves the problem, and we give thanks.

2 Peter 2:15-22 Thursday QT Greed blinds your eyes. Verse 15. Balaam, son of Beor, is the representative of false teachers. The motive for their false teaching is the wages of unrighteousness, that is, greed. People do not do things because of their beliefs, but ultimately because of what they want, their desires. Verse 16. These people are worse than donkeys. (Even after 4,000 years, the Bible testifies to their infamy.) Verse 17. Their judgment will be severe. Verse 18. Their doctrines are 1. subtly boastful, and 2. provide sensational carnal pleasure. -> Human corruption has a set route after the fall. Once a path is made on a mountain, countless animals and people tread it. It is very difficult to remove a path once it is made. Verse 19. Carnal pleasure is not freedom, but becoming a slave of destruction, a slave of the devil, a slave of sin, doing whatever they are told. The flesh must die and the spirit must live. (You get mind-controlled by dark archons. It feels disgusting.) Verse 20. If you know the truth and then fall away again, it is no joke. Look at the people around you who have betrayed their faith and are proudly consistent in their apostasy. Simply put, if an angel falls, they become a demon, not a semi-angel. Verse 21. "I wish I had never known from the beginning" is not just in love songs. How severe must the judgment be for someone to say this? Fools know no fear. Ignorance breeds bravery. Verse 22. Let us not fall into these proverbs in our lives. (Pig, dog) The Lord lived a life that fulfilled God's Word and will. Will you follow dogs and pigs? Or will you follow the Word and the Spirit? Decide now. * Greed turns people into beasts and leads them to make such vulgar decisions (encounters, actions, words, choices). What is truly pitiful is falling away from the truth within the church. You served and strived with great effort, but if you are deceived by falsehood, it is like throwing away your efforts for nothing. Paul preached the gospel with fear and trembling. If pride, lust, and envy are lurking, you are acting as an enemy of the cross.

Partial things will disappear when the complete comes. When we knew partially, we condemned, but when we know the whole, we forgive and have compassion. Ah! That's why they did that, how hard it must have been!, how much it must have hurt! When we know a little and act arrogantly, we all condemn. Satan works through condemnation. But when the complete person comes, they save lives. They forgive. Love responds to the whole.

Satan deceives. We make resolutions, but we are easily deceived. We are easily deceived by circumstances, rumors, and emotions. When we listen to the voice of the Lord, who knows me better than my own confident judgment, we can avoid being deceived. In Ephesians, Paul exhorts believers to put on the sword of truth and the belt of truth for the last days. All falsehood will crumble before the truth.

Satan's attempt to condemn us through hidden sins is an attempt to make us feel like 'worthless beings.' However, our regrettable mistakes are evidence that we have been hurt, angered, and especially lonely. Satan tries to prevent us from approaching the Lord with these things by making us doubt and hide alone, but the Lord loves us even more, forgives us, and has compassion on us. What I truly need and what I must have is His love, because He has fully paid the price for it on the cross.

People live bound by cause and effect, by causality. That is the life of general principles. However, when truth and the Spirit come, they overturn these relationships and, guided by the Spirit regarding sin and righteousness and judgment, fight against darkness and move forward.
If you hide a wounded heart, it festers and calls forth spirits of darkness, but if you offer it to the Lord, it becomes a pleasing sacrifice.

Do not be hurried by circumstances, but rather rule over them. To fully exercise the dominion the Lord has delegated, you must overcome the evil enemy, and that power is to rule with 'God's overflowing grace.' Darkness has never been our match from the beginning.

People think they can open their hearts a little to the world and close them immediately if necessary, and they enjoy the thrill. The moment you have such thoughts, you become a slave to the devil. This is the devil's typical method. The Bible clearly states that the issue of lust, in particular, is not a matter of self-control or ethics, but a problem of evil spirits working behind the scenes. Lust is the work of the devil. An old pastor who had served for a long time once said this: Once addicted to lust, it is difficult to break free, and when you do, it requires a life-or-death commitment. I think this: Even with a 'life-or-death resolve,' it is difficult. It is only possible through the 'law of the Spirit.' (Romans 8:1-4) And I want to testify this: It is a war that can be won if you fully rely on the Holy Spirit. (Be courageous) Two things must be kept in mind. 1. The current tendency towards sin will be tolerated.
There is a light that drives out the darkness that engulfs the world, and where there are signs of something spiritual, there is always a leader holding a candle.

Those who have passed through the cross will seek the Holy Spirit. They know they have died in faith, but the gap between reality and the Word is too great. Fullness of the Spirit begins with longing.

The traditional temptation from the devil is about priorities. It's about turning stones into bread to eat. Would the devil, who tempted the Son of God, spare weak humans? There is a huge difference between 'eating and then doing' and 'doing and then eating.' Living solely according to desire (利) versus seeking His kingdom and His righteousness (義) is not only fundamentally different but creates an eternal difference.
Darkness removes windows, clocks, and mirrors from people. It's an attempt to prevent self-reflection. In fact, casinos don't have windows, clocks, or mirrors.

The greatest sin is a lack of fervor. Without fervor, how can one overcome the fierce attacks of worldly sin? One must burn brightly enough to melt the darkness of the world and consume the temptations of sin.

Four things the secular world pursues (Ephesians 2:1,3): Even if the world promises happiness, it is deceptive, and it seeks to dominate souls through these four things (threats + enticements). 1. The power of sin - "As for you, you were dead in your transgressions and sins." 2. Worldly values - "in which you used to live when you followed the ways of this world." 3. The work of the devil - "and of the ruler of the kingdom of the air, the spirit who is now at work in those who are disobedient." 4. The desires of the flesh - "among whom we all once lived in the passions of our flesh, doing what our flesh and thoughts wanted." - Eph 2:1,3
Though we became living beings from dust by God's breath, sin causes us to return to dust (the flesh). However, when we hear God's voice, we are revived and become people of the Spirit.

The moment you are not focused on Jesus and His Word, you have already entered into sin. Due to pride, you cannot comprehend, your vision is obscured, and like a car with failed brakes, evil and corrupt thoughts will surge. 1 Timothy 6:4,5 But if a life focused solely on the Lord is sufficient, you will be sanctified from all these things. 1 Timothy 6:6
The path we walk is not safe. Amidst many challenges and trials, we sometimes see casualties. However, it is not despairing. It is because of His sovereignty. Be prepared to die.

When the enemy comes against me, should I attack or defend? David confessed, "You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies." - Psalm 23. If you think you are being chased and attacked by the enemy, you might think of killing Saul in the cave, but David thought he was being led by God. Do not retaliate against the attack; instead, be invited to the dinner God has prepared for you.

Isaiah chapter 14 contains the devil's confession. In those short two verses, "I will" appears five times. The stubborn heart that says "I will" means the initiative is always with oneself. If you go into the wilderness, receive grace, and are broken and shattered, you will become tender-hearted and follow the Lord's will. Leave those with strong teeth to themselves. All justification and rationalization will be shattered before the hardships of the wilderness.

The enemy has never been stronger. It is because Israel has become weaker. Whether you serve God or idols is the dividing line between peace and disaster. The true war is whether you will worship your own emotions, desires, and wounds, or the one true Lord.

When passing through a narrow gate, sometimes the gate becomes even narrower. At such times, you must let go of everything and hold onto only what is essential. Focusing on revering the Lord and resisting the devil is all that matters. When fighting, you need the focus to strike down just one enemy. For we thought it right, as it seemed good to the Holy Spirit and to us, to lay no additional burden on you, except these necessary things - Acts 15:28

There is frustration, but there is no despair. In Jesus, everything becomes hope. He is almighty, and as long as I do not allow despair, it is impossible in Jesus. Let us testify to the Lord who works among us by faith, to the darkness that makes us see only the present situation. We will be ashamed and retreat.
When you sin, Satan tells you to disappear. However, the Holy Spirit tells you to repent.

I resolve many times not to sin, but I always fall. To be angry and complain is to become Satan's prey and to enter the war already defeated. Cease from anger, put away wrath; do not fret; it only leads to doing evil - Psalm 37:8.
There is an aspect of the old self that must be shared and resolved only with the Lord. That sin cannot be overcome; it must die.

Wisdom transcends the laws of cause and effect in the world. For example, it is realizing that seemingly unrelated things, such as disrespecting the Lord leading to adversaries and unending conflict, are connected. Before looking at the problems in my reality, I must understand the cause from an attitude of reverence for the Lord.

If you truly want to win, worldly logic defines a winner as someone influential or successful, but the victory Jesus desires is repentance. The one who repents is the victor. Therefore, consider where you have fallen from, repent, and do the things you did at first... To the one who conquers, I will grant the right to eat from the tree of life, which is in the paradise of God. Rev 2:5,7.

Most problems faced by young people are solvable with fullness. They are not broken, just extinguished. If Jesus is not full within you, it is appropriate to consider yourself in a severe trial. You will sin, hate, be discouraged, and be anxious for no reason.

Deception and schemes are the devil's profession and are still very effective tactics for deceiving those close to us. The Bible describes them as flaming arrows. What we need is the shield of faith (trust) and the spirit of wisdom and revelation. Never be deceived. that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give you the Spirit of wisdom and of revelation in the knowledge of him— Eph 1:17, so that you may be able to stand against the schemes of the devil. Eph 6:11. In all circumstances take up the shield of faith, with which you can extinguish all the flaming darts of the evil one. Eph 6:16.

Saturday Message Summary: James 4:4-10. Do not live oppressed. Many people live losing relationships, peace, and health under oppression. 1. If you become friends with the world, you become an enemy of God. 2. Submit to the Lord and resist the devil, and he will flee from you. 3. If you honor God, God will honor you.

Paul confesses his weakness. He speaks of conflict, which means two forces are fighting. What he didn't know when he was in darkness, he now knows as a child of light. However, he lacks the strength to win this war, so he groans. Then he experiences a turning point. This is possible through Jesus. What is impossible for humans to do with the body of death, God does, and He does it through the Spirit. The Holy Spirit is received through obedience, hearing, and repentance.

There is a theory of pipes. They function as conduits to transmit important things. However, Christians must go a step further. Instead of remaining a pipe, one must become a living branch. By blocking what is bad and letting what is good flow through, not only are all saved, but the branch itself also lives. The faith of a living branch is not mechanical church attendance. Therefore, we pray without bargaining with the Lord.
There are times when God lowers us infinitely, down to the roots of the mountains at the bottom of the sea. From there, repentance erupts, and the Word begins to live and be experienced.

God desires to give us unimaginable grace. The challenges and trials we must face are far greater than our own level. If we fight without receiving grace, it is like a child trying to defeat an adult; we will be defeated every time. We are no match in strategy or strength. How can we fight? Grace comes to the humble.

In this war, God is not telling us to fight the enemy, but to fight the idols within us. The Lord's intention is not for us to be preoccupied with overpowering others, but for our idols to be burned and our broken worship to be restored.

When you are not in your rightful place but in a position that increases probability, you leave the place of the Lord's protection. You return beaten and depressed. They say it's a spiritual attack. No, it's spiritual truancy, where you get beaten up by neighborhood thugs. Guard your position.

When I return to Korea, it feels like all my strong will disappears, and even the air seems to try to knock me down. Also, here the rain of grace was a downpour, but why is it a drizzle there? In Korea, I had to pay a price to satisfy my thirst. In London, there were people who paid for me. I must seek it myself and prepare myself. I keenly realize that my faith life here was not my strength but entirely the Lord's grace. However, if you hold onto the Lord with all your heart, you will know that He is everywhere. Hold on. And keep going.

There is no way to live without repentance. There is no presence that comes automatically without the 'effort of seeking and searching' for the Lord. Ultimately, the battle is between whether Jesus is truly within you or you are pretending. If Jesus is simply within me, the problem ends there.

After hating someone to death, losing all faith and grace, and then after a long time barely recovering, you realize the cause was not that person but yourself. The reason for hating to death was that Satan made you hate to death. Do not be deceived.

Ultimately, this world is a battle of dominion. The success or failure of my life is also a battle of dominion. Dominion resides in the territory and people subject to the sovereignty of that nation. If God's sovereignty extends to the church but is rejected in relationships, change and glory will not appear. True holiness appears when I yield my sovereignty to the Lord. Acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty.
Please examine if your desires come from the Lord. If your desires are fulfilled, are you truly fearing the devil?
Wake up. To do so, you must stop rationalizing.

*Sexual Sin Sex is a gift from God. It is also a holy gift. Because it is holy, it can also be a serious sin and a danger. If sex were not such a beautiful blessing, it would not be dangerous. The Ark of the Covenant in the Holy of Holies was so holy that those who handled it improperly were surely destroyed.
What the devil truly wants is for us to focus on problems rather than the Word.
Instead of trying to control the situation before me, I must aim myself toward the Lord of heaven. Wicked imaginations gnaw away at life.

Everyone is excited about the party, and I sit in an unavoidable place amidst the loud music, wearing an awkward smile. My heart aches infinitely because of the Father's heart, and with the Father's heart. Because of the silent cries of the flock given to me and His heart...

1. God opposes the sin of pride, especially proud people. (God opposes the proud... 1 Peter 5:6) 2. This is because this sin was the beginning of the devil. (You said in your heart, 'I will ascend to heaven; I will raise my throne above the stars of God.' Isaiah 14:14) 3. Therefore, a proud person secretly becomes the most devil-like person. 4. Pride is Satan. In the face of humility, all darkness crumbles by God's great grace. If we are humble, we can make Satan like candy. Young men, likewise, be subject to one another in all humility, for God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble. - 1 Peter 5:5

Looking at myself through my own eyes and looking at others is deception. We must look at ourselves and others through the eyes of the Lord. That is discernment. It is truly a generation that desperately needs the discernment of spirits.

The most terrifying thing is not threats or death. It is the withdrawal of the Lord's presence. That is why David, in Psalm 51, does not even mention preserving his kingship but pleads for His presence. He asks not to be cast away. The most foolish person is one who is not afraid of the Lord's absence. Those without the Lord's presence sin recklessly. The Lord is a delicate being, and when we speak carelessly or love sin, we no longer feel His presence. The presence we feel is not a feeling but the reality of the Lord who is Immanuel.

If you have confusion of heart and overwhelming worry and anxiety, it is not because you are in the wrong situation, but because you have the wrong perspective. If you look at problems and the world, that is how you live. Stephen saw the Lord in the face of death. His eyes met, and he saw the pupils of His eyes. The one who looks to the Lord is a martyr.

The most threatening person is not an unbeliever. It is someone who has not received grace and been transformed. A person who has heard the Word and squandered the opportunity from a place of grace becomes a Judas Iscariot. If you are pricked but try to suppress it, this person does not remain in a neutral zone of not being transformed, but rather becomes a persecutor and an abuser. That is a curse. Repentance is being pricked and transformed.

The way to extinguish a raging wildfire is to set a backfire. By intentionally setting a fire in a place where there is no fire yet, you ensure there is nothing left to burn. What has already burned has nothing more to burn, so it does not spread further. If I am completely burned before the altar, the trial will subside at my boundary. May you achieve the death of self through the cross.

If what I boast about now is not the Lord, it is undeniable proof of an 'spiritual problem.' If the complete fellowship with the Lord, the Word, and prayer is broken, we will 'subtly' boast about empty and perishable things. (There is a difference between sophistication and skill, but ultimately, what is boasted about is not the Lord.) What do I want to be recognized for and to show? Boasting is my spirituality.
In despondency and worry, proclaim thanksgiving and praise. That becomes my strength and the power to change the situation. The battle belongs to God, so when we exalt Him, the enemy crumbles.
Look into the root of my pain and suffering. My desires will be revealed. If it is not tears for Jesus and for those who act as enemies of Jesus, then you are clinging to perishable things.
Rise high. The grace God pours out is higher than the sins you have committed and the temptations of Satan.

In martial arts and boxing, the first training is learning to take hits. Specifically, they learn not to close their eyes when a punch is coming. If you get hit without knowing, you'll be knocked out. But if you know and get hit, it will be bearable. Various people exist. Understand your opponent and get hit, even if you get hit. It becomes so cute that it transcends pain.

The absence of oil itself is not the problem. The problem is that the gas station is far away and the fuel lamp is broken. If you are lacking, you can fill it up. You will often be lacking. Never be arrogant without the oil of the Holy Spirit. You must fill it when you can.
Repentance_and_Restoration: Never, Never go along with it. When you justify your wounds and greed, the Holy Spirit will surely give you an uncomfortable feeling. Only a very small number of people overlook the Holy Spirit's conviction and, by revealing their hidden motives and manipulations, repent and turn away, even deceiving themselves and God. Wounds, greed, and self-defense can easily be disguised as love, loyalty, and justice. The glory of transformation and revival is not common. This is because it requires contrition that reveals hidden fears, anger, and attachments, not the justification of words and actions. Walk the narrow path of not just going along with things. Be thorough about your motives. Confess to God, who examines the heart, rather than superficially covering the sky. In your own eyes, all your actions may seem pure, but the Lord examines the heart. - Proverbs 16:2

* Wrong Encounters If you introduce your best friend to your girlfriend, and she starts meeting him secretly behind your back, it becomes a 'wrong encounter.' The same applies to encounters without God.

* Do not criticize. Not criticizing means not criticizing without self-reflection or hypocritically (Matthew 7:1-5). A society, community, or individual without self-reflection becomes terrible. Usually, power that should be criticized demands silence. A truly pure person, who needs no criticism, is open to any criticism.
* Reason for Collapse The reason for collapsing in public is due to problems with God, who sees in secret.
* When the collapse is severe, do not self-flagellate with guilt, but repent for not enjoying the Lord.

* Diner Food VS Hospital Food The difference between the hospital choosing the meal and the driver choosing it creates results in taste, price, and speed. If I decide, I will fall into corruption, but if God decides, it will be holy.

* Be Cautious When You Have Much People tend to move away from God when they feel prosperous. They draw near to Him when, even after giving and giving, they still feel a sense of lack. Though I call day and night, and offer praise, I always feel a sense of lack. - Hymn 82
* Extravagance Q God, are the things I desire extravagant? A Yes, they are extravagant. Q Yes?, Ah~~, Yes! ㅠㅠ
* Change Direction Go in the right direction that God desires, not the good direction you think.
* True Spiritual Discernment It is not about discerning others, but about looking back at yourself, humbling yourself, and looking to the Lord.
* Reason for Giving Power He wants us to rule ourselves with the Word rather than to oppress others with power.

* Walk in the Light Living in the light does not mean becoming good and sterile. It means coming into the light and living by confessing sins without hiding anything. Then He will cleanse us from all unrighteousness. Darkness means living in concealment. Repentance is power. If we walk in the light, as He is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus His Son cleanses us from all sin. - 1 John 1:7

A broken heart has always existed and will continue to exist. Do not let that broken heart become a desire of sin, but offer it as a sacrifice the Lord desires. The sacrifice the Lord desires is a broken heart.

To love more, one who has been forgiven much loves more. Repentance is power. Therefore I say to you, her many sins have been forgiven—for she loved much. But he who has been forgiven little loves little. -Luke 7:47
God solves the problem. With complete silence, you must focus on what you should be thankful for and what you should repent of. Correcting your attitude before God is the true solution.
The one who overcomes must forgive if they truly want to overcome.
Cases that progress to severe illness often occur because there are no warning signs, or because even after repeatedly facing death, the symptoms are easily forgotten and old habits are resumed.

God does not say, "I will accept you if you change your behavior." He accepts you as you are the moment you turn to Him. Believers change their lives not to be saved, but because they have been saved. To the woman caught in adultery, Jesus said, "I do not condemn you." Then He said, "Go, and sin no more." True change is not about a few external modifications, but a sincere response from the heart to the love of the Lord who accepts us as we are.

He will not give it until you let go. If you receive it before letting go, you will experience severe side effects. The means become the end. In this way, you become someone who pursues greediness in the pursuit of God, and you will constantly ride the roller coaster of pride and frustration.
Wounds are created when you try to control what you cannot handle. Instead of trying to fix others' lives, repenting and humbling yourself to seek the Lord's help is the first step to recovery.
The Lord did great things not because of 'that,' but 'in spite of it.' Therefore, the more grace you receive, the more repentance overflows.
If you cast away lust and selfish desires and seek His kingdom and righteousness, you will hear His voice.

There are two ways to untangle a knot: one is to cut it with scissors, rendering it unusable, and the other is to miraculously find and remove a single strand of the tangle, making it whole. Let go of the need for validation. The tangles of your life will be unraveled.
Fulfill the purpose of the wilderness faithfully. It is about hearing the words you did not hear and humbling yourself.
You cannot be thankful for what you have been given, and you try to possess what you have not been given. This is because you lack the awareness of God's sovereignty.

Jacob meets Laban. Everyone else knew how cunning Jacob was, but he himself did not. Only after meeting his uncle Laban did he realize how painful such behavior was to others. When contemplating relationship difficulties, you must see the other person within yourself.
He truly grieves when we grieve over what is perishable.
You are not unhappy because you are rejected. You are not unhappy because people ignore you or the world does not give you opportunities. You become unhappy because you ignore the Lord.

If you do not guard your lips, you will have labored in vain and given it to dogs. The background of using rude language is that self-centered thinking leaves no room to empathize with the pain of others. Ultimately, you will be the only one unaware that you will thoroughly reap its fruits and will not be welcomed. Empathy is power.
If you are delaying even though you are ready, it clearly means you are not ready. Especially, your own center is not ready.
You can live even after abandoning the Lord's will. It is called mere survival. It is a decision you will deeply regret. It is like living like a zombie; it is not truly living.

Rejoicing in the Lord is the key to everything. The compulsion to commit sin departs, and there is happiness in deeply entering the Lord's presence. This means becoming a new being. It is a thirst that worldly desires and fleshly lust can never satisfy, a thirst that only God can fill.

Do not just bail out water. When a ship is wrecked, you must not just bail out water. First, you must plug the hole. Bailing water when the incident occurred due to a lack of anointing is madness. Bailing water will never bring resolution, even if you do it your whole life.

When an emotional downpour occurs, wait. When an emotional downpour rages, it is difficult to see clearly. If you do not rush, but step back and wait for the downpour to subside, things will become clearer, and you will see the greed hidden behind hasty judgments. That greed causes you to miss the best in pursuit of the good, and sometimes it appears in an incredibly religious guise.

When you get angry at someone clueless, you get more angry at the fact that they still don't know than at the mistake itself. The problem is that the standard for that mistake is too varied, self-centered, and not sufficiently shared. Things that are too embarrassing to share also play a part. There is no one who can realize this on their own and adjust; the only one who knows is the one who has no intention of adjusting. That is why many good people scratch their heads and wonder, "What on earth is the problem?"

Which side is easier? What we want is status, honor, health, material possessions, and popularity. These are on the easy side for God. What God wants is for us to repent, be contrite, receive forgiveness of sins, and receive grace. This is not easy for God either.

Judgment and slander will always find their owner. If you do not want to be judged, do not judge. Slander destroys faith and causes the least to stumble, leading them to self-judgment, but mercy triumphs over judgment. What you pride yourself on as a cool and firm character is actually an anchor that wrecks your life.
Those who deceive themselves are people who ask without intending to listen. They do not listen until they hear the answer they want.
Amen. Can you be more humbled and broken to repent? So that I can use you.
To return to the Father, stop eating the carob pods that the pigs eat. Then you will have the desire to return. Stop eating the rotten carob pods and run home to your Father's house.

The reason to repent is that the kingdom of heaven is near. That means settling accounts according to what you have done. Therefore, cast off the shameful deeds of darkness and come into the light. The darkness you deal with now will not be mentioned later.
The greatest work of the Holy Spirit is to cause repentance.

The reason you cannot keep the Lord's Day holy is not because you sleep too much or have too much work. It is not because there is someone you dislike at church. It is simply because your relationship with God is so distant that you begrudge even two or three hours for Him. God is life.
If you feel pressed for time, try fasting. You will realize that time is actually very long. It is only because you are wasting time on useless, perishable things.

Seek His kingdom and His righteousness first. The Pharisees were excellent in their observance of the law, but because they first tried to prove themselves through human relationships, they were rejected. The tax collector, though full of flaws, was shown mercy through his relationship with the Lord. Happiness and destiny are distinguished by the priority of relationships.
Tyrant and Worshipper: If you take out your own broken heart on people, you become a tyrant, but if you offer it to God, you become a true worshipper.
Repentance: Do not harbor all your faults; confess them before the merciful Lord. He will bless you.
When you are suffering, the thing you must do is to become lower. At first, you become lower as a reaction to the pain, but ultimately it becomes a humility that saves life.

The Subject of Repentance: Let us repent for not being zealous. Lack of zeal means lack of passion, and lack of passion means lack of fullness. The greatest sin is to have the Lord absent and to fill that void with other things. Therefore, to be zealous and to repent are the same. What harms a love relationship is not deficiency or mistakes, but boredom and indifference. You should overflow, not be squeezed. As many as I love, I rebuke and discipline. So be zealous and repent. - Revelation 3:19

Foolishness and Wisdom: A foolish person blames others, but a wise person finds the cause within themselves. Saul's wickedness does not exempt David from his trials. It is wiser before the Lord to repent by examining our own wrong actions rather than complaining about the words and deeds of others.

The Conclusion of Prejudice: A person who is bound by prejudice towards others, no matter how outstanding, gifted, or spiritual they may be, is called a 'Jonah.' If God prepared 'Jonah' for the repentance of Nineveh, where 120,000 people who could not distinguish their right hand from their left lived, then to show God's love to the stubborn Jonah, He prepared many things such as 'ships and sailors going to Tarshish,' 'a fish,' 'a gourd vine,' 'a scorching east wind,' and 'a worm.'

When you sin and turn away from God, two phenomena occur. The joy of salvation disappears, and a willing spirit is lost. (Psalm 51:12) This is because you grieve and quench the Holy Spirit. (Psalm 51:11) Consequently, if you lose holiness, the Lord's presence disappears, and no transformation will occur, no matter how much ministry you do. (Psalm 51:13)

Change is necessary, but criticizing for the sake of change is ineffective and should never be done. This is because only a select few can perform brain surgery with a sharp scalpel. Judgment belongs to God alone, and if humans dare to judge, they will kill life and the community. "Lest, while you gather the weeds, you also uproot the wheat. Let both grow together until the harvest." - Matthew 13

When changing, you must pay attention. You must discern whether it is change or corruption. When a cynical person is transformed into tears of emotion, it is the work of the Holy Spirit; the opposite is the work of darkness. Regardless of the reason, moving away from God is close to corruption.

Gratitude for Gifts: If you do not appreciate a gift and treat it carelessly, God will take it away and give it to those who are grateful. The value of a gift is not its face value but the preciousness of the giver. Samuel said to him, "The Lord has torn the kingdom of Israel from you this day and has given it to a neighbor of yours who is better than you." - 1 Samuel 15:28

When vitamins are deficient, illness and even death can occur. If the Lord is deficient in me, far more serious damage and pain will come. Do not seek solutions in the wrong places; come to your senses quickly and come before the Lord. Otherwise, 'self-pity' will occupy the place where the Lord is absent, leading you to resentment and emptiness.

How do we distinguish between criticism and discernment? Is it by a cold standard and justification? No. Criticism proves faults, gains sympathy, and provides pleasure, but discernment brings tears from God. Criticism is addictive. Pointing out others' flaws does not automatically make you righteous. Instead, you will face judgment without mercy.
The greatest gift you can give someone is forgiveness. God grants the grace of forgiveness before opening a new history.
People may shed tears and beat their chests, but in the end, they will walk in the direction they desire. This is truly sad and heartbreaking.

When people fall into sin, they become addicted to the things of the earth, which are the works of darkness, that God has permitted us to dominate and possess. What God has permitted is to manage and rule for a time, and these are the things of heaven.
Actions without love are hypocrisy. Without love, they are useless and mean nothing. Even when the world ends, love remains, and only the motive will be recognized. Love fervently.

Ministry can be summarized in one word: 'change.' When this 'change' occurs in new believers, it becomes 'establishment,' and when it occurs in existing believers, it becomes 'revival.' 'Change' occurs when the church and believers restore 'holiness,' and when they become 'holy,' they gain 'influence,' and ultimately 'change' happens. Ultimately, everything begins with repentance. Just as the Pyongyang Revival Movement began with the repentance of the proud missionary Hardy, systemic change is a small change, while inner change is a great earthquake-like change.
Restoration is done by the Lord. It is not restored by circumstances or a change of mood. Only the Lord restores me because restoration is returning to the Lord.

Deficiency is due to His kindness. If I lack what I desire, it is because the Lord has not given it to me. Instead of lamenting what the Lord has not given, it is right to be humble about my lack of maturity and faith to receive what I desire. The Lord is always good and kind.

Life cannot be manipulated. You may calculate and act to adjust parts and moments to your liking, but ultimately, life comes to its maker and returns to Him, empty-handed. Lay down your cunning and receive heavenly wisdom, and receive the ability to be temperate, labor, and be faithful.

Samson caught three hundred foxes, burned the Philistines' grain, and said, "Even if I do this, I will be blameless." When people become angry, they become extremely creative in finding ways to get revenge. That is called waste. Be creative in love.
To love someone by forcing them into your own mold is violence. If you understand, you will understand; if you forgive, you can love.

Antitrust Laws - Rule by Serving, Not Dominating: The motive of monopoly is selfishly seeking happiness through domination. It may seem like you will succeed by dominating markets and hegemony, but no individual, nation, or company, after reaching a high position, will find happiness in monopolistic ownership if they unhesitatingly kick away the ladder; it will turn into disaster. Ultimately, God did not call us rulers, but to rule and serve. Those who rule and serve are gentle and humble, fulfilling their calling rather than their ambitions and selfishness. "I will send a winnowing wind upon them from a distance; I will cleanse them. I will send them from the north and gather them from the west; I will bring them from the land of Egypt and from Gilead, and they will come and fill the land of Israel." - Jeremiah 51:2 Those who change the world will be foreigners with different cultures and worldviews.

The Mechanism of Tolerance: Tolerance comes from memory. It is remembering that I was a debtor who had 10,000 talents forgiven. Lack of the joy of salvation, spiritual amnesia, makes people cruel. "Should not you also have had mercy on your fellow servant, as I had mercy on you?" - Matthew 18:33

The Way to Win Even When Envious: You do not lose because you are ashamed and envious. You lose by avoiding. "But my righteous one will live by faith; and if he shrinks back, my soul has no pleasure in him." - Hebrews 10:38

The characteristic of losing the guidance of the Holy Spirit is becoming defensive. This is because you have lost freedom by acting according to your own desires. Please identify what preceded the Holy Spirit, that event, that person, that desire. Even with a driver's license, you often fail in similar courses.

Forgiveness: Forgiveness is not something you give out of kindness, but something you must do to live. The Lord clearly said, "If you do not forgive, you will not be forgiven, and by the judgment you give, you will be judged." But mercy will overcome judgment and boast.

Do you think I made a mistake? You get annoyed and complain to a fellow believer who received the same Holy Spirit in the same worship service and praises the same One. There is a reason for everything. At such times, the Lord asks, "Do you think I made a mistake?" I hope you will hold onto the small things before you lose the big ones. The love that the Lord requires is not easy, but it is not impossible.

Whether you will be happy or unhappy is determined by whether you stake your life on bad choices or good choices. Do not be swayed by arbitrary feelings and emotions; make your decisions based on truth. Then your heart will prevail.
Envy, jealousy, and pride steal joy, block anointing, and prevent growth. Do not react to people; react to God. The power to exalt and to humble belongs to God alone. Psalm 75

The reform of systems and institutions is a fundamental solution. However, it is not the true cause. The cause is human greed—sin. If the fundamental cause is not addressed, the reform of systems will return to square one. Christians need the insight to see priorities and the patience to pursue them.
When we see the stubbornness of believers, what should the church do? It should not be anger and criticism, but tears. Empathy is power.

No matter how meticulously you wash dishes with water flowing from garbage, they will not become clean. No matter how much the Lord wants to pour living water, if my vessel remains in the sewer, all sorts of filth and poison will flow out through me. Leave the place of filth and darkness and come to the place of worship, prayer, and the proclamation of the Word.
Are you shedding tears of mourning? You are truly blessed. Sincere tears are like bombs that break through blocked paths. Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted. Matthew 5:4
Foolish greed and wise aversion. The greed of a fool and the aversion of a wise person are the same. It is forced respect and love.

The reason we need grace and mercy. Even when we try our best, it is undeniable that we can hurt and cause pain to those close to us due to thoughtlessness and shortcomings in various big and small matters. Nevertheless, I keenly feel the grace that my evaluation by others is always more generous than my actual self. Today, I can only be grateful for my growth in God's boundless grace and the mercy of my beloved fellow workers.

The purpose of piety. In 1990, at the Homyongsan Prayer Mountain in Daegu, the Lord rebuked me sternly. He told me not to put on a show for people with His blessings. Many will begin to regard piety as a means to gain advantage. This is a sin of using God by becoming one's own master instead of His servant. Woe to those who seek the approval of men. No matter how pious they may appear, they will be utterly ashamed before the judgment seat of the Lord. Seek the Lord's pleasure.

Finite opportunities. Opportunities are not always available. We only have the opportunity to sow love and reap life while we have breath. For believers, there will be a judgment for rewards, and for unbelievers, a judgment for sin. For it is appointed for man to die once, and after that comes judgment. Hebrews 9:27. To each of them was given a white robe, and they were told to rest a little longer until the number of their fellow servants and their brothers should be complete, as they themselves had been killed. Revelation 6:11
None of us plan to fail. Failure comes naturally and gradually. If we refuse to learn from our mistakes, the problems will only grow.

We are patients with a serious illness. When undergoing our own immense surgery, we can go to the brink of death. It is truly ruthless surgery. Through this major surgery, we extract the diseased self within us. The scalpel and forceps that God uses are none other than our own authorities who are so adept at digging into our wounded hearts and weaknesses.

The land of Canaan did not become holy because it was an idol-worshipping land. Mount Horeb, where Moses always went, was not called a holy mountain because it was a holy mountain. It is still a land full of sin, still a barren and desolate land, but it is called a holy land because the Lord's presence is there. Where you stand, if you take off (give up) your shoes (rights), it will become a land where God is with you and reigns. In the land where you are suffering now, acknowledge the Lordship of the Lord. It will be acknowledged through your servantship. The more you suffer, the more you should take off your shoes. And he said, "Do not come near; take off your sandals from your feet, for the place on which you are standing is holy ground." Exodus 3:5

Dedication made with grumbling... the body will be tired, and the Lord may not accept it. Let us do it gladly. Let us love and do it with joy as if today were the last day. 2 Corinthians 9:7. Each one must do as he has decided in his heart, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.

Humans can never deceive God. He knows my sitting and rising, my lying down and my waking. He discerns the twisted self-love disguised behind brilliant good deeds and the well-intentioned motives hidden by painful failures. In the end, we will all stand before God.
If you truly do not want to regret your life, you must live by loving. If you do not love, you become arrogant, give and receive wounds. Ultimately, it is a meaningless life.

When life doesn't go as planned, we look inward, even if we don't want to. It is God's grace. We must break the cycle of making our own plans without the Holy Spirit and becoming angry when they don't go as intended. Without the Holy Spirit, I am nothing. Let's venture with the Holy Spirit.

The more self-controlled a person is, the longer and harder the trials will be. 'Self-defense,' reacting to protect oneself, becomes a poison. It is easy if you acknowledge and entrust yourself to the Holy Spirit.

We live in an era where people easily condemn without understanding themselves. This is despite the fact that the Lord has never entrusted such condemnation to us. I am not in a position to say... Brothers, do not speak evil against one another. The one who speaks against a brother or judges his brother, speaks against the law and judges the law. If you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law but a judge. There is only one Lawgiver and Judge, who is able to save and to destroy. But who are you to judge your neighbor? James 4:10,11
If you boast, the Lord will surely reclaim it.

You must examine your inner self. There are not many fools who are consciously arrogant. Pride is strong in the subconscious. Pride is a murderer we don't know. Ambition disguised as a calling deceives me and others, but it never deceives the Lord. Let us fall before the Lord's fiery gaze and melt away.
The book of Proverbs often mentions 'the fool.' The fool mentioned there is not someone with a low IQ or someone inflexible, but someone so intelligent that they do not rely on the Word of the Lord.
If you truly love someone, you must learn to love them. Uninformed love easily becomes rude love. And it is bound to cause deep wounds.
Greater sins than lust, envy, and pride are not seeking and calling upon the Lord. If you had sought and called, there would have been no fruit of such sins. Return to God.

The secret to overcoming sin. "Now the child grew and became strong in spirit, and was in the wilderness until the day of his public appearance to Israel." Luke 2:40. The grace of God was upon Jesus! What happens when God's grace is poured upon a person? The secret is in Romans 6:14. "For sin will have no dominion over you, since you are not under law but under grace."
Being overly weak in the face of sin and worry is due to excessive and unnecessary concern. What does it have to do with me? We simply need to go to the Lord who seeks and calls us.

Even if you gather all your sins, worries, anger, and hatred and burn them, it only manages your expression; the bondage of slavery is not automatically broken. Even if you change your environment, due to the 'law of problem preservation,' life's conflicts will continue. Become a servant of Jesus, the only Truth. Then you will be truly free. Where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom, and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.

If sin and repentance are repeated, you are always walking in place. If you walk by the Spirit, you will not fulfill the desires of the flesh. This is because the Spirit does not permit it and gives the power to overcome and rule. Receive the fullness of the Spirit and the power of self-control.
He was a friend of sinners and tax collectors.

If sinners were left as sinners, all the faithful in the Bible would not have been saved. We will see revival when we stop blaming and people who take responsibility stand up. Wounds bring wounds, and grace gives birth to grace. There are no perpetrators. Everyone is just someone who needs forgiveness. In acceptance and forgiveness, a miracle of amazing change occurs, which cannot be valued by worldly standards.

Do not condemn yourself, being caught in guilt. Condemnation leads to self-abuse, cutting you off from fellowship with the Lord and causing you to commit more serious sins. Do not condemn, but repent. We are prone to sin and we do sin. If sinning is because we are sinners, it would be despair. However, we sin even though we are righteous in the Lord's sight through His precious blood. Therefore, we rely on and confess to the Lord even more. The condemnation of sinners leads to death, but the repentance of the righteous leads to life.

When you continuously look only at the Lord, you realize that you don't even have the leisure to criticize others. God's revival begins from the repentance and confession of my spirit, not from stopping blame.
Humble yourselves under the mighty hand of God.
True love also lays down pride.

Sunday Message: 1. Do not live as you wish, but live a life based on necessity. You may want many pens, but you only need one to write. You may want to date many people, but you will marry one. Do not live by greed, but live by being filled. 2. He honors those who honor Him, and despises those who despise Him. Do not treat God carelessly. Serve Him with all your heart. Reflect on who your master is and what the cause of your actions is. 3. There must be mourning and sorrow. Do not stand by as if watching a fire while your life and community crumble. Ask for God's rule in your workplace, academy, family, and relationships. Those who do not ask have no right to happiness.
If you are experiencing deep sorrow in your heart right now, confess this: I have died. Then you will see what the Lord does.

If I had what I regret not having now, what God did not permit, I would have become closer to sin and further from God. Do not lament not having more, but be thankful that because of it, you received the Lord as a gift.
It is by Your great grace that I have come this far. How can I express the immeasurable love of Your grace? I, the chief of sinners, can only bow my head and shed tears.

True restoration does not begin with tidying up a desk or changing one's hairstyle. It begins with eradicating the idols within us. An idol is anything we rely on more than God, or anything that exerts greater influence on our decision-making. Before this nation, this church, or this household can be set right, we must first stand firm for everyone to be saved. Joshua also said to the people, "Consecrate yourselves, for tomorrow the Lord will do amazing things among you." - Joshua 3:5

The truly frightening diseases are those without warning signs. If there is no pain, we will not know if our limbs are rotting away. To escape spiritual leprosy, one must experience excruciating pain. That is a blessing.

The most foolish man in the world is the one who tries to conquer a woman. Nations and peoples also perish when hardliners display futile pride. The power given by the Lord is not to crush the other person, but rather to build them up and yield to them.
Do not get angry to save face. Especially with family... People are more important than saving face.

Lions on the savanna do not run at any time. Their momentary speed is incredibly fast, but with their massive bodies, they cannot run for long like other herbivores. However, when the chance comes, they give their all. And once they catch it, they never let go. Starting Friday evening is a revival meeting. If you want to recover your first love and have a true encounter with God, be sure to attend. And please encourage your other friends to attend. I love you in the Lord.

Have you recently heard someone tell you to repent, recover your first love, and overcome? This is a very clear and irreplaceable message of utmost importance. Furthermore, this is not comparable to mere pleasantries; it is directly related to your relationship with Jesus and your life, concerning "where my attention and affection flow." Blessed are those who hear the voice of God that they ought to hear. Blessed are those who have someone who tells them such things. But I have this against you: You have abandoned the love you had at first. Therefore, remember from where you have fallen, repent, and do the things you did at first. If you do not, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place. - Revelation 2:4,5
If you become addicted to pleasure, you will be buried up to your wings.

The prodigal son was able to return home because there was no one even giving him the carob pods he was eating. If one receives just enough, they can never repent until they die. Being driven to a corner with nothing to rely on can be a blessing. Let them rely only on the Lord, with nothing else.
To have a truly new relationship with God means to have a different relationship with sin as well. How has God possessed my heart and worked within me?
God does not condemn or accuse me for the faults I reveal without hiding them. Instead, He gives me the power to break free from them.
God awakens our sleeping souls through 'problems.' However, He does not immediately solve those problems. He waits a little longer until we truly desire change.
God leaves us to our own devices when we try to calculate and scheme, but He will surely deal with us later. This is because the Lord works with low cost and high efficiency.
A people who do not know gratitude will not perish. They do not need to try to perish. Because they have already perished in the greatest way.

God sometimes gives us uncomfortable emotions that we cannot understand. He is telling us to turn back. He is telling us to stop looking at people's opinions and the environment, and to look only at the Lord.

There is a truth that is finally realized when we cease our complaints and greed before God: that He desires to give Himself to us more than for us to gain something. "Be still, and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth." Psalm 46:10. Keep watch over your mouth and guard the words of your lips. Proverbs 5:2.
If you love the people God has sent more than God, He will take them away. There are priorities in life. He will take away anything that comes before God.

When David sinned, God lamented that He had 'already given enough' and 'would have given more if it were needed.' To take what the Lord has not given through human means is rebellion. Instead of rebelling, repent and be thankful.
People do not change through threats. Rather, they change when they are forgiven. "Neither do I condemn you; go, and sin no more."

The dark events happening in my workplace, home, relationships, and studies are simply signals to return to the essence. They are telling me to step out of superficial problems and stand at the true center. Such problems arise not because of bad luck, but because of the absence of Jesus.
Be transformed by the renewing of your mind, that you may discern what is the good, pleasing, and perfect will of God.

What if it had rained when David sinned and he hadn't seen Bathsheba bathing? He would still have sinned. This is because his state was already one of sin. The lack of fullness in the Lord is already a grave sin. The sun will rise someday, temptations will come, and trials will be faced. The important thing is what fills me within. There is no vacuum in the world. Everything is filled with something.
The reason it was so difficult was because you couldn't let go. If you let go, you have nothing more to lose, and from there, the Lord's miracles begin.

The difficulties and hurts I experience are not reality. We must first consider why such pain came and who allowed it. The reality is that I am not looking at the Lord properly and am avoiding Him. The other person has not become an adversary; rather, Jehovah has placed them as a thorn in my side to call me to repentance.

There is a saying, "Fall down seven times, stand up eight." This is the power of positivity. This saying is a disaster for those without Jesus. It is like Pharaoh and the Egyptians facing ten plagues, saying, "Let's try again," or the prodigal sons with absolute positivity trying to get up from the pigsty without even pigweed. This is truly writing the worst-case scenario.

Gathering a lot of carob pods to eat with the pigs was not success. Blessed is the one who, even when falling short and starving, remembers his father's house and turns back. Do not slowly perish just enough to avoid death; realize the futility and turn back quickly.

Do not desire too many things. That is greed. Greed surely gives birth to death. Be thankful for the handful of air to breathe that was given to me, the Bible, and a heart that can run only by loving the Lord. That is enough.

In the wilderness, shortcuts do not work. Only the Lord's presence was the way to live. However, in Canaan, it became possible to live to some extent through farming and trade. That is when idols entered. Trying to live well without His presence is a great sin.

"Do you love me?" Peter, who had denied the Lord, hesitantly says he loves Him. Now, that love is fulfilled not by his own strength or boasts, but only when the Lord's sovereignty permits it, within the Lord's grace that makes me who I am. Therefore, no one has anything to boast about. For love is here, not that we loved God, but...
**Disarmament** Trying to defend oneself is the instinct of sinful humanity. When we lay down self-defense and disarm, we experience death, and in grace, the Lord protects us.
Past can hurt, but as I see it, you can either run from it, or learn from it!

Pray and serve: This is the Lord's voice. "Date now": This is the voice of hormones. Separation is more important than meeting. If there is no sobriety and no self-reflection, you are being led not by the Lord's guidance, but by greed and emotions. God is almighty and can faithfully lead His way without my "anxiety." Therefore, selling faith to seek profit must be eradicated. If the same mistake is repeated, it is not an accident but a greed that earnestly desires the "mistake."
In the wilderness, only God commands advancement. We move forward as much as we have changed. Therefore, He truly wants us to become humble. So that the Lord can work sufficiently.

**God's Solution** 1. Many things happen simultaneously in the church. 2. The greatest concern is that believers are drifting away from worship and from God. 3. There are many reasons for this: relationships, heartbreak, self-pity, victim mentality, self-blame, doubt, family, and sometimes circumstances do not cooperate. 3. A competent leader is one who effectively eradicates all these problems. Then they would be a top candidate for a large church's invitation. 4. However, the person God desires is not such a person. It is someone who tells the Lord about all their problems in detail and clings to the cross. 5. Even at this moment, His salvation, who stands on the rock struck by the staff, shedding the blood of the cross, is accomplished for us. (Acknowledging that it is not my problem) The people quarreled with Moses, saying, "Give us water to drink." Moses said to them, "Why do you quarrel with me?" - Exodus 17:2 (If you implore the Lord) Moses cried out to the Lord, saying, "What shall I do with this people?" - Exodus 17:4 (The Lord accomplishes salvation through the cross) Take the staff in your hand and go, strike the rock, and water will come out of it... - Exodus 17:5,6

If your life is so packed that there is no empty space for the Lord to enter, then such possessions and attachments are not a blessing but a curse. His presence must take precedence over work. He who sent me is with me. He has not left me alone, for I always do what pleases him. - John 8:29

If there is someone who constantly upsets you, making it difficult to love them, you do not yet fully love God. If you cannot love a visible person because you dislike them, it is because you cannot love the invisible God. This is deceiving yourself with beautiful thoughts about God. Anyone who claims to love God but hates his brother is a liar, for he who does not love his brother whom he has seen cannot love God whom he has not seen. We have received this commandment from Him, that he who loves God should love his brother also. - 1 John 4:20,21

You wear your best clothes to a wedding. It would be shameful to stand there with a dirty body, hands, and feet while wearing your best clothes. Create in me a clean heart, O God, and renew a right spirit within me. - Psalm 51:10

I gained much, but strangely, I was not satisfied. Even though I drank much, I was not content. Even wearing good branded clothes, it strangely did not feel warm. It was as if I was bringing a lot of money home after receiving wages, but it was like a leaky purse, and the wealth did not accumulate. It was truly strange. "For you have sown much, but harvested little; you eat, but you are not satisfied; you drink, but you are not filled; you clothe yourselves, but no one is warm; and he who earns wages earns wages to put into a bag with holes." - Haggai 1:6 Why was this? "You looked for much, but behold, it came to little. And when you brought it home, I blew it away. Says the Lord of hosts. Because of my house that is a wilderness, while each of you is busy with his own house." - Haggai 1:9

The reason I fell is because I did not look fully to the Lord. Even if you fall today, give thanks and rejoice. You can get up again by looking at the cross. Please look at yourself not as the "self" you condemn, but as the "self" God loves. It was not because the disciples were great, but because the Lord's cross was love.

You should be grieved by things that grieve your heart. You should mourn for not being able to look to the Lord. The Lord seeks such people. The composition of tears may be similar, but the source of the tears must be different.
A fish leaving the water is not called freedom. It is called fish soup.

I am exceedingly displeased with your worship, finding it truly bothersome and difficult, and I will not accept it. - Malachi 1:13 The Lord does not accept formal worship that lacks heart, soul, and intention. The Lord honors those who honor Him and despises those who despise Him. If you go out with human skill and fire that the Lord has not commanded, not relying on the precious blood and truth, the result is that there is no response of God's fire on our altar, fabricated excitement abounds, and division and trials arise. We must be restored to worship with the Spirit and truth. - John 4:24
If I remove a little negativity and a bit more cynicism from my heart, I can extend my hand to more people.

When you see the faults of others and feel condemnation in your heart, you are full. You are full of worldly desires, so you cannot see God. Just as one who suffers physical hardship ceases from sin, if you bear the Lord's cross, you will only feel sorry and thankful.

1 Corinthians 13:11 - If your relationships with others are twisted, it means you have moved further away from God. Forgiveness and acceptance are not just for the other person. The greatest beneficiary is yourself.

If you want to be crucified, you must desire the Word to become a sword and pierce your heart. It is painful and hurts when pierced, but only within that piercing, new comfort is poured out from the blood that flows.
Trying to solve everything in its place with my own methods is excessive greed. The problem is solved, but it leaves greater wounds. There is a time for the Lord in all things.

* Only nations that realize the value of youth will survive. 1. Greece and Iceland, two countries, borrowed astronomical sums of money in a low-interest rate era and engaged in risky investments. Thanks to this, they enjoyed a lavish boom when interest rates were low, but when credit crunch began with the 2008 global financial crisis, they were ruined in an instant. 2. Greece poured all its remaining financial capacity into repaying the debts of banks and large corporations, and by prioritizing budget cuts for the younger generation, astronomical national debt was transferred to the youth who were deprived of basic benefits. 3. As a result of this sacrifice of youth, instead of recovering, Greece is at the bottom with the worst economic recession. Growth rate -3.4% 4. On the other hand, Iceland indicted about 90 people, including bankers and politicians, for causing the financial crisis or failing to respond appropriately. Contrary to Greece, Iceland increased social security spending by a whopping 36% compared to before the financial crisis. Thanks to the social safety net, young people in Iceland could all receive vocational training and attempt re-employment, and the young people who succeeded in their comeback injected surprising vitality into the collapsing Icelandic economy. 5. As a result, Iceland achieved an astonishing economic growth rate of 3.5%, far exceeding the European average, and its unemployment rate was 4.9%, less than half the European average. 6. We see how a nation that abandons its youth crumbles. 7. Even Daewongun in the late Joseon Dynasty said that history at least fears.
